usage.name: Abort cycle safely
usage.statement: Stop a running cycle and place the system in a safe state during an abnormal event.
usage.description: This matters when a fault, leak, or emergency requires halting the process without hazard.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that cleaning or sterilizing media enter sterile product paths during the abort
needs.plain: When stopping the cycle, keep cleaning or germ-killing fluids and gases out of the sterile product paths. This protects the product paths during an emergency or planned stop.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of operator exposure to released hot or caustic process media during the abort
needs.plain: Protect workers from hot or harmful process liquids that may be released when a cycle is stopped early.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that a pressure surge during the abort damages piping, vessels, or seals
needs.plain: Prevent a sudden rise in pressure while stopping the process from damaging pipes, containers, or seals. This helps stop equipment damage and possible leaks.

needs.stmt: Reduce the degree of ambiguity in the operator's confirmation that the CIP/SIP system is in a safe state after the abort
Reduce the time from abort initiation until hazardous flow, pressure, and temperature reach safe conditions

usage.name: Adjust cycle parameters
usage.statement: Modify permitted process setpoints while a cleaning or sterilization cycle is actively running.
usage.description: Operator changes flow rate, dwell time, temperature, or other permitted variables mid-cycle to correct deviations or accommodate process conditions.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that a mid-cycle adjustment leaves part of the system below required cleaning or sterilization exposure
needs.plain: When changing the cleaning or germ-killing cycle, make sure every part still gets enough treatment. This helps prevent areas from being left insufficiently cleaned or disinfected.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of incorrect setpoint values entered during an active cycle
Reduce the degree to which indicated process conditions differ from actual conditions when selecting an adjustment
Minimize the likelihood that the setpoint needed to correct the deviation falls outside the permitted range
Reduce the duration of delay between recognizing a deviation and applying the permitted setpoint change

usage.name: Advance cycle phase
usage.statement: Manually advance the active cycle from one programmed phase to the next.
usage.description: Operator intervenes to progress the cycle when automatic phase-transition criteria cannot be met or when a non-standard sequence is required.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of manually advancing the active cycle before required cleaning or sterilization conditions are achieved
needs.plain: Prevent the active process from being moved forward by hand before cleaning or sterilizing is complete. This helps ensure the required safety conditions are actually met.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of operator exposure to hazardous pressure, heat, or cleaning chemicals during the phase transition
needs.plain: Protect the operator from dangerous pressure, heat, or cleaning chemicals while moving the machine to the next step. The change should only happen when it is safe to do so.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of manually advancing the active cycle to an unintended programmed phase
needs.plain: Prevent the active process from being moved by hand to the wrong next step. This helps avoid running the process in an unintended way.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of ambiguity in cycle-state and transition-readiness information available to the operator
needs.plain: Give the operator clear information about where the processing cycle is and whether it is ready to move to the next step. This helps prevent advancing at the wrong time.

needs.stmt: Reduce the frequency of manual phase-advance attempts blocked by unresolved transition interlocks

usage.name: Circulate cleaning solution
usage.statement: Circulate detergent and rinse solutions through the process circuit during a cycle.
usage.description: This matters because effective soil removal depends on sustained fluid flow across all wetted surfaces.

needs.stmt: Minimize the amount of detergent remaining in the process circuit after final rinse circulation
needs.plain: Remove as much cleaning liquid as possible from the equipment after the final rinse. This helps prevent cleaning liquid from remaining in the process equipment.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of directing detergent or rinse solution through the wrong circuit path during the cycle
needs.plain: Prevent cleaning or rinsing liquid from going through the wrong set of pipes or equipment. This helps ensure the right parts are cleaned and avoids unintended mixing or damage.

needs.stmt: Minimize the amount of cleaning solution escaping from the process circuit during the cycle
needs.plain: Keep as much cleaning liquid as possible inside the system while it is being cleaned. This reduces leaks, waste, and exposure to the cleaning liquid.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration that solution flow remains below the required circulation threshold during the cycle
Reduce the percentage of intended process circuit sections bypassed by the circulated solution during the cycle

usage.name: Control cycle temperature
usage.statement: Maintain the solution temperature within the setpoint range throughout the cycle.
usage.description: This matters because temperature directly affects the chemical reaction rate and sterilization effectiveness.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of solution temperature nonuniformity among locations in the circulation path during the cycle
needs.plain: Keep the liquid at nearly the same temperature throughout the circulation path during the cycle. This helps the whole batch receive the intended treatment.

needs.stmt: Minimize the duration that the solution temperature remains below the setpoint range during the cycle
needs.plain: Reduce the time that the solution is colder than the allowed temperature range during the process. This helps keep the process within its required conditions.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of difference between the indicated and actual solution temperature during the cycle
needs.plain: Keep the temperature shown by the equipment as close as possible to the solution’s actual temperature during the process. This helps ensure the solution is treated correctly.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree to which the solution temperature exceeds the setpoint range during the cycle
needs.plain: Keep the solution from getting hotter than the allowed temperature range during the process.

needs.stmt: Reduce the frequency of heating or cooling utility interruptions during the cycle

usage.name: Document cycle results
usage.statement: Record cycle parameters and outcomes for each completed cleaning or sterilization event.
usage.description: Quality technician or operator captures time-stamped data — temperatures, flow rates, chemical concentrations, pass/fail status — for each cycle to support traceability and compliance.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of attributing recorded parameters and outcomes to the wrong completed cycle
needs.plain: Make sure recorded settings and results are assigned to the correct completed production cycle. This prevents records from being mixed up between cycles.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that a completed cycle record is unavailable when needed for compliance review
needs.plain: Make sure the finished record of each completed process run can be found when people need to check that requirements were followed.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of completed cycle records containing inaccurate parameter or outcome values
Reduce the percentage of completed cycle records with required parameters or outcomes omitted
Reduce the time between cycle completion and recording the cycle parameters and outcomes
needs.plain: There should be less time between finishing a production run and recording its settings and results. This helps people quickly see what happened during the run.

usage.name: Dose cleaning chemicals
usage.statement: Deliver cleaning agents at target concentrations into the circulating solution during a cycle.
usage.description: System meters detergent, acid, alkali, or sanitizer into the circuit to achieve and hold programmed concentration setpoints.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of the wrong cleaning agent entering the circulating solution during a cycle
needs.plain: Make sure the correct cleaning liquid is added so the wrong chemical does not enter the liquid being circulated. This prevents poor cleaning or damage to the equipment.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of incompatible cleaning agents mixing in the circuit during dosing
needs.plain: Prevent cleaning chemicals that should not be combined from mixing in the equipment. This helps avoid unsafe reactions and ineffective cleaning.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of cleaning-agent supply being depleted before cycle completion
needs.plain: Make sure there is enough cleaning solution to finish the entire cleaning cycle. Running out could leave the equipment only partly cleaned.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration that cleaning-agent concentration remains outside the programmed target band during a cycle
Reduce the amount of cleaning chemical released outside the closed circuit during dosing

usage.name: Initiate cleaning cycle
usage.statement: Start an in-place cleaning cycle on the target process circuit or vessel.
usage.description: Operator triggers a programmed CIP cycle to begin automated cleaning of connected process equipment.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of initiating the cleaning cycle while the target circuit or vessel remains connected to an active product path
needs.plain: Do not start cleaning while the equipment to be cleaned is still connected to a live product supply. This helps prevent cleaning liquid from entering the product path or product from being affected.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of selecting the wrong cleaning program or target for the cycle
needs.plain: Make sure the correct cleaning program and the correct equipment or area are selected. This helps ensure the intended place is cleaned properly.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of required cleaning media or utilities being unavailable when cycle initiation is attempted
needs.plain: Make sure the water, cleaning materials, and other supplies needed for cleaning are available before cleaning starts. This prevents the cleaning cycle from being delayed or stopped.

needs.stmt: Reduce the degree of ambiguity in the indication that the cleaning cycle has started on the intended target
Reduce the duration of cycle initiation delay caused by unresolved start-permissive conditions

usage.name: Manage return flow
usage.statement: Direct returning cycle media to the designated destination.
usage.description: This controls whether returning media follows the intended recirculation, recovery, treatment, or discharge path.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of contaminated or out-of-spec media entering the recirculation or recovery path
needs.plain: Avoid sending contaminated or unsuitable material back into the return or recovery route. This prevents bad material from spreading through the process.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of the designated destination lacking capacity when returning cycle media arrives
needs.plain: Make sure the receiving location has enough room before the returning liquid or material arrives. This helps prevent overflow, delays, and spills.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of return-flow interruption caused by a blocked or restricted path
Reduce the amount of returning cycle media lost through leakage before reaching the designated destination
Reduce the percentage of returning cycle media directed to an unintended destination

usage.name: Monitor cycle parameters
usage.statement: Track flow, temperature, conductivity, and pressure during an active cycle.
usage.description: This matters because real-time monitoring confirms the cleaning is proceeding within validated limits.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of interpreting cycle readings against incorrect validated limits
needs.plain: Make sure process readings are compared with the correct approved limits for that cycle. Using the wrong limits could make a problem appear acceptable when it is not.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of deviation between indicated and actual cycle parameter values
needs.plain: Keep the values shown by the equipment as close as possible to the values that are actually occurring. This helps ensure the process is running as intended.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that a critical parameter excursion is overlooked amid simultaneous cycle notifications
needs.plain: Reduce the chance that an important measurement changing beyond its safe range is missed among many alerts. This helps people notice problems quickly.

needs.stmt: Minimize the percentage of the active cycle with unavailable flow, temperature, conductivity, or pressure readings
Reduce the time between a flow, temperature, conductivity, or pressure excursion beyond validated limits and operator recognition

usage.name: Operate in manual mode
usage.statement: Run individual cleaning steps manually to handle a non-standard cleaning task.
usage.description: This matters during commissioning, testing, or unusual soiling that automated recipes do not cover.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that a manually controlled cleaning or sterilization step falls outside validated parameter limits
needs.plain: Keep hand-controlled cleaning or germ-killing steps within the safe, tested ranges so the equipment is properly cleaned or made sterile.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of cleaning media entering an unintended process path during manual operation
needs.plain: When operating the equipment by hand, keep cleaning liquid on its intended route. This prevents it from entering the wrong part of the process.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of technician exposure to hot, pressurized, or chemically hazardous media during manual operation
needs.plain: Keep the technician or operator from coming into contact with hot, pressurized, or chemically dangerous materials while controlling the machine by hand. This helps prevent burns, other injuries, and harmful exposure.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of required cleaning steps omitted or executed out of sequence during a manual task
Reduce the number of manually controlled steps lacking complete execution records

usage.name: Pause active cycle
usage.statement: Pause the active cycle at an allowable operating point.
usage.description: This permits temporary intervention without immediately terminating the entire cycle.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that pausing the active cycle compromises required cleaning or sterilization conditions
needs.plain: Lower the chance that pausing a running cycle causes the required cleaning or germ-killing conditions to be lost. This helps ensure the process can resume safely and correctly.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the paused cycle resumes before the temporary intervention is complete
needs.plain: Make sure the paused machine does not start again until the temporary work is finished. This helps prevent injury, damage, or an incomplete repair.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of operator exposure to hazardous pressure, temperature, or cleaning agents while the active cycle is paused
needs.plain: Keep workers safe from dangerous pressure, heat, or cleaning chemicals while the running cycle is temporarily stopped. Pausing the equipment should not expose them to these hazards.

needs.stmt: Reduce the degree of uncertainty about whether the active cycle is paused at an allowable operating point
Reduce the time the process operator waits for the active cycle to reach an allowable operating point

usage.name: Recover reusable solutions
usage.statement: Capture and return spent detergent or rinse solutions for reuse in later cycles.
usage.description: This matters when solution recovery reduces chemical and water consumption per cleaning cycle.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that recovered detergent or rinse solution contains contaminants above reuse limits
needs.plain: Reduce the chance that saved cleaning liquid contains too much dirt or other unwanted material to be safely used again. This helps prevent contamination during later cleaning.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that incompatible recovered solutions are commingled during capture or return
needs.plain: Keep different recovered liquids that should not be mixed separate when they are collected or returned. This helps prevent unwanted reactions or makes the recovered liquids unusable.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration that captured solution remains unavailable for a later cleaning cycle
Reduce the amount of reusable detergent or rinse solution lost to discharge during recovery
Reduce the percentage of recovered solution outside chemical-strength limits for reuse

usage.name: Respond to cycle alarms
usage.statement: Acknowledge and respond to alarms generated during an active cleaning or sterilization cycle.
usage.description: Operator or control room personnel assess alarm conditions, take corrective action, and clear alarms to allow the cycle to continue or abort safely.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of allowing the cycle to continue when the alarm condition requires a safe abort
needs.plain: Make sure the process does not continue when a warning means it must be stopped safely. Stopping at the right time helps prevent damage, danger, or contamination.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of operator exposure to hazardous cleaning agents, hot fluids, or steam during alarm response
needs.plain: Keep the operator from touching dangerous cleaning chemicals, hot liquids, or steam while responding to an alarm. This helps prevent burns, poisoning, or other injuries.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of misidentifying the condition that triggered the cycle alarm
needs.plain: Correctly identify what caused the cycle warning before taking action. This helps the worker respond to the real problem instead of making the situation worse.

needs.stmt: Reduce the frequency of conflicting corrective actions by field and control room personnel during alarm response
Reduce the time between cycle alarm generation and operator acknowledgment

usage.name: Resume interrupted cycle
usage.statement: Resume an interrupted cycle from an approved operating state.
usage.description: This allows operation to continue after the interruption has been assessed and cleared.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the interruption condition remains unresolved when the cycle resumes
needs.plain: Resolve the problem that stopped the process before allowing the cycle to continue.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the cycle is resumed from an operating state other than the approved state
needs.plain: Make sure an interrupted production run can restart only from the correct, approved condition. This helps prevent the process from resuming in an unsafe or incorrect state.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the approved process hold period expires before the cycle resumes
Minimize the likelihood that the sterile boundary is compromised before the cycle resumes
Reduce the number of cycle steps skipped or repeated after resumption

usage.name: Route cycle media
usage.statement: Direct cleaning or sterilization media through the selected in-place circuit via valve sequencing.
usage.description: Operator or control system positions valves and selects flow paths to route the active cycle medium through the intended process circuit.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of routing active cycle medium through the wrong process circuit
needs.plain: Lower the chance that the liquid or gas used in an active cycle is sent through the wrong set of pipes. This helps ensure the process reaches the intended equipment and avoids unintended routes.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of active cycle medium leaking across isolation boundaries into an unselected sterile process path
needs.plain: Prevent the active process liquid from leaking across a separation into a sterile path that was not selected. This helps keep the wrong liquid from entering a sterile process.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of unsafe pressure buildup from a blocked flow path during valve sequencing
needs.plain: Prevent dangerous pressure from building up if a passage is blocked while valves are opened or closed in sequence. This protects the equipment and nearby people.

needs.stmt: Reduce the amount of active cycle medium discharged through unintended drain paths
Reduce the percentage of the selected in-place circuit bypassed by active cycle medium

usage.name: Select cleaning program
usage.statement: Select the appropriate cleaning or sterilization program for the target equipment before starting a cycle.
usage.description: Operator chooses from pre-validated programs stored in the system to match the soil type, product residue, and equipment to be cleaned.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of selecting a stored program that is obsolete or unapproved for the current equipment and residue
needs.plain: Choose a current, approved cleaning process that suits the machine and the leftover material being removed. This helps ensure the equipment is cleaned properly.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of selecting a program outside its validated equipment configuration
needs.plain: Make sure the selected cleaning program is made for the machine being cleaned. This helps prevent the wrong cleaning process from being used.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the required pre-validated program is unavailable when the operator prepares to start a cycle
needs.plain: Make sure the approved cleaning program is available when the operator is ready to start. This helps ensure the equipment is cleaned using the correct instructions.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the target soil or product residue is incorrectly identified before program selection
Reduce the degree of ambiguity about the required cleaning or sterilization endpoint before program selection

usage.name: Sequence multiple circuits
usage.statement: Coordinate cleaning across multiple tanks, lines, or vessels in one operation.
usage.description: This matters when a shared skid must service several process circuits without cross-contamination.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of transferring product residues from one process circuit to another during the cleaning sequence
needs.plain: Reduce the chance that leftover product from one set of pipes is carried into another set during cleaning. This helps prevent different products from mixing or contaminating one another.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of starting cleaning before a process circuit is released and ready
needs.plain: Do not start cleaning a set of connected equipment until it has been approved and is ready.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of routing a cleaning or sterilization cycle to a circuit other than the scheduled circuit
needs.plain: A cleaning or germ-killing cycle should be sent only to the pipe system scheduled for it. This helps prevent the wrong part of the equipment from being cleaned or treated.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of required tanks, lines, or vessels omitted from the coordinated cleaning sequence
Reduce the duration of idle waiting between successive circuits in the cleaning sequence

usage.name: Sterilize equipment surfaces
usage.statement: Apply steam or heated solution to sterilize the process equipment in place.
usage.description: This matters when equipment must be rendered free of microbial contamination before production resumes.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of sterilized equipment surfaces becoming recontaminated before production resumes
needs.plain: Prevent germs from getting back onto equipment surfaces after they have been sterilized and before production starts. This helps keep the product clean.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of viable microorganisms remaining on process equipment surfaces after sterilization
needs.plain: Make sure no living germs remain on equipment surfaces after they have been treated to kill them. This helps prevent the product from becoming contaminated.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of seals, sensors, or surface finishes damaged by sterilization conditions
Reduce the number of operator exposure incidents involving steam or heated solution during sterilization
Reduce the duration of production restart delay caused by failed sterilization acceptance

usage.name: Troubleshoot cycle deviations
usage.statement: Diagnose and resolve alarms or out-of-spec conditions during a cycle.
usage.description: This matters when a cleaning cycle fails to reach parameters and production is delayed.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of compromising the sterile boundary while troubleshooting the cycle deviation
needs.plain: Keep the protected, germ-free area sealed while investigating why a process did not follow its expected steps. This prevents the investigation itself from contaminating the product or work area.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of misidentifying the source of an alarm or out-of-spec condition during the cycle
needs.plain: Make sure workers can correctly identify what caused a warning or problem during the process. This helps them fix the right issue instead of guessing.

needs.stmt: Reduce the frequency of the cycle deviation recurring after the system is returned to use
Reduce the duration of sterile fill-finish production delay caused by an unresolved cycle deviation
Reduce the percentage of diagnostic evidence from the failed cycle that is missing or inconsistent

usage.name: Verify rinse completion
usage.statement: Confirm that the final rinse meets acceptance criteria before releasing equipment for production use.
usage.description: Quality technician or operator checks conductivity, turbidity, or other indicators at cycle end to validate rinse water quality and authorise equipment release.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that a final rinse outside acceptance criteria is accepted for equipment release
needs.plain: Make sure a final rinse is accepted for use only when it meets the required cleanliness standards.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that an acceptable final rinse is rejected during release verification
Reduce the number of required cycle-end indicator readings that are missing or invalid
Reduce the duration of unresolved equipment release status caused by conflicting indicator results
Reduce the percent of final-rinse samples that do not represent the condition of the equipment

disposal.name: Archive compliance records
disposal.statement: Archive validation, maintenance, and compliance records from the system according to retention requirements.
disposal.description: Compile and archive all regulatory compliance, validation, and maintenance documentation associated with the system for the required retention period.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of archived system records becoming unreadable before retention obligations expire
Minimize the percentage of required validation, maintenance, and compliance records omitted from the retirement archive
needs.plain: Preserve all required records showing that the equipment was checked, maintained, and handled according to rules when it is retired.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of archived records assigned incorrect retention periods
needs.plain: Keep stored records from being given the wrong length of time to keep them. This helps the organization follow its record-keeping requirements.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of archived records linked to the wrong retired system, configuration, or production history
needs.plain: Keep stored records from being linked to the wrong retired system, setup, or production history. This helps people rely on the correct information when checking past operations.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of delay in completing the archive beyond the approved system-retirement deadline
needs.plain: Finish storing the required records by the approved deadline for retiring the system. This helps meet recordkeeping requirements and prevents late closeout.

disposal.name: Cancel service contracts
disposal.statement: Cancel service and supply contracts associated with the system.
disposal.description: Service, chemical supply, and remote monitoring contracts tied to the system must be ended to avoid ongoing charges.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of service, chemical supply, and remote monitoring contracts that remain active after system disposal
needs.plain: Prevent service, chemical-supply, and remote-monitoring agreements from continuing after the system has been thrown away or removed. This avoids paying for services that are no longer needed.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of contracts automatically renewing before cancellation is completed
needs.plain: Prevent a service agreement from renewing on its own before the cancellation is fully completed. This helps avoid paying for service that is no longer wanted.

needs.stmt: Reduce the time cancellation requests remain pending because authorization or contract records are missing
needs.plain: Spend less time waiting to cancel a service agreement when approval or contract information is missing. This helps the cancellation go through without avoidable delays.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of service or supply coverage ending before final system shutdown activities are complete
needs.plain: Avoid ending service or supply support before all work to shut down the system is finished. Support may still be needed until the shutdown is complete.

needs.stmt: Reduce the amount of charges disputed because the purchaser and provider use different cancellation dates
needs.plain: Avoid charges caused by the buyer and provider using different dates to end a service. This helps ensure the service stops on the intended date and prevents extra billing.

disposal.name: Decommission the system
disposal.statement: Decommission the cleaning and sterilization in place system at end of service life.
disposal.description: Formally retire the system from operational use following established decommissioning procedures.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of personnel or environmental exposure to hazardous residues during decommissioning
Minimize the likelihood of an unexpected release of stored energy or pressure from the system during decommissioning
Reduce the duration of connected sterile fill-finish production disruption caused by system retirement
needs.plain: Keep the production of sterile products and their final packaging from being disrupted for long when the equipment is retired. This helps the changeover happen with less interruption.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of required decommissioning records left incomplete at formal retirement
needs.plain: Complete every required record when equipment is formally retired, so the retirement is properly documented and can be verified later.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of regulated system materials sent to an incompatible disposal stream
needs.plain: Send materials from the controlled system to a disposal method that is safe and suitable for those materials. This helps prevent unsafe handling and improper disposal.

disposal.name: Decontaminate product-contact surfaces
disposal.statement: Decontaminate product-contact surfaces of the system before removal or transfer.
disposal.description: Apply decontamination procedures to all product-contact surfaces to neutralize biological or chemical hazards prior to disassembly, removal, or transfer of the system.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of personnel exposure to biological or chemical hazards during decontamination
Reduce the percentage of treated product-contact surfaces retaining biological or chemical hazards after decontamination
needs.plain: Leave as little biological or chemical danger as possible on surfaces that touch the product after cleaning. This helps keep the product safe from contamination.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of product-contact surfaces omitted from decontamination before system disassembly, removal, or transfer
needs.plain: Leave fewer product-contact surfaces untreated before the equipment is taken apart, moved, or handed over. This helps protect people and prevent contamination.

needs.stmt: Reduce the amount of hazardous decontamination effluent released outside its designated containment boundary
needs.plain: Keep as much contaminated cleaning liquid as possible inside its assigned containment area. This helps prevent dangerous liquid from spreading into surrounding areas.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of system handover delay caused by inconclusive decontamination evidence
needs.plain: Spend less time waiting to hand over the system when cleaning evidence does not clearly show that product-contact surfaces are free of contamination. This helps the next team take over with confidence.

disposal.name: Disconnect utility connections
disposal.statement: Disconnect the system from utility and process connections.
disposal.description: Piping and vessels must be safely disconnected from steam, water, drain, and process utility connections during removal.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of an unexpected release of pressure, hot fluids, steam, or chemical residue during disconnection
Minimize the likelihood of contaminants entering exposed sterile-process or utility connections after disconnection
Minimize the likelihood of facility piping or connection points being damaged during separation
Minimize the likelihood that the technician disconnects the wrong utility or process connection
needs.plain: Make sure the technician disconnects the correct pipes, cables, or other connections. This helps prevent damage, spills, or unsafe conditions.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of utility or process connections left attached before system removal
needs.plain: Make sure all pipes, cables, and other service connections are detached before the system is removed. This helps prevent damage, leaks, and injuries.

disposal.name: Dispose of hazardous chemicals
disposal.statement: Dispose of hazardous cleaning and sanitizing chemicals from the system through approved channels.
disposal.description: Remove and dispose of residual hazardous chemical agents remaining in the system at end of life in compliance with applicable regulations.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of hazardous chemical release during residue removal or transfer
Minimize the likelihood of incompatible chemical residues mixing during disposal handling
Reduce the amount of hazardous chemical residue remaining in the system after disposal
needs.plain: Leave as little dangerous chemical residue as possible in the system before it is thrown away or taken apart. This reduces the risk of exposing people or the environment to harmful chemicals.

needs.stmt: Reduce the frequency of hazardous waste consignments rejected by approved disposal channels
needs.plain: Prevent shipments of dangerous waste from being refused by approved waste handlers. This helps the waste leave the site safely and legally.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of hazardous chemical disposals with incomplete or inaccurate required records
needs.plain: Make sure the required records for throwing away dangerous chemicals are complete and accurate.

disposal.name: Document final condition
disposal.statement: Document the final physical and operational condition of the system at end of service.
disposal.description: Record the system's physical condition, outstanding defects, remaining component life, and operational status at the point of retirement to support disposal, transfer, or replacement decisions.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of residual hazardous contamination being omitted from the final condition record
Minimize the degree of error in the documented remaining service life of critical components at retirement
Reduce the number of system functions with incorrectly documented operational status at retirement
needs.plain: Avoid recording the wrong working condition of system features when the system is being taken out of service. This helps create an accurate final record.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of condition-critical system areas inaccessible during the final physical assessment
needs.plain: Make sure important parts of the system can be reached during the final inspection, so their condition can be checked properly.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of known outstanding defects omitted from the final condition record
needs.plain: Make sure the final condition report includes every known problem that is still unfinished. This gives an honest record of the equipment's condition at the end.

disposal.name: Drain residual chemicals
disposal.statement: Drain residual cleaning and sanitizing chemicals from the system.
disposal.description: Residual caustic, acid, and sanitizer chemicals in tanks and lines must be drained and neutralized before the unit is removed.

needs.stmt: Minimize the amount of drained chemical released into the surrounding sterile manufacturing area
needs.plain: Limit the amount of leftover cleaning chemical released into the nearby clean production area when it is drained. This helps protect people, equipment, and the surrounding area.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of incompatible residual chemicals mixing and reacting during drainage
Minimize the likelihood of operator contact with residual caustic, acid, or sanitizer during drainage and neutralization
Reduce the degree to which drained chemicals remain outside waste acceptance limits after neutralization
needs.plain: Make sure the drained chemicals meet the limits for what can be accepted as waste after they are made less harmful. This helps them be handled and disposed of safely.

needs.stmt: Reduce the amount of caustic, acid, or sanitizer remaining in tanks and lines after drainage
needs.plain: Remove as much strong cleaning chemical as possible from tanks and pipes after they are drained. This reduces the leftover chemicals that could create a safety or handling risk.

disposal.name: Export cycle records
disposal.statement: Export historical cycle and validation records from the system.
disposal.description: Batch records, cleaning validation logs, and cycle data stored in the controller must be exported before shutdown for compliance and audit purposes.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of record export not being completed before system shutdown
Minimize the likelihood of authorized compliance reviewers being unable to access or interpret exported records
Reduce the percentage of required batch, cleaning validation, and cycle records omitted from the export
needs.plain: Include all required production, cleaning-check, and operating records in the exported files, so the complete history can be reviewed.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of exported records corrupted or altered during export
needs.plain: Make sure production-cycle records are not damaged or changed while being exported. Accurate records are needed to rely on what happened during each cycle.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of exported records whose identifiers and timestamps fail to preserve traceability between cycles and validation evidence
needs.plain: Make sure exported records keep the correct names and times so each production cycle can be linked to the evidence showing it was checked. This makes it possible to follow the history of each cycle.

disposal.name: Migrate system configurations to replacement equipment
disposal.statement: Migrate compatible system configurations and parameters to the replacement equipment.
disposal.description: Identify, extract, and transfer applicable control configurations, cleaning cycle parameters, and validated settings from the retiring system to the replacement system.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of ambiguity about which validated configuration version is approved for migration
Minimize the percentage of transferred cleaning cycle parameters and validated settings that differ from the approved source baseline
needs.plain: Make sure the cleaning instructions and tested settings moved to the replacement equipment match the approved original settings. This helps ensure the new equipment cleans in the expected way.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of applicable control configurations that cannot be mapped correctly to the replacement equipment
needs.plain: Make sure each relevant operating setting can be correctly transferred to the replacement equipment. This helps the new equipment work as intended.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of obsolete or retiring-equipment-specific settings transferred to the replacement equipment
needs.plain: Move only the settings needed for the replacement equipment. This prevents old equipment-specific settings from causing problems on the new equipment.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of replacement system unavailability caused by configuration extraction or transfer failures
needs.plain: Spend less time waiting for replacement equipment when copying or moving its settings fails. This helps the replacement system become usable sooner.

disposal.name: Notify regulatory bodies of system retirement
disposal.statement: Notify applicable regulatory bodies of the system's retirement from regulated service.
disposal.description: Submit required notifications or deregistration filings to applicable regulatory authorities when retiring a system that was registered, validated, or permitted for use in a regulated environment.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of a retirement filing being rejected because required system or validation details are incomplete or inaccurate
Minimize the likelihood of the system being retired from regulated service before required regulatory clearance is obtained
Reduce the number of applicable regulatory bodies not notified of the system's retirement from regulated service
needs.plain: Make sure every relevant government agency is told when the system is no longer being used for a regulated purpose. This helps keep official records and required notifications up to date.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration a required retirement notification remains unsubmitted after its deadline
needs.plain: Submit the required notice of retirement as soon as possible after its deadline has passed. This helps meet reporting obligations and avoid a prolonged delay.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of discrepancies in retirement details across regulatory filings and facility records
needs.plain: Make sure the equipment retirement information is the same in official reports and facility records. This helps ensure the retirement is reported accurately to the authorities.

disposal.name: Notify stakeholders
disposal.statement: Notify stakeholders of the system's removal from service.
disposal.description: Downstream process lines, utilities, and monitoring teams must be informed that the cleaning system is being taken out of service.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of affected downstream process, utility, and monitoring stakeholders omitted from the removal notice
needs.plain: Leave out fewer people responsible for later production steps, building services, or monitoring when announcing the machine’s removal. This helps everyone affected prepare for the change.

needs.stmt: Minimize the time between the decision to remove the cleaning system from service and notification of affected stakeholders
needs.plain: Notify the affected people as soon as the decision is made to stop using the cleaning system. This gives them time to respond to the change.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of affected process lines, utilities, and monitoring responsibilities missing from the notification
needs.plain: Leave out fewer production lines, building services, or monitoring duties that will be affected by the change. This helps ensure every affected area is included in the notice.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of conflicting removal dates, boundaries, or affected connections communicated to stakeholders
needs.plain: Give everyone the same information about when removal will happen, what area is included, and which connections will be affected. This prevents people from preparing for different work.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of changes to the removal timing or scope not communicated before taking effect
needs.plain: Make sure people are told ahead of time when something will be removed and what will be removed. This gives them time to prepare.

disposal.name: Purge control system data
disposal.statement: Remove stored recipes, parameters, and configuration data from the control system before release.
disposal.description: Erase proprietary process recipes, cleaning parameters, and configuration data stored in the control system to prevent unauthorized access after disposal.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of erasing records subject to retention requirements when purging the control system
Minimize the percentage of targeted control-system data remaining recoverable after the purge
needs.plain: Leave as little targeted control-system information recoverable as possible after it is erased. This helps protect information that should no longer be available.

needs.stmt: Reduce the degree of uncertainty about whether all targeted control-system data was removed before release
needs.plain: Be more certain that all data used to control the system has been removed before the system is released. This helps prevent sensitive or unwanted information from remaining behind.

needs.stmt: Reduce the time the scheduled purge is delayed because required administrative access is unavailable
needs.plain: The system’s stored information should be deleted on time instead of waiting for special administrative access. This helps complete the cleanup without delay.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the control system is de-energized or disconnected before its stored data is purged
needs.plain: Make sure the control system stays powered and connected until all of its saved information has been erased. This prevents the data from being left behind or lost before it is cleared.

disposal.name: Recycle recoverable materials
disposal.statement: Recycle recoverable materials from the retired system through appropriate recycling channels.
disposal.description: Sort and route recyclable materials — including stainless steel, metal components, and other recoverable materials — from the decommissioned system to appropriate recycling streams.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of personnel being exposed to hazardous residues while handling recoverable components
Reduce the number of residue-contaminated components routed into conventional recycling streams
needs.plain: Reduce the number of parts with leftover material that are sent to ordinary recycling. This helps ensure contaminated parts are handled in a suitable way.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of recoverable material lots assigned to incorrect recycling streams
needs.plain: Fewer batches of reusable material should be sent to the wrong recycling category. Correct sorting helps the material reach the right recycling process.

needs.stmt: Minimize the percentage of recoverable materials discarded with nonrecyclable waste
needs.plain: Keep reusable materials out of ordinary trash and send them for recycling or recovery whenever possible.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of recycling loads being rejected because required material or decontamination records are missing
needs.plain: Reduce the chance that a recycling company refuses a shipment because records about the material or its cleaning are missing. This helps recyclable materials be accepted.

disposal.name: Remove system identification markings
disposal.statement: Remove or obliterate system identification markings, asset tags, and nameplates before transfer or scrapping.
disposal.description: Strip, deface, or remove facility-specific asset tags, identification markings, calibration labels, and proprietary nameplates from the system prior to transfer to a new owner or scrapping, to prevent misuse of facility identification.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of damage to system surfaces or components during marking removal
Minimize the amount of label residue or removal debris left on cleanroom-facing or product-contact surfaces
Reduce the number of facility-specific markings left legible after de-identification
needs.plain: Make sure markings that identify the facility are no longer readable after the system is made anonymous. This helps prevent the facility from being identified from the equipment.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that required safety, regulatory, or ownership markings are removed unintentionally
needs.plain: Make sure important safety, rule-related, and ownership labels are not removed by accident. These labels help people know what the equipment is, who it belongs to, and how to handle it safely.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of transfer or scrapping delay caused by unresolved marking disposition requirements
needs.plain: Shorten the delay in transferring or scrapping equipment while the team is still deciding what to do with its labels and identifying marks. This helps the equipment be moved or discarded sooner.

disposal.name: Salvage reusable components
disposal.statement: Salvage serviceable components from the retired system.
disposal.description: This retains pumps, valves, instruments, controls, or other eligible parts for approved reuse.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of technician exposure to residual process material or cleaning chemicals during component removal
Reduce the percentage of salvaged components that fail serviceability requirements after removal
needs.plain: Make sure parts removed for reuse still meet the requirements for working properly and safely. This matters so faulty parts are not put back into service.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of losing component identity, configuration, or maintenance history during salvage
needs.plain: Keep each salvaged part’s identity, setup information, and repair history with it. This helps people know what each part is and how it was maintained.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of serviceable components damaged during disassembly or handling
needs.plain: Avoid damaging usable parts while taking a system apart or moving its parts. This preserves parts that can be used again.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of component-removal delays caused by seized connections or restricted access
needs.plain: Remove reusable parts without long delays when connections are stuck or hard to reach. This helps recover useful parts safely and efficiently.

disposal.name: Transfer system ownership
disposal.statement: Transfer ownership of the system to a new owner or operator.
disposal.description: Execute the legal and logistical transfer of system ownership, including documentation and handover, to a new owner or operator.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that legal ownership transfer is blocked by unresolved liens or conflicting title records
needs.plain: Make sure no unpaid debts or conflicting ownership records prevent the legal transfer of the system.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of required validation and lifecycle records missing from the handover package
needs.plain: Reduce the number of records missing from the package handed over with the system, including records showing that it was checked and maintained over time. This gives the new owner the information needed to operate it properly.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that product or cleaning-agent residues remain in the system at custody handover
needs.plain: Make it unlikely that product or cleaning-fluid leftovers remain in the system when it is handed over to the new owner. This helps ensure the system is clean and safe to receive.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percent of inventory-listed components and dedicated spares missing at custody handover
needs.plain: Have fewer listed parts and specially kept backup parts missing when the system changes hands. This helps the new owner receive everything they are supposed to receive.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of the ownership handover delay caused by unmet recipient prerequisites
needs.plain: Shorten the wait when handing system ownership to the new owner because the new owner has not yet completed all required preparations. This helps the handover happen without unnecessary delay.

disposal.name: Transition operations to replacement system
disposal.statement: Transition operational processes to the replacement system at the end of the retired system's service life.
disposal.description: Plan and execute the operational handover from the decommissioned system to its replacement, ensuring continuity of cleaning and sterilization operations.

needs.stmt: Minimize the duration of cleaning and sterilization unavailability during operational handover
Minimize the likelihood of incorrect process or utility connections during the transition
Minimize the likelihood that replacement system cycles fall outside validated cleaning or sterilization limits during handover
needs.plain: Make sure the replacement system's cleaning and sterilizing cycles stay within the approved safe ranges during the changeover. This helps ensure the system remains properly cleaned and safe to use.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of required cleaning and sterilization parameters transferred incorrectly to the replacement system
needs.plain: Transfer all required cleaning and sterilizing settings correctly to the replacement system, so it can be operated safely and consistently.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of critical operational handover tasks left without clear ownership
needs.plain: Avoid leaving important tasks for handing over daily operations without a clearly assigned person. Clear ownership helps ensure nothing is missed during the changeover.

acquisition.name: Arrange site assessment
acquisition.statement: Arrange a pre-purchase site assessment for installation feasibility.
acquisition.description: Site surveys confirm space, utilities, and access needed to install the system.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of disagreement between the engineer, facility team, and assessor about the areas, utilities, and deliverables covered by the assessment
Minimize the likelihood that the site assessment cannot be scheduled within the pre-purchase decision window
needs.plain: The site visit should be arranged before the time allowed for making the purchase decision runs out. This gives the buyer the information needed to decide.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that facility access and safety clearances remain unapproved on the assessment date
needs.plain: Make sure all permissions to enter the facility and required safety approvals are granted before the assessment day.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of required information about the installation space, utilities, and access route missing when the assessment scope is finalized
needs.plain: Make sure the assessment team has complete information about the available space, needed services such as power and water, and the route to the equipment. This helps them prepare a site visit safely and efficiently.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of necessary facility and installation stakeholders unavailable during the assessment
needs.plain: Make sure the people needed to assess the site are available when the assessment takes place. Their input helps identify facility and installation needs correctly.

acquisition.name: Assess process compatibility
acquisition.statement: Assess compatibility of the system with existing process equipment and piping.
acquisition.description: Evaluates whether candidate systems can be integrated with the facility's existing process lines, vessels, and piping topology.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that integration with existing piping creates trapped fluid in product-contact paths
needs.plain: Make sure the connections to existing pipes do not leave liquid trapped in areas that touch the product, where it could cause problems.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that candidate cycle conditions exceed the pressure, temperature, or flow limits of existing equipment and piping
needs.plain: Make sure the proposed operating conditions do not put more pressure, heat, or liquid movement through the existing equipment and pipes than they can safely handle. This helps prevent damage or unsafe operation.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of connection, dimension, material, or rating mismatches overlooked during the compatibility evaluation
needs.plain: Catch differences in connections, size, materials, or safe operating limits during the compatibility check. This helps prevent equipment from being connected or used when it is not suitable.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of required facility interface data that is missing or outdated during the evaluation
needs.plain: Make sure the information about how the equipment connects with the facility is complete and up to date during the review. This helps determine whether the equipment will work properly with the facility.

needs.stmt: Reduce the time spent waiting for access to inspect existing lines, vessels, and tie-in points
needs.plain: Spend less time waiting to inspect the existing pipes, containers, and connection points. This helps determine sooner whether the new equipment will fit and work with what is already there.

acquisition.name: Compare system specifications
acquisition.statement: Compare technical specifications and performance ratings across candidate systems.
acquisition.description: Side-by-side evaluation of cleaning coverage, cycle times, flow rates, temperature/pressure ratings, and other technical parameters across shortlisted systems.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of required technical parameters absent from candidate system specifications
needs.plain: Reduce the number of system descriptions that leave out important required details. This helps people compare the systems fairly and choose one that meets their needs.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of comparing cleaning coverage, flow, temperature, or pressure ratings derived under non-equivalent operating conditions
needs.plain: Compare cleaning performance and operating limits only when the systems were tested under similar conditions. Otherwise, the comparison may give a misleading picture of how well they work or what pressure, temperature, and liquid flow they can handle.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of candidate specification entries whose units, reference points, or rating definitions are ambiguous
needs.plain: Avoid comparing machine details that use unclear units, starting points, or definitions for performance ratings. Clear definitions help people understand which option actually performs better.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of shortlisted system performance claims lacking traceable supporting evidence
needs.plain: Make sure claims about how well a system works are supported by evidence that can be traced back to a reliable source. This makes it easier to judge whether the claims are trustworthy.

needs.stmt: Reduce the frequency of candidate specification revisions after the formal comparison has begun
needs.plain: Avoid changing the list of requirements after the formal comparison of options has started. This keeps the comparison fair and consistent.

acquisition.name: Complete purchase transaction
acquisition.statement: Complete the purchase contract and finalize commercial terms.
acquisition.description: The purchase contract, warranty terms, and delivery schedule must be finalized.

needs.stmt: Reduce the degree of disagreement between purchaser and seller over CIP/SIP system scope and acceptance criteria
needs.plain: Make sure the buyer and seller agree on what the cleaning and sterilizing system includes and how its completion will be judged. This prevents disputes about what was purchased and whether it meets expectations.

needs.stmt: Reduce the amount by which the final contract price exceeds the purchaser's approved project budget
needs.plain: Keep the final purchase price within the buyer’s approved project budget. This prevents the completed purchase from exceeding available funds.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of critical CIP/SIP system failures excluded from warranty coverage
needs.plain: Make sure serious failures in the system used to clean and sterilize equipment are covered by the warranty, so the customer is not left without support for important problems.

needs.stmt: Minimize the time contracted delivery falls after the sterile fill-finish project need date
needs.plain: Avoid having the promised delivery date fall after the date the sterile production project needs the purchase. Late delivery could delay the project.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that required purchaser approvals remain outstanding at the signing deadline
needs.plain: Make sure all required buyer approvals are finished before the signing deadline. This allows the purchase to be signed on time.

acquisition.name: Coordinate system delivery
acquisition.statement: Coordinate delivery and rigging of the system to the facility.
acquisition.description: Manages logistics, rigging contractors, and site access to bring the capital system from supplier to the installation site.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of a personnel safety incident during unloading or rigging of the CIP/SIP system
Minimize the likelihood of a delivery-route or facility-access obstruction preventing the CIP/SIP system from reaching the installation site
Minimize the likelihood of the CIP/SIP system being damaged during transport or rigging
needs.plain: Avoid damaging the system that cleans and sterilizes equipment while it is being moved or lifted. Careful handling protects the system before installation.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of unresolved conflicts in CIP/SIP system weight and dimension information before dispatch
needs.plain: Resolve differences in the system’s recorded weight and size before it is shipped. This helps ensure the equipment can be transported, received, and installed safely.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration the CIP/SIP system waits because delivery, rigging, and site-readiness schedules are misaligned
needs.plain: Reduce waiting caused by mismatched delivery, lifting, and site-preparation schedules. This helps the cleaning and sterilizing system arrive when the people and site are ready for it.

acquisition.name: Define system requirements
acquisition.statement: Define the required performance and operational capabilities for the cleaning and sterilization in place system.
acquisition.description: Establishes the technical, regulatory, and operational requirements that candidate systems must satisfy, forming the basis for evaluation and vendor engagement.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of ambiguity in regulatory compliance and acceptance criteria for the system
Reduce the number of critical cleaning and sterilization capabilities omitted from the system requirements
needs.plain: Avoid leaving important cleaning and sterilizing abilities out of the system’s requirements. Clearly listing them helps ensure the system can clean and sterilize as needed.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of system requirements based on incorrect site utility and process-interface data
needs.plain: Avoid setting system needs using incorrect information about the site’s available services and how the process connects to other equipment. This helps ensure the planned system will work at the site.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of unresolved conflicts among process, quality, validation, and production requirements
needs.plain: Resolve disagreements about process, quality, testing, and production needs before defining what the system must do. This helps create requirements that all affected teams can support.

needs.stmt: Reduce the time spent waiting for approved process and facility inputs needed to define system requirements
needs.plain: Spend less time waiting for approved information about the work process and facility before deciding what the system must do. This helps define the right system sooner.

acquisition.name: Evaluate total cost of ownership
acquisition.statement: Evaluate the total cost of ownership including capital, utilities, consumables, and maintenance.
acquisition.description: Calculates and compares lifecycle financial impact of candidate systems to support acquisition decision-making.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of discovering unbudgeted facility integration, qualification, or production-disruption expenses after budget approval
Minimize the degree of deviation between assumed and representative utility and consumable usage for the sterile fill-finish cycle profile
Reduce the degree of inconsistency in cost boundaries, evaluation horizons, and financial assumptions across candidate systems
needs.plain: Compare all possible systems using the same cost limits, time period, and financial assumptions, so the cost comparison is fair.

needs.stmt: Reduce the degree of variation in projected ownership costs across plausible throughput, cycle, utility-price, and consumable-price assumptions
needs.plain: Be more certain about the total cost of owning the equipment under realistic changes in output, work time, energy prices, and supply prices. This helps prevent unexpected ownership costs.

needs.stmt: Reduce the time spent waiting for validated cost and usage inputs from suppliers and internal stakeholders
needs.plain: Spend less time waiting for suppliers and coworkers to confirm the costs and expected use of an option. This helps compare its full cost over time sooner.

acquisition.name: Navigate procurement approval
acquisition.statement: Navigate the internal procurement and capital approval process.
acquisition.description: Capital equipment purchases require documented approval through procurement and finance.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of the CIP/SIP system capital approval package being rejected because required documentation is incomplete
needs.plain: Avoid having the request for funding the cleaning and sterilizing equipment rejected because required papers are missing. A complete set of documents helps approval go through.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration the CIP/SIP system capital approval request remains pending between required procurement and finance reviews
needs.plain: Get the major equipment approval request through purchasing and finance reviews sooner. This helps the request avoid sitting between the two review steps.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of discrepancies in CIP/SIP system scope and cost information across approval documents
needs.plain: Make sure the documents used for approval agree about what the cleaning and sterilizing system includes and how much it costs. This supports an accurate purchasing decision.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of CIP/SIP system approval being blocked because the acquisition exceeds the authorized capital budget
needs.plain: Reduce the chance that approval for the cleaning and sterilizing system is stopped because its purchase costs more than the approved budget. This helps the purchase move forward within authorized spending limits.

needs.stmt: Reduce the frequency of CIP/SIP system capital approval package resubmissions caused by scope or quoted-cost changes
needs.plain: Avoid having to send the investment request back for approval because the work or price has changed. This helps the purchase move forward without repeated reviews.

acquisition.name: Procure initial spare parts kit
acquisition.statement: Procure an initial spare parts and consumables kit alongside the system acquisition.
acquisition.description: Sources and purchases recommended spare parts, seals, and consumables bundled with or alongside the capital equipment purchase to ensure operational readiness.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of critical spare parts, seals, or consumables omitted from the initial kit
needs.plain: Make sure the first spare-parts kit includes all the important replacement parts, seals, and supplies needed. This helps prevent delays when something needs to be replaced.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that initial kit items are incompatible with the delivered CIP/SIP system configuration
needs.plain: Make sure the first replacement parts fit the delivered cleaning and sterilizing system, so they can be used when needed.

needs.stmt: Reduce the time the initial spare parts and consumables kit remains unavailable after the system is ready for commissioning
needs.plain: Have the first set of spare parts and supplies ready when the system is ready to start, so setup is not held up.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of initial kit items supplied without required material, traceability, or compliance documentation
needs.plain: Make sure every item in the starter spare-parts kit comes with the required material information, proof of where it came from, and compliance documents. This prevents parts from being unusable or difficult to verify.

needs.stmt: Reduce the amount by which the quoted initial kit cost exceeds the approved acquisition budget
needs.plain: Keep the quoted cost of the first set of replacement parts within the approved budget. This helps avoid overspending when starting up.

acquisition.name: Qualify system supplier
acquisition.statement: Qualify the supplier of the cleaning and sterilization in place system.
acquisition.description: This assesses the supplier's technical capability, manufacturing controls, delivery record, service coverage, and parts support.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that critical contamination-control deficiencies are overlooked during supplier assessment
needs.plain: Make sure important problems that could allow contamination are not missed when checking a supplier.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the supplier's technical capability is incorrectly judged adequate for sterile fill-finish requirements
needs.plain: Make sure the supplier truly has the skills and equipment needed to safely fill and finish sterile products. This helps prevent choosing a supplier that cannot meet the required conditions.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percent of required manufacturing-control evidence that is missing, outdated, or internally inconsistent
needs.plain: Have fewer required records about how the product is made that are missing, out of date, or contradictory. Consistent, current records help show that the supplier can support proper production.

needs.stmt: Reduce the time spent waiting for supplier responses and assessment access needed to complete qualification
needs.plain: Spend less time waiting for suppliers to answer questions and allow the checks needed to approve them. This helps determine sooner whether a supplier is qualified.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of supplier delivery, service-coverage, and parts-support claims that cannot be corroborated
needs.plain: Make sure suppliers can support their claims about delivery, service coverage, and spare-parts help with reliable evidence. This helps show that the supplier can provide support after the purchase.

acquisition.name: Register warranty and service coverage
acquisition.statement: Register the system and activate warranty and service coverage with the supplier.
acquisition.description: Completes ownership registration and activates contractual warranty, service agreements, and support entitlements.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of registration rejection due to a mismatch between the delivered CIP/SIP system identifiers and supplier records
needs.plain: Reduce the chance that warranty and service coverage is rejected because the equipment numbers do not match the supplier’s records. This helps ensure the correct equipment receives coverage.

needs.stmt: Reduce the time spent waiting for required ownership and acceptance documentation after receipt
needs.plain: Spend less time waiting for the ownership papers and acceptance records needed after equipment arrives. This helps ensure that service and warranty protection are recorded promptly.

needs.stmt: Minimize the duration of the gap in warranty and service coverage after the system comes under site responsibility
needs.plain: Avoid a long gap in repair and support coverage after the site becomes responsible for the system. This helps ensure the system can still receive needed help.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of contracted warranty and service entitlements omitted from the supplier coverage record
needs.plain: Make sure all promised warranty protection and service benefits are recorded in the supplier coverage record. This helps ensure the buyer can receive everything that was paid for or agreed.

acquisition.name: Request quotation and proposal
acquisition.statement: Request a detailed quotation and engineering proposal from candidate system suppliers.
acquisition.description: Formally solicits pricing, lead times, scope of supply, and engineering documentation from vendors under consideration.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of inconsistency among supplier assumptions, exclusions, and scope boundaries
Reduce the number of critical process, utility, compliance, and validation requirements omitted from the quotation request
needs.plain: Avoid leaving important production, utility, legal, or testing needs out of the request sent to potential suppliers. Including them helps suppliers provide complete and comparable offers.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of delay between the requested and actual supplier submission dates
needs.plain: Have the supplier send its response by the requested date instead of later. This helps the buyer compare offers and make a decision on time.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of candidate suppliers that do not receive the quotation request
needs.plain: Make sure every possible supplier receives the request for prices and a work proposal. Otherwise, a suitable supplier may be left out of the competition.

acquisition.name: Research system options
acquisition.statement: Research available cleaning and sterilization in place system configurations and technologies.
acquisition.description: Identifies and surveys the landscape of CIP/SIP system types, configurations, and enabling technologies prior to formal evaluation.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of relevant CIP/SIP configurations and enabling technologies omitted from the market survey
needs.plain: Include all important equipment and methods for cleaning and sterilizing the system in the market review. This helps ensure that suitable options are not missed.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that configuration information used in the research is outdated or inaccurate
needs.plain: Make sure the information used to compare system choices is current and correct. Wrong information could lead to choosing an unsuitable option.

needs.stmt: Reduce the degree of ambiguity caused by inconsistent terminology across descriptions of available configurations
needs.plain: Use clear, consistent words when describing the different system choices. This makes it easier to understand what each choice includes and compare them accurately.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of surveyed options lacking information relevant to sterile fill-finish requirements
needs.plain: Avoid considering options when important information about filling and finishing products without germs is missing. This helps ensure the chosen system fits the actual need.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of waiting for technical information needed to complete the landscape survey
needs.plain: Receive the technical details needed to finish comparing the available choices without a long wait. This helps the person conducting the survey make an informed comparison.

acquisition.name: Seek vendor references
acquisition.statement: Seek references and case studies from comparable installations.
acquisition.description: Facilities seek references and case studies from similar operations to validate vendor claims.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of vendor references that do not represent comparable sterile fill-finish installations
needs.plain: Use supplier references from installations that are genuinely similar to the sterile product-filling work being considered. This makes the references more useful for judging the supplier's experience.

needs.stmt: Reduce the time required to receive responses from reference contacts for comparable cleaning and sterilization in place installations
needs.plain: Get answers from customers who have used similar systems for cleaning and sterilizing equipment without taking it apart more quickly. This helps the buyer assess the vendor’s past work.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that confidentiality restrictions prevent access to decision-critical details about comparable installations
needs.plain: Make sure privacy rules do not block access to important details about similar installations. Without those details, it may be harder to judge whether the supplier and equipment are suitable.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of material vendor claims unsupported by evidence from case studies and reference accounts
needs.plain: Make sure important claims made by a supplier are backed by real examples and customer references. Evidence helps people judge whether the supplier can deliver what it promises.

needs.stmt: Reduce the degree of conflict between reference customers' accounts and vendor case-study claims about cleaning and sterilization performance
needs.plain: Make sure customer experiences agree with the supplier’s claims about how well its equipment cleans and sterilizes. This helps buyers judge whether the claims match real results.

acquisition.name: Track system shipment
acquisition.statement: Track the cleaning and sterilization in place system during shipment.
acquisition.description: This supports receiving coordination when delivery timing, shipment status, or transport exceptions could affect site readiness.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that a transport exception is reported after site receiving preparations must change
needs.plain: Make sure any change in receiving plans is reported before it causes a problem with the shipment. This helps the receiving site prepare for the delivery.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of shipment status updates that are stale or inaccurate during transit
needs.plain: Keep shipment updates accurate and current while the shipment is on the way. This helps people know where the shipment really is and when to expect it.

needs.stmt: Reduce the frequency of changes to the expected delivery date after receiving resources are scheduled
needs.plain: Keep the expected arrival date from changing after staff and equipment have been scheduled to receive the shipment. This helps the receiving team plan its work.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that shipment updates cannot be matched to the correct cleaning and sterilization in place system because of inconsistent identifiers
needs.plain: Make sure shipment updates can be linked to the correct cleaning and sterilizing system, even when different identification numbers are used. This prevents records from being attached to the wrong system.
