needs.stmt: Reduce the degree of deviation between mounted sensor locations and specified detection positions
needs.plain: Place the presence sensors as close as possible to their specified positions. This helps them detect people or objects reliably.

preparation.name: Set Response Timing
preparation.statement: Set detection response timing to match the filling-machine sequence.
preparation.description: Appropriate timing compensates for transport speed, sensor position, control latency, and actuation delay.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that an absence signal reaches fill control after filling begins
needs.plain: Prevent the system from being told that something is missing after filling has already started. A late signal could make the filling process react at the wrong time.

preparation.name: Set up container recipes
preparation.statement: Set up and save detection configuration profiles for each container format run on the line.
preparation.description: Different containers require stored recipes for quick changeover.

needs.stmt: Reduce the rate of absent-container conditions classified as present by the saved detection profile
needs.plain: Reduce how often the system says a container is present when it is actually missing. This helps prevent the wrong container setup from being used.

preparation.name: Test detection accuracy
preparation.statement: Run test containers through the line to verify the presence control system detects and responds correctly.
preparation.description: A validation run confirms the system detects and responds correctly before production.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of missing test containers passing the detection point undetected during validation

preparation.name: Train operators on system
preparation.statement: Train line operators on operating, monitoring, and responding to alerts from the presence control system.
preparation.description: Operators need to understand how to run and monitor the system day to day.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of trained operators selecting an incorrect presence-alert response

preparation.name: Unpack and inspect delivered components
preparation.statement: Unpack the presence control system and verify all sensors, controllers, and mounting hardware against the packing list.
preparation.description: Confirms shipment completeness and physical integrity before installation begins.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that delivered component damage remains undetected during inspection

preparation.name: Verify Site Readiness
preparation.statement: Verify the installation site against the presence-control system requirements.
preparation.description: This confirms that the machine location can support the system's mechanical, electrical, and environmental needs.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that mounting or cable-routing obstructions remain undiscovered during site verification

preparation.name: Wire signal connections
preparation.statement: Wire the sensor and output signal lines between the presence control system and the filling machine controller.
preparation.description: Sensor and control signals must be wired into the filling machine's control circuitry.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that signal miswiring produces an unintended fill command
needs.plain: Make sure wiring mistakes cannot send an unwanted command to start filling. This helps prevent containers from being filled at the wrong time.

disposal.name: Archive equipment records
disposal.statement: Archive maintenance, calibration, and asset records for the retired control system.
disposal.description: Transfer all historical maintenance logs, calibration records, and asset documentation for the presence control system into long-term archival storage per applicable regulatory and organizational retention requirements.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of required maintenance, calibration, and asset records omitted from the archive

disposal.name: Cancel connected services
disposal.statement: Cancel network licenses and monitoring subscriptions linked to the control system.
disposal.description: Matters when networked or subscription-based monitoring services tied to the system must be ended.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that shared-service cancellation disrupts other filling systems

disposal.name: Cancel linked services
disposal.statement: Cancel services linked to the retired presence control system.
disposal.description: Terminate all network licenses, monitoring subscriptions, maintenance contracts, and connected services associated with the retired control system to prevent unnecessary charges.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of linked services omitted from the cancellation scope

disposal.name: Conduct final safety inspection before removal
disposal.statement: Conduct a final safety inspection of the presence control system before initiating physical removal.
disposal.description: Perform a documented pre-removal safety check to verify that all energy sources are isolated, hazardous conditions are controlled, and the work area is safe for disassembly — distinct from energy isolation (candidate 2) and deactivation (candidate 16).
disposal.userGroup: Technician / Environmental Health and Safety Technician

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that a residual energy source remains undetected during final inspection
needs.role: Technician / Environmental Health and Safety Technician

disposal.name: Deactivate the presence control system
disposal.statement: Deactivate the filling machine presence control system prior to physical decommissioning.
disposal.description: Execute the controlled shutdown and deactivation sequence for the presence control system, disabling all active control functions before physical removal begins.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of a fill cycle occurring without a container during deactivation

disposal.name: Decommission the presence control system
disposal.statement: Decommission the filling machine presence control system from the production line.
disposal.description: Execute the formal decommissioning procedure to retire the presence control system from active service.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of filling-machine functions disrupted by unidentified control dependencies during decommissioning

disposal.name: Decontaminate retired components
disposal.statement: Remove process contamination from retired presence control components before handling or disposal.
disposal.description: Clean or decontaminate sensors, housings, and other components that have been exposed to process media (e.g., food-grade, chemical, or pharmaceutical substances) to comply with handling and disposal requirements.

needs.stmt: Minimize the amount of hazardous process residue remaining on retired components after decontamination

disposal.name: Dispose of hazardous materials
disposal.statement: Dispose of hazardous materials such as batteries and capacitors per regulations.
disposal.description: Matters when the system contains batteries, capacitors, or optical elements needing regulated handling.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that hazardous components receive the wrong regulatory waste classification

disposal.name: Document final disposition
disposal.statement: Document the final condition and disposition of the presence control system at retirement.
disposal.description: Record the final operational state, condition assessment, and disposition decision (e.g., recycled, sold, scrapped) for the control system as part of the formal asset closure process.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of required disposition fields left blank in the asset closure record

disposal.name: Donate serviceable components
disposal.statement: Donate serviceable sensors and controllers to a training or charitable organization.
disposal.description: Matters when a functional unit can serve training or lower-priority lines rather than being scrapped.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that residual contamination makes donated components unsafe to handle

disposal.name: Erase stored operational data
disposal.statement: Erase proprietary production, recipe, and log data from the control system memory before disposal.
disposal.description: Purge all proprietary and sensitive operational data from the control system's storage to prevent unauthorized access after the unit leaves the facility.

needs.stmt: Minimize the percentage of proprietary operational data remaining recoverable after erasure

disposal.name: Export configuration and calibration data
disposal.statement: Export stored configuration, calibration, and sensor threshold data from the control system before retirement.
disposal.description: Retrieve and preserve all system configuration, calibration parameters, and operational threshold data stored in the control system for future reference or migration.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of stored configuration and calibration parameters omitted from export

disposal.name: Notify affected stakeholders of system removal
disposal.statement: Notify operators, maintenance personnel, and supervisors of the control system removal.
disposal.description: Formally communicate the retirement and physical removal of the presence control system to all personnel whose work or responsibilities are affected by the change.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of affected personnel omitted from the removal notification

disposal.name: Recover reusable components
disposal.statement: Recover reusable components from the retired presence control system.
disposal.description: This preserves serviceable sensors, controllers, connectors, mounting hardware, or spare assemblies for future use.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of serviceable components damaged during removal

disposal.name: Recycle electronic components
disposal.statement: Recycle the sensors, controllers, and wiring from the retired control system through electronic waste channels.
disposal.description: Route end-of-life electronic components — including sensors, controllers, and associated wiring — through certified e-waste recycling streams in compliance with applicable regulations.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of retired electronics routed outside compliant recycling channels

disposal.name: Return under warranty or recall
disposal.statement: Return defective or recalled control components to the manufacturer.
disposal.description: Matters when a defective batch or recalled sensor module qualifies for warranty or manufacturer return.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of return rejection because eligibility evidence is incomplete

disposal.name: Transfer ownership or resell
disposal.statement: Transfer ownership or resell the presence control components to another operator.
disposal.description: Matters when photoelectric or capacitive sensors and controllers still hold resale value on the secondary market.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of transferring components with residual pharmaceutical contamination

disposal.name: Transition to replacement system
disposal.statement: Transition line operation from the old control system to its replacement.
disposal.description: Matters when replacing an aging system to ensure the new presence control unit is ready before removal.

needs.stmt: Reduce the rate of filling cycles initiated without a container after transition

disposal.name: Uninstall mounted hardware
disposal.statement: Uninstall sensors, brackets, and controllers physically mounted on the filling machine.
disposal.description: Remove all physically mounted components of the presence control system — including sensors, mounting brackets, and controllers — from the filling machine structure.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of detached hardware entering sterile or product-contact areas during uninstallation

acquisition.name: Arrange system shipment delivery
acquisition.statement: Arrange delivery of the ordered presence control system to the designated receiving location.
acquisition.description: Coordinate with the supplier and logistics provider to schedule and confirm delivery of the ordered system to the facility's designated receiving location.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that confirmed delivery falls outside the installation window

acquisition.name: Assess detection performance specifications
acquisition.statement: Assess the detection performance specifications of candidate presence control systems against line requirements.
acquisition.description: Evaluate whether a candidate system's published detection performance specifications meet the throughput, accuracy, and reliability requirements of the filling line.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of line detection requirements lacking measurable acceptance thresholds

acquisition.name: Assess supplier support and service capability
acquisition.statement: Assess the technical support and service capability of candidate presence control system suppliers.
acquisition.description: Evaluate suppliers' ability to provide application engineering support, on-site service, spare parts availability, and response time commitments before committing to a purchase.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of supplier service commitments lacking verifiable evidence before purchase

acquisition.name: Compare candidate systems against selection criteria
acquisition.statement: Compare candidate filling machine presence control systems against defined selection criteria.
acquisition.description: Systematically evaluate shortlisted presence control systems against documented technical, commercial, and regulatory selection criteria to identify the preferred option.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of critical sterile fill-finish requirements omitted from selection criteria

acquisition.name: Compare pricing and quotes
acquisition.statement: Compare pricing and quotations from suppliers of presence control systems.
acquisition.description: Pricing across purchase, installation, and support drives the procurement decision.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of supplier quotations omitting required purchase, installation, or support charges

acquisition.name: Complete purchase transaction
acquisition.statement: Complete the purchase transaction for the presence control system.
acquisition.description: The formal order must move through internal procurement and approval workflows.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of discrepancies between the formal order and approved system requirements

acquisition.name: Confirm regulatory and certification compliance
acquisition.statement: Confirm the regulatory and certification compliance of candidate presence control systems.
acquisition.description: Verify that candidate systems hold the applicable regulatory certifications (e.g., safety, electrical, food-contact) required for the intended operating environment.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of applicable regulatory requirements omitted from compliance review

acquisition.name: Evaluate machine compatibility
acquisition.statement: Evaluate compatibility of the presence control system with the existing filling machine.
acquisition.description: The system must physically and electrically integrate with the existing filling machine and controls.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of electrical or control-interface incompatibilities overlooked during evaluation

acquisition.name: Evaluate total cost of ownership
acquisition.statement: Evaluate the total cost of ownership of candidate presence control systems over their expected service life.
acquisition.description: Estimate and compare the full lifecycle costs of candidate systems — including purchase price, installation, maintenance, consumables, and expected service life — to support the purchase decision.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of expected sterile-line interruption and requalification costs omitted from ownership estimates

acquisition.name: Register system ownership with supplier
acquisition.statement: Register the acquired presence control system with the supplier to activate warranty and support entitlements.
acquisition.description: Complete the supplier's product registration process after delivery to establish ownership records and activate warranty coverage and support services.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that submitted ownership registration is not recorded by the supplier

acquisition.name: Request system demonstration or trial
acquisition.statement: Request a demonstration or trial of a candidate presence control system prior to purchase.
acquisition.description: Arrange for a supplier to demonstrate or provide a trial unit of a candidate presence control system so that performance can be assessed under realistic conditions before committing to a purchase.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the offered trial configuration is incompatible with the filling line

acquisition.name: Research presence control options
acquisition.statement: Research available filling machine presence control system technologies and suppliers.
acquisition.description: Identify and gather information on available presence control system technologies and potential suppliers before making a selection.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of viable presence-control options overlooked during research

acquisition.name: Review warranty and support terms
acquisition.statement: Review warranty and support terms for the presence control system.
acquisition.description: Warranty and service terms determine ongoing support obligations after purchase.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of critical warranty coverage conditions left unspecified

acquisition.name: Secure Purchase Approval
acquisition.statement: Secure authorization to purchase the selected filling machine presence control system.
acquisition.description: This satisfies internal budget, technical, operational, or capital-expenditure approval requirements.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the purchase request is rejected because required decision evidence is missing

acquisition.name: Seek supplier recommendations
acquisition.statement: Seek recommendations and references for presence control system suppliers.
acquisition.description: Reputation and reliability feedback from other operators reduce selection risk.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of recommendations from operators without comparable sterile fill-finish applications

acquisition.name: Track Shipment Status
acquisition.statement: Track the shipment status of the ordered system.
acquisition.description: This allows installation resources or production schedules to be adjusted when fulfillment delays occur.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that shipment status is unavailable for installation scheduling decisions

acquisition.name: Verify environmental and hygienic ratings
acquisition.statement: Verify the environmental and hygienic ratings of candidate presence control systems against the production environment requirements.
acquisition.description: Confirm that a candidate system's IP/NEMA rating, washdown tolerance, and hygienic construction meet the demands of the intended production environment before purchase.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of environmental and hygienic claims lacking verifiable evidence

maintenance.name: Back up configuration settings
maintenance.statement: Back up the presence control system's configuration and threshold settings.
maintenance.description: Configuration profiles and threshold settings must be preserved to recover after failures or replacements.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of configuration profiles and threshold settings omitted from backup

maintenance.name: Calibrate presence sensors
maintenance.statement: Calibrate the presence-detection sensors to their reference thresholds.
maintenance.description: Aging or misaligned sensors drift and misreport container presence, causing missed or double fills.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that reference thresholds are set outside the valid detection range during calibration
needs.plain: The detection limits should be set within the range where the sensors can reliably notice something. Incorrect limits could cause the system to miss an item or report one that is not there.

maintenance.name: Clean sensor lenses
maintenance.statement: Clean the presence-detection sensor lenses and optical windows.
maintenance.description: Remove contaminants from optical sensing surfaces to maintain reliable detection performance.

needs.stmt: Reduce the amount of contaminant remaining on optical sensing surfaces after cleaning

maintenance.name: Document maintenance history
maintenance.statement: Document the maintenance and calibration history of the presence control system.
maintenance.description: Service records support audits, warranty claims, and root-cause analysis of recurring faults.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of maintenance and calibration events omitted from documented history

maintenance.name: Inspect enclosure seals and ingress protection
maintenance.statement: Inspect the enclosure seals and ingress-protection ratings of presence control housings.
maintenance.description: Examine gaskets, cable glands, and housing integrity to confirm the system maintains its rated IP protection class in washdown and harsh environments.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that deteriorated enclosure seals remain undetected during inspection

maintenance.name: Inspect wiring and connectors
maintenance.statement: Inspect the sensor wiring, connectors, and cable seals for wear or damage.
maintenance.description: Vibration and washdown loosen connectors and cabling, causing intermittent signal loss.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that loose connections causing intermittent signal loss remain undetected during inspection
needs.plain: Make it less likely that a loose connection causing occasional signal loss will be missed during an inspection. Finding it can prevent unreliable machine operation.

maintenance.name: Protect components during washdown
maintenance.statement: Protect susceptible presence control components during equipment washdown.
maintenance.description: Apply covers, seals, or isolation measures to prevent ingress damage to non-rated components during scheduled washdown.

needs.stmt: Reduce the amount of washdown liquid entering susceptible control components during washdown

maintenance.name: Realign sensor position
maintenance.statement: Realign the sensor mounting position relative to the container path.
maintenance.description: Restore correct geometric alignment between the presence sensor and the detection zone after mechanical disturbance or changeover.

needs.stmt: Reduce the degree of residual misalignment between the detection zone and container path after adjustment

maintenance.name: Repair control wiring
maintenance.statement: Repair damaged wiring within the presence control system.
maintenance.description: Restore electrical continuity and signal integrity by repairing or splicing damaged conductors and terminations.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of intermittent signal loss from repaired wiring after return to service

maintenance.name: Replace failed sensors
maintenance.statement: Replace failed or degraded presence-detection sensors.
maintenance.description: Swap out inoperative or out-of-spec sensing devices to restore system detection capability.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of installing a sensor incompatible with the presence-control system

maintenance.name: Replace mounting hardware
maintenance.statement: Replace worn or damaged presence sensor mounting hardware.
maintenance.description: Serviceable mounting hardware keeps sensing components stable despite vibration or repeated adjustment.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of replacement mounting hardware loosening under machine vibration

maintenance.name: Schedule preventive service
maintenance.statement: Schedule preventive maintenance for the presence control system.
maintenance.description: Scheduled preventive service reduces unplanned line stoppages caused by detection failures.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that preventive service is scheduled after the required maintenance window

maintenance.name: Store spare sensing components
maintenance.statement: Store spare presence-detection sensors and replacement parts in controlled conditions.
maintenance.description: Maintain an on-hand inventory of critical sensing components stored per environmental specifications to minimise unplanned downtime.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that a critical sensing component is unavailable when maintenance requires it

maintenance.name: Test detection response
maintenance.statement: Test the presence-detection response using reference containers.
maintenance.description: Verify correct sensor triggering by passing representative containers through the detection zone.

needs.stmt: Reduce the degree of mismatch between reference containers and production containers during response testing

maintenance.name: Troubleshoot detection faults
maintenance.statement: Troubleshoot and diagnose recurring presence-detection faults.
maintenance.description: Isolate root causes of intermittent or persistent detection errors to guide corrective action.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of diagnostic conclusions assigning an incorrect root cause to recurring detection faults

maintenance.name: Update control firmware
maintenance.statement: Update the firmware and control software of the presence control system.
maintenance.description: Firmware updates fix detection logic bugs and add features to the control system.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the presence-control system accepts an absent container after firmware update
needs.plain: Make sure the system does not treat a missing container as if it were present after its software is updated. This helps prevent the process from continuing when a container is not actually there.

maintenance.name: Upgrade detection modules
maintenance.statement: Upgrade the presence-detection modules or controller components.
maintenance.description: Install higher-capability sensing or control hardware to extend or improve system functionality.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that upgraded detection modules permit filling without a container
needs.plain: Make sure the upgraded detection devices prevent filling when no container is in place. This helps avoid spills and wasted material.

usage.name: Activate breach response
usage.statement: Activate the isolator's containment response after a boundary breach.
usage.description: This limits release or exposure when glove integrity, transfer integrity, or enclosure integrity is lost.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree to which the required containment response remains inactive after activation
needs.plain: Make sure the required response to a breach starts fully and quickly after it is turned on. This helps limit the effects of a loss of containment.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the containment response fails to initiate after the operator command
needs.plain: Make sure the system starts containing a leak or other breach when the operator gives the command. This limits the spread of hazardous material.

needs.stmt: Minimize the time between operator recognition of the boundary breach and containment response activation
needs.plain: Start containing the breach as soon as the operator notices it. A faster response can limit the spread or effects of the problem.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of operator exposure to hazardous material while activating the containment response
needs.plain: Protect operators from dangerous material while they start the response to contain a breach. They should be able to act without being exposed.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the incorrect containment zone is activated after the breach
needs.plain: Make sure the response activates the correct area for containing a leak or breach. This helps keep the hazard from spreading to the wrong place.

usage.name: Activate containment operation
usage.statement: Activate the enclosure's designated operating mode for the current process.
usage.description: Operator initiates the appropriate operating mode — positive pressure, negative pressure, or recirculation — on the enclosure control system prior to production.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the designated operating mode fails to establish the required pressure direction and airflow conditions
needs.plain: Make sure the selected operating setting creates the needed direction of pressure and movement of air. This helps keep contamination from entering the protected area.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of loss of containment while transitioning into the designated operating mode
needs.plain: Prevent the protected area or material from escaping while the system switches into its assigned operating state. This helps maintain protection during the changeover.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of activating an incorrect operating mode for the current sterile fill-finish process
needs.plain: Make sure the correct operating mode is turned on for the current clean filling process.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of the wait for the enclosure to stabilize after operating-mode activation
Minimize the likelihood that unmet enclosure prerequisites block operating-mode activation before production

usage.name: Activate the isolator operating mode
usage.statement: Activate the isolator's operating mode for active containment.
usage.description: Operator initiates the isolator's active containment state, confirming airflow, pressure, and safety interlocks are engaged before work begins.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that operating status falsely indicates established containment during activation
needs.plain: The machine’s status should not falsely say that the product is safely enclosed when that protection has not really been established. Accurate status information helps prevent unsafe operation.

usage.name: Adapt work technique to isolator geometry
usage.statement: Adapt manual work technique to accommodate the spatial constraints of the isolator enclosure.
usage.description: Operator modifies grip, posture, and tool use to work effectively within the fixed geometry and reach limits imposed by the isolator's glove ports, sleeves, or half-suit entry points.

needs.stmt: Minimize the percent of required manipulations that cannot be completed within operator reach limits

usage.name: Adjust airflow and pressure settings
usage.statement: Adjust the isolator's airflow and pressure settings for the task at hand.
usage.description: Operator modifies airflow rate and pressure differential setpoints via the control interface to match the requirements of the current task.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of incorrect airflow or pressure setpoints entered for the current task

usage.name: Adjust operating setpoints
usage.statement: Adjust authorized setpoints on the enclosure control system for the active operation.
usage.description: Authorized operator modifies pressure, airflow, temperature, or alarm threshold setpoints via the control interface to match process requirements.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that an adjusted alarm threshold suppresses detection of a genuine enclosure excursion
needs.plain: Avoid changing an alarm limit so much that it hides a real loss of protection inside the sealed enclosure. The warning should still appear when conditions become unsafe.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that enclosure conditions leave qualified limits after a setpoint adjustment
needs.plain: After a setting is changed, the conditions inside the enclosure should remain within the approved safe range. This helps keep the process working as intended.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of adjusted setpoints that do not match approved process requirements
needs.plain: Make sure the operating settings that workers change match the approved process requirements. Incorrect settings can affect how the process performs.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that an authorized setpoint change fails to take effect
needs.plain: Make sure an approved change to a machine setting actually takes effect. Otherwise, the machine may continue operating at the wrong setting.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of encountering conflicting setpoint requirements for the active operation

usage.name: Check glove integrity
usage.statement: Inspect enclosure gloves for signs of integrity loss during active operation.
usage.description: Operator visually and tactilely examines gloves and sleeve assemblies for tears, punctures, or deformation while the enclosure is running.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of glove and sleeve integrity losses that remain undetected during inspection
needs.plain: Find damaged gloves or sleeves during the inspection instead of leaving the damage unnoticed. This helps keep contaminants from reaching the product or worker.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that inspection handling damages a glove or sleeve assembly during active operation
needs.plain: Avoid tearing or damaging the attached gloves or protective sleeves while checking them during operation. This helps keep the operator and the work area protected.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that inspection handling transfers contamination within the enclosure during active operation
needs.plain: Make sure checking or handling items does not spread unwanted material inside the enclosure while it is operating. This helps protect the work and the products inside.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of glove and sleeve surfaces left unexamined during active operation
Reduce the number of intact gloves or sleeves misclassified as compromised during inspection

usage.name: Collect process samples
usage.statement: Withdraw in-process samples for testing while preserving containment.
usage.description: Sampling for quality testing must occur without breaking containment or introducing contamination.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of releasing contained process material into the technician's work area during sample withdrawal
needs.plain: Prevent process material from escaping into the technician’s work area while a sample is being taken. This helps protect the technician and keep the surrounding area clean.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of introducing contaminants into the sterile process during sample withdrawal
needs.plain: Keep dirt or germs from entering the sterile process while taking a sample. This protects both the sample and the remaining product.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of withdrawn samples assigned to the wrong batch or sampling point
Reduce the percentage of withdrawn samples that are not representative of the process material at the designated sampling point
Minimize the likelihood of withdrawing less sample material than required for scheduled testing

usage.name: Contain hazardous compounds
usage.statement: Contain potent or hazardous materials while dispensing, weighing, or processing them inside the enclosure.
usage.description: Operator uses the enclosure to prevent operator and environmental exposure when handling highly potent or toxic substances during active processing.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of operator contact with hazardous compounds through a breached handling interface during active manipulation
needs.plain: Prevent workers from touching dangerous substances through a damaged barrier while handling them. Keeping the barrier intact protects people from harmful exposure.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of hazardous compounds escaping from the enclosure into the surrounding cleanroom
needs.plain: Keep dangerous chemicals from escaping the enclosed area into the surrounding clean room. This protects people and keeps the room clean and safe.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of containment conditions remaining outside validated limits during processing
needs.plain: Shorten the time that the conditions needed to safely contain dangerous chemicals are outside their approved safe limits during processing. This reduces the chance of chemicals escaping.

needs.stmt: Reduce the time containment loss remains undetected during active processing
needs.plain: Detect as quickly as possible if hazardous material starts escaping during processing. This limits how long the escape can continue unnoticed.

needs.stmt: Reduce the amount of hazardous residue leaving the enclosure on removed materials or tools
needs.plain: Keep as much hazardous material as possible from leaving the enclosed area on removed parts or tools. This helps protect people and the surrounding work area.

usage.name: Coordinate multi-operator use
usage.statement: Coordinate simultaneous work by multiple operators at shared glove ports.
usage.description: Larger isolators support several operators working the same enclosure on collaborative tasks.

needs.stmt: Reduce the frequency of collisions between operators' hands or tools inside the isolator

usage.name: Coordinate multi-operator work
usage.statement: Coordinate simultaneous or sequential operator access to the enclosure during a production run.
usage.description: Lead operator manages glove-port assignments, task sequencing, and communication among multiple operators working at the same enclosure.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of containment being disrupted by uncoordinated simultaneous operator movements
needs.plain: Prevent several workers from making conflicting moves at the same time and breaking the sealed area. Everyone needs to coordinate so the material stays safely contained.

needs.stmt: Reduce the frequency of enclosure tasks being started before prerequisite operator tasks are complete
Reduce the number of conflicting glove-port assignments during the production run
Reduce the number of operator handoffs with misunderstood task status during the production run
Reduce the duration of operator waiting caused by contention for assigned enclosure access
needs.plain: Reduce the time operators spend waiting to use the assigned enclosed work area when others need it too. This helps several operators complete their work with fewer interruptions.

usage.name: Document isolator operating activity
usage.statement: Record operational parameters and activities performed within the isolator.
usage.description: Operator or designated recorder logs operating conditions, material identities, and procedural actions taken during the isolator session.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of required operating parameters and activities omitted from the isolator session record

usage.name: Document usage activity
usage.statement: Record operating parameters, batch details, and interventions during use.
usage.description: Regulatory and quality requirements mandate a record of what happened during each run.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of pharmaceutical isolator run records linked to the wrong batch
Reduce the number of recorded operating parameters that differ from actual pharmaceutical isolator run conditions
Reduce the percentage of interventions omitted from the pharmaceutical isolator run record
Minimize the likelihood that a completed pharmaceutical isolator run record is unavailable for quality review
Reduce the duration between a pharmaceutical isolator run event and its documentation
needs.plain: Shorten the time between an event during use of the sealed medicine-making enclosure and writing it down. This helps keep the activity record timely and accurate.

usage.name: Handle hazardous substances within the isolator
usage.statement: Handle toxic, potent, or infectious substances within the contained workspace.
usage.description: Operator works with hazardous materials inside the isolator enclosure, relying on the containment barrier for protection.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of a hazardous substance escaping from the isolator into the surrounding cleanroom during handling
needs.plain: Prevent a dangerous substance from escaping the enclosed work area into the surrounding clean room. This helps protect workers, the room, and nearby products.

usage.name: Handle varying batch scales
usage.statement: Process material across different batch volumes within the same enclosure.
usage.description: Different batch sizes and processes require the isolator to accommodate varying throughput.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of losing sterile containment at higher material throughput
needs.plain: Keep the product protected from germs and other contamination when handling batches of different sizes.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of material load exceeding the usable enclosure capacity at the required batch volume
Reduce the degree of airflow variation around material across batch volumes
Reduce the percent of material lost to residual hold-up when processing smaller batches
Reduce the duration of enclosure downtime when transitioning between batch volumes
needs.plain: Spend less time with the enclosed equipment out of service when changing the amount made in a production batch. This helps the machine handle different production sizes more quickly.

usage.name: Maintain aseptic conditions
usage.statement: Maintain aseptic conditions inside the isolator throughout the operation.
usage.description: Sterile processing requires ongoing prevention of contamination during every handling step.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of microbial contamination entering through sterile-to-nonsterile contact inside the isolator
needs.plain: Prevent germs from passing from an unclean area into the clean product area inside the enclosed work chamber. This helps keep the product safe from contamination.

usage.name: Monitor environmental conditions
usage.statement: Monitor pressure, airflow, humidity, and particulate levels inside the enclosure during operation.
usage.description: Operator continuously reads and interprets environmental parameter displays to confirm the enclosure remains within specification.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of deviation between displayed pressure, airflow, humidity, and particulate values and actual enclosure conditions
needs.plain: Make the displayed readings for pressure, airflow, moisture, and particles match the real conditions inside the enclosure. This helps people make decisions based on accurate information.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of pressure, airflow, humidity, or particulate reading unavailability during operation
needs.plain: Keep readings about pressure, air movement, moisture, and particles available during operation, so people can quickly see whether conditions are safe and acceptable.

needs.stmt: Reduce the time between the onset of an out-of-specification condition and operator recognition
needs.plain: Notice as quickly as possible when conditions go outside the allowed range. Fast recognition gives workers more time to respond.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that displayed acceptance limits differ from the current operating specification
Minimize the likelihood that the operator misinterprets enclosure compliance because parameter units or status indications are unclear

usage.name: Observe interior workspace
usage.statement: Observe the interior workspace through the isolator's viewing panels during operation.
usage.description: Continuous visual monitoring is required to perform precise manipulations safely.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of workspace detail obscured by condensation or residue on viewing panels

usage.name: Operate ancillary equipment inside
usage.statement: Operate instruments and equipment placed inside the isolator during use.
usage.description: Balances, pumps, and analyzers must be run from within the enclosure without breaking containment.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of ancillary equipment puncturing an isolator glove or sleeve during manipulation
needs.plain: Make it unlikely that supporting equipment will tear or puncture the protective glove or sleeve while someone is working inside the enclosed area. This matters because a breach could expose the product or the worker to harm.

usage.name: Operate integrated equipment
usage.statement: Operate scales, filling equipment, and instruments installed inside the isolator.
usage.description: Instruments and process equipment placed inside must be operated within the sealed enclosure.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of losing isolator containment while operating installed equipment
needs.plain: Keep the sealed enclosure intact while the built-in equipment is running. This prevents hazardous material from escaping or outside contamination from entering.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of containers filled outside the specified target due to mismatched scale and filling responses
needs.plain: As few containers as possible should be filled with too much or too little product because the weighing and filling equipment do not respond consistently. This helps make sure each container has the intended amount.

needs.stmt: Reduce the frequency of unexpected filling-equipment stoppages during sterile filling
needs.plain: Prevent the filling equipment from stopping unexpectedly during germ-free filling. This helps the filling work continue without interruptions.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood the operator is unable to actuate equipment controls through the sealed enclosure
Reduce the duration that usable instrument readings are unavailable or unclear during processing

usage.name: Operate through glove ports
usage.statement: Operate the enclosed process using integrated glove-and-sleeve access ports.
usage.description: Operator inserts hands into glove ports to physically manipulate materials, tools, and equipment inside the sealed enclosure.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of a glove or sleeve breach during enclosed manipulation
needs.plain: Reduce the chance that a glove or sleeve tears or develops a hole while someone works inside a closed enclosure. This helps keep the worker and the materials protected.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of transferring contaminants from glove surfaces to sterile materials during manipulation
needs.plain: Prevent germs or other unwanted material on the gloves from getting onto clean materials while they are being handled. This helps keep the materials safe to use.

needs.stmt: Reduce the frequency of enclosure pressure excursions during glove-and-sleeve movement
needs.plain: Prevent the pressure inside the enclosure from changing too often while workers move their hands and sleeves through its built-in gloves, so the protected conditions stay stable.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of materials or tools dropped inside the enclosure during glove-port manipulation
Reduce the number of required materials or controls beyond safe reach through the glove ports

usage.name: Perform aseptic manipulations
usage.statement: Perform product handling and processing steps inside the enclosure.
usage.description: Operator conducts aseptic handling operations — weighing, filling, assembling — within the isolator workspace using glove-port access.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the glove barrier is breached during aseptic manipulation
needs.plain: Avoid tearing or opening the glove barrier while handling materials in the germ-free area. A damaged barrier could let germs reach the product.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that sterile product or product-contact surfaces touch nonsterile surfaces during handling
needs.plain: Keep the germ-free product and surfaces that touch it from touching unclean surfaces during handling. This helps prevent the product from becoming contaminated.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of filled units containing a product quantity outside specification
needs.plain: Make sure each filled unit contains the required amount of product. This prevents units from having too much or too little.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of component assemblies with missing, reversed, or misaligned parts
Reduce the frequency of product or components being dropped or spilled inside the isolator workspace

usage.name: Practice manipulation techniques
usage.statement: Practice glove-port manipulation and transfer procedures during training use.
usage.description: New personnel must build dexterity and aseptic technique before performing live work.

needs.stmt: Reduce the rate of trainee contact with non-aseptic surfaces during manipulation practice

usage.name: Purge enclosure atmosphere
usage.statement: Purge the enclosure atmosphere with inert or conditioned gas prior to or during processing.
usage.description: Operator initiates a nitrogen or dry-air purge cycle to displace oxygen or moisture from the enclosure interior before introducing sensitive materials.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that escaped purge gas creates oxygen-deficient conditions in the operator area
needs.plain: Prevent gas used to clear the enclosure from escaping and lowering the oxygen level where workers stand, which could make it unsafe to breathe.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that enclosure pressure moves outside the validated range during purging
needs.plain: Keep the air pressure inside the enclosure within the approved range while it is being cleared of unwanted material. This helps maintain the enclosure’s protective conditions.

needs.stmt: Reduce the degree to which residual oxygen or moisture remains above the specified threshold after purging
needs.plain: After clearing the sealed work area, keep oxygen and moisture below the required levels. This helps maintain the conditions needed for the work.

needs.stmt: Reduce the frequency of ambient-air ingress into the enclosure during or after purging
needs.plain: Prevent outside air from entering the sealed work area during or after the air-clearing process. This helps keep the inside conditions protected from contamination.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of the delay before sensitive materials can be introduced after purging begins

usage.name: Recover interrupted operation
usage.statement: Restore the isolator to its approved operating state after a utility interruption.
usage.description: This is required when power, ventilation, controls, or another supporting utility disrupts containment operation.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of compromised isolator containment after the utility interruption
needs.plain: Shorten the time that the enclosed clean area remains unprotected after a loss of electricity or another needed service. Faster recovery helps keep the product from being contaminated.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that sterile product, components, or critical surfaces become contaminated during the interruption and recovery
needs.plain: Keep the product, its parts, and important clean surfaces free of germs while the interrupted process is stopped and restarted. This protects product safety during recovery.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of unresolved control, alarm, or interlock faults after supporting utilities return
needs.plain: Reduce the number of control, warning, or safety-device problems that remain unsolved after supporting services come back on. This helps the equipment return to safe operation.

needs.stmt: Reduce the frequency of renewed supporting-utility loss during recovery
needs.plain: Prevent the services needed to run the equipment from failing again while an interrupted operation is being restarted. This helps recovery continue smoothly.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of required operating-state checks that remain inconclusive during recovery

usage.name: Remove process waste
usage.statement: Remove process waste through the isolator's contained discharge interface.
usage.description: This limits operator exposure when extracting contaminated or hazardous residual material.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of operator exposure to contaminated or hazardous residue during process waste discharge
needs.plain: Protect the worker from touching or breathing in dangerous or contaminated waste while it is being emptied.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of deviation from the required isolator pressure differential during process waste discharge
Minimize the amount of process waste remaining inside the isolator after discharge
Minimize the likelihood of receiving capacity being exhausted before process waste discharge is complete
Minimize the duration of blockage at the contained discharge interface during process waste removal

usage.name: Remove processed materials from the isolator
usage.statement: Remove processed materials and waste from the isolator via the transfer system.
usage.description: Operator extracts finished or waste materials from the isolator interior through the transfer port at the conclusion of a task.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of containment loss while processed materials pass through the transfer port
needs.plain: Reduce the chance that processed material or surrounding air escapes while the material passes through the sealed opening. This helps keep the material contained.

usage.name: Respond to containment alarms
usage.statement: Respond to pressure, filter, or containment alarms triggered during use.
usage.description: Operator assesses and acts on active alarms generated by the isolator's monitoring systems to address potential containment deviations.

needs.stmt: Reduce the time from containment-alarm activation until operator recognition

usage.name: Sustain high-volume campaigns
usage.statement: Run the isolator continuously through extended high-throughput production campaigns.
usage.description: Continuous or intensive campaigns push the isolator to sustained high-volume operation.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of losing isolator containment during an extended high-throughput campaign
needs.plain: Keep the sealed enclosure secure during a long period of high-volume work, so harmful material does not escape or enter.

needs.stmt: Reduce the degree of deviation in critical chamber conditions from validated limits during sustained operation
needs.plain: Keep important conditions inside the processing chamber close to their approved safe ranges during long periods of high-volume production.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of unplanned isolator stoppages during an extended production campaign
Reduce the amount of process residue accumulating inside the isolator work zone during an extended campaign
Reduce the frequency of product or component transfer blockages during sustained high-volume operation

usage.name: Transfer materials into the isolator
usage.statement: Transfer materials and samples into the isolator interior through the transfer system.
usage.description: Operator moves materials from outside into the contained workspace using the designated transfer port or rapid-transfer system.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that incoming materials introduce contamination into the isolator interior
needs.plain: Avoid bringing dirt or germs into the enclosed work area with incoming materials. This helps protect the product while it is being made.

usage.name: Transfer materials through ports
usage.statement: Transfer components and materials in and out of the isolator through transfer ports.
usage.description: Operators must transfer materials in and out without breaching the containment barrier.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of breaching the isolator containment barrier during material transfer
needs.plain: Prevent the sealed protective enclosure from being opened or damaged while materials are moved through its openings. This helps keep harmful substances or germs from getting in or out.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of incoming transfers that carry contaminants into the sterile isolator chamber
needs.plain: Prevent incoming materials from carrying dirt or germs into the germ-free chamber. This helps protect the product from contamination.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of transfer events involving incorrect components or materials
Reduce the percent of transferred components damaged while passing through the transfer port
Reduce the duration of transfer interruption caused by material lodged in the transfer port

usage.name: Trigger emergency shutdown of the isolator
usage.statement: Trigger the isolator's emergency shutdown function during an unsafe operating event.
usage.description: Operator activates the emergency stop or shutdown sequence to halt isolator operation when a safety-critical condition is detected.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of hazardous isolator activity after emergency shutdown activation

usage.name: Troubleshoot glove breaches
usage.statement: Troubleshoot glove or seal breaches detected during active use.
usage.description: Compromised gloves during work threaten containment and must be managed without halting critical tasks improperly.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of incorrectly identifying the glove or seal location responsible for the breach

usage.name: Troubleshoot operational alarms
usage.statement: Diagnose and resolve alarms and deviations that occur during operation.
usage.description: Deviations, alarms, or breaches must be resolved without compromising the batch or containment.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of sterile batch contamination while the operator diagnoses or resolves an alarm or deviation
needs.plain: Prevent the sterile batch from becoming contaminated while a worker investigates or fixes a warning or problem. This protects the product while the issue is being handled.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of loss of containment while the operator diagnoses or resolves an alarm or deviation
needs.plain: Prevent material or harmful substances from escaping while the operator investigates or fixes a warning or problem. The equipment should remain safely closed during this work.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of misidentifying the condition causing an alarm or deviation
needs.plain: Make it unlikely that the cause of an alarm or problem will be mistaken. Correctly identifying the cause helps workers fix the right issue safely and quickly.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of the sterile fill-finish interruption while an alarm or deviation remains unresolved
Reduce the frequency of the same alarm recurring after the operator returns the isolator to operation

disposal.name: Archive lifecycle documentation
disposal.statement: Archive qualification, validation, calibration, change-control, maintenance, and decommissioning records associated with the isolator.
disposal.description: Consolidate and archive the full set of lifecycle documentation in a durable, retrievable format per applicable record-retention requirements.

needs.stmt: Minimize the percentage of required isolator lifecycle records missing from the archive
Minimize the percentage of archived records linked to the wrong isolator or lifecycle event
Minimize the likelihood that isolator records are destroyed before the required retention period ends
Minimize the likelihood that archived isolator records become unreadable or corrupted during the retention period
needs.plain: Make sure stored records about the sealed work area remain readable and undamaged throughout the required storage period. This preserves the information for future use.

needs.stmt: Reduce the time required to retrieve archived isolator records when requested
needs.plain: Find stored records about the enclosed equipment more quickly when they are requested. This helps people review its history without delay.

disposal.name: Archive lifecycle records
disposal.statement: Archive qualification, calibration, and maintenance records associated with the retired isolator.
disposal.description: Consolidation and long-term storage of all lifecycle documentation — validation reports, calibration logs, maintenance histories — in a retrievable archive per applicable retention requirements.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of required qualification, calibration, and maintenance records missing from the retired isolator archive

disposal.name: Assess residual value of the isolator
disposal.statement: Assess the residual market or salvage value of the decommissioned isolator to inform disposition decisions.
disposal.description: Evaluate the isolator's condition, remaining useful life, and market demand to determine whether resale, donation, cannibalization for parts, or scrapping provides the best economic outcome.

needs.stmt: Reduce the degree of uncertainty about residual contamination that could restrict resale, donation, parts recovery, or recycling
Minimize the likelihood of overlooking critical wear or obsolescence that reduces the isolator's remaining useful life
Minimize the amount of decontamination, removal, transportation, and disposal costs omitted from the residual value assessment
Reduce the percentage of valuation-relevant maintenance, qualification, and modification records that are unavailable
needs.plain: Make sure records about maintenance, safety checks, and changes that affect the equipment's remaining worth are available. This helps people judge its value accurately.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of unsupported assumptions about buyer demand and achievable resale price
needs.plain: Base the estimate of the isolator’s remaining value on evidence about likely buyers and selling price, rather than guesses. This supports a more reliable sale decision.

disposal.name: Cancel service contracts
disposal.statement: Cancel maintenance, calibration, and monitoring service contracts tied to the isolator.
disposal.description: Matters when ongoing calibration, monitoring, or service contracts tied to the isolator must be ended.

needs.stmt: Minimize the amount of unplanned early-termination charges incurred when canceling isolator service contracts
Minimize the likelihood that a service contract automatically renews after its cancellation deadline
needs.plain: Prevent a service agreement from renewing automatically after the deadline for canceling it. This helps avoid paying for service that is no longer wanted.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of contracts tied to the isolator that remain active after the planned termination date
needs.plain: Avoid having service agreements for the isolator still running after the date they are supposed to end. This prevents paying for or managing services that should already be finished.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that a cancellation request is rejected because contract details or purchaser authorization are incomplete
needs.plain: Make sure a request to cancel a service agreement is not refused because required contract information or proof of the buyer’s permission is missing. This helps the cancellation go through correctly.

needs.stmt: Minimize the duration of the service coverage gap before isolator decommissioning activities are complete
needs.plain: Avoid a long gap in service support before work to take apart and remove the sealed work enclosure is finished. This helps ensure support remains available until the work is complete.

disposal.name: Clear remaining materials from chamber
disposal.statement: Remove all remaining materials from the isolator chamber prior to decontamination and decommissioning.
disposal.description: Physical removal of any residual samples, reagents, equipment, or waste from the isolator interior to prepare for decontamination procedures.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of residual items left inside the isolator chamber before decontamination

disposal.name: Decommission the isolator
disposal.statement: Decommission the containment isolator from active production service.
disposal.description: Formally retire the isolator from operational use, completing all required decommissioning steps per facility and regulatory requirements.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of hazardous residues escaping the isolator into adjacent controlled areas during disassembly or removal
needs.plain: Keep harmful leftover material contained while the enclosed unit is being taken apart or removed. This protects nearby work areas and the people in them.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of technician exposure to hazardous residue or stored energy while disconnecting the isolator
needs.plain: Prevent the technician from touching dangerous residue or being harmed by stored pressure or movement while disconnecting the enclosure. This helps protect the technician during equipment removal.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of hazardous utilities remaining connected to the isolator when dismantling begins
needs.plain: Before taking apart the enclosed equipment, the person should make sure that no dangerous pipes, wires, or other supplies are still connected. This prevents unexpected exposure to danger during dismantling.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of residual contamination on isolator surfaces at release from active production service
needs.plain: Leave as little contamination as possible on the surfaces of the enclosed work area when it is taken out of active production. This helps make the area safe and clean after production ends.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of required decommissioning records that are missing, inconsistent, or unapproved at project closeout
needs.plain: Make sure all closing records for the enclosed work chamber are complete, agree with one another, and have the required approval. This shows that the equipment was properly taken out of use.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of adjacent production or research activities interrupted by isolator decommissioning work
needs.plain: Limit interruptions to nearby production or research work while the enclosed work area is being taken out of service. This helps other activities continue with less disruption.

disposal.name: Decontaminate isolator surfaces
disposal.statement: Decontaminate all interior and exterior surfaces of the containment isolator before removal from service.
disposal.description: Systematic surface decontamination using validated agents and procedures to render the isolator safe for handling, disassembly, or transfer.

needs.stmt: Minimize the percentage of isolator surface area retaining hazardous contamination after decontamination
needs.plain: Reduce the amount of an enclosed work area's surface that still has dangerous contamination after cleaning. This helps protect workers and prevent the contamination from spreading.

disposal.name: Decontaminate the isolator prior to disposal
disposal.statement: Decontaminate the isolator interior and surfaces prior to disposal.
disposal.description: Apply approved decontamination procedures to all internal surfaces, pass-throughs, and external contact zones before the unit leaves the facility.

needs.stmt: Reduce the amount of hazardous contamination remaining on the isolator interior, pass-throughs, and external contact zones after decontamination
needs.plain: Remove as much dangerous contamination as possible from the inside, transfer openings, and outside touch points before the equipment is thrown away. This helps protect people and the surrounding area.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of hazardous material escaping from the isolator into the surrounding facility during decontamination
needs.plain: Prevent harmful material from escaping the sealed enclosure into the building while it is being cleaned before disposal.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of technician exposure to hazardous residues or decontaminating agents during isolator decontamination
needs.plain: The technician should be able to clean the sealed enclosure before it is discarded without touching harmful leftovers or cleaning chemicals. This helps protect the technician from injury or illness.

needs.stmt: Reduce the degree of uncertainty about the isolator's contamination history and hazard status before decontamination begins
Reduce the duration of decontamination stoppages caused by unavailable required utilities
needs.plain: Avoid long stops in cleaning the enclosed equipment before disposal when needed supplies such as power, water, or air are unavailable. This helps the equipment be cleaned and discarded sooner.

disposal.name: Disconnect facility services
disposal.statement: Disconnect the containment isolator from facility power, gases, ventilation, drainage, and data services.
disposal.description: This isolates the retired equipment from building systems before removal or dismantling.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of pharmaceutical residues escaping into the facility during ventilation or drainage disconnection
needs.plain: Prevent drug-product residue from escaping into the building when air or liquid connections are disconnected.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that facility power or pressurized gas remains live when isolator disconnection begins
needs.plain: Before disconnecting the enclosed equipment, the person should make sure that electricity and pressurized gas have been safely turned off. This prevents electric shock, sudden gas release, or unexpected equipment activity.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of interrupting a shared facility service used by active fill-finish equipment
Minimize the likelihood that required isolator records become inaccessible after data service disconnection
needs.plain: Make sure the equipment's records can still be reached after its facility connections are shut off. This prevents important information from being lost when the equipment is disconnected.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of work stoppage caused by unrecorded service connections
needs.plain: Shorten work stoppages caused by service connections that were not recorded. This helps workers safely disconnect building services without unexpected delays.

disposal.name: Dismantle and remove
disposal.statement: Dismantle and physically remove the isolator from the facility.
disposal.description: Matters when the large, sealed enclosure must be safely disassembled and removed from a cleanroom environment.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of residual hazardous material escaping from the isolator into the cleanroom during dismantling
needs.plain: Keep dangerous leftover material from escaping the enclosed machine into the cleanroom while it is taken apart. This protects the people and equipment nearby.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of an unisolated utility connection releasing hazardous energy during dismantling
needs.plain: Prevent an un disconnected utility line from releasing stored pressure, electricity, or other dangerous energy while equipment is taken apart. This protects the people doing the dismantling.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of an isolator section shifting or falling during disassembly
Minimize the likelihood that dismantled isolator sections cannot pass through the designated removal route
Minimize the amount of damage to cleanroom surfaces or facility infrastructure during isolator removal

disposal.name: Dispose of hazardous materials
disposal.statement: Dispose of contaminated filters, gloves, and consumables as regulated hazardous or biohazardous waste.
disposal.description: Matters because captured contaminants inside the isolator classify components as regulated waste.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of contaminated isolator waste assigned an incorrect regulatory classification

disposal.name: Document final condition
disposal.statement: Document the final decontaminated and decommissioned state of the isolator for the disposal record.
disposal.description: Creation of a formal end-of-life record capturing physical condition, decontamination status, and disposition method, providing an auditable closure document.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of discrepancy between the recorded and observed final condition of the isolator

disposal.name: Donate usable equipment
disposal.statement: Donate a serviceable decontaminated isolator to an educational or nonprofit institution.
disposal.description: Matters when equipment is retired for upgrade but remains functional for lower-demand users.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of residual hazardous contamination remaining in the isolator at handoff
needs.plain: Make sure dangerous leftover material is removed from the enclosed equipment before it is handed over, so others are not exposed to it.

disposal.name: Export operational data
disposal.statement: Export logged environmental, alarm, and operational data from the isolator control system before shutdown.
disposal.description: Retrieval and secure transfer of all process and monitoring records stored on the isolator's control system to a facility data repository prior to decommissioning.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of required environmental, alarm, and operating records missing from the export

disposal.name: Export retained operational records
disposal.statement: Export historical monitoring, alarm, audit, and batch records from the isolator control system.
disposal.description: Extract all electronically stored operational records from the isolator's control system to a durable, facility-managed repository before decommissioning.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of retained monitoring, alarm, audit, and batch records omitted from the export
needs.plain: Make sure the exported records include all saved measurements, warnings, activity history, and production records. This matters so important past events and operating information are not lost.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that exported record content differs from the source content after transfer
needs.plain: Make sure the transferred records contain exactly the same information as the original records.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of exported records missing timestamps, source identifiers, alarm states, or batch associations needed for interpretation
needs.plain: Make sure saved operating records say when events happened, where the information came from, what warnings occurred, and which production group they belong to.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that exported records are inaccessible from the facility-managed repository after control system retirement
needs.plain: Make sure the transferred records can still be found and used in the facility’s own storage after the machine’s control software is retired.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the record export remains incomplete when isolator decommissioning begins
needs.plain: All needed operating records should be copied before the enclosed machine is taken out of service. This prevents important information from being lost.

disposal.name: Notify stakeholders of isolator retirement
disposal.statement: Notify quality, regulatory, and facility stakeholders of the isolator's removal from service.
disposal.description: Communicate the planned or completed decommissioning to all relevant internal and external parties, including quality assurance, regulatory affairs, and facilities management.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of inconsistency in the isolator retirement status communicated to quality, regulatory, and facility stakeholders
Reduce the number of required facility, safety, and regulatory stakeholders omitted from the retirement notification
Reduce the number of required quality, regulatory, and facility stakeholders omitted from the isolator retirement notification
needs.plain: Tell every needed quality, testing, supplier, and building-related person or group that the sealed safety enclosure will be taken out of service, so no one is left unaware.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of delay between a change in the isolator's retirement status and notification of stakeholders
needs.plain: Tell the affected people promptly when the decision to retire the enclosed equipment changes. This helps everyone act on the same information without unnecessary delay.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the isolator retirement notification states an incorrect removal date or service status
needs.plain: Make sure the notice about retiring the enclosed work unit gives the correct removal date and accurately says whether it is still in service. This prevents people from acting on incorrect information.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of required stakeholders whose receipt of the isolator retirement notification remains unconfirmed
needs.plain: Make sure every person who needs to know about taking the enclosed equipment out of use has received and acknowledged the notice. Confirmed receipt helps ensure no responsible person is missed.

disposal.name: Recycle recoverable isolator components
disposal.statement: Recycle eligible containment isolator components — including structural, glazing, and electronic parts — through qualified recovery channels.
disposal.description: Identify, segregate, and route recyclable materials and components from the decommissioned isolator to qualified recycling or material-recovery facilities in compliance with applicable regulations.
disposal.userGroup: EHS, facilities, and waste management teams

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of residual hazardous contamination remaining on isolator components presented for recycling
needs.role: EHS, facilities, and waste management teams

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of eligible isolator components being routed to recovery facilities that lack required qualifications
needs.role: EHS, facilities, and waste management teams

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of eligible isolator components rejected because required decontamination, material, or custody records are incomplete
needs.role: EHS, facilities, and waste management teams

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of recoverable isolator materials commingled with nonrecoverable waste during segregation
needs.plain: Keep reusable parts from enclosed sterile work equipment from being mixed with waste that cannot be reused. This makes it easier to recycle the parts and prevents usable material from being thrown away.
needs.role: EHS, facilities, and waste management teams

needs.stmt: Reduce the amount of recoverable glazing, structural, or electronic material damaged during dismantling and handling
needs.plain: Protect reusable glass, supporting parts, and electronic parts from being damaged while the enclosure is taken apart and moved. This allows more of these materials to be recovered and recycled.
needs.role: EHS, facilities, and waste management teams

disposal.name: Recycle structural components
disposal.statement: Recycle the stainless steel, glass, and metal components of the isolator after decontamination.
disposal.description: Matters because bulk materials have recovery value and support sustainability targets.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of residual hazardous contamination remaining on isolator components presented for recycling

disposal.name: Remove residual materials
disposal.statement: Remove residual pharmaceutical materials from the containment isolator before retirement.
disposal.description: This prevents remaining product, process waste, or cleaning agents from complicating decontamination and disposal.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of operator exposure to hazardous residual materials during removal
needs.plain: Prevent the operator from coming into contact with dangerous leftover material while removing it. This helps protect the operator from harm.

needs.stmt: Minimize the amount of residual pharmaceutical material released beyond the isolator during removal
needs.plain: Keep leftover medicine from escaping the enclosed work area when it is removed. This protects people and the surrounding area from exposure.
