needs.stmt: Reduce the number of authorized personnel whose assigned role omits permissions required for their isolator duties
needs.plain: Make sure each approved worker's role includes every permission needed to do their duties in the enclosed work area. This prevents workers from being unable to perform required tasks.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of active isolator accounts without a current authorized owner
needs.plain: Every active account for the enclosed work unit should have a current, approved person responsible for it. This makes it clear who manages each account and helps prevent unmanaged access.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of isolator role assignments lacking documented authorization
needs.plain: Make sure every person given a role for an isolator has written permission to have that role. This helps prevent unauthorized access or actions.

preparation.name: Connect to power
preparation.statement: Connect the isolator to its electrical power supply.
preparation.description: The isolator requires electrical supply for blowers, controls, and instrumentation before it can operate.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of technician exposure to hazardous electrical energy while connecting the isolator
Minimize the likelihood of connecting the isolator to supply characteristics outside its electrical requirements
Reduce the number of incorrect conductor terminations at the isolator power interface
Reduce the duration the isolator remains unpowered because the required facility electrical supply is unavailable
needs.plain: Restore power to the enclosed work chamber sooner when the building’s required electrical supply is unavailable. This helps the equipment return to operation more quickly.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of interruption to adjacent sterile fill-finish activities caused by energizing the isolator
needs.plain: Spend less time interrupting nearby sterile filling and packaging work while power is turned on for the enclosed equipment. This helps surrounding work continue sooner.

preparation.name: Connect utilities and ducting to isolator
preparation.statement: Connect the isolator to facility HVAC ducting, exhaust, gas, and other utility services.
preparation.description: The installer connects all required utility feeds — supply and exhaust HVAC ducting, inert gas lines, and other facility services — to the isolator in accordance with the site utility schedule.

needs.stmt: Minimize the amount of contamination introduced through open isolator utility interfaces during connection
needs.plain: Keep dirt and other unwanted material from getting inside when workers connect the outside services to the sealed enclosure.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of leakage from a gas or exhaust connection after hookup
needs.plain: Make it unlikely that gas or exhaust will leak after the connection is made. A tight connection helps protect workers and keeps the system working as intended.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of connecting a facility service to the wrong isolator port
Minimize the degree of misalignment between facility ducting or utility lines and corresponding isolator ports
Minimize the likelihood that a required utility feed is unavailable at the isolator connection point during commissioning

preparation.name: Define and document material transfer protocols
preparation.statement: Define and document the material transfer procedures to be used for introducing and removing items through isolator transfer ports.
preparation.description: The process engineer or operator lead establishes written procedures specifying the sequence, dwell times, and decontamination steps for transferring materials in and out of the isolator through rapid transfer ports (RTPs) or pass-through chambers, ensuring containment is not compromised during transfers.

needs.stmt: Minimize the amount by which specified decontamination dwell periods fall short of required exposure
needs.plain: Make sure materials are exposed to the cleaning treatment for nearly all of the required time, without falling short. This helps ensure harmful contamination is removed before materials are moved.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the documented transfer sequence creates an open pathway between the isolator and surrounding environment
needs.plain: Make sure the written material-moving steps never create an uncovered route between the enclosed clean space and the outside air.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of critical transfer or decontamination steps omitted from the documented procedures
needs.plain: Leave out as few important steps as possible from the written instructions for moving materials and removing harmful contamination, so workers can follow the process safely and consistently.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of foreseeable abnormal transfer conditions lacking documented response actions
Reduce the number of material, transfer-direction, or port scenarios with ambiguous instructions

preparation.name: Establish internal environmental monitoring layout
preparation.statement: Establish the placement and configuration of internal environmental monitoring points within the isolator.
preparation.description: The validation or qualification specialist defines and documents the locations of particle counters, temperature probes, and other monitoring sensors inside the isolator chamber based on process risk assessment, ensuring representative monitoring during operation.

needs.stmt: Minimize the percentage of critical isolator zones omitted from representative monitoring coverage
needs.plain: Make sure all important enclosed work areas are included in the environmental checks, leaving as few areas as possible unmonitored. This helps detect conditions that could affect product safety.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of monitoring points configured with parameters unsuitable for expected isolator conditions
needs.plain: Make sure each monitoring location uses settings that fit the conditions expected inside the protected area. This helps detect environmental problems accurately.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that monitoring-point placement disturbs airflow within the isolator chamber
Reduce the number of monitoring points that conflict with process equipment, material flow, or operator interventions
Minimize the degree of mismatch between the documented monitoring layout and the intended locations and configurations

preparation.name: Integrate process equipment within isolator enclosure
preparation.statement: Install and arrange process equipment within the isolator enclosure to support the intended process operation.
preparation.description: The integration specialist or operator mounts, positions, and connects process equipment — such as filling systems, dispensing tools, or sampling devices — inside the isolator chamber before process qualification or start-up.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of damaging or leaving the containment boundary unsealed during equipment integration
needs.plain: Avoid damaging the enclosed work area and make sure every connection remains tightly sealed when equipment is added. This keeps the barrier protecting the product and workers intact.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of surfaces requiring decontamination that are made inaccessible by the equipment arrangement
needs.plain: Arrange the equipment so workers can reach every surface that needs cleaning and treatment. This helps prevent hidden areas from staying contaminated.

needs.stmt: Reduce the degree to which the installed equipment makes required process manipulations unreachable through the glove ports
Minimize the likelihood of equipment interfaces being incompatible with enclosure utility and process connections
Minimize the likelihood of process equipment colliding with enclosure features or blocking door closure during installation

preparation.name: Perform initial decontamination
preparation.statement: Perform the initial decontamination cycle inside the isolator chamber.
preparation.description: A first-time decontamination or bio-decontamination cycle establishes the sterile or clean baseline inside the chamber.

needs.stmt: Minimize the percentage of chamber locations that remain above the viable-contamination acceptance threshold after the initial cycle
needs.plain: After the first cleaning cycle, reduce the number of chamber areas where living germs are still above the allowed level. This helps make the chamber safe for the process.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of decontaminating agent escaping from the isolator chamber into the surrounding work area
needs.plain: Prevent the chemical used for cleaning and removing contamination from escaping the enclosed chamber. This protects people working nearby from exposure.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the initial cycle is interrupted by loss of required chamber conditions
needs.plain: Make sure the first cleaning cycle is not stopped because the chamber loses a condition it needs. Completing the cycle is important for removing unwanted contamination.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of decontamination exposure damaging chamber surfaces or internal components
needs.plain: Prevent the cleaning process from damaging the inside surfaces or parts of the chamber. This helps keep the equipment safe and usable.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of chamber unavailability caused by residual decontaminating agent after the initial cycle

preparation.name: Position and level isolator
preparation.statement: Position the isolator in its installation site and level it.
preparation.description: The isolator must be moved to and leveled in its cleanroom or lab location so airflow and drainage behave as designed.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of isolator deviation from the specified level tolerance at installation
Minimize the degree of isolator deviation from the specified installation coordinates and orientation
Minimize the likelihood of isolator damage while moving it into its installation site
needs.plain: Prevent the protective enclosure from being damaged while it is moved into place. This helps ensure it can be installed and used properly.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the isolator shifts out of level after initial leveling
needs.plain: Make sure the enclosed work unit stays level after it has first been set up. This helps it operate properly and safely.

needs.stmt: Minimize the amount of contamination introduced into the cleanroom while positioning and leveling the isolator
needs.plain: Bring as little dirt or other unwanted material as possible into the cleanroom while moving the equipment into place and making it level.

preparation.name: Practice enclosure techniques
preparation.statement: Rehearse glove-port handling and transfer procedures during operator training.
preparation.description: New operators must learn to work within the enclosure before performing live production.

needs.stmt: Reduce the frequency of operator contact with critical surfaces during rehearsed transfers
needs.plain: Avoid touching important clean surfaces during practice transfers, so workers can learn the task without contaminating them.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of operator hand movements obscured from the trainer during rehearsal
Minimize the likelihood that enclosure gloves tear or become dislodged during rehearsal
needs.plain: Make sure the gloves attached to the sealed work area do not tear or come loose during practice.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that trainer feedback conflicts with the approved transfer procedure
needs.plain: Make sure the trainer’s advice matches the approved steps for moving material through the enclosure. This helps people learn the correct and approved method.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of mismatch between rehearsal conditions and live production conditions
needs.plain: Make practice runs as similar as possible to real production, so enclosure work that succeeds during practice is likely to work during actual production.

preparation.name: Store isolator accessories properly
preparation.statement: Store isolator accessories and consumable spares under conditions that preserve their integrity.
preparation.description: Place gloves, sleeves, filter cartridges, gaskets, and other consumables in controlled storage conditions (temperature, humidity, contamination) to prevent premature degradation before installation.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of accessories and consumable spares becoming contaminated during storage
Minimize the degree of premature degradation caused by temperature or humidity excursions during storage
Minimize the likelihood of gloves, sleeves, filter cartridges, or gaskets being damaged or deformed while stored
Reduce the percentage of stored consumable spares that expire before installation
Reduce the number of stored accessories with missing or unreadable identity or suitability status

preparation.name: Track isolator shipment
preparation.statement: Track the pharmaceutical containment isolator during shipment to its destination.
preparation.description: Monitor the transit status of the ordered isolator from dispatch to arrival, identifying delays or exceptions and communicating progress to relevant stakeholders.
preparation.frequency: Continuously during transit, once per shipment

needs.stmt: Minimize the time between occurrence of a critical isolator transit exception and its identification
needs.plain: Find serious problems during shipment as soon as they happen. Early detection can help prevent damage, delay, or other consequences.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of unavailable isolator location and transit status during shipment
needs.plain: Shorten the time when the shipment’s location and travel status are unavailable. This helps people know where the enclosed production equipment is and whether it is moving as expected.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of isolator transit updates that misstate its location or shipment milestone
needs.plain: Make sure shipment updates accurately say where the sealed work chamber is and what stage of delivery it has reached. Accurate updates help people plan for its arrival.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the isolator arrives outside the communicated delivery window
needs.plain: Make sure the enclosed work unit arrives within the delivery period that was communicated. This helps people prepare for its arrival and installation.

needs.stmt: Minimize the time between identification of an isolator shipment exception and notification of relevant stakeholders
needs.plain: Tell the right people about a problem with a shipment as soon as the problem is found. This gives them time to respond or correct it.

preparation.name: Train operators in containment operating procedures
preparation.statement: Train operators in the containment and aseptic operating procedures specific to the pharmaceutical containment isolator.
preparation.description: The trainer delivers structured training to operators covering gowning, glove changes, material transfer, decontamination, alarm response, and emergency procedures relevant to isolator operation.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of trained operators who cannot correctly demonstrate critical containment and aseptic procedures
Minimize the degree of inconsistency between training content and approved site-specific isolator procedures
Reduce the number of required operating scenarios omitted from isolator training
needs.plain: Teach operators what to do in every important situation they may face while using the sealed safety enclosure, so they can operate it safely and correctly.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that required isolator access, protective garments, or training materials are unavailable when a session begins
needs.plain: Make sure the work area, protective clothing, and training materials are ready when the lesson starts. This allows operators to learn how to work safely while keeping unwanted material contained.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of training interruptions caused by controlled-area access restrictions or production conflicts
needs.plain: Reduce training interruptions caused by limits on entering the controlled work area or by conflicts with production. This helps operators complete their training without repeated pauses.

preparation.name: Unpack, inspect, and verify delivered isolator
preparation.statement: Unpack the delivered isolator, inspect it for transit damage or missing items, and verify it matches the ordered configuration.
preparation.description: Upon delivery, remove packaging, examine the isolator for physical damage, contamination, or shortages, and confirm that the unit received corresponds to the specified order — combining the incoming inspection and order-verification steps into one controlled receipt activity.
preparation.userGroup: Receiving/logistics staff and procurement or quality personnel

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of contamination present on the delivered isolator after unpacking
needs.role: Receiving/logistics staff and procurement or quality personnel

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that transit damage to the delivered isolator remains undetected before installation
needs.role: Receiving/logistics staff and procurement or quality personnel

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that a mismatch between the delivered and ordered isolator configurations remains unidentified before installation
needs.role: Receiving/logistics staff and procurement or quality personnel

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that missing required isolator components or documents remain unidentified before installation
needs.role: Receiving/logistics staff and procurement or quality personnel

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of the delivered isolator shifting or tipping during unpacking and inspection
needs.plain: Avoid allowing the delivered sealed work enclosure to move or tip over while it is being unpacked and checked. Keeping it stable prevents damage and helps keep the equipment safe to use.
needs.role: Receiving/logistics staff and procurement or quality personnel

preparation.name: Verify containment integrity before use
preparation.statement: Verify that the enclosure boundary is intact before beginning a production operation.
preparation.description: Operator performs a pre-use pressure-hold or glove pressure test to confirm enclosure integrity is acceptable before introducing product or hazardous materials.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of accepting an enclosure with an undetected boundary breach
needs.plain: Avoid approving a sealed work area that has a hidden opening or leak in its protective boundary. Finding such damage first helps keep contamination out.

needs.stmt: Minimize the percentage of the enclosure boundary omitted from the pre-use integrity check
needs.plain: Check all of the enclosure’s edges before use so no part of it is missed. This helps confirm that it can safely contain what is inside.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of damaging a glove, sleeve, or boundary seal during integrity testing
needs.plain: Avoid damaging gloves, sleeves, or protective seals while checking that they keep contaminants out. This helps protect both the worker and the product.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that acceptance criteria for the test result are unavailable or conflicting
Reduce the duration of waiting for enclosure pressure to stabilize under test conditions
needs.plain: Wait as little as possible for the pressure inside the enclosure to become steady during the test. This allows the person testing it to confirm whether it keeps materials safely contained.

preparation.name: Verify containment integrity of assembled isolator
preparation.statement: Verify the leak-tightness and glove integrity of the assembled isolator prior to first use.
preparation.description: The technician performs pressure-hold or tracer-gas leak tests and glove integrity checks on the fully assembled isolator to confirm that the containment boundary meets specified performance requirements.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that a leak in a fixed enclosure joint or penetration remains undetected before first use
needs.plain: Find any leak in the enclosure's fixed connections or openings before the enclosure is used for the first time. This confirms that it can keep materials safely contained.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that a breach in an installed glove or sleeve remains undetected before first use
needs.plain: Make sure any tear or hole in an installed glove or sleeve is found before the equipment is used. This helps keep harmful contamination from entering the enclosed work area.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that penetrations or utility connections are configured differently from the specified first-use configuration during testing
Minimize the likelihood that required test instrumentation lacks valid calibration when containment testing is scheduled
Reduce the degree of test-reading distortion caused by ambient temperature or pressure changes

preparation.name: Witness factory acceptance test
preparation.statement: Witness the factory acceptance test of the fabricated isolator prior to shipment.
preparation.description: Attend and observe the supplier's factory acceptance test to confirm the built isolator meets specified performance and quality criteria before shipment.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of critical deviations observed during testing that remain unresolved before shipment release
Minimize the likelihood that factory acceptance test results are invalid because measurement instruments lack current calibration status
Reduce the percentage of specified critical isolator requirements omitted from the factory acceptance test scope
Minimize the degree of disagreement between the buyer and supplier over isolator acceptance criteria
Reduce the duration of delay caused by the isolator or test area not being ready at the scheduled start
needs.plain: Have the enclosed testing area ready when the scheduled test begins. This prevents people from waiting to observe and approve the equipment test.

acquisition.name: Assess total cost of ownership
acquisition.statement: Assess the total cost of ownership of candidate isolators before committing to purchase.
acquisition.description: Estimate and compare lifecycle costs—including purchase price, installation, qualification, maintenance, and consumables—across candidate isolators to support an informed acquisition decision.

needs.stmt: Minimize the percentage of relevant lifecycle cost categories omitted from candidate isolator estimates
Minimize the degree of inconsistency in cost boundaries and assumptions across candidate isolator estimates
Reduce the number of lifecycle cost inputs lacking substantiated vendor or engineering data
Minimize the degree of deviation between assumed and expected isolator utilization in sterile fill-finish production
needs.plain: Make the expected use of the enclosed sterile-production equipment match its actual use as closely as possible. This helps make its long-term costs more realistic.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of discovering site-specific installation and qualification costs after business-case approval
needs.plain: Identify the site's installation and approval costs before the project is approved. This helps decision-makers understand the full cost rather than facing unexpected expenses later.

acquisition.name: Compare candidate isolators
acquisition.statement: Compare candidate isolators across containment level, features, and price.
acquisition.description: Selection hinges on comparing containment level, footprint, and decontamination method across suppliers.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of mismatch between assessed decontamination methods and sterile fill-finish requirements
needs.plain: Choose enclosed work chambers whose cleaning and germ-removal methods closely meet the needs of making and sealing sterile products. This helps prevent ineffective cleaning or sterilization.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of variation in test conditions underlying candidate isolator containment claims
Minimize the likelihood that a candidate judged suitable does not fit the allocated space or access path
Reduce the time spent resolving missing or conflicting supplier information needed to compare candidate isolators
Reduce the amount of acquisition and integration costs excluded from candidate prices
needs.plain: Reduce the purchase and setup costs that are left out of the prices used to compare the available enclosures. This makes the comparison reflect the real cost of each choice.

acquisition.name: Complete purchase transaction
acquisition.statement: Place the order and complete the purchase transaction for the approved pharmaceutical containment isolator.
acquisition.description: Issue the purchase order to the selected supplier and execute all contractual and financial steps required to conclude the procurement, following receipt of authorisation.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of mandatory quality, containment, acceptance, or liability obligations omitted from the executed contract
Reduce the number of discrepancies among the approved quotation, purchase order, and contract in isolator scope, price, delivery, or payment terms
Reduce the duration of waiting for supplier acknowledgment or countersignature after purchase-order issuance
needs.plain: Get the supplier’s confirmation or signature quickly after sending the order. This makes it clear that the supplier has accepted the purchase.

needs.stmt: Minimize the amount of unapproved cost introduced by taxes, currency conversion, freight, or payment conditions during transaction execution
needs.plain: Prevent unapproved extra costs from taxes, exchange rates, shipping, or payment rules from being added during the purchase. This keeps the final cost under control.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the supplier's legal, tax, or banking details fail validation during transaction setup
needs.plain: Make sure the supplier’s company, tax, and payment details are correct and accepted before setting up the purchase. This helps prevent the transaction from being stopped.

acquisition.name: Define containment requirements
acquisition.statement: Define the containment performance and dimensional requirements the isolator must satisfy.
acquisition.description: Capture and document all containment performance, airflow, pressure, and dimensional constraints before engaging suppliers.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of hazardous materials or process steps omitted from the containment basis
needs.plain: Make sure all dangerous materials and all steps that could release them are included when planning how to contain them. This helps prevent people and the surrounding area from being exposed.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of containment performance limits lacking measurable acceptance criteria
Reduce the number of conflicts between specified airflow and pressure conditions and facility or process constraints
Minimize the likelihood that specified isolator dimensions obstruct installation, operator access, material transfer, or maintenance
Reduce the frequency of containment requirements revised after supplier engagement because project assumptions were not confirmed

acquisition.name: Evaluate process compatibility
acquisition.statement: Evaluate whether a candidate isolator is compatible with the intended process and facility.
acquisition.description: Assess isolator fit against process parameters, material flows, ergonomics, and facility constraints to confirm suitability before purchase.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of critical sterile fill-finish requirements omitted from the compatibility assessment
Minimize the likelihood of discovering facility space, utility, or structural constraints after candidate selection
Reduce the number of material and personnel flow conflicts missed during layout evaluation
Reduce the number of operator reach and maintenance-access limitations missed before purchase
Reduce the percentage of required vendor performance and interface evidence that is unavailable or noncomparable

acquisition.name: Qualify prospective suppliers
acquisition.statement: Qualify prospective containment isolator suppliers before placing an order.
acquisition.description: Assess and formally approve suppliers against quality, technical, regulatory, and financial criteria to create an approved supplier list prior to procurement.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of approving a supplier unable to fulfill containment isolator delivery and lifecycle support commitments
needs.plain: Avoid approving a supplier that cannot deliver a sealed safety enclosure or support it throughout its useful life. This helps ensure the equipment and its ongoing support will be available as promised.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of approving a prospective containment isolator supplier with undetected quality-system or regulatory deficiencies
needs.plain: Prevent approval of a supplier whose quality practices or legal compliance problems have not been found. This helps ensure the supplier can provide safe and reliable enclosed equipment for handling clean products.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of mandatory sterile fill-finish containment requirements left unverified during supplier qualification
Reduce the degree of inconsistency among quality, technical, and procurement evaluations of the same prospective supplier
Reduce the time required to obtain the supplier records and site access needed for qualification
needs.plain: Get the supplier records and permission to visit the supplier’s site more quickly. This helps the buyer check whether the supplier is suitable.

acquisition.name: Register equipment asset
acquisition.statement: Register the isolator in asset and warranty tracking systems.
acquisition.description: Warranty and traceability require the equipment to be logged into asset and quality systems.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that required isolator identification and warranty documents are unavailable at registration
needs.plain: Make sure the equipment's identity details and warranty papers are available when it is added to the company records. This helps the equipment be registered correctly and covered properly.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of incorrect isolator serial numbers, models, or installed locations recorded
needs.plain: Make sure the recorded serial number, model, and installed location are correct for each piece of equipment.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of conflicting isolator identifiers across asset and warranty tracking systems
needs.plain: Keep the equipment identification numbers consistent in the asset and warranty records. This helps people know they are referring to the same equipment.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of registering the isolator after the warranty enrollment deadline
needs.plain: Reduce the chance that the sealed enclosure is recorded after the deadline for signing it up for warranty coverage. This helps preserve the equipment’s coverage.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of duplicate asset records created for the same isolator
needs.plain: Make sure the same machine is not entered into the equipment list more than once. This keeps ownership, maintenance, and other records accurate.

acquisition.name: Request supplier proposals
acquisition.statement: Request configured proposals or quotations from qualified suppliers for the specified containment isolator.
acquisition.description: Formally approach shortlisted or qualified suppliers to obtain detailed technical and commercial proposals — including pricing, lead time, and configuration — for the required isolator specification.
acquisition.frequency: Once per procurement event, potentially repeated across multiple suppliers

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of mandatory isolator configuration requirements or commercial instructions that are missing or contradictory in the proposal request
Reduce the frequency of isolator requirement revisions issued after suppliers begin preparing proposals
Reduce the duration of proposal submission delays caused by unresolved supplier questions
needs.plain: Resolve supplier questions quickly so proposals can be sent in on time. This gives the buyer the information needed to compare options.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of qualified suppliers that decline to submit a proposal for the specified containment isolator
needs.plain: Fewer capable suppliers should refuse to send an offer for the specified sealed equipment. This matters because more offers give the buyer a better chance of finding a suitable option.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of qualified suppliers omitted from the proposal request distribution
needs.plain: Fewer capable suppliers should be left out when requests for offers are sent. This matters because including them gives the buyer more possible choices.

acquisition.name: Research isolator options
acquisition.statement: Research available isolator configurations and technologies on the market.
acquisition.description: Survey the market landscape of containment isolator types, configurations, and suppliers to build an informed long list of candidates.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of candidate isolator configurations with missing sterile fill-finish suitability information
Reduce the percentage of candidate isolator profiles based on outdated or inaccurate source information
Reduce the percentage of viable isolator suppliers or configurations omitted from the market survey
needs.plain: Include all suitable suppliers and setup choices in the search for sealed-chamber options. This helps avoid overlooking a better or more suitable choice.

needs.stmt: Reduce the degree of ambiguity in supplier terminology used to describe isolator configurations and capabilities
needs.plain: Make the supplier's descriptions of protective enclosures and their abilities easy to understand and compare. This helps people choose an option that provides the protection they actually need.

needs.stmt: Reduce the time spent waiting for suppliers to provide information needed to evaluate candidate isolators
needs.plain: Spend less time waiting for suppliers to provide information needed to compare possible enclosed work units. This helps people choose among the options sooner.

acquisition.name: Review design proposal
acquisition.statement: Review the vendor's proposed isolator design against the procurement specification.
acquisition.description: Evaluate the technical and commercial detail of a supplier's design proposal to determine conformance with specified requirements before committing to purchase.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that critical deviations from the procurement specification remain unidentified before purchase commitment
Reduce the number of procurement requirements lacking sufficient proposal evidence for a conformance decision
Reduce the number of conflicting technical or commercial details across the quotation, drawings, and technical descriptions
Minimize the likelihood that commercial exclusions or lifecycle cost obligations remain unidentified before purchase commitment
Reduce the duration of proposal review delay caused by unavailable quality, validation, safety, or operations input
needs.plain: Get all needed reviews of the proposed design without waiting for input from safety, quality, testing, or operations staff. This helps the proposal be reviewed and decided on sooner.

acquisition.name: Secure purchase approval
acquisition.statement: Secure authorization to purchase the selected pharmaceutical containment isolator.
acquisition.description: This completes the applicable capital, quality, safety, technical, and procurement approval process.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the approval package is rejected because required evidence is missing
Reduce the number of conflicting acceptance criteria preventing authorization of the selected isolator purchase
Reduce the amount by which the selected isolator purchase commitment exceeds the available capital allocation
needs.plain: Keep the commitment to buy the selected protective enclosure within the money set aside for the purchase. This helps secure approval without exceeding available funds.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration that authorization remains stalled while required functional sign-offs are outstanding
needs.plain: Get required approvals sooner instead of letting the request wait for people to sign off. This helps the purchase move forward more quickly.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that selected isolator configuration changes invalidate completed reviews
needs.plain: Make sure changes to the chosen sealed work chamber do not make earlier checks and approvals unusable.

acquisition.name: Seek supplier references
acquisition.statement: Seek references and reviews for candidate isolator suppliers.
acquisition.description: Peer references and factory visits inform confidence in a supplier's containment track record.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that reference claims about containment performance cannot be authenticated
Reduce the percentage of supplier-provided references that omit adverse containment-isolator project experiences
needs.plain: Get supplier references that mention any bad experiences with projects involving sealed containment chambers, instead of leaving those experiences out. This helps reveal possible problems before choosing a supplier.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of finding no references covering comparable sterile fill-finish isolator installations
needs.plain: Find examples from suppliers of similar enclosed equipment used to make sterile products. These examples help show whether a supplier has relevant experience.

needs.stmt: Reduce the degree of conflict between references and reviews about the supplier's containment track record
needs.plain: Make sure customer references and published reviews give a consistent picture of how well the supplier has handled problems and kept them from spreading. This helps assess the supplier’s real-world performance.

needs.stmt: Reduce the time spent waiting for reference contacts to respond during supplier evaluation
needs.plain: Spend less time waiting for people who can give references to reply during a supplier review. This helps the buyer judge the supplier sooner.

acquisition.name: Transition operations to a replacement isolator
acquisition.statement: Transition active operations, validated configurations, and trained personnel to a replacement isolator.
acquisition.description: Manage the operational handover from the retiring isolator to its replacement, including configuration transfer, SOPs, and personnel familiarisation.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of product contamination during transfer of active sterile operations to the replacement isolator
needs.plain: Reduce the chance that the product becomes dirty or unsafe while ongoing sterile work is moved to the replacement sealed enclosure. This helps protect product quality during the changeover.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of unplanned sterile fill-finish production interruption during the isolator handover
needs.plain: Avoid unexpected stoppages in the production of sterile products while operations are moved to replacement enclosed equipment. This helps production continue during the changeover.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of validated isolator configuration parameters transferred incorrectly or omitted at handover
needs.plain: When operations move to a replacement sealed chamber, transfer all approved setup details correctly and leave nothing out. This helps the replacement operate in the intended way.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of SOP instructions that are outdated or conflict with replacement-isolator operation at handover
needs.plain: The written work instructions should be current and should match how the replacement enclosed work unit operates when it is handed over. This helps staff use the new equipment correctly from the start.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of assigned personnel unable to perform their replacement-isolator responsibilities correctly at handover
needs.plain: Have fewer assigned workers who cannot correctly carry out their duties for the replacement sealed equipment when responsibility is handed over. This helps keep operations safe and continuous.

acquisition.name: Verify regulatory compliance
acquisition.statement: Verify the isolator meets applicable regulatory and containment standards.
acquisition.description: Confirm that a candidate isolator's design and certifications satisfy relevant regulatory requirements for the intended operating environment.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of applicable regulatory or containment requirements omitted from the isolator compliance assessment
Minimize the likelihood that isolator certifications are expired or invalid for the intended operating jurisdiction
Minimize the likelihood that the candidate isolator configuration falls outside the scope of its certifications
Reduce the percentage of isolator compliance claims lacking verifiable supporting evidence
Reduce the duration of isolator compliance verification caused by unresolved conflicting interpretations of applicable requirements

maintenance.name: Back up configuration data
maintenance.statement: Back up the isolator control-system configuration data.
maintenance.description: Current backups support restoration of validated settings, recipes, user configurations, and audit records after a control-system failure.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of required validated settings, recipes, user configurations, and audit records omitted from the backup
Minimize the likelihood that backed-up isolator configuration data is corrupted when restoration is required
Minimize the likelihood that the isolator configuration backup is inaccessible when restoration is required
Reduce the amount of validated configuration change absent from the latest backup
Minimize the likelihood of associating a configuration backup with the wrong isolator or configuration version
needs.plain: Make sure each saved setup file is linked to the correct sealed work enclosure and the correct version of its settings. A mix-up could restore the wrong settings to the equipment.

maintenance.name: Calibrate pressure sensors
maintenance.statement: Calibrate the differential pressure and monitoring instrumentation.
maintenance.description: Accurate pressure readings are essential to prove containment and require periodic calibration.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of containment integrity being compromised during calibration activities
needs.plain: Keep the sealed area intact while pressure-measuring devices are adjusted, so nothing can leak out or get in.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of differential pressure instruments outside approved tolerance after calibration
needs.plain: Make sure pressure-measuring devices give readings within the approved range after they are checked and adjusted. Accurate pressure readings help confirm that the process is operating properly.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that reference standards have expired status or incomplete traceability records
Minimize the degree of isolator pressure fluctuation while calibration readings are taken
Reduce the duration of isolator unavailability beyond the scheduled maintenance window

maintenance.name: Clean and decontaminate isolator interior
maintenance.statement: Clean the isolator's interior surfaces and perform decontamination to remove particulate, chemical, and microbial residues.
maintenance.description: Execute scheduled or triggered cleaning of the interior working chamber followed by a validated decontamination cycle to restore the isolator to the required cleanliness and microbial-control standard between uses or maintenance events.
maintenance.frequency: Scheduled and event-driven; multiple times during service life

needs.stmt: Minimize the percentage of isolator interior surface area retaining particulate, chemical, or microbial residues after cleaning and decontamination
needs.plain: Remove as much dust, chemicals, and germs as possible from the inside surfaces after cleaning and treatment. This helps prevent the next product from being contaminated.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of operator exposure to hazardous product residues or decontamination agents during interior cleaning and decontamination
needs.plain: Protect workers from dangerous leftover product or cleaning chemicals while they clean the inside of the enclosed work area. This reduces the chance of harmful exposure.

needs.stmt: Reduce the frequency of decontamination cycles interrupted before completion
Reduce the number of isolator interior surfaces, seals, or components damaged during a cleaning and decontamination event
Reduce the time required to resolve missing or inconsistent cleaning and decontamination records before isolator release

maintenance.name: Document maintenance history
maintenance.statement: Record all maintenance, calibration, and repair activities for the isolator.
maintenance.description: Maintain a complete, traceable log of maintenance events to support regulatory compliance and operational continuity.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of isolator maintenance, calibration, and repair activities omitted from the history
Reduce the rate of isolator history entries containing inaccurate activity details
needs.plain: Fewer maintenance history records for enclosed equipment should contain wrong details about what work was done. Accurate records help people understand the equipment’s past maintenance.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of isolator history entries lacking required traceability identifiers or approvals
needs.plain: Make sure each maintenance record for an isolator includes the required identifying details and approvals. This shows which equipment the work involved and whether it was properly approved.

needs.stmt: Minimize the time between completion of an isolator maintenance activity and its entry in the history
needs.plain: Enter completed upkeep work on the enclosed equipment in its history as soon as possible. This keeps a current record of what was done.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that required isolator history records are unavailable during an audit or maintenance review
needs.plain: Make sure the equipment's past service records can be found during an inspection or repair review. These records help people understand what work has already been done.

maintenance.name: Inspect containment boundaries
maintenance.statement: Inspect the isolator containment boundary for deterioration or damage.
maintenance.description: Examine all structural and sealing surfaces of the containment envelope for signs of wear, chemical attack, or physical damage.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of misclassifying observed deterioration or damage during containment boundary inspection
needs.plain: Make sure visible wear or damage to the protective enclosure is identified correctly. Accurate checks help prevent leaks or unsafe exposure.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of failing to detect deterioration or damage on examined containment surfaces
needs.plain: Make it unlikely that damage or wear on the surfaces that keep material contained will go unnoticed. Finding problems early helps prevent leaks or loss of containment.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of introducing contamination into the controlled environment during containment boundary inspection
needs.plain: Make it unlikely that dirt or other unwanted material will enter the controlled area during the inspection. Keeping the area clean helps protect the work and products inside.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of damaging structural or sealing surfaces during containment boundary inspection
Reduce the percentage of containment boundary surfaces left unexamined during periodic inspection

maintenance.name: Inspect seals and gaskets
maintenance.statement: Inspect door seals, gaskets, and transfer port seals for wear or damage.
maintenance.description: Visually and physically examine all elastomeric and mechanical sealing surfaces for degradation, cracking, or deformation.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of introducing contamination into the isolator controlled environment during physical examination
needs.plain: Lower the chance that germs or dirt enter the protected work area while seals are being examined. This helps keep the area suitable for making sterile medicine.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of damaging or deforming a seal during physical examination
needs.plain: Prevent a seal from being cut, crushed, or misshaped while it is being examined. Keeping it in good condition helps it continue to block leaks.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of seal wear, cracking, or deformation remaining undetected during inspection
Minimize the degree of uncertainty in classifying observed seal wear or damage against maintenance criteria
Reduce the number of door seal, gasket, and transfer port seal surfaces omitted from each periodic inspection

maintenance.name: Inspect viewing panels
maintenance.statement: Inspect the isolator windows and viewing panels for cracks or clouding.
maintenance.description: Panel integrity is part of the containment barrier and affects operator visibility.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of subtle cracks or clouding being overlooked in inspected panel areas
Minimize the likelihood of surface residue or reflections being misclassified as panel cracks or clouding
Reduce the frequency of conflicting crack or clouding classifications among technicians applying the inspection criteria
Reduce the percentage of isolator-window and viewing-panel surfaces left uninspected because access is obstructed
needs.plain: Make sure all parts of the sealed work chamber's windows and viewing panels can be reached and checked. Blocked areas could hide damage or dirt.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of isolator unavailability caused by delays in completing the panel inspection
needs.plain: Spend less time with the enclosed equipment unavailable because checking its viewing windows takes too long. This helps the equipment return to use sooner.

maintenance.name: Repair mechanical faults
maintenance.statement: Repair failed or damaged mechanical components of the isolator.
maintenance.description: Diagnose and restore failed transfer ports, doors, hinges, latches, or other mechanical assemblies to operational condition.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of losing isolator containment during mechanical repair
needs.plain: Keep the sealed enclosure secure while fixing mechanical problems, so harmful material does not escape or enter.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood the repaired mechanical assembly fails post-repair verification
Reduce the number of adjacent isolator components damaged during repair
Minimize the likelihood of misidentifying the source of the mechanical fault
Reduce the time the repair is delayed by unavailable compatible replacement components

maintenance.name: Replace air filtration media
maintenance.statement: Replace the isolator's HEPA, ULPA, or other air filtration media at end of service life or following a failed integrity test.
maintenance.description: Remove and safely dispose of exhausted or failed high-efficiency filtration units and install qualified replacement media to restore specified air-handling performance.
maintenance.frequency: Periodic, based on manufacturer intervals or test results

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of installing filtration media with specifications incompatible with the isolator's containment requirements
needs.plain: Install air filters that provide the level of protection the enclosed work area needs. The wrong filters may not keep unwanted material contained.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of maintenance technicians being exposed to hazardous residues retained by exhausted filtration media
needs.plain: Reduce the chance that maintenance workers come into contact with harmful substances trapped in used air filters. This helps protect them while they remove and replace the filters.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of contaminants escaping the isolator during removal and disposal of exhausted filtration media
needs.plain: Keep harmful particles from escaping the enclosed work area while used air-filter material is removed and discarded.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of air bypass around the newly installed filtration media at its sealing interface
Reduce the duration of isolator unavailability caused by qualified replacement media not being ready when maintenance begins

maintenance.name: Replace glove and sleeve assemblies
maintenance.statement: Replace worn or breached glove and sleeve assemblies on the isolator.
maintenance.description: Remove degraded glove and half-suit sleeve components and install new assemblies to maintain containment integrity.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of introducing microbial or particulate contamination into the isolator during glove and sleeve assembly replacement
needs.plain: Make it less likely that germs or particles enter the enclosed clean chamber while its flexible gloves and sleeves are replaced. This helps keep the product and work area clean.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of technician exposure to residual hazardous material during glove and sleeve assembly removal
needs.plain: Protect the technician from contact with dangerous material left inside the equipment when protective gloves and sleeves are removed. This reduces the risk of harmful exposure.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the installed glove or sleeve assembly fails containment integrity verification
needs.plain: Make sure the replacement glove or sleeve assembly keeps the enclosed area sealed when it is checked.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of selecting an incompatible glove or sleeve assembly for the isolator
Minimize the likelihood that the required glove or sleeve assembly is unavailable when maintenance begins

maintenance.name: Replenish sterilant supply
maintenance.statement: Replenish the sterilant agent supply used for chamber decontamination.
maintenance.description: Refill or replace the sterilant consumable reservoir to ensure decontamination cycles can be completed.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of loading incompatible or out-of-spec sterilant agent into the reservoir
needs.plain: Make sure the correct sterilizing chemical, in the required condition, is put into the supply tank. The wrong chemical or quality could prevent proper sterilization.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of sterilant leakage from the reservoir after replenishment
needs.plain: Prevent the disinfecting chemical from leaking out of its container after it is refilled. This protects people, equipment, and the surrounding area.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of introducing contamination into the sterilant supply during replenishment
needs.plain: Keep dirt or other unwanted material from getting into the supply of sterilizing liquid while it is being refilled. This helps the cleaning process work safely and properly.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of a sterilant spill or operator exposure during reservoir replenishment
needs.plain: Make it as unlikely as possible for sterilizing liquid to spill or touch the worker while a supply tank is being refilled. This protects both the worker and the workplace.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the required sterilant agent is unavailable when reservoir replenishment is needed

maintenance.name: Requalify containment performance
maintenance.statement: Requalify the isolator's containment and sterility performance.
maintenance.description: Execute formal requalification protocols to confirm the isolator continues to meet defined containment and environmental standards.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that isolator containment leakage exceeds the defined acceptance limit during requalification
needs.plain: When checking the enclosed equipment again, the amount of material escaping from it should stay within the allowed limit. This helps keep harmful material contained.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that isolator environmental conditions fail defined sterility acceptance criteria during requalification
needs.plain: Make sure the enclosure’s conditions still meet the required cleanliness standards when its protective performance is checked again. This confirms it can continue to prevent contamination.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that required calibration records or approved protocol prerequisites are unavailable when requalification testing begins
Reduce the percentage of requalification records invalidated by protocol deviations or incomplete evidence
Reduce the duration of scheduled sterile fill-finish disruption caused by requalification overruns

maintenance.name: Schedule preventive service
maintenance.statement: Schedule preventive maintenance intervals for the isolator system.
maintenance.description: Planned servicing avoids unplanned downtime and keeps the unit in a qualified state.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the isolator leaves its qualified state before scheduled preventive service
Minimize the likelihood that preventive maintenance intervals remain unchanged after isolator use conditions change
Reduce the amount of unnecessary isolator downtime caused by overly short preventive maintenance intervals
Reduce the number of required isolator maintenance activities omitted from the interval schedule
Reduce the frequency of scheduled preventive service conflicts with sterile fill-finish campaigns

maintenance.name: Service air-handling components
maintenance.statement: Service the isolator's air-handling components.
maintenance.description: Inspect, clean, lubricate, and replace blowers, dampers, ducting, and other air-handling elements to maintain airflow performance.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of technician exposure to hazardous residues while servicing air-handling components
needs.plain: Protect the technician from dangerous leftover material while working on the parts that move or control air. This reduces the chance of harmful exposure during servicing.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of introducing contaminants into the isolator during air-handling service
needs.plain: Prevent dirt or other unwanted material from entering the sealed work area while its air-handling parts are being serviced.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that a degraded air-handling component remains undetected during inspection
needs.plain: Make it very unlikely that an air-moving part that is wearing out goes unnoticed during a check. Finding problems early helps keep air movement safe and reliable.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of air-handling components incorrectly reassembled after service
needs.plain: Reduce the number of air-moving parts that are put back together incorrectly after service. Correct assembly helps the equipment work safely and properly.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration the isolator remains unavailable while awaiting a required replacement component

maintenance.name: Test enclosure leak-tightness
maintenance.statement: Perform a pressure-based leak test on the isolator enclosure.
maintenance.description: Conduct a pressure decay or equivalent leak-rate test on the full enclosure boundary to verify containment.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of technician exposure to hazardous residue while accessing the enclosure boundary
needs.plain: Prevent the technician from touching dangerous residue while checking whether the enclosure keeps air from leaking. This helps protect the technician during the test.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that pressure measurements fall outside calibration tolerance during the enclosure leak test
needs.plain: Make sure the pressure readings used during the leak test are accurate enough. Inaccurate readings could give a false result about whether the enclosure leaks.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that excessive test pressure damages an enclosure boundary component
needs.plain: Keep the pressure used to check for leaks low enough that it does not damage the enclosure or its parts. Too much pressure could cause damage during the test.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of enclosure penetrations left open or improperly sealed during the test
Reduce the amount of measurement error caused by enclosure temperature drift during the test

maintenance.name: Test glove and sleeve integrity
maintenance.statement: Test the isolator gloves and sleeves for leaks or breaches.
maintenance.description: Perform pressure or tracer-based integrity tests on all glove and sleeve assemblies to confirm containment.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of a leaking glove or sleeve assembly passing the integrity test
needs.plain: Make sure a glove or flexible sleeve with a leak cannot pass the safety check. This protects workers and keeps the clean area from being exposed to unwanted material.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of technician exposure to contained material during integrity testing
needs.plain: Protect the technician from contact with contained material while checking whether the gloves and sleeves are intact. This helps prevent exposure during the test.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of an intact glove or sleeve assembly incorrectly failing the integrity test
Reduce the percentage of glove and sleeve assemblies omitted from periodic integrity testing
Reduce the number of integrity results assigned to the wrong glove or sleeve assembly

maintenance.name: Update control software
maintenance.statement: Update the isolator's control system software or firmware.
maintenance.description: Install revised software or firmware versions on the isolator control system to maintain functionality, security, and compliance.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the revised software or firmware causes incorrect isolator control behavior after installation
needs.plain: Make sure a software update does not make the enclosed equipment behave incorrectly. This helps keep its controls working safely after the update.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that an interrupted update leaves the isolator control system unusable
Reduce the amount of isolator configuration, recipe, or audit record data lost or altered during the update
Reduce the duration of isolator unavailability that disrupts scheduled sterile fill-finish activities
Reduce the percentage of required update records and validation evidence remaining incomplete after installation

maintenance.name: Verify protective control functions
maintenance.statement: Verify each protective control function of the isolator operates correctly.
maintenance.description: Test interlocks, alarms, emergency stops, and other engineered protective controls to confirm correct operation.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that protective-control testing exposes the technician to a hazardous state
needs.plain: Make it very unlikely that testing a safety feature puts the person doing the test in danger. The test should confirm the feature works without creating a hazardous situation.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that a failed protective control incorrectly passes verification
needs.plain: Make it very unlikely that a broken safety feature is incorrectly judged to be working. Testing must clearly identify failures before the equipment is used.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that protective-control testing compromises isolator containment
needs.plain: Make sure testing the safety controls does not break the sealed barrier that keeps harmful material inside. This protects people and the surrounding area during testing.

needs.stmt: Minimize the percent of required protective control functions left untested
needs.plain: Test as many required safety features as possible, leaving as few unchecked as possible. This helps show that the equipment can protect people and the process.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that a correctly functioning protective control incorrectly fails verification

usage.name: Activate breach response
usage.statement: Activate the isolator's containment response after a boundary breach.
usage.description: This limits release or exposure when glove integrity, transfer integrity, or enclosure integrity is lost.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree to which the required containment response remains inactive after activation
needs.plain: Make sure the required response to a breach starts fully and quickly after it is turned on. This helps limit the effects of a loss of containment.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the containment response fails to initiate after the operator command
needs.plain: Make sure the system starts containing a leak or other breach when the operator gives the command. This limits the spread of hazardous material.

needs.stmt: Minimize the time between operator recognition of the boundary breach and containment response activation
needs.plain: Start containing the breach as soon as the operator notices it. A faster response can limit the spread or effects of the problem.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of operator exposure to hazardous material while activating the containment response
needs.plain: Protect operators from dangerous material while they start the response to contain a breach. They should be able to act without being exposed.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the incorrect containment zone is activated after the breach
needs.plain: Make sure the response activates the correct area for containing a leak or breach. This helps keep the hazard from spreading to the wrong place.

usage.name: Activate containment operation
usage.statement: Activate the enclosure's designated operating mode for the current process.
usage.description: Operator initiates the appropriate operating mode — positive pressure, negative pressure, or recirculation — on the enclosure control system prior to production.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the designated operating mode fails to establish the required pressure direction and airflow conditions
needs.plain: Make sure the selected operating setting creates the needed direction of pressure and movement of air. This helps keep contamination from entering the protected area.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of loss of containment while transitioning into the designated operating mode
needs.plain: Prevent the protected area or material from escaping while the system switches into its assigned operating state. This helps maintain protection during the changeover.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of activating an incorrect operating mode for the current sterile fill-finish process
needs.plain: Make sure the correct operating mode is turned on for the current clean filling process.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of the wait for the enclosure to stabilize after operating-mode activation
Minimize the likelihood that unmet enclosure prerequisites block operating-mode activation before production

usage.name: Activate the isolator operating mode
usage.statement: Activate the isolator's operating mode for active containment.
usage.description: Operator initiates the isolator's active containment state, confirming airflow, pressure, and safety interlocks are engaged before work begins.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that operating status falsely indicates established containment during activation
needs.plain: The machine’s status should not falsely say that the product is safely enclosed when that protection has not really been established. Accurate status information helps prevent unsafe operation.

usage.name: Adapt work technique to isolator geometry
usage.statement: Adapt manual work technique to accommodate the spatial constraints of the isolator enclosure.
usage.description: Operator modifies grip, posture, and tool use to work effectively within the fixed geometry and reach limits imposed by the isolator's glove ports, sleeves, or half-suit entry points.

needs.stmt: Minimize the percent of required manipulations that cannot be completed within operator reach limits

usage.name: Adjust airflow and pressure settings
usage.statement: Adjust the isolator's airflow and pressure settings for the task at hand.
usage.description: Operator modifies airflow rate and pressure differential setpoints via the control interface to match the requirements of the current task.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of incorrect airflow or pressure setpoints entered for the current task

usage.name: Adjust operating setpoints
usage.statement: Adjust authorized setpoints on the enclosure control system for the active operation.
usage.description: Authorized operator modifies pressure, airflow, temperature, or alarm threshold setpoints via the control interface to match process requirements.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that an adjusted alarm threshold suppresses detection of a genuine enclosure excursion
needs.plain: Avoid changing an alarm limit so much that it hides a real loss of protection inside the sealed enclosure. The warning should still appear when conditions become unsafe.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that enclosure conditions leave qualified limits after a setpoint adjustment
needs.plain: After a setting is changed, the conditions inside the enclosure should remain within the approved safe range. This helps keep the process working as intended.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of adjusted setpoints that do not match approved process requirements
needs.plain: Make sure the operating settings that workers change match the approved process requirements. Incorrect settings can affect how the process performs.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that an authorized setpoint change fails to take effect
needs.plain: Make sure an approved change to a machine setting actually takes effect. Otherwise, the machine may continue operating at the wrong setting.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of encountering conflicting setpoint requirements for the active operation

usage.name: Check glove integrity
usage.statement: Inspect enclosure gloves for signs of integrity loss during active operation.
usage.description: Operator visually and tactilely examines gloves and sleeve assemblies for tears, punctures, or deformation while the enclosure is running.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of glove and sleeve integrity losses that remain undetected during inspection
needs.plain: Find damaged gloves or sleeves during the inspection instead of leaving the damage unnoticed. This helps keep contaminants from reaching the product or worker.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that inspection handling damages a glove or sleeve assembly during active operation
needs.plain: Avoid tearing or damaging the attached gloves or protective sleeves while checking them during operation. This helps keep the operator and the work area protected.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that inspection handling transfers contamination within the enclosure during active operation
needs.plain: Make sure checking or handling items does not spread unwanted material inside the enclosure while it is operating. This helps protect the work and the products inside.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of glove and sleeve surfaces left unexamined during active operation
Reduce the number of intact gloves or sleeves misclassified as compromised during inspection

usage.name: Collect process samples
usage.statement: Withdraw in-process samples for testing while preserving containment.
usage.description: Sampling for quality testing must occur without breaking containment or introducing contamination.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of releasing contained process material into the technician's work area during sample withdrawal
needs.plain: Prevent process material from escaping into the technician’s work area while a sample is being taken. This helps protect the technician and keep the surrounding area clean.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of introducing contaminants into the sterile process during sample withdrawal
needs.plain: Keep dirt or germs from entering the sterile process while taking a sample. This protects both the sample and the remaining product.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of withdrawn samples assigned to the wrong batch or sampling point
Reduce the percentage of withdrawn samples that are not representative of the process material at the designated sampling point
Minimize the likelihood of withdrawing less sample material than required for scheduled testing

usage.name: Contain hazardous compounds
usage.statement: Contain potent or hazardous materials while dispensing, weighing, or processing them inside the enclosure.
usage.description: Operator uses the enclosure to prevent operator and environmental exposure when handling highly potent or toxic substances during active processing.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of operator contact with hazardous compounds through a breached handling interface during active manipulation
needs.plain: Prevent workers from touching dangerous substances through a damaged barrier while handling them. Keeping the barrier intact protects people from harmful exposure.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of hazardous compounds escaping from the enclosure into the surrounding cleanroom
needs.plain: Keep dangerous chemicals from escaping the enclosed area into the surrounding clean room. This protects people and keeps the room clean and safe.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of containment conditions remaining outside validated limits during processing
needs.plain: Shorten the time that the conditions needed to safely contain dangerous chemicals are outside their approved safe limits during processing. This reduces the chance of chemicals escaping.

needs.stmt: Reduce the time containment loss remains undetected during active processing
needs.plain: Detect as quickly as possible if hazardous material starts escaping during processing. This limits how long the escape can continue unnoticed.

needs.stmt: Reduce the amount of hazardous residue leaving the enclosure on removed materials or tools
needs.plain: Keep as much hazardous material as possible from leaving the enclosed area on removed parts or tools. This helps protect people and the surrounding work area.

usage.name: Coordinate multi-operator use
usage.statement: Coordinate simultaneous work by multiple operators at shared glove ports.
usage.description: Larger isolators support several operators working the same enclosure on collaborative tasks.

needs.stmt: Reduce the frequency of collisions between operators' hands or tools inside the isolator

usage.name: Coordinate multi-operator work
usage.statement: Coordinate simultaneous or sequential operator access to the enclosure during a production run.
usage.description: Lead operator manages glove-port assignments, task sequencing, and communication among multiple operators working at the same enclosure.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of containment being disrupted by uncoordinated simultaneous operator movements
needs.plain: Prevent several workers from making conflicting moves at the same time and breaking the sealed area. Everyone needs to coordinate so the material stays safely contained.

needs.stmt: Reduce the frequency of enclosure tasks being started before prerequisite operator tasks are complete
Reduce the number of conflicting glove-port assignments during the production run
Reduce the number of operator handoffs with misunderstood task status during the production run
Reduce the duration of operator waiting caused by contention for assigned enclosure access
needs.plain: Reduce the time operators spend waiting to use the assigned enclosed work area when others need it too. This helps several operators complete their work with fewer interruptions.

usage.name: Document isolator operating activity
usage.statement: Record operational parameters and activities performed within the isolator.
usage.description: Operator or designated recorder logs operating conditions, material identities, and procedural actions taken during the isolator session.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of required operating parameters and activities omitted from the isolator session record

usage.name: Document usage activity
usage.statement: Record operating parameters, batch details, and interventions during use.
usage.description: Regulatory and quality requirements mandate a record of what happened during each run.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of pharmaceutical isolator run records linked to the wrong batch
Reduce the number of recorded operating parameters that differ from actual pharmaceutical isolator run conditions
Reduce the percentage of interventions omitted from the pharmaceutical isolator run record
Minimize the likelihood that a completed pharmaceutical isolator run record is unavailable for quality review
Reduce the duration between a pharmaceutical isolator run event and its documentation
needs.plain: Shorten the time between an event during use of the sealed medicine-making enclosure and writing it down. This helps keep the activity record timely and accurate.

usage.name: Handle hazardous substances within the isolator
usage.statement: Handle toxic, potent, or infectious substances within the contained workspace.
usage.description: Operator works with hazardous materials inside the isolator enclosure, relying on the containment barrier for protection.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of a hazardous substance escaping from the isolator into the surrounding cleanroom during handling
needs.plain: Prevent a dangerous substance from escaping the enclosed work area into the surrounding clean room. This helps protect workers, the room, and nearby products.

usage.name: Handle varying batch scales
usage.statement: Process material across different batch volumes within the same enclosure.
usage.description: Different batch sizes and processes require the isolator to accommodate varying throughput.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of losing sterile containment at higher material throughput
needs.plain: Keep the product protected from germs and other contamination when handling batches of different sizes.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of material load exceeding the usable enclosure capacity at the required batch volume
Reduce the degree of airflow variation around material across batch volumes
Reduce the percent of material lost to residual hold-up when processing smaller batches
Reduce the duration of enclosure downtime when transitioning between batch volumes
needs.plain: Spend less time with the enclosed equipment out of service when changing the amount made in a production batch. This helps the machine handle different production sizes more quickly.

usage.name: Maintain aseptic conditions
usage.statement: Maintain aseptic conditions inside the isolator throughout the operation.
usage.description: Sterile processing requires ongoing prevention of contamination during every handling step.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of microbial contamination entering through sterile-to-nonsterile contact inside the isolator
needs.plain: Prevent germs from passing from an unclean area into the clean product area inside the enclosed work chamber. This helps keep the product safe from contamination.

usage.name: Monitor environmental conditions
