needs.stmt: Minimize the amount of required project cost omitted from the agreed purchase price
needs.plain: Make sure the agreed purchase price includes every project cost that is needed. This prevents unexpected charges later.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of contract execution delay caused by unresolved purchaser and supplier approvals
needs.plain: Avoid delaying the purchase because the buyer and supplier have not finished approving it. This helps the agreement be signed and carried out sooner.

acquisition.name: Configure system options
acquisition.statement: Configure the required system options and specifications for the intended installation.
acquisition.description: Select and specify the configuration options, accessories, and technical parameters of the batch preparation system to match operational and site requirements before placing an order.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that selected options conflict with sterile processing and cleaning requirements
needs.plain: Choose settings that will not interfere with keeping the equipment clean and free from harmful germs. This helps prevent contamination during use.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that selected configuration options and accessories are incompatible

acquisition.name: Confirm controls compatibility
acquisition.statement: Confirm the batch preparation system's compatibility with existing control and communication infrastructure.
acquisition.description: Assess whether the candidate system's control interfaces, communication protocols, data formats, and security requirements are compatible with the site's existing automation and IT infrastructure.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of incompatibilities between candidate-system controls and planned site interfaces and protocols
Minimize the likelihood that assessed interfaces differ from the system configuration being acquired

acquisition.name: Confirm lead time and availability
acquisition.statement: Confirm the supplier's manufacturing lead time and component availability for the specified system configuration.
acquisition.description: Obtain a firm lead-time commitment and confirm that long-lead components (e.g., specialised instrumentation, custom vessels) are available to meet the project schedule.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the supplier's committed completion date falls after the required site-delivery date
needs.plain: Make sure the supplier can finish the order early enough for it to arrive at the work site by the required date. This helps keep the work on schedule.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that critical custom components are unavailable when manufacturing must begin
needs.plain: Make sure important specially made parts will be available when manufacturing needs to start.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of specified configuration requirements omitted from the supplier's commitment
needs.plain: Make sure the supplier agrees to every required setup or feature. This prevents important requirements from being left out of the supplier's promise.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of waiting for the supplier to provide a firm manufacturing and component-availability commitment
needs.plain: Get a definite promise from the supplier about when the item can be made and whether its parts are available. This helps the buyer know when the order can realistically be delivered.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that confirmed component availability changes before purchase terms are finalized
needs.plain: Make sure the parts that are currently available will still be available when the purchase agreement is completed. A change could delay the order.

acquisition.name: Confirm regulatory compliance
acquisition.statement: Confirm the system meets applicable regulatory and industry compliance standards.
acquisition.description: Regulated industries require the system to meet applicable hygiene and safety standards.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of unresolved deviations from sanitary, safety, or regulatory standards in the proposed plant
needs.plain: The proposed facility should have as few unresolved problems as possible with cleanliness, safety, and legal requirements. This helps ensure it can operate safely and be approved to run.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of supplier compliance documents that do not correspond to the proposed plant configuration and intended sterile fill-finish process
Reduce the percentage of required compliance evidence that is missing or incomplete
Reduce the percentage of required compliance evidence missing from the acquisition record
Reduce the number of applicable jurisdictional or industry requirements omitted from the compliance review
needs.plain: Make sure the review covers every relevant government rule and industry requirement. This helps the organization avoid breaking rules or missing required protections.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of required supplier compliance evidence remaining unavailable before purchase commitment
needs.plain: Reduce the chance that required proof of a supplier’s compliance with rules is still missing before the purchase is approved. This helps ensure the supplier meets the company’s requirements.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of required supplier certifications that are expired, invalid, or unverifiable
needs.plain: Make sure all supplier certificates required by law or company rules are current, genuine, and possible to verify. This helps confirm that the supplier still meets the requirements.

acquisition.name: Confirm spare-parts and service availability
acquisition.statement: Confirm the availability of spare parts and field service support from candidate suppliers.
acquisition.description: Verify that the vendor can supply critical spare parts and provide qualified field service technicians within acceptable lead times, as part of the pre-purchase evaluation.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of critical skid components omitted from supplier spare-parts availability information

acquisition.name: Coordinate delivery logistics and track progress
acquisition.statement: Coordinate shipment, freight, delivery scheduling, and progress tracking for plant components.
acquisition.description: Manages all outbound logistics from supplier to site, including freight arrangement, scheduling, and real-time progress monitoring, to ensure plant components arrive on time and in the correct sequence.
acquisition.userGroup: Procurement managers, project engineers, logistics coordinators
acquisition.frequency: Once per acquisition, throughout the delivery period

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of plant components arriving outside the required installation sequence
needs.role: Procurement managers, project engineers, logistics coordinators

acquisition.name: Define plant specifications
acquisition.statement: Define the required specifications for the viscous product processing plant.
acquisition.description: Document the process, capacity, material-handling, utility, and compliance specifications that candidate plants must meet.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of required sterile-processing and contamination-control criteria omitted from the plant specification

acquisition.name: Define process requirements
acquisition.statement: Define the process requirements the skid must satisfy before vendor engagement.
acquisition.description: Establish the functional, performance, and capacity requirements that any candidate skid must meet, forming the baseline for evaluation and procurement.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of critical sterility and containment conditions omitted from skid requirements before vendor engagement

acquisition.name: Define system requirements
acquisition.statement: Define the required characteristics of the batch preparation system.
acquisition.description: Establish the technical, operational, regulatory, and site requirements that a batch preparation system must meet, forming the basis for supplier selection and evaluation.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of critical sterility, containment, or product-contact requirements omitted from the specification
needs.plain: Make sure the equipment requirements do not leave out important needs for preventing contamination, containing hazards, or protecting the product. Missing requirements can lead to unsafe or unsuitable equipment.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of batch preparation requirements lacking verifiable acceptance criteria
Reduce the number of critical sterile fill-finish requirements omitted from the requirements baseline
needs.role: Process engineers, project managers, procurement leads.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of mismatch between specified batching capacity and projected production demand
needs.role: Process engineers, project managers, procurement leads.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of unresolved conflicts among quality, production, engineering, and procurement requirements
needs.plain: Resolve disagreements among quality, production, engineering, and purchasing needs when describing the system. This helps prevent conflicting instructions and costly changes later.
needs.role: Process engineers, project managers, procurement leads.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of ambiguity in requirements for material transfers, utilities, cleaning, and environmental interfaces
needs.plain: Clearly state what is needed for moving materials, supplying services such as water or electricity, cleaning, and connecting to the surrounding environment. This prevents important requirements from being missed.
needs.role: Process engineers, project managers, procurement leads.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that specified requirements conflict with available space, utilities, or material-flow constraints
needs.plain: Make sure the requested system features fit the available room, power and other services, and the way materials move through the site.
needs.role: Process engineers, project managers, procurement leads.

acquisition.name: Evaluate and benchmark candidate skid options
acquisition.statement: Compare competing skid configurations, benchmark total cost of ownership, and assess vendor lead times against defined project requirements and milestones.
acquisition.description: Consolidates technical configuration comparison, cost-of-ownership benchmarking, and lead-time/schedule assessment into one structured evaluation activity performed before vendor selection.
acquisition.userGroup: Engineering, procurement, and project management teams conducting vendor selection.
acquisition.frequency: Once per acquisition cycle, during the vendor evaluation phase.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of defined project requirements with undetected gaps in candidate skid configurations
needs.role: Engineering, procurement, and project management teams conducting vendor selection.

acquisition.name: Evaluate capacity and specification fit
acquisition.statement: Evaluate whether candidate systems' capacity, throughput, and technical specifications match defined production requirements.
acquisition.description: Combines evaluation of batch capacity and throughput (#1) with comparison of technical specifications against requirements (#20), which are performed in the same decision activity and overlap in scope.
acquisition.userGroup: Process engineers, procurement leads, project managers.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of overlooking a required batch-size or operating-range mismatch during evaluation
needs.role: Process engineers, procurement leads, project managers.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of critical sterile fill-finish specifications evaluated without supporting supplier evidence
needs.role: Process engineers, procurement leads, project managers.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that candidate throughput is overstated by excluding cleaning, changeover, and planned downtime
needs.plain: Make sure the expected output includes time for cleaning, switching between products or tasks, and planned stoppages. Otherwise, the actual output may be less than promised.
needs.role: Process engineers, procurement leads, project managers.

needs.stmt: Reduce the degree of inconsistency in how engineering, procurement, and project stakeholders interpret production requirements
needs.plain: Make sure the engineering, purchasing, and project teams all understand the production needs in the same way before choosing equipment.
needs.role: Process engineers, procurement leads, project managers.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of supplier specifications presented on incompatible operating bases or units
needs.plain: Avoid comparing supplier information when it uses different conditions or measurement units. This makes it possible to compare the options fairly and accurately.
needs.role: Process engineers, procurement leads, project managers.

acquisition.name: Evaluate process fit
acquisition.statement: Evaluate whether a candidate plant fits the target liquid's physical and chemical properties.
acquisition.description: Matters when confirming the plant will handle the specific liquid's viscosity, temperature, and chemistry.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of target-liquid degradation under the candidate plant's shear, thermal, and residence conditions
needs.plain: Make sure the liquid will not be damaged by the candidate plant's mixing force, heat, or time spent in the equipment. This helps determine whether the process is suitable for the liquid.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the candidate plant loses process control at the extremes of the target liquid's viscosity and temperature ranges
needs.plain: Make sure the plant can keep the process under control when the liquid is at the thickest, thinnest, hottest, or coolest expected condition. This helps show that the process will work reliably across its intended range.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that wetted-material incompatibility with the target liquid goes undetected
needs.plain: Make sure the parts that touch the liquid are suitable for that liquid. Problems caused by an unsuitable material should be found before the process begins.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of uncertainty in the candidate plant's allowable viscosity, temperature, pressure, and chemical exposure limits
needs.plain: Clearly determine the limits of the possible plant for thickness, temperature, pressure, and contact with chemicals. Knowing these limits helps people judge whether the plant can safely handle the process.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of required target-liquid property values that are missing, outdated, or inaccurate

acquisition.name: Evaluate regulatory compliance evidence
acquisition.statement: Evaluate compliance documentation, certifications, and regulatory evidence supplied by candidate system suppliers.
acquisition.description: Combines confirming the system meets certification standards (#3) with evaluating the compliance documentation and evidence supplied (#23). Both jobs occur in the same evaluation step and address the same compliance gate.
acquisition.userGroup: Procurement leads, quality engineers, regulatory affairs personnel.
acquisition.frequency: Once per acquisition event, per candidate system evaluated.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that submitted certifications are expired, invalid, or outside the proposed batching system's scope
needs.role: Procurement leads, quality engineers, regulatory affairs personnel.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of mismatch between compliance evidence and the proposed batching system configuration
needs.role: Procurement leads, quality engineers, regulatory affairs personnel.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of applicable sterile fill-finish requirements unsupported by supplier evidence
needs.role: Procurement leads, quality engineers, regulatory affairs personnel.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of material contradictions among compliance documents, certifications, and supplier claims
needs.plain: Make sure compliance documents, certificates, and supplier statements do not significantly contradict one another. Consistent evidence makes it easier to judge whether the product or supplier meets the required rules.
needs.role: Procurement leads, quality engineers, regulatory affairs personnel.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of the compliance-gate delay caused by late supplier clarifications
needs.plain: Spend less time waiting at the required compliance checkpoint because the supplier provides explanations late. This helps the work move forward sooner.
needs.role: Procurement leads, quality engineers, regulatory affairs personnel.

acquisition.name: Evaluate regulatory compliance of candidate skid
acquisition.statement: Evaluate whether a candidate skid meets applicable regulatory and quality standards by reviewing the compliance evidence supplied by the prospective supplier.
acquisition.description: Assesses regulatory conformance of candidate skids by examining supplier-provided documentation, certificates, and test records against applicable standards and organizational quality requirements.
acquisition.userGroup: Quality assurance and regulatory affairs personnel supporting the acquisition.
acquisition.frequency: Once per vendor evaluated, during the acquisition selection phase.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of required compliance evidence missing from the supplier submission
needs.role: Quality assurance and regulatory affairs personnel supporting the acquisition.

acquisition.name: Evaluate rental options
acquisition.statement: Evaluate rental or lease arrangements for temporary or trial batching capacity.
acquisition.description: Some batching needs are temporary or trial, making rental or lease options relevant.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of rental candidates missing qualification, cleaning, or material-compatibility records required for sterile fill-finish use
Minimize the likelihood of finding no rental or lease arrangement that meets the required batching capacity, sterile-processing requirements, and production window
needs.plain: Find a rental or lease option that can handle the needed amount of production, meet cleanliness requirements, and be available during the planned production period. This helps ensure the temporary equipment will actually meet the project's needs.

needs.stmt: Minimize the amount by which the total rental or lease expense exceeds the project budget because required charges were omitted
needs.plain: Include every required charge so the total rental or lease cost stays as close as possible to, and no higher than, the project budget.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of accepting lease terms that conflict with the trial schedule, decontamination obligations, or return conditions
needs.plain: Choose rental terms that fit the testing timetable, cleaning responsibilities, and requirements for returning the equipment. Conflicting terms could cause delays, extra work, or unexpected costs.

needs.stmt: Minimize the time required to obtain the technical, commercial, and availability information needed to approve a rental arrangement
needs.plain: Gather the price, practical details, and availability needed to approve renting something as quickly as possible. This helps the buyer choose and approve a rental without delay.

acquisition.name: Evaluate total cost of ownership
acquisition.statement: Evaluate the total cost of ownership for the processing plant.
acquisition.description: Assess capital, installation, operating, maintenance, and decommissioning costs over the plant's projected service life.

needs.stmt: Reduce the amount of qualification and regulatory compliance expenditure omitted from the ownership-cost estimate
Minimize the degree of inconsistency in scope boundaries and financial assumptions used to calculate candidate plant costs
needs.plain: Use the same definition of what is included and the same money-related assumptions when estimating the cost of each possible plant. This makes the estimates fair to compare.

needs.stmt: Minimize the amount of installation and commissioning expense left unbudgeted because sterile-site integration, qualification, or production-interruption requirements were overlooked
needs.plain: Include all costs for installation and getting the equipment ready to use, including connecting it to the sterile production area, checking that it works properly, and handling any production stoppage.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of deviation between projected energy expense and energy expense under the expected production profile and utility tariffs
needs.plain: Make the predicted energy cost match the energy cost the machine is likely to use during normal production and under the local energy prices. This makes the total cost estimate more reliable.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that lifecycle maintenance expense is underestimated because parts, labor, service, or replacement needs are omitted
needs.plain: Include all expected costs for parts, workers, repairs, services, and replacements, so the long-term cost is not estimated too low.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of candidate ownership-cost inputs that lack supporting evidence
needs.plain: Fewer estimated costs of owning and using the equipment should be based on unsupported information. This helps people compare options using trustworthy cost estimates.

acquisition.name: Execute purchase contract
acquisition.statement: Execute the purchase contract for the selected viscous product processing plant.
acquisition.description: Formally sign and execute the agreed purchase contract with the selected plant supplier, completing the legal commitment to acquire the plant.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the purchaser and supplier sign different versions of the purchase contract

acquisition.name: Inspect delivered equipment
acquisition.statement: Receive and inspect delivered plant components for transit damage and conformance to the order specification and purchase documentation.
acquisition.description: Covers the complete goods-receipt inspection: checking for physical transit damage, verifying delivered configuration against purchase documentation, and reconciling components against the order specification — all performed at the point of delivery.
acquisition.userGroup: Site engineers, project managers, receiving inspection teams

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of delivered configuration deviations not identified during receiving inspection
needs.role: Site engineers, project managers, receiving inspection teams

acquisition.name: Inspect received equipment
acquisition.statement: Inspect the delivered plant equipment for completeness, specification conformity, and transit damage against the purchase order and bill of materials.
acquisition.description: Confirms that all ordered items have arrived undamaged and match the agreed specification and BOM before acceptance.
acquisition.userGroup: Procurement managers, site engineers, quality inspectors.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of specification deviations in delivered equipment that remain undetected before acceptance
needs.role: Procurement managers, site engineers, quality inspectors.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of delivered items with transit damage that are accepted without detection
needs.plain: Make sure damage caused during shipping is noticed before delivered items are accepted, so problems can be reported and addressed.
needs.role: Procurement managers, site engineers, quality inspectors.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of accepting a consignment with missing bill-of-materials items
needs.plain: Make sure the delivered equipment includes every part listed for it before accepting the shipment. Missing parts could prevent the equipment from being used as intended.
needs.role: Procurement managers, site engineers, quality inspectors.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of conflicts between the purchase order and bill of materials that prevent a conformance decision
needs.plain: Make sure the order matches the list of parts received so staff can decide whether the delivery is correct. This prevents disagreements about whether anything is missing or wrong.
needs.role: Procurement managers, site engineers, quality inspectors.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of inspection delay caused by inaccessible identification and inspection points
needs.plain: Spend less time waiting to inspect delivered equipment because its labels or inspection points are difficult to reach. This helps staff check the equipment sooner.
needs.role: Procurement managers, site engineers, quality inspectors.

Minimize the degree of mismatch between trial conditions and sterile fill-finish production conditions

acquisition.name: Verify compliance certifications
acquisition.statement: Verify that each candidate plant holds the required hygiene, safety, regulatory, and conformity certifications.
acquisition.description: Confirms that candidate plants carry all mandatory compliance certifications and conformity evidence — including hygiene, safety, and regulatory approvals — relevant to the intended application and jurisdiction.
acquisition.userGroup: Quality managers, regulatory affairs teams, procurement engineers
acquisition.frequency: Once per candidate plant during evaluation

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of certifications whose scope excludes the intended sterile fill-finish application or jurisdiction
needs.role: Quality managers, regulatory affairs teams, procurement engineers

acquisition.name: Verify material compatibility
acquisition.statement: Verify the system's compatibility with the intended batch materials and formulations.
acquisition.description: Confirm that the candidate batch preparation system can handle the specific materials, chemistries, viscosities, temperatures, or other formulation properties required.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that system contact materials introduce contaminants into the formulation
needs.plain: Make sure the parts that touch the product do not add dirt or other unwanted substances to it. This helps keep the finished product safe and suitable for use.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that adverse formulation and system-material interactions remain undetected
needs.plain: Make it very unlikely that harmful reactions between the mixture and the equipment materials go unnoticed. This helps prevent damage, contamination, or changes to the product.

acquisition.name: Verify order documentation
acquisition.statement: Verify that all required order and turnover documentation accompanies the delivered skid.
acquisition.description: Confirm receipt and completeness of manuals, certificates, test reports, and other required documents delivered with the skid as specified in the purchase order.

needs.stmt: Minimize the percentage of required manuals, certificates, and test reports missing from the turnover package

acquisition.name: Verify site compatibility
acquisition.statement: Verify the batch preparation system against site space, utility, environmental, and structural constraints.
acquisition.description: Confirm that the candidate batch preparation system can be physically installed and operated within the available facility footprint, utility supply, environmental controls, and structural load limits.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that system utility demands exceed available site supply
Reduce the percentage of facility space, utility, environmental, and structural constraint data that is inaccurate or outdated
Minimize the percentage of required candidate-system dimensional, utility, and connectivity specifications that are missing or unverified during technical evaluation
needs.plain: Check and confirm that a possible replacement system will fit the site, have the needed services, and connect properly before choosing it.
needs.role: Facilities engineers, process engineers, project managers.

needs.stmt: Reduce the degree of discrepancy between documented site constraints and actual installation conditions
needs.plain: Make sure the written description of the site matches the conditions that are actually there, so the equipment will fit and work properly.
needs.role: Facilities engineers, process engineers, project managers.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of required site utilities whose available capacity or quality falls below candidate-system requirements
needs.plain: Make sure the site’s water, power, air, and other needed services have enough capacity and suitable quality for the equipment. This helps prevent operating problems after installation.
needs.role: Facilities engineers, process engineers, project managers.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the candidate system or its transport modules cannot traverse site access paths or fit within the installation envelope
needs.plain: The proposed system and the parts used to move it should fit through the site’s entrances and travel routes and fit in the planned installation space. This prevents the equipment from being impossible to deliver or install.
needs.role: Facilities engineers, process engineers, project managers.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of required process, control, and data connections that are incompatible with site interfaces
needs.plain: Make sure the equipment’s needed processes, controls, and information connections work with the systems already at the site, so everything can connect and operate together.
needs.role: Facilities engineers, process engineers, project managers.

acquisition.name: Verify utility compatibility
acquisition.statement: Verify the skid's compatibility with existing facility utilities and interfaces.
acquisition.description: Confirm that the candidate skid's utility connections, electrical requirements, and physical interfaces align with what the receiving facility provides, prior to committing to purchase.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that skid utility demand exceeds available facility capacity in intended operating states

acquisition.name: Witness factory acceptance test
acquisition.statement: Witness the factory acceptance test of the completed skid prior to shipment.
acquisition.description: Attend and observe the vendor-conducted factory acceptance test (FAT) to verify the skid performs to specification before it leaves the manufacturing facility.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of critical skid functions that fail approved acceptance criteria during factory acceptance testing

acquisition.name: Witness system demonstration
acquisition.statement: Witness a demonstration of a candidate liquid batching and preparation system.
acquisition.description: This provides direct evidence of system operation, control behavior, batching performance, accessibility, and operator interaction before purchase.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of mismatch between the demonstrated configuration and conditions and the intended sterile fill-finish application
needs.plain: Make sure the demonstration setup and conditions closely match how the system is meant to be used to fill and finish germ-free products.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of critical batching or control functions that fail during the demonstration
needs.plain: Avoid having important mixing, measuring, or control functions fail during the demonstration. The demonstration should show that the system can perform its essential jobs.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of purchase-critical acceptance criteria left without observable evidence after the demonstration
needs.plain: Leave fewer important purchase requirements without clear proof after the equipment demonstration. This helps confirm that the equipment actually meets what was promised.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the scheduled demonstration is postponed because required materials, utilities, records, or qualified personnel are unavailable
needs.plain: The demonstration should take place as planned, with all needed supplies, services, records, and trained people available. This prevents the demonstration from being delayed.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of restrictions on observation, component access, or operator interaction during the demonstration
needs.plain: Keep the system open for viewing, hands-on access, and operator participation during the demonstration. This lets people properly examine and try the system.

usage.name: Adjust batch parameters
usage.statement: Adjust batch preparation parameters mid-cycle to correct for observed process variation.
usage.description: Operator modifies active process parameters during a running batch cycle in response to observed deviations from target conditions.

needs.stmt: Reduce the degree to which critical process conditions exceed validated limits after a mid-cycle adjustment
needs.plain: Keep important process conditions from going beyond their approved safe ranges after making a change during the process. This helps prevent the batch from becoming unsafe or unusable.

needs.stmt: Reduce the degree to which batch conditions exceed validated limits after a parameter adjustment
needs.plain: After changing the settings, keep the batch within its approved operating limits. This helps prevent the product from being made under unsuitable conditions.

usage.name: Adjust control setpoints during operation
usage.statement: Adjust an authorized control setpoint during active skid operation.
usage.description: Modify a process control setpoint on the skid within authorized limits while the skid is actively running a process.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of a critical parameter exceeding its authorized limit after setpoint adjustment
needs.plain: Make sure changing a target setting does not cause an important value to go beyond its allowed limit.

usage.name: Adjust mixing parameters
usage.statement: Adjust mixing parameters during the batch to achieve target consistency.
usage.description: Tuning speed, temperature, or timing during use accommodates ingredient behavior.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that a parameter adjustment compromises the sterile state of the batch
needs.plain: Reduce the chance that changing mixing settings makes the batch no longer germ-free. This helps protect the batch while achieving the desired mixing.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that adjusted settings exceed validated operating limits during the batch
needs.plain: Keep changed mixing settings within the tested and approved operating range during production. Settings outside that range could affect the product.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of deviation from target consistency after a parameter adjustment
needs.plain: Keep the mixture as close as possible to the desired evenness after changing the mixing settings. This helps maintain consistent product quality.

needs.stmt: Minimize the time between detecting a consistency deviation and applying the corresponding parameter adjustment
Reduce the frequency of consistency oscillations caused by successive parameter adjustments during the batch

usage.name: Adjust process setpoints
usage.statement: Adjust an active process setpoint — including temperature, flow, timing, and dosing parameters — through the batching system controls.
usage.description: Combines controlling batch temperature (#56) with adjusting process setpoints (#74). Temperature control is a specific instance of setpoint adjustment; #74 is the correct Level-1 framing that subsumes #56.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that a setpoint adjustment moves a critical parameter outside validated operating limits during an active batch
needs.plain: Lower the chance that changing a machine setting during production pushes an important condition outside its tested safe range. This helps keep the batch within safe and approved conditions.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of selecting the wrong batch phase, parameter, or target value when adjusting an active process setpoint
needs.plain: Avoid choosing the wrong step, setting, or desired value when changing an active process. This helps prevent the process from running incorrectly.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that an entered setpoint fails to take effect in the active batch
needs.plain: Make sure a new target setting actually takes effect in the batch that is running. Otherwise, the process may continue using the old setting.

needs.stmt: Reduce the time between recognizing a process deviation and completing the required setpoint correction
Reduce the frequency of process-parameter oscillation caused by competing setpoint changes during an active batch

usage.name: Adjust processing sequence for alternate formulation
usage.statement: Adapt the active processing sequence to handle a different viscous product formulation mid-run.
usage.description: The operator modifies setpoints, stage durations, or routing to switch the plant from one product grade or recipe to another without a full shutdown, covering partial changeovers or recipe transitions during a shift.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree to which active sequence parameters deviate from approved values for the alternate formulation

usage.name: Adjust processing throughput
usage.statement: Scale the plant's processing volume up or down during operation.
usage.description: Modify the plant's operating rate in response to demand, feed availability, or downstream capacity.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of critical process parameters leaving validated limits during throughput adjustment
needs.plain: Reduce the chance that important production conditions move outside the tested safe range when the production rate is changed. This helps keep the process under control as output changes.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of losing sterile conditions during a throughput change
needs.plain: Keep the work area sterile when the production speed or amount changes, so the product remains protected from contamination.

needs.stmt: Reduce the time required for the plant to stabilize at the requested throughput
Reduce the duration that plant output exceeds downstream acceptance capacity after adjustment
Reduce the amount of liquid product discarded while transitioning between throughput levels

usage.name: Back up process data
usage.statement: Back up the process control and batch data generated by the processing plant.
usage.description: Process data logs must be backed up to preserve batch records and support traceability.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of process control or batch data being overwritten before backup completion
Minimize the likelihood of backed-up process control or batch records being altered or deleted without authorization
Reduce the number of process control and batch records omitted from backups
needs.plain: Fewer production instructions and batch records should be left out of backup copies. This helps prevent important production information from being lost.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of backed-up process control and batch records that are corrupted or unreadable
needs.plain: Fewer saved production instructions and records should be damaged or impossible to open. This helps the production process be reviewed or restored when needed.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of backed-up records with missing or mismatched batch identifiers and timestamps
needs.plain: Make sure saved copies of records have the correct production-batch numbers and times. This helps people match each record to the right production activity.

usage.name: Charge input materials
usage.statement: Charge input materials into the skid.
usage.description: Raw material, buffer, or media must be introduced into the skid at the correct point and rate for the process step.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that incorrect input material is charged into the skid for the batch step
needs.plain: Make sure the correct material is put into the equipment for the batch. The wrong material could spoil the batch or create a safety problem.

usage.name: Clean batch system between runs
usage.statement: Clear residual material from the batch preparation system between consecutive batch cycles.
usage.description: Operator or cleaning system flushes, rinses, or purges residual batch material from the system's wetted components between production runs to prevent cross-contamination or carryover.

needs.stmt: Reduce the amount of previous-batch material remaining in wetted components after cleaning
needs.plain: Remove as much material from the previous batch as possible from parts that contact the product during cleaning. This helps prevent one batch from contaminating the next.

needs.stmt: Reduce the amount of previous-batch material remaining on wetted components after cleaning
needs.plain: Remove as much material from the previous batch as possible from parts that contact the product during cleaning. This helps prevent one batch from contaminating the next.

usage.name: Collect in-process samples
usage.statement: Withdraw an in-process sample through the skid's designated sampling interface.
usage.description: Collect a representative process sample via the skid's sampling port or assembly during an active run for quality or process monitoring purposes.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that contamination enters the process through the sampling interface during withdrawal
needs.plain: Taking a sample should not allow dirt or germs to enter the process through the sampling opening. This helps keep the material being made clean.

needs.stmt: Reduce the degree to which the withdrawn sample differs from material at the defined sampling location

usage.name: Confirm batch system readiness before start
usage.statement: Verify that the batch preparation system is in a ready state before initiating a batch cycle.
usage.description: Operator checks that all system interlocks, material levels, and pre-conditions are satisfied before triggering the batch start, ensuring the system is safe and configured to run.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of accepting an unverified cleaning or sterilization prerequisite as satisfied before batch start
needs.plain: Do not accept cleaning or germ-killing steps as complete unless they have been checked. This helps ensure the batch starts in a properly prepared system.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that an unsatisfied safety interlock condition remains undetected before batch start
needs.plain: Make sure no required safety condition is missing or unnoticed before the batch starts. This helps prevent the machine from starting when it is not safe or ready.

usage.name: Contain process spills
usage.statement: Contain liquid spills or leaks that occur during active plant operation.
usage.description: Identify and isolate unintended liquid releases at processing equipment, valves, or pipework to prevent product loss and maintain safe operation.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that released liquid contaminates a sterile production area
needs.plain: Reduce the chance that spilled liquid spreads into an area where germ-free production takes place. This helps protect the area and the product from contamination.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of liquid spread from the release point onto adjacent equipment or walkways
needs.plain: Keep a liquid spill close to where it starts, so it does not spread onto nearby equipment or places where people walk.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of operator exposure to the released liquid during containment
needs.plain: Prevent workers from coming into contact with liquid released during a spill. This helps protect them from possible harmful effects of the liquid.

needs.stmt: Reduce the amount of liquid product lost before the release is contained
needs.plain: Limit how much liquid product escapes before the spill is stopped. This reduces waste and the risk of harm.

needs.stmt: Reduce the time required for the operator to identify and isolate the release source
needs.plain: Help the operator quickly find where a spill is coming from and stop or contain it. This can limit the spread of the spilled material.

usage.name: Control material transfer
usage.statement: Control the skid's transfer of process material between vessels or process steps.
usage.description: Manage the initiation, rate, volume, and completion of process material transfers executed through the skid.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of process material entering an unintended vessel or step during skid transfer

usage.name: Control process conditions
usage.statement: Control temperature, pressure, or timing conditions throughout the active batch cycle.
usage.description: Operator or control system manages key process variables such as temperature, pressure, and cycle timing to keep the batch within specified limits.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of difference between indicated and actual batch conditions during the cycle
needs.plain: Keep the displayed process conditions as close as possible to the real conditions in the batch. Accurate readings help workers control the process safely and correctly.

needs.stmt: Minimize the percentage of the active batch cycle spent outside specified temperature, pressure, or timing limits
needs.plain: Reduce the amount of time a production run spends outside its required temperature, pressure, or timing limits. This helps keep the process consistent and within safe operating conditions.

usage.name: Control process parameters
usage.statement: Regulate and adjust plant setpoints to maintain the required operating state during the processing cycle.
usage.description: Ensures temperature, pressure, flow, and other process variables remain within specified bounds throughout the run.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of temperature, pressure, or flow excursions beyond specified bounds during the production run
needs.plain: Keep temperature, pressure, and liquid or gas movement within the allowed range during production. If they go outside that range, bring them back as quickly as possible.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of applying a setpoint for the wrong processing phase during a phase transition
needs.plain: Make sure the setting for one stage of the process is not used during a change to another stage.

needs.stmt: Reduce the time from detecting an out-of-bound process variable to completing the required setpoint adjustment
Reduce the duration of unavailable or stale process-variable readings while setpoint adjustment is required
Reduce the number of setpoint changes omitted from operator handoffs during a production run
needs.plain: Make sure all changes to the target operating settings are passed on when operators change shifts during production. Missing a change could cause the process to run incorrectly.

usage.name: Coordinate concurrent processing streams
usage.statement: Coordinate multiple simultaneous liquid processing streams within the plant.
usage.description: Manage and synchronise parallel processing lines or split-stream operations running at the same time, balancing loads and sequencing appropriately.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of routing a liquid stream to the wrong vessel or processing stage during concurrent processing
needs.plain: Make sure each liquid goes to the correct container or step in the process when several activities happen at the same time.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percent of processed liquid outside the prescribed flow balance across parallel streams
Reduce the duration of disruption across concurrent streams after an upset in one stream
Minimize the degree of sequencing mismatch between dependent concurrent streams
Minimize the likelihood that concurrent streams require the same shared plant resource simultaneously

usage.name: Coordinate multi-operator use
usage.statement: Coordinate batching system use across multiple operators during production.
usage.description: Manage task handoffs, access, and sequencing when more than one operator is working with the batching system simultaneously or across shifts.

needs.stmt: Reduce the frequency of sterile-boundary breaches caused by overlapping operator activity
needs.plain: Two or more operators working at the same time should not cause a break in the clean boundary around the process. This helps keep the product protected from contamination.

needs.stmt: Reduce the frequency of batching tasks performed out of the required sequence during multi-operator production
Minimize the likelihood of conflicting operator actions altering the same batch simultaneously
Reduce the number of shift handoffs that omit current batch status or pending actions
Reduce the duration of production delays caused by operator access conflicts

usage.name: Coordinate shift handover
usage.statement: Transfer operational control and status between operating shifts.
usage.description: Multiple operators, shifts, and control-room staff must coordinate control of the plant during operation.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of sterility-control conditions reported incorrectly during shift handover
Reduce the number of active alarms, deviations, or temporary operating restrictions omitted from shift handover
Minimize the degree of conflict between the outgoing operator's account and current plant records
Reduce the duration of unclear operational control ownership during the shift transition
needs.plain: Shorten the time when it is unclear who is responsible for controlling operations during a shift change. Clear responsibility helps the work continue safely and smoothly.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the incoming shift is unavailable at the scheduled handover
needs.plain: Make sure the workers taking over the shift will be available at the agreed handover time. This prevents gaps in responsibility and important information.

usage.name: Coordinate shift operation
usage.statement: Coordinate operation of the plant across operators and shifts.
usage.description: Coordinating multiple operators and shifts across the plant maintains continuous, consistent production.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of critical plant-status details omitted during shift handover

usage.name: Coordinate skid with connected process units
usage.statement: Coordinate the skid's operation with connected upstream and downstream process units.
usage.description: Synchronize material flows, timing, and status signals between the skid and interfaced upstream/downstream equipment during operation.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that material transfer starts before the receiving process unit is ready

usage.name: Diagnose operating faults from plant indications
usage.statement: Identify the source of an operating fault from plant indications.
usage.description: The process technician interprets alarms, instrument readings, and observable plant behaviour to pinpoint the cause of a fault during or following a processing run.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of identifying the wrong source of an operating fault from plant indications

usage.name: Diagnose process deviations
usage.statement: Diagnose a process deviation from the system's operational data.
usage.description: This helps isolate abnormal ingredient delivery, vessel conditions, control responses, or equipment behavior during use.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that measurement faults are mistaken for actual batch conditions
Minimize the likelihood of assigning the wrong initiating event when responses cascade through the batching system
Reduce the number of batch-affecting manual interventions absent from the operational record
Minimize the percentage of critical operational records missing from the deviation interval
Minimize the degree of timestamp misalignment across ingredient delivery, vessel, control, and equipment records

usage.name: Discharge completed batch
usage.statement: Discharge the completed batch from the system to downstream handling.
usage.description: Transferring the finished batch out frees the system and delivers the product.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of exposing the completed batch to contamination during discharge
needs.plain: Avoid exposing the finished batch to dirt or germs while removing it from the equipment. This helps keep the completed product safe.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of contamination entering the completed batch during discharge
needs.plain: Make it very unlikely that germs or other unwanted material get into the finished batch while it is being emptied. This helps keep the batch safe and usable.

usage.name: Discharge finished batch
usage.statement: Discharge the finished batch from the system to downstream processes.
usage.description: Operate the system's discharge or transfer function to move the completed liquid batch to the next process step, storage, or filling line.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the finished batch becomes contaminated during discharge
needs.plain: Keep the completed batch from becoming contaminated while it is being emptied or transferred. This protects the quality of the finished product.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the finished batch is transferred to the wrong downstream destination
Reduce the amount of finished batch released outside the intended transfer path during discharge
Reduce the amount of finished batch remaining in the batching system after discharge
Reduce the duration of finished-batch hold while the downstream destination is unavailable

usage.name: Discharge process output
usage.statement: Discharge the process output from the skid.
usage.description: Finished product or intermediate must be transferred out of the skid at the end of a process step.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of process output becoming contaminated during discharge from the skid
needs.plain: The product leaving the equipment should not become contaminated during discharge. This helps keep the finished output safe and usable.

usage.name: Discharge processed material from plant
usage.statement: Manage the discharge and collection of processed viscous product from the plant.
usage.description: The operator oversees the release of finished or intermediate viscous material from the plant through designated output paths, containers, or transfer lines.

needs.stmt: Minimize the amount of processed viscous product left in the plant after discharge

usage.name: Divert nonconforming output
usage.statement: Divert nonconforming liquid output away from the normal discharge path.
usage.description: This prevents unsuitable output from entering downstream storage or distribution during process deviations.

needs.stmt: Reduce the amount of diverted liquid released into the sterile processing area
needs.plain: Release less unwanted liquid into the clean work area when separating out material that does not meet requirements.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that diverted liquid exceeds the available holding capacity during a process deviation
needs.plain: Make sure the container receiving rejected liquid does not fill beyond its available space during a problem. This helps prevent spills and keeps the diverted liquid contained.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the diversion path is unavailable when nonconforming output must be redirected
needs.plain: Make sure a working alternate route is available when output that fails requirements must be redirected. This prevents rejected material from continuing along the normal path.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of nonconforming liquid that continues into the normal discharge path after diversion begins
needs.plain: Make sure liquid that fails the required checks is fully kept out of the normal drain or outlet once diversion starts. This prevents unsuitable material from continuing through the usual path.

needs.stmt: Minimize the time between confirmation of nonconforming output and redirection away from the normal discharge path
needs.plain: Move product away from the normal outlet as soon as it is confirmed to be unacceptable. This helps keep it from being mixed with acceptable product.

usage.name: Document batch record
usage.statement: Document run data and events in the batch record during operation.
usage.description: Record process events, deviations, operator actions, and run data in the official batch record throughout the process run.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of required events, deviations, or operator actions omitted from the official batch record

usage.name: Dose measured quantities
usage.statement: Dose measured quantities of each ingredient into the batch preparation system during preparation.
usage.description: Operator or automated system dispenses precise, pre-specified quantities of each material into the batch during the active preparation cycle.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of ingredient doses outside prescribed quantity tolerance
needs.plain: Make sure each ingredient is added in an amount that stays within the required limits. Correct amounts help ensure the finished product is made properly.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of ingredient doses outside specified quantity tolerance

usage.name: Dose processing inputs
usage.statement: Dose required processing inputs into the liquid stream.
usage.description: This applies when the plant introduces additives, treatment agents, catalysts, or other process inputs.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that an incorrect processing input is introduced into the liquid stream
needs.plain: Make sure the correct processing material is added to the liquid flow. This prevents the wrong material from entering the product.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of microbial or particulate contamination entering the liquid stream during input addition
needs.plain: Keep germs, dust, and other unwanted material out of the liquid while ingredients or other materials are added. This helps keep the liquid clean and suitable for processing.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree to which processing input introduction occurs outside the required process window
needs.plain: Make sure materials are added during the required part of the process, not too early or too late. This helps the process produce the intended result.

needs.stmt: Reduce the amount of deviation between the processing input added and the required dose
needs.plain: Add an amount of processing material that is as close as possible to the amount required. This helps the process work as intended.

needs.stmt: Reduce the degree of nonuniformity in processing-input concentration throughout the liquid stream

usage.name: Draw in-process sample
usage.statement: Draw an in-process sample from the system's designated sampling point.
usage.description: This supports intermediate evaluation of the liquid before the batch cycle is completed.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of introducing microbial or particulate contamination into the batch during the sample draw
needs.plain: Taking a sample should not introduce germs or particles into the batch. This helps keep the batch clean and trustworthy.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of drawing the in-process sample from the wrong batch or sampling point
needs.plain: Make sure the sample comes from the correct batch and the correct place, so its test results describe the product being made.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of difference between the sample composition and the liquid at the designated sampling point
Minimize the likelihood that the designated sampling point is inaccessible or obstructed when the sample is required
Minimize the likelihood of an uncontrolled liquid release during the sample draw

usage.name: Enter batch recipe
usage.statement: Enter the recipe or formulation parameters for the batch to be prepared.
usage.description: Operator inputs the target formulation or recipe into the batch preparation system prior to initiating the batch cycle.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of formulation parameter values entered incorrectly for the scheduled batch
Reduce the number of formulation parameters entered with incorrect values

usage.name: Execute addition sequence
usage.statement: Execute the batching system's programmed ingredient-addition sequence.
usage.description: Combines operating the agitation function (#55) with executing the ingredient-addition sequence (#73). Agitation is an embedded function within the addition sequence execution; both are sub-activities of running a batch cycle, with #73 being the correct Level-1 framing.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of adding an ingredient not specified for the current programmed step
needs.plain: Add only the ingredient called for in the current step. This prevents the wrong ingredient from being added to the batch.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of breaching the sterile boundary during ingredient addition
needs.plain: Keep the germ-free area sealed while ingredients are added. This prevents outside contamination from entering the product.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of deviation between the specified and actual ingredient quantity added at a programmed step
needs.plain: Make the amount of ingredient added at each planned step match the required amount as closely as possible. This helps ensure the final product has the intended recipe.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of programmed ingredient-addition steps skipped during a batch run
Reduce the duration of interrupted agitation while the ingredient-addition sequence is active

usage.name: Execute clean-in-place cycle
usage.statement: Execute a clean-in-place cycle on the skid's wetted flow path.
usage.description: Running an automated or manual CIP sequence to clean internal flow paths without disassembly, using appropriate cleaning agents and validated procedures.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of the skid wetted path retaining product residue after the clean-in-place cycle
needs.plain: Make sure little or no product is left inside the equipment's product-carrying passages after cleaning. This helps prevent old product from mixing with the next batch.

usage.name: Execute emergency shutdown
usage.statement: Execute the plant's emergency shutdown function when continued operation is unsafe.
usage.description: This rapidly places active processing equipment into a defined protective state during a critical event.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of losing sterile containment while equipment transitions to the protective state
needs.plain: Avoid breaking the sealed, germ-free barrier while the equipment is being shut down in an emergency. This helps protect the product during the change to a safer state.

needs.stmt: Minimize the time between recognition that continued operation is unsafe and initiation of emergency shutdown
needs.plain: Make the time as short as possible between noticing that it is unsafe to continue and starting the emergency stop. A faster stop can reduce harm.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of active processing equipment units that remain operating after emergency shutdown is initiated
needs.plain: Stop as much of the operating equipment as possible once an emergency shutdown begins. This helps limit danger and prevent the situation from getting worse.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of personnel exposure to hazardous pressure, liquid, or equipment movement during shutdown
needs.plain: Prevent people from being exposed to dangerous pressure, liquids, or moving equipment while the machine is being stopped in an emergency. This helps protect them from serious injury.

needs.stmt: Reduce the degree of ambiguity in indications that the defined protective state has been reached
needs.plain: Make it clear when the equipment has reached the required safe condition after an emergency stop.

usage.name: Execute emergency stop
usage.statement: Stop the plant through its emergency control when continued operation is unsafe.
usage.description: Immediate shutdown limits escalation during hazardous process or equipment conditions.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that hazardous plant equipment continues operating after emergency-control activation
needs.plain: Make sure dangerous factory equipment stops after the emergency control is activated. This helps protect people from continued movement or operation.

usage.name: Execute production batch
usage.statement: Execute a production batch through the process skid.
usage.description: Operate the skid to run a full production batch from initiation to completion.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that batch sterility is compromised during skid processing
needs.plain: Prevent germs from getting into the group of product while it is being processed. This helps keep the finished product safe to use.

usage.name: Execute steam-in-place sterilization
usage.statement: Execute a steam-in-place sterilization cycle on the skid.
usage.description: Running an SIP sequence to sterilize the skid's internal fluid paths using pressurized steam, ensuring microbial control before processing.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of microorganisms surviving in skid fluid paths after the sterilization cycle
needs.plain: Make sure the heat treatment kills all germs left inside the fluid lines and equipment. This helps prevent the next product from becoming contaminated.

usage.name: Feed viscous material into processing path
usage.statement: Introduce raw viscous material into the plant's active processing path.
usage.description: The operator loads or pumps feedstock into the inlet of the plant at the start of or during a production run.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that incorrect feedstock enters the active processing path during a production run
needs.plain: Make sure only the correct material enters the part of the machine currently processing the product. This prevents the wrong material from mixing with or damaging the product.

usage.name: Halt batch on fault
usage.statement: Halt the batch cycle when a fault or unsafe condition is detected.
usage.description: Stopping the process quickly prevents damage or unsafe output during abnormal events.

needs.stmt: Reduce the time from halt initiation until hazardous batch activity ceases
needs.plain: Make sure dangerous activity in the batch stops quickly after the stop command is given. This helps limit the risk to people, equipment, and product.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the batch cycle continues after the operator initiates the halt
needs.plain: Make sure the production run stops promptly after the operator tells it to stop because of a problem. This limits the effects of the fault.

usage.name: Handle input variation
usage.statement: Process and compensate for liquid inputs that vary in viscosity, composition, or temperature.
usage.description: Maintains stable plant operation and output quality when incoming feedstock properties deviate from nominal values.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of finished liquid falling outside required quality specifications when feedstock properties change
needs.plain: Make it less likely that the finished liquid fails its required quality checks when the starting materials change. This helps keep the final product within the expected requirements.

needs.stmt: Reduce the frequency of process instability during abrupt changes in feedstock viscosity, composition, or temperature
needs.plain: The process should continue working steadily when the incoming material suddenly changes in thickness, makeup, or temperature. This helps prevent interruptions and poor results.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of applying incorrect compensation because the feedstock deviation is mischaracterized
needs.plain: People should be as unlikely as possible to make the wrong adjustment because they misunderstood how the incoming material differs from what was expected. Correct adjustments help keep production on target.

needs.stmt: Minimize the time from a feedstock deviation entering the active production run until it is recognized
needs.plain: A change in the incoming material should be noticed as soon as possible after it enters an active production run. Early notice gives people more time to adjust the process and protect the product.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of high-viscosity feedstock obstructing movement through required processing stages

usage.name: Handle input variations
usage.statement: Adjust plant settings to accommodate variations in incoming material properties.
usage.description: Operators must respond to shifts in raw material properties by tuning plant parameters mid-run.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that operator intervention needed to accommodate an ingredient property variation compromises the sterile boundary during batching
needs.plain: Let the operator handle differences in an ingredient without breaking the protected clean barrier around the batch. This helps keep the batch free from contamination.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of batches with composition outside target because incoming ingredient concentration differs from its expected value
needs.plain: Reduce the number of batches whose mixture is off target because an ingredient arrives stronger or weaker than expected. This helps keep each batch’s composition consistent.

needs.stmt: Reduce the degree of batch nonuniformity caused by variation in incoming ingredient viscosity
needs.plain: Keep each production run as consistent as possible when the incoming ingredient is thicker or thinner than usual. This helps the finished product remain uniform.

needs.stmt: Reduce the amount of in-process material produced outside acceptance criteria while plant settings are adjusted
Minimize the likelihood that incoming ingredient temperature variation causes degradation before batching is complete
Reduce the duration of batching beyond the allowable processing window while the operator evaluates and responds to unexpected ingredient properties

usage.name: Initiate production run
usage.statement: Initiate a liquid product processing run on the plant.
usage.description: Start-up sequence to begin a new processing run, including confirming readiness and activating the plant.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of initiating liquid product processing with a compromised sterile process boundary
needs.plain: Production should not start if the barrier keeping the product clean and protected is damaged or not properly closed. This helps prevent the liquid product from being contaminated.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of activating the plant with incorrect batch-specific processing parameters
needs.plain: Prevent the plant from starting a batch with the wrong product-specific settings. Correct settings help the batch be made as intended.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that a required plant utility is unavailable at scheduled startup
Reduce the frequency of startup sequences halted by unresolved safety or equipment interlock conditions
Reduce the duration of production-start delay caused by unavailable or unreleased batch inputs

usage.name: Load batch ingredients
usage.statement: Load the specified ingredients into the batch preparation system.
usage.description: Operator physically introduces raw materials or ingredients into the batch preparation system at the start of or during a batch cycle.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of loading an ingredient that does not match the batch specification
needs.plain: Make sure the ingredient loaded into production matches the approved recipe for that product. The wrong ingredient could spoil the product or make it unsafe.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of loading an ingredient that differs from the batch specification
needs.plain: Make sure the ingredients loaded match the recipe for that batch. This helps prevent the wrong materials from being used.

usage.name: Load ingredient components
usage.statement: Load ingredient components into the batching system for a new batch.
usage.description: Physically or automatically load raw liquid and/or dry ingredients into the system's intake for a new production batch.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of loading an incorrect ingredient component or lot into the new batch
needs.plain: Make sure the new batch receives the correct ingredient and batch lot. This prevents the wrong material from being used in the product.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of introducing microbial, particulate, or cross-product contamination while loading ingredient components
needs.plain: Prevent germs, dust, particles, or material from another product from getting into the ingredients while they are being loaded. This helps keep the ingredients clean and prevents products from being mixed accidentally.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of deviation between the ingredient quantity loaded and the batch target
needs.plain: Load an ingredient amount that is as close as possible to the amount required for the batch. This helps keep the finished product consistent.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of ingredient loading interruptions caused by an intake blockage
Minimize the likelihood of operator exposure to hazardous ingredient material during loading
needs.plain: Protect workers from breathing in or touching dangerous ingredient material while loading it. This helps prevent harm to their health.

usage.name: Manage output flow
usage.statement: Direct and control the finished liquid output from the plant.
usage.description: Finished liquid must be routed, dosed, or transferred to downstream vessels or filling equipment as it exits the plant.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of finished liquid becoming contaminated during transfer to the sterile filling stage
needs.plain: Prevent the finished liquid from becoming contaminated while it is moved to the clean filling stage. This helps preserve its safety and quality.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of finished-liquid doses falling outside the required quantity
needs.plain: Keep the amount of liquid in each finished dose within the required range. This ensures people receive the intended amount.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of finished liquid being routed to the wrong downstream destination
needs.plain: Send the finished liquid to the correct next destination. This prevents usable product from going to the wrong equipment or process.

needs.stmt: Reduce the amount of finished liquid lost during routing, dosing, or transfer
Reduce the duration of finished-liquid output interruption caused by unavailable downstream receiving capacity

usage.name: Manage residual liquid streams
usage.statement: Direct residual liquid streams to their designated handling paths.
usage.description: Route off-spec, waste, or by-product liquid streams to appropriate disposal, recycle, or containment systems during operation.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of residual liquid backflow into product-contact areas during routing
needs.plain: Prevent leftover liquid from flowing backward into areas that touch the product. This helps keep those areas clean and prevents unwanted liquid from entering the product.

needs.stmt: Reduce the amount of residual liquid released outside its designated containment boundary during routing
needs.plain: Keep leftover liquid inside the area meant to contain it while it is being moved. This helps prevent spills or leaks outside the safe area.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of directing residual liquid to an incorrect disposal, recycle, or containment path
needs.plain: Send leftover liquid to the correct place for disposal, reuse, or safe containment. This prevents spills and keeps unsuitable liquids from being mixed or misrouted.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of residual liquid accumulation while its designated handling path is unavailable
Reduce the number of residual liquid streams with an unresolved handling designation during operation

usage.name: Meter process additions
usage.statement: Deliver a measured process addition through the skid's integrated dosing functions.
usage.description: Command the skid's dosing system to deliver a precise, metered quantity of a process addition (e.g., buffer, reagent) during operation.

needs.stmt: Minimize the percentage deviation of the delivered dose from the required dose

usage.name: Mix batch contents
usage.statement: Mix or blend the batch contents to achieve the required consistency.
usage.description: Combining materials to a uniform state is central to producing a usable batch.

needs.stmt: Reduce the degree of concentration variation across the batch after mixing
Minimize the degree of composition variation across the batch after mixing

usage.name: Monitor batch progress
usage.statement: Monitor batch progress and system status during operation.
usage.description: Observe live system indicators, alarms, and process data to track batch status and detect anomalies throughout the batching cycle.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of critical batch deviations not recognized before affecting the batch
needs.plain: Catch serious problems in a batch before they can affect the product. This helps prevent a damaged or unusable batch from continuing unnoticed.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that a critical batch deviation remains unrecognized during the cycle
needs.plain: Make sure a serious problem with a production batch is noticed while the batch is still being made. Early notice allows people to respond before the problem gets worse.

needs.stmt: Reduce the time between onset of a critical batch anomaly and operator recognition
Minimize the likelihood that a critical alarm is obscured by simultaneous noncritical alarms
Minimize the degree of discrepancy between indicated batch status and the actual process state
Minimize the duration of unavailable or frozen live process data during a batching cycle
Minimize the duration of gaps in active batch monitoring during operator handovers

usage.name: Monitor process conditions
usage.statement: Monitor real-time process conditions during operation.
usage.description: Continuous observation of temperature, pressure, flow, and other variables is required to ensure the process stays within specification.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration that a critical process excursion remains unnoticed while the operator observes concurrent readings
needs.plain: Notice unusual process changes as quickly as possible while watching several readings at once. Early warning gives the operator more time to respond.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree to which live flow, temperature, pressure, and level readings differ from actual process conditions
needs.plain: Make the live readings for flow, temperature, pressure, and liquid level match the actual conditions as closely as possible. Reliable readings help workers control the process safely.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that required flow, temperature, pressure, or level readings are unavailable during plant operation
needs.plain: Make sure important readings, such as flow, temperature, pressure, and level, are available while the plant is running. Missing readings can make it difficult to control the process safely.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of deviation between indicated and actual temperature, pressure, and flow conditions during operation
Minimize the likelihood that the operator misinterprets a process reading because its identifier, unit, or status indication is ambiguous
Reduce the duration of the lag between an actual process change and its appearance in the live readings

usage.name: Monitor product viscosity in-process
usage.statement: Monitor the viscosity of the product at defined points during processing.
usage.description: The operator or technician observes inline or at-line viscosity measurements to verify the product remains within specification during the run.

needs.stmt: Minimize the time between product viscosity leaving specification and operator recognition
