needs.plain: Shorten the time between the medicine becoming too thick or too thin and the worker noticing it. This allows the problem to be corrected sooner.

usage.name: Monitor skid condition
usage.statement: Monitor the skid's operating condition.
usage.description: Tracking equipment health indicators such as vibration, temperature, and runtime hours to detect early signs of degradation and support predictive maintenance decisions.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that early component degradation remains hidden by normal skid operating variation

usage.name: Operate at high volume
usage.statement: Operate the processing plant at high throughput during peak production.
usage.description: Running the plant at higher throughput supports peak demand periods while maintaining product quality.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of viscous product produced outside critical quality specifications at peak throughput
needs.plain: Keep thick product within the required quality range while making it at the highest production rate. This prevents large amounts of unusable product.

usage.name: Operate for training
usage.statement: Operate the skid in a training or demonstration context.
usage.description: Trainees operate the skid under supervision or in simulation to learn correct procedures before independent use.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of trainee action driving skid parameters outside safe limits during supervised operation

usage.name: Operate manual controls
usage.statement: Control an individual batch preparation system function in manual operating mode.
usage.description: Authorized operator directly actuates a specific system function using manual controls, bypassing automatic sequencing, to complete a step or respond to a system need.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of actuating the wrong batch preparation function in manual mode
Minimize the likelihood that an unintended function is actuated in manual mode

usage.name: Operate system in manual mode
usage.statement: Operate the batching system in manual mode to complete a batch step.
usage.description: Bypass automated sequencing and control individual system functions (pumps, valves, agitators) manually to complete a batch step during trials, failures, or non-standard conditions.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of nonsterile or unintended material entering the batch through a manually established flow path
needs.plain: Make sure unwanted or unclean material cannot enter the product through a path set up by hand. This helps protect the product when the equipment is being operated manually.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree to which critical process conditions deviate from approved limits during manual control
Reduce the number of required pump, valve, or agitator actions omitted or performed out of sequence during the batch step
Minimize the likelihood of exceeding the allowable batch hold limit while completing the step manually
Minimize the likelihood of loss of liquid containment while independently actuating system functions

usage.name: Operate system under supervision
usage.statement: Operate the batch preparation system under supervision during operator training.
usage.description: Trainee operator runs the batch preparation system under the direct oversight of an experienced operator or trainer to develop hands-on proficiency.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration that a process parameter remains outside approved limits before supervisory correction
needs.plain: Correct a process setting quickly when it moves outside its allowed range, so the system returns to an acceptable condition sooner.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of trainee control inputs that move critical parameters outside approved limits

usage.name: Override automatic control manually
usage.statement: Apply an authorized manual override to an automatic skid control function.
usage.description: Temporarily place a skid automated control function into manual mode to apply a direct command, within authorized limits, during operation.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that a direct command outside authorized limits is applied during manual override

usage.name: Pause batch cycle
usage.statement: Suspend the active batch preparation cycle.
usage.description: This is required when processing must be temporarily halted without intentionally terminating the batch.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of deviation in batch conditions from acceptable limits while paused
Minimize the likelihood that batch material leaves validated holding conditions during suspension
Minimize the likelihood that the active batch cycle continues after the operator issues the pause command
Minimize the likelihood that pausing the batch cycle leaves the system outside safe pressure or temperature limits
Minimize the likelihood that the operator pauses the wrong batch cycle during an urgent intervention
Reduce the degree of uncertainty about whether the active batch cycle has fully paused
Minimize the likelihood that the operator cannot access the pause controls because of the current control mode or access authority

usage.name: Process liquid feedstock
usage.statement: Pass liquid feedstock through the plant's active processing equipment.
usage.description: The core act of running liquid material through unit operations — mixing, heating, separating, reacting — to transform it into finished product.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of microbial or particulate contamination entering the liquid feedstock while it passes through active processing equipment
needs.plain: Prevent germs or particles from getting into the liquid material while it moves through operating equipment.

needs.stmt: Reduce the degree to which liquid feedstock is exposed to temperature, mixing, or reaction conditions outside validated limits
needs.plain: Keep the liquid ingredients from being exposed to temperatures, mixing, or chemical changes outside the tested safe ranges. This helps preserve the intended quality of the material.

needs.stmt: Reduce the amount of liquid feedstock released from the processing path during operation
needs.plain: Reduce the amount of liquid material that escapes from the process while the equipment is running. This helps prevent waste, spills, and possible exposure.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of liquid feedstock being routed through an incorrect sequence of unit operations
needs.plain: Make sure the liquid material follows the correct series of processing steps, so it is made properly and safely.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of unplanned liquid feedstock flow interruptions through active processing equipment
needs.plain: Avoid unexpected stops in the flow of liquid material through the working equipment, so processing can continue steadily.

usage.name: Recirculate process material
usage.statement: Return viscous material through a designated processing loop.
usage.description: Recirculation is used when material requires additional residence time or repeated treatment.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of product degradation caused by repeated passage through the processing loop
needs.plain: The product should change or lose quality as little as possible when it repeatedly travels through the processing system. This helps preserve the product while it is being made.

usage.name: Record batch data
usage.statement: Record the batch preparation parameters and execution results for the production record.
usage.description: Operator or system captures process parameters, ingredient quantities, and cycle outcomes during or immediately following a batch run to create an auditable production record.

needs.stmt: Minimize the percentage of required batch data fields left blank in the production record
Reduce the percentage of required process parameters or cycle results omitted from the batch record
Minimize the likelihood of process data being linked to an incorrect batch identifier for a production run
Reduce the percentage of required process data absent from the batch record for a production run
Minimize the likelihood of a stored batch record becoming inaccessible when needed for traceability or reporting
Minimize the degree of mismatch between recorded timestamps and actual production events
Reduce the time from production-run completion until the complete batch record is available for traceability and reporting

usage.name: Record processing parameters and results
usage.statement: Record processing parameters and results for each production run.
usage.description: The operator or technician logs key process variables (temperatures, pressures, flow rates, batch IDs) and outcome data to maintain a traceable production record.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of required processing parameters and results omitted from each production-run record

usage.name: Record production data
usage.statement: Document processing parameters and output data for each run.
usage.description: Log key operating data — flows, temperatures, pressures, yields — during and after each processing run for traceability.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of discrepancy between documented processing values and observed readings for each run
needs.plain: Make sure the values written down for each production run match what was actually observed. This keeps the production records reliable.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of required flow, temperature, or pressure readings omitted from each run record
Minimize the likelihood that processing or output data are linked to the wrong run, batch, or processing stage
Reduce the duration between observing processing data and documenting them in the run record
needs.plain: Write down production measurements and observations as soon as they are seen. This helps keep an accurate record of what happened during the production run.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of changes to run records lacking operator, timestamp, or reason attribution
needs.plain: Keep people from changing production records without saying who made the change, when it happened, or why. This makes the records trustworthy and easier to check.

usage.name: Recover interrupted batch
usage.statement: Resume an interrupted batch from an approved system state.
usage.description: This enables controlled continuation after a stoppage without restarting the entire run.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of sterile batch contamination during the interruption or resumption
needs.plain: Prevent the medicine from becoming contaminated when production is stopped and then started again. The process must be brought back safely without exposing the batch to unwanted material.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of resuming the batch cycle from an incorrect or unauthorized recovery point
needs.plain: Make sure an interrupted batch is restarted only from the correct, approved point. This helps prevent skipped steps or unauthorized changes.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the batch cycle resumes from an incorrect or unauthorized recovery point
Minimize the likelihood of resuming the interrupted batch from an incorrectly identified system state
Minimize the degree of deviation from approved batch-condition limits during the interruption
Minimize the likelihood that the approved interruption hold limit is exceeded before batch resumption
Reduce the time the interrupted batch waits for required utilities after resumption is initiated

usage.name: Recover interrupted process sequence
usage.statement: Resume skid operation from an authorized recovery point after an interruption.
usage.description: Identify an authorized recovery point and resume the process sequence on the skid following an unplanned interruption, alarm, or hold condition.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that skid and material states conflict with prerequisites for the selected recovery point

usage.name: Regulate material flow rate through plant
usage.statement: Regulate the rate of viscous material flow through the plant.
usage.description: Adjusts pump speeds, valve positions, or other flow-control elements to set and maintain the required volumetric or mass flow rate of viscous material through the processing plant.
usage.frequency: Continuously or as needed during each production run

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of material-flow deviation from the specified production target
needs.plain: Keep the amount of material flowing through the plant as close as possible to the planned production amount. This helps production stay steady and on target.

usage.name: Regulate material temperature during processing
usage.statement: Regulate the temperature of viscous material at defined control points during plant operation.
usage.description: Monitors and adjusts thermal inputs to the process — via heating, cooling, or jacketing systems — to maintain material temperature within the specified processing range throughout a production run.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of production-run intervals with material temperature outside the specified processing range
needs.plain: Keep the material within the required temperature range throughout production. This helps the process work correctly and protects the quality of the product.

usage.name: Regulate mixing intensity
usage.statement: Regulate the agitation intensity applied to viscous material within the processing vessel.
usage.description: The operator adjusts mixer speed or agitation mode to achieve required shear, dispersion, or homogeneity of the viscous product during processing.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of divergence between applied agitation intensity and the required mixing condition as product viscosity changes
needs.plain: The strength of the mixing should stay as close as possible to what the product needs, even when the product becomes thicker or thinner. This helps mix the product evenly.

usage.name: Replace single-use assemblies
usage.statement: Replace the single-use tubing, filters, and bag assemblies on the skid.
usage.description: Removal and disposal of expired or spent single-use fluid-path components and installation of new assemblies to restore skid functionality.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of contaminating the skid fluid path during single-use assembly replacement
needs.plain: Prevent germs or other unwanted material from entering the equipment’s liquid path while disposable parts are replaced.

usage.name: Respond to process alarms
usage.statement: Respond to plant alarms during active liquid processing.
usage.description: This enables timely operator intervention when monitored conditions exceed configured operating limits.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that intervention on an alarmed condition compromises the sterile processing boundary
needs.plain: Make sure responding to a warning does not let germs into the protected product area. This keeps the product and its surroundings clean and safe.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of a critical alarm being obscured by simultaneous lower-severity alarms
needs.plain: Make sure the most serious warning cannot be hidden by several less serious warnings appearing at the same time. This helps workers notice and respond to the greatest danger first.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the operator response worsens the out-of-limit process condition
needs.plain: Make sure the operator’s actions do not make a process problem worse when an alarm shows that conditions are outside the allowed limits. This helps bring the process back to a safe and acceptable state.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that a critical process alarm fails to reach the control room operator during active liquid processing
needs.plain: Make sure an important process warning reaches the person watching the operation while liquid is being handled. This allows that person to respond quickly to a problem.

needs.stmt: Reduce the degree of ambiguity about the process condition associated with an alarm
needs.plain: Make it clear what condition caused an alarm. This helps the person responding know what action to take.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that a critical alarm is overlooked when multiple skid alarms occur together

usage.name: Respond to system alarms
usage.statement: Acknowledge an active batch-system alarm.
usage.description: This enables the operator to recognize an abnormal condition before taking the permitted operational response.

needs.stmt: Reduce the time from activation of a batch-system alarm to operator recognition
Reduce the degree of ambiguity in identifying the abnormal condition associated with an active alarm

usage.name: Rinse between batches
usage.statement: Rinse the batching system's wetted surfaces between consecutive batch runs.
usage.description: Flush or rinse product-contact surfaces, lines, and vessels between batches of the same or different formulations to prevent cross-contamination or carry-over.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of introducing microbial or particulate contaminants to product-contact surfaces during rinsing
needs.plain: Keep germs and bits of unwanted material from getting onto product-touching surfaces while they are rinsed between batches.

needs.stmt: Minimize the percent of prior-batch residue remaining on wetted surfaces after rinsing
Minimize the amount of rinse fluid remaining in the batching system when the next batch begins
Reduce the duration of batching system unavailability caused by between-batch rinsing
needs.plain: Keep the equipment that prepares products in batches from being unavailable for long while it is rinsed between batches. This helps the next batch start sooner.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of rinse-fluid leaks or spills during between-batch rinsing
needs.plain: Avoid spilling or leaking rinse liquid while cleaning between production runs. This helps keep people, equipment, and the work area safe and clean.

usage.name: Route liquid streams
usage.statement: Route liquid streams through the required plant flow path.
usage.description: This directs feedstock, intermediate streams, or output to the appropriate processing stages.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of contaminating a liquid stream while it passes through the required plant flow path
needs.plain: Keep the liquid clean while it travels through the required parts of the facility. Contamination could make the liquid unsafe or unusable.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of pressure exceeding allowable limits while routing a liquid stream
needs.plain: Reduce the chance that pressure becomes dangerously high while a liquid is being sent through the system. This helps protect the equipment and the people nearby.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of directing a liquid stream to the wrong processing stage
needs.plain: Make sure each liquid is sent to the correct step in the process. This helps prevent incorrect mixing, wasted material, or damage to equipment.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of the required plant flow path being unavailable when routing must begin
needs.plain: Make sure the needed pipes and valves are ready before liquid movement begins. This helps ensure the liquid can be sent along the correct route.

needs.stmt: Reduce the amount of liquid lost from the plant flow path during routing

usage.name: Run precision batches
usage.statement: Run small precision batches of viscous product to specification.
usage.description: Small precision batches for specialty or trial formulations demand tight control of every parameter.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of the finished batch outside composition-uniformity limits
needs.plain: Make sure the ingredients are spread evenly throughout the finished batch. This helps ensure that each portion has the intended mixture.

usage.name: Sample batch material
usage.statement: Withdraw a sample of in-process batch material for testing.
usage.description: Technician or operator draws a representative sample from the batch preparation system during an active cycle to support in-process quality or process checks.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree to which the withdrawn sample differs from batch material at the specified sampling point and stage
Minimize the degree to which the withdrawn sample differs from the batch at the specified process point

usage.name: Sample process stream
usage.statement: Draw in-process samples for quality checks while the plant runs.
usage.description: Collect representative liquid samples from designated sampling points during active operation for analytical testing.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of introducing contamination into the sterile process stream during sampling
needs.plain: Prevent germs and other unwanted material from getting into the product while a sample is being taken. This helps ensure the sample and the product remain trustworthy and safe.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of quality technician exposure to hazardous process liquid during sampling
needs.plain: Protect the quality worker from touching or being splashed by harmful liquid while taking a sample. The sampling method should keep the person safely separated from the liquid.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of contaminating the sample during collection or handling
needs.plain: Prevent unwanted material from getting into the sample while it is collected or handled. A clean sample gives a more trustworthy picture of the process.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of difference between the collected sample and the process stream at the designated sampling point
Minimize the likelihood of collecting a sample from the wrong sampling point or production lot

usage.name: Scale batch size
usage.statement: Scale the batch size to match the required production volume.
usage.description: Adjust the system's batch quantity and associated parameters to produce a larger or smaller volume than the default recipe specifies.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that ingredient proportions are incorrect after scaling the batch quantity
needs.plain: Make sure the ingredient amounts stay in the correct proportions when the batch size is changed. This helps produce the intended mixture.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of composition nonuniformity within the scaled batch
needs.plain: Make the materials in the larger batch as evenly mixed as possible. This helps ensure that each portion has the intended composition.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the scaled batch quantity falls outside the system's validated operating range
Minimize the likelihood that the scaled batch exceeds validated sterile hold limits before filling
Minimize the likelihood that required ingredients are unavailable in sufficient quantities for the enlarged batch

usage.name: Select batch recipe
usage.statement: Select the required recipe from the batch preparation system's recipe library.
usage.description: Operator chooses the appropriate pre-stored recipe from the system's available formulations before initiating a batch cycle.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of selecting a recipe that does not match the scheduled product and batch specification
needs.plain: Choose instructions that match the product and production batch that are scheduled. This helps prevent the wrong product from being made or handled.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the selected recipe is inconsistent with batch requirements
needs.plain: Make sure the chosen production instructions match what the batch requires. This helps ensure the batch is made correctly.

usage.name: Sequence multiple batches
usage.statement: Sequence consecutive batches to sustain continuous production throughput.
usage.description: Operator or production scheduler plans and initiates a series of consecutive batch cycles to maintain uninterrupted downstream production flow.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that preceding-batch residue contaminates a subsequent batch because of sequence incompatibility
needs.plain: Lower the chance that leftovers from one batch spoil the next batch because the two batches cannot safely follow each other. This helps prevent unwanted material from carrying over.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that a prepared batch exceeds its approved holding limit before filling

usage.name: Set batch quantity
usage.statement: Enter the required batch quantity into the batch preparation system.
usage.description: Operator specifies the target output quantity or batch size in the system before initiating the batch cycle.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of accepted batch quantities outside validated processing limits
needs.plain: As few production batches as possible should be accepted when their amounts were made outside the approved operating range. This helps keep each batch consistent and acceptable.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of batch quantity entries differing from the approved target

usage.name: Set process parameters
usage.statement: Set process parameters on the skid controller before or during a run.
usage.description: Configure control setpoints, limits, and sequence parameters on the skid's process controller prior to or during operation.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of entering incorrect setpoints, limits, or sequence values for the current batch

usage.name: Shut down processing line
usage.statement: Shut down the viscous product processing line at the end of production.
usage.description: Safely shutting down the line at end of production protects equipment and prevents material solidifying in the system.

needs.stmt: Minimize the amount of residual viscous product left inside the line to solidify after shutdown

usage.name: Shut down production run
usage.statement: Bring the processing plant to a controlled stop at end of run.
usage.description: Bringing a run to a controlled end protects product quality and equipment integrity.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of deviation from approved pressure, flow, and thermal limits during shutdown
needs.plain: Keep pressure, liquid movement, and temperature close to their approved limits while production is being stopped. This helps prevent unsafe or damaging shutdown conditions.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of losing sterile conditions in product-contact paths during shutdown
needs.plain: Reduce the chance that product-contact paths stop being clean and germ-free when production is shut down. This helps keep the equipment ready for safe use later.

needs.stmt: Minimize the amount of liquid product unintentionally released during shutdown
needs.plain: As little product as possible should accidentally spill when production is stopped. This reduces waste and helps keep the work area safe and clean.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of plant sections unintentionally left flowing, energized, or pressurized after shutdown
needs.plain: After production stops, make sure no plant sections are accidentally still carrying material, powered, or under pressure. This helps make the shutdown safe and complete.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of product-contact equipment being damaged during shutdown

usage.name: Start batch cycle
usage.statement: Initiate the batch preparation cycle on the system.
usage.description: Triggering the run begins the automated or manual sequence of preparation steps.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of initiating the batch cycle before required sterile conditions are established
needs.plain: Do not start the batch until the required clean, germ-free conditions are in place. Starting too soon could contaminate the batch.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of starting the batch cycle with a configuration that does not match the intended product
needs.plain: Make it unlikely that a batch will be started with settings or equipment choices for the wrong product. Matching the setup to the intended product helps prevent wasted or incorrect batches.

usage.name: Start batching cycle
usage.statement: Start the batching cycle to begin preparing the liquid batch.
usage.description: Initiate the automated or manual batching cycle sequence after recipe and quantity parameters are confirmed.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of initiating the batching cycle with parameters that differ from the confirmed recipe or quantity
needs.plain: Reduce the chance of starting a mixing or production run with settings or an amount different from the approved instructions. This helps ensure the run makes the intended product in the intended quantity.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of initiating the batching cycle while sterile boundary conditions are compromised
needs.plain: Do not start mixing and batching while the barriers that keep the process clean are damaged, open, or otherwise not working properly. This helps prevent contamination of the batch.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the batching cycle initiates before the operator's intentional start command
Minimize the likelihood that unresolved process or safety interlocks block cycle initiation
Reduce the duration of waiting for the batching system to accept the start command after all start conditions are satisfied
needs.plain: Have the mixing system begin its work quickly once every required condition is ready. This avoids wasting time after the operator has given the start instruction.

usage.name: Start up processing line
usage.statement: Start up the viscous product processing line to begin production.
usage.description: This matters whenever the plant is brought online to begin transforming raw viscous materials into product.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of applying incorrect production parameters during line startup
needs.plain: Prevent the wrong operating settings from being used when the production line starts. This helps avoid producing defective or unsafe product.

usage.name: Switch between recipes
usage.statement: Switch the batching system between different batch recipes during production.
usage.description: Combines setting batch recipe parameters before a run (#62) with switching between recipes during production (#67). Setting parameters before a run is a sub-step performed when switching between recipes; both address the same usage-phase recipe-selection activity.
usage.frequency: Per recipe change event during production.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of residual material from the previous formulation contaminating the next batch during the recipe change
needs.plain: Keep leftover material from the previous product out of the next batch when changing recipes. This prevents the new batch from being mixed with the wrong product.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of incorrect recipe parameters being activated for the next batch
needs.plain: Reduce the chance that the next batch starts with the wrong instructions or settings. This helps prevent the batch from being made incorrectly.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of selecting an unapproved or obsolete recipe version for the next batch
needs.plain: Reduce the chance that someone chooses a recipe that has not been approved or is no longer current for the next batch. This helps ensure the batch is made using the correct instructions.

needs.stmt: Reduce the amount of batch material discarded after an incorrect recipe change
Reduce the duration of production interruption while switching the batching system to the next recipe

usage.name: Switch product formulation
usage.statement: Switch the batch preparation system to a different product formulation between batch cycles.
usage.description: Operator reconfigures the batch system to run a different product formulation, including updating recipe parameters and verifying readiness before starting the next batch.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that previous-formulation residue contaminates the next batch
needs.plain: Leftover material from the previous product should not mix into the next batch. This helps ensure the new batch has the intended ingredients and quality.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of sterile product-contact boundaries being compromised during reconfiguration
needs.plain: Keep the sterile surfaces and connections protected while changing from one product mixture to another. This helps prevent contamination during the changeover.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of prior-recipe residue contaminating the new formulation during changeover
needs.plain: Prevent leftover material from the previous product from getting into the new product when changing formulas.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of process parameters set outside the new recipe specifications
needs.plain: Make sure the equipment settings match the requirements for the new product recipe. Correct settings help the product turn out as intended.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of unavailable production capacity during formulation changeover
Reduce the amount of liquid discarded during flushing and transition to the new formulation

usage.name: Switch product formulations
usage.statement: Switch the plant between different viscous product formulations.
usage.description: Switching between different formulations or product grades requires reconfiguring the plant between runs.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of prior-formulation residue contaminating the first batch after a formulation switch
needs.plain: As little leftover material from the previous product as possible should get into the first batch of the new product. This helps prevent the new batch from being mixed with the old one.

usage.name: Switch skid configuration between products
usage.statement: Switch the skid configuration between different processes or products.
usage.description: Reconfigure the skid's flow path, parameters, and assemblies to accommodate a different process or product.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of prior-product residue remaining on product-contact surfaces after the configuration switch
needs.plain: Make sure no leftover product remains on surfaces that touch the next product after changing the equipment setup. This helps prevent products from mixing or becoming contaminated.

usage.name: Synchronize timing of connected processing stages
usage.statement: Coordinate the operating timing of connected processing stages.
usage.description: The control-room operator aligns the start, rate, and pacing of sequential or parallel processing stages to prevent bottlenecks, hold-ups, or material overflow between stages.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of material exceeding its validated sterile hold limit while waiting between connected stages
needs.plain: Reduce the chance that material stays waiting so long between connected steps that it passes the longest tested time it can remain clean. This helps preserve its cleanliness before the next step.

usage.name: Transfer batch to intermediate vessel
usage.statement: Transfer the prepared batch to an intermediate holding or surge vessel.
usage.description: Operate the system's transfer function to move a completed batch into a buffer tank or intermediate vessel when direct downstream processing is not immediately available.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of batch contamination during transfer to the intermediate vessel
needs.plain: Prevent germs or other unwanted material from getting into the batch while it is moved into another container. This protects the batch from being spoiled or made unsafe.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of routing the prepared batch to the wrong intermediate vessel
Minimize the likelihood of exceeding the approved hold limit while awaiting intermediate vessel availability
Reduce the amount of batch lost through leakage or overflow during transfer
Reduce the percent of the prepared batch remaining in the source vessel after transfer

usage.name: Transfer material between stages
usage.statement: Move viscous material from one processing stage to the next.
usage.description: Stage-to-stage transfer is required in plants containing sequential vessels or processing units.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of mismatch between transfer flow and receiving-stage demand during continuous processing

usage.name: Trigger emergency shutdown
usage.statement: Activate the batch preparation system's emergency shutdown function.
usage.description: This provides immediate operational intervention when continued equipment activity presents an urgent hazard.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of hazardous motion, pressure, or energy after emergency shutdown activation
needs.plain: After an emergency stop, quickly end any remaining dangerous movement, pressure, or stored power.

needs.stmt: Minimize the duration of hazardous equipment activity after shutdown activation
needs.plain: Stop dangerous equipment activity as quickly as possible after the emergency stop is activated, so people and equipment are protected.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the skid emergency shutdown function is unavailable during an immediate hazard
needs.plain: Make sure the equipment unit can shut down immediately when there is a serious danger. This gives people a fast way to stop the hazard from getting worse.

usage.name: Troubleshoot batching faults
usage.statement: Troubleshoot batching faults that occur during a production run.
usage.description: Identify and resolve faults or alarms that interrupt or degrade the batching process while the system is in operation.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of compromising sterile conditions while troubleshooting the batching fault
Minimize the likelihood that the technician misidentifies the underlying cause of the batching fault
Minimize the likelihood of altering a critical batch parameter while testing the suspected fault
Minimize the likelihood of the batch exceeding allowable hold limits before the batching fault is resolved
Reduce the degree of ambiguity in the alarm and batch-state indications available to the technician

usage.name: Troubleshoot process faults
usage.statement: Troubleshoot faults that arise during viscous product processing.
usage.description: Detecting and resolving faults such as blockages or pump cavitation keeps the line running during operation.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of misidentifying the root cause of a blockage or pump cavitation

usage.name: Troubleshoot process upsets
usage.statement: Diagnose and correct process faults that occur during operation.
usage.description: Identify the root cause of deviations or upsets and apply corrective actions to restore normal processing.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of compromising sterile processing conditions while diagnosing and correcting a process fault
needs.plain: Avoid allowing contamination into the germ-free process while finding and fixing a problem. The process must stay protected during troubleshooting.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of the technician misidentifying the root cause of a process upset
needs.plain: Help the technician correctly identify the real reason a process went wrong. This allows the right problem to be fixed instead of only treating its effects.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of technician exposure to hazardous liquid during fault investigation
needs.plain: Prevent technicians from coming into contact with dangerous liquid while they investigate why the process went wrong.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of the liquid product processing interruption while locating and correcting the fault
needs.plain: The interruption to liquid production should last as little time as possible while people find and fix the problem. This helps reduce lost production and delays.

needs.stmt: Reduce the amount of liquid product discarded because of the process upset or corrective action

usage.name: Verify batch accuracy
usage.statement: Verify that the batching system delivers the correct quantity and composition of each batch within specified tolerances.
usage.description: Periodic end-to-end accuracy verification of batch outputs against target recipes to confirm that cumulative instrument drift or mechanical wear has not degraded dosing performance.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that offsetting ingredient deviations conceal an out-of-tolerance dose during end-to-end verification
needs.plain: Make it very unlikely that one ingredient being too high hides another ingredient being too low during a full batch check. Each ingredient amount should be correct on its own.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that a verification sample changes composition through contamination before analysis
Minimize the likelihood that verification results are biased by out-of-tolerance measurement references
Reduce the number of recipe ingredient pathways not exercised during accuracy verification
Reduce the number of recipe targets or tolerance limits that conflict across verification records

usage.name: Verify batch endpoint
usage.statement: Verify the batch endpoint from the batching system's process readings.
usage.description: Confirm that the batch has reached its defined endpoint criteria using process readings available on the system before authorising discharge.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of batches confirmed at endpoint while defined criteria remain unmet
Minimize the degree of disagreement among process readings used to verify the batch endpoint
Minimize the likelihood that endpoint criteria cannot be matched unambiguously to the available process readings
Reduce the number of required process readings unavailable during endpoint verification
Reduce the duration that process readings lag behind the batch's actual condition during endpoint verification

usage.name: Verify batch quality
usage.statement: Verify batch quality against specification during preparation.
usage.description: Sampling or in-line measurement during use confirms the batch meets specification.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of deviation between reported and actual batch quality attributes
needs.plain: Make sure the reported product quality matches the product’s actual quality. This matters because decisions should be based on accurate results.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of introducing contamination into the sterile batch during sampling
needs.plain: Prevent germs or other unwanted material from getting into the sterile batch while a sample is taken. This helps keep the batch safe and usable.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that a collected sample does not represent the batch as a whole
needs.plain: Make sure the collected sample accurately reflects the entire lot, rather than just one part of it. This makes the quality decision more reliable.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that batch results are evaluated against an incorrect or obsolete specification
needs.plain: Make it unlikely that a batch is judged using quality limits that are wrong or no longer current. This matters because the decision about the batch could be incorrect.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of the batch hold while awaiting quality verification results
needs.plain: Spend less time keeping a medicine batch on hold while waiting for quality test results. This helps the batch be released or addressed sooner.

usage.name: Verify ingredient dosing
usage.statement: Confirm each ingredient reaches its specified target quantity during batch preparation.
usage.description: Operator or automated system verifies that each ingredient has been dispensed to its specified batch quantity before or during the active cycle, ensuring formulation accuracy.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of falsely accepting an off-target ingredient dose as on target
needs.plain: Make it very unlikely that an ingredient amount outside the required range is wrongly accepted as correct. This helps ensure the product has the right recipe.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of difference between the indicated and delivered ingredient quantity

maintenance.name: Align rotating equipment
maintenance.statement: Align coupled rotating equipment within specified tolerances.
maintenance.description: Alignment is required after installation disturbance or detected vibration to prevent premature drivetrain wear.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of unexpected rotation of the coupled equipment during alignment
needs.plain: Prevent the connected equipment from turning unexpectedly while it is being lined up. This helps protect workers from moving machinery and injuries.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of introducing contaminants into the sterile fill-finish area during alignment
Reduce the degree of alignment drift after process loads are restored
Reduce the degree of alignment shift while the coupled equipment is being secured
Minimize the likelihood of applying incorrect alignment tolerances because configuration information conflicts

maintenance.name: Back Up Control Configurations
maintenance.statement: Back up the plant's control configurations.
maintenance.description: Current backups allow controller programs, operating parameters, recipes, and interface settings to be restored after a failure or replacement.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that backed-up configurations cannot be restored to their intended control assets

maintenance.name: Back up control and recipe data
maintenance.statement: Back up batch recipes, calibration records, controller settings, user configurations, and event logs from the batching system's control platform.
maintenance.description: Periodic export and secure storage of all critical process data and system configurations to enable recovery from data loss, hardware failure, or software corruption.

needs.stmt: Minimize the percentage of required batch recipes, calibration records, controller settings, user configurations, and event logs omitted from each backup
needs.plain: Include all needed production instructions, equipment adjustment records, operating settings, user choices, and activity histories in every backup. This helps restore operations if the original information is lost.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that exported backup files are corrupted or unreadable
needs.plain: Make sure saved copies of the machine’s operating settings and product instructions are complete and can be opened.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that related recipes, calibration records, controller settings, and user configurations are captured from inconsistent system states
needs.plain: Make sure recipes, measurement records, machine settings, and user preferences are saved from one consistent version of the system. This helps prevent missing or conflicting information during recovery or transfer.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of unauthorized access to sensitive process data during export and storage
needs.plain: Protect sensitive information about how the process is run when it is copied and stored. This prevents people without permission from accessing it.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that completed backups cannot be located or retrieved when recovery is required
needs.plain: Make sure saved copies of control and recipe information can be found and opened when needed to restore it. A backup is not useful if no one can locate or retrieve it.

maintenance.name: Back up control configurations
maintenance.statement: Back up the skid's control configurations.
maintenance.description: This enables recovery of validated settings, logic, parameters, and operator-interface configurations after a failure or change.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of mismatch between the backed-up configuration and the skid's validated state

maintenance.name: Back up recipe data
maintenance.statement: Back up recipe, configuration, and batch record data.
maintenance.description: Recipe data, batch records, and configuration settings must be backed up to prevent loss.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of recipe, configuration, or batch records omitted from the backup
Minimize the likelihood that backed-up recipe and configuration data cannot be restored after data loss

maintenance.name: Calibrate measurement instruments
maintenance.statement: Calibrate the flow meters, load cells, level sensors, temperature probes, and dosing devices to their reference standards to maintain measurement accuracy.
maintenance.description: Combines initial calibration at commissioning (#44, Preparation) with periodic recalibration during service life (#80, Maintenance). Periodic recalibration is the recurring Maintenance job; initial calibration at commissioning is a sub-step of Preparation commissioning covered by #46. Homing the canonical job in Maintenance.
maintenance.userGroup: Instrumentation technicians, maintenance engineers, quality personnel.
maintenance.frequency: At scheduled calibration intervals (typically annually or per quality plan).

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of introducing contamination into sterile product-contact areas during calibration
needs.plain: Prevent germs from entering areas that touch the product while measuring equipment is being adjusted. This keeps the product protected during the adjustment.
needs.role: Instrumentation technicians, maintenance engineers, quality personnel.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of contaminating sterile product-contact areas during calibration
needs.plain: Adjusting the measuring tools should be as unlikely as possible to dirty clean surfaces that touch the product. This helps keep the product safe from contamination.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of residual deviation between instrument readings and reference-standard values after calibration
needs.plain: After adjustment, make sure the instrument's readings are as close as possible to trusted correct values.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of calibrating an instrument against an expired or untraceable reference standard
needs.plain: Make sure the instrument is checked against a trusted reference that is still valid and can be traced to its source.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of batch-control instruments remaining outside specified tolerance after calibration
needs.plain: Make sure the instruments used to control a production batch give readings within the allowed range after they are checked and adjusted. Accurate readings help keep the batch under control.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of measurement instruments remaining outside specified tolerance after calibration
needs.plain: Make sure measuring devices give readings within the allowed range after they are checked and adjusted. Accurate readings are important for making reliable decisions.

needs.stmt: Reduce the time the measurement device remains in service beyond its approved calibration interval
needs.role: Instrumentation technicians, maintenance engineers, quality personnel.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of calibration records assigned to the wrong measurement device or reference standard
needs.role: Instrumentation technicians, maintenance engineers, quality personnel.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of deviation from the specified tolerance after calibration
needs.role: Instrumentation technicians, maintenance engineers, quality personnel.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the required reference standard is outside its certification validity period when calibration begins
needs.role: Instrumentation technicians, maintenance engineers, quality personnel.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of instruments calibrated using incorrect range or tolerance requirements
Reduce the duration of scheduled production disruption caused by instruments remaining unavailable for calibration

maintenance.name: Calibrate process instrumentation
maintenance.statement: Calibrate the plant's sensors, meters, and control instrumentation against traceable reference standards.
maintenance.description: Ensures measurement accuracy and regulatory traceability for all process instruments used in plant operation.
maintenance.frequency: At defined calibration intervals per instrument management plan.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of calibrated process instruments left with measurement error outside approved tolerance
needs.plain: As few measuring tools as possible should still give inaccurate readings after being checked and adjusted. Accurate readings help people control the process safely and correctly.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of contaminating sterile processing areas during instrument access and calibration
Minimize the likelihood that required traceable reference standards are unavailable or outside their validity period at scheduled calibration
Reduce the number of calibration records missing required as-found, as-left, reference traceability, or authorization evidence
needs.plain: Make sure each measurement-check record includes the instrument’s condition before and after the check, proof that the reference was reliable, and approval from the responsible person. This provides evidence that the check was done properly.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of production interruption caused by instrument isolation, calibration, and return to service
needs.plain: Keep production from stopping longer than necessary while measuring devices are taken out, checked, and put back into use. This helps the process restart sooner and keeps measurements reliable.

maintenance.name: Calibrate process instruments
maintenance.statement: Calibrate the skid's sensors, meters, and control instruments.
maintenance.description: Performing scheduled calibration of all process measurement instruments on the skid to ensure accuracy and traceability to reference standards.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of skid instruments remaining outside specified tolerance after calibration

maintenance.name: Clean external skid surfaces
maintenance.statement: Clean the skid's external surfaces.
maintenance.description: Wiping down or washing the external housing, frames, and exposed panels of the skid to remove dust, spills, and surface contamination.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of external skid surfaces retaining dust, spill residue, or contamination after cleaning

maintenance.name: Clean product-contact surfaces
maintenance.statement: Clean the interior surfaces of tanks, vessels, and product-contact components of the processing plant.
maintenance.description: Removal of residue, deposits, and contaminants from all surfaces that contact the liquid product to maintain hygiene and prevent cross-contamination.

needs.stmt: Minimize the amount of cleaning-agent residue remaining on product-contact surfaces after cleaning
needs.plain: As little cleaning liquid as possible should remain on surfaces that touch the product after cleaning. This helps prevent the product from being exposed to cleaning chemicals.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of recontaminating cleaned product-contact surfaces before batch materials are introduced
needs.plain: Keep cleaned surfaces that touch the product from getting dirty again before the materials are added. This helps keep the product free from unwanted contamination.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of cleaned product-contact surfaces becoming recontaminated before processing resumes
needs.plain: Keep cleaned surfaces that touch the product from becoming dirty again before work starts. This helps keep the product safe.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of cleaned product-contact surfaces with product residue or deposits remaining
needs.plain: Leave as little product buildup as possible on surfaces that touch the product after cleaning. This helps prevent old material from affecting the next batch.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of product-contact components omitted from cleaning
needs.plain: Clean every surface and part that touches the product, without leaving any out. This helps prevent leftover material or contamination from affecting the next product.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of introducing microbial contamination onto product-contact surfaces during cleaning
needs.plain: Prevent germs from getting onto surfaces that touch the product while those surfaces are being cleaned. This helps keep the product safe.

needs.stmt: Reduce the amount of residual viscous product left on product-contact surfaces and inside vessels after cleaning
needs.plain: Remove as much thick leftover product as possible from surfaces and containers after cleaning. This helps prevent old product from remaining in the equipment.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of product-contact surface area retaining previous-batch residue after cleaning
needs.plain: Remove as much leftover material from product-touching surfaces as possible, so residue from the previous batch does not affect the next one.

needs.stmt: Reduce the amount of cleaning-agent residue remaining in tanks, lines, pumps, mixers, valves, and dispensing points after cleaning
needs.plain: As little cleaning liquid as possible should remain in the tanks, pipes, pumps, mixing equipment, valves, and points where product is dispensed. This helps prevent the cleaning liquid from getting into the product.

needs.stmt: Minimize the amount of damage to product-contact surfaces caused during cleaning
needs.plain: Avoid damaging the surfaces that touch the product while cleaning them. This helps keep the equipment usable and prevents harm to the product.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of batch-start delay when product-contact surfaces do not meet pre-use acceptance criteria

maintenance.name: Clean, sanitize, and decontaminate product-contact surfaces
maintenance.statement: Clean, sanitize, and decontaminate the batch preparation system's product-contact vessels and surfaces to maintain hygienic conditions and release the system for continued or new service.
maintenance.description: Covers all hygiene restoration activities on product-contact surfaces and vessels, whether performed manually (clean and sanitize) or via automated clean-in-place cycle, and including decontamination before releasing the system from service. Executed between batch runs, on schedule, or as required by product-changeover or regulatory protocols.
maintenance.userGroup: Operators, sanitation/hygiene technicians, quality assurance personnel.
maintenance.frequency: Between batch runs and/or on a scheduled maintenance cycle; decontamination as required before service release.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that microbial or endotoxin contamination remains on product-contact surfaces after sanitation
needs.plain: Make sure product-contact surfaces are free of germs and harmful substances after cleaning and treatment. This helps prevent contamination of the product.
needs.role: Operators, sanitation/hygiene technicians, quality assurance personnel.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of product-contact surface area with detectable product residue after cleaning
needs.plain: After cleaning, as little product residue as possible should remain on surfaces that touch the product. This helps prevent leftover material from affecting the next production run.
needs.role: Operators, sanitation/hygiene technicians, quality assurance personnel.

maintenance.name: Descale heated surfaces
maintenance.statement: Descale and remove buildup from heat-transfer and jacketed surfaces.
maintenance.description: Thermal processing of viscous product leaves deposits that reduce heat transfer efficiency and must be removed.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of heat-transfer and jacketed surface area retaining buildup after descaling

maintenance.name: Document maintenance history
maintenance.statement: Document maintenance actions, part replacements, and service records for the plant.
maintenance.description: Accurate records support compliance, warranty, and predictive planning for the processing equipment.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of completed maintenance activities omitted from the batch preparation system history
Reduce the number of completed maintenance activities omitted from the maintenance history
Reduce the percentage of completed maintenance actions omitted from the plant maintenance history
Reduce the number of completed maintenance, calibration, or cleaning events omitted from skid history
Reduce the number of completed maintenance activities omitted from the plant history
needs.plain: Make sure every finished maintenance job is added to the plant’s history. This helps people know what work was done and plan future upkeep.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of maintenance entries containing inaccurate service or repair details
needs.plain: Keep the maintenance history accurate about the service and repairs that were done. This helps future workers know what work the machine actually received.

needs.stmt: Minimize the time between completion of a maintenance activity and its entry in the plant history
needs.plain: Enter completed upkeep work in the plant's history as soon as possible. This helps keep the plant's records current.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of maintenance records linked to the wrong plant component
needs.plain: Link each maintenance record to the correct part of the plant. This prevents work on one part from being mistaken for work on another.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of maintenance entries lacking required inspection or repair evidence
needs.plain: Make sure every maintenance entry includes the required proof of inspections or repairs. This shows that the work was properly checked and completed.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of completed inspections, calibrations, replacements, repairs, updates, and service events omitted from the batching-system history
needs.plain: Fewer completed checks, adjustments, part changes, repairs, updates, and other service work should be missing from the production system's history. This provides a complete record of what was done.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of maintenance records containing incorrect inspection findings, calibration results, replacement details, repair details, update details, or service dates
needs.plain: Keep maintenance records accurate about inspection results, equipment checks, replaced parts, repairs, changes, and service dates. Reliable details help workers know the machine’s true condition and service history.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of calibration and service records missing required certificates, approvals, or sign-offs
needs.plain: Fewer adjustment and repair records should be missing required certificates, approvals, or signatures. This helps show that the work was properly completed and accepted.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of maintenance events represented by conflicting records in the batching-system history
needs.plain: Make sure the batching system does not show conflicting versions of the same maintenance event. One consistent record prevents confusion about what work happened.

needs.stmt: Reduce the time between completion of a maintenance activity and its entry into the batching-system history
needs.plain: There should be less time between finishing maintenance work and recording it in the production system’s history. This keeps the work record up to date.

maintenance.name: Drain and flush process lines
maintenance.statement: Drain and flush the batch preparation system's process lines prior to shutdown or product changeover.
maintenance.description: Evacuate residual product, solvents, or process fluids from pipework, valves, and vessels to prevent contamination, corrosion, or solidification during idle periods or between product runs.

needs.stmt: Minimize the amount of residual product remaining in process lines, valves, or vessels after flushing
needs.plain: Leave as little product as possible in the pipes, valves, and tanks after they are emptied and flushed. This helps prevent waste and unwanted mixing with the next product.

needs.stmt: Reduce the amount of residual product remaining in pipework, valves, or vessels after flushing
needs.plain: Leave as little product behind as possible after the pipes and containers are rinsed, so less material is wasted or carried into the next use.

maintenance.name: Flush and preserve plant circuits
maintenance.statement: Flush residual viscous material from pipes, pumps, and transfer lines, and preserve plant circuits prior to extended shutdown.
maintenance.description: Clears residual product and process fluids from the plant's transfer and process circuits using flush media, then applies preservation measures to protect internal surfaces and components during idle or extended shutdown periods.
maintenance.frequency: At end of each production campaign or before extended shutdown

needs.stmt: Reduce the amount of residual viscous material remaining in pipes, pumps, and transfer lines after flushing

maintenance.name: Inspect electrical and control connections
maintenance.statement: Inspect electrical wiring, terminal connections, conduit, and control panel components of the batching system for integrity and safety.
maintenance.description: Periodic physical inspection of all electrical infrastructure associated with the batching system to detect loose connections, insulation degradation, corrosion, and potential failure points before they cause downtime or safety incidents.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of contaminants entering controlled production areas while batching-system electrical infrastructure is accessed
needs.plain: Keep dirt and other unwanted material out of the controlled production area while electrical parts of the mixing system are being accessed. This protects the cleanliness of the area.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of unexpected energization of electrical circuits while the technician accesses batching-system connections
needs.plain: Prevent electricity from unexpectedly turning on while a technician reaches the machine's mixing-system connections. This helps protect the technician from electric shock or injury.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of loose, degraded, or corroded batching-system connections remaining undetected after inspection
Minimize the percentage of required wiring and terminal connection points that remain inaccessible or obscured during inspection
needs.plain: Make all needed wires and connection points visible and reachable during inspection, so workers can check them properly and find problems.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of batching-system electrical components misidentified because records conflict with installed wiring
needs.plain: Avoid wrongly identifying electrical parts because the paperwork does not match the wires and parts that are actually installed. Accurate identification helps people inspect, repair, and operate the system safely.

maintenance.name: Inspect for wear
maintenance.statement: Inspect the system for wear, corrosion, leaks, and damage.
maintenance.description: Visual and physical inspection catches corrosion, cracks, or leaks before they compromise a batch.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of wear, corrosion, cracks, or leaks remaining undetected in inaccessible system areas
Minimize the likelihood that corrosion, cracks, leaks, or wear remain undetected during inspection

maintenance.name: Inspect for wear and damage
maintenance.statement: Inspect the plant's vessels, piping, and components for wear, corrosion, and leaks.
maintenance.description: Systematic visual and physical inspection of pressure vessels, pipework, and mechanical components to detect deterioration before failure.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of the technician encountering residual pressure or hazardous liquid during inspection
needs.plain: Prevent trapped pressure or dangerous liquid from surprising or reaching the technician during inspection. This helps keep the inspection safe.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of actual wear, corrosion, or leaks that remain undetected during inspection
needs.plain: Find as many real signs of wear, rust, or leaks as possible during inspection. This helps prevent hidden damage from causing a failure later.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of introducing contamination into sterile processing areas during inspection
Reduce the duration of inspection delays caused by inaccessible vessel, piping, or component surfaces
needs.plain: Spend less time waiting to check tanks, pipes, and parts for wear or damage because their surfaces are difficult to reach. This helps staff find problems sooner.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of inspection findings assigned to the wrong vessel, pipework section, or component
needs.plain: Make sure each inspection problem is linked to the correct vessel, pipe section, or part. This helps the right problem get fixed in the right place.

maintenance.name: Inspect for wear and leaks
maintenance.statement: Inspect the batching system's tanks, piping, pumps, valves, mixers, sensors, and fittings for leaks, corrosion, deterioration, and component wear.
maintenance.description: Combines #81 (inspect for leaks, corrosion, wear) with #95 (inspect tanks, piping, pumps, valves, mixers, sensors, fittings for deterioration, wear, damage). Both describe the same periodic inspection job; #95 adds component specificity to #81.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that accessing product-contact areas during inspection introduces contamination
needs.plain: Avoid bringing dirt or germs onto surfaces that touch the product while checking for wear and leaks. This helps keep the product safe.

needs.stmt: Minimize the percentage of existing leaks, corrosion, deterioration, and component wear not detected during the inspection
Minimize the likelihood that accessing tanks, piping, pumps, valves, mixers, sensors, or fittings for inspection causes damage or misalignment
Reduce the number of required tank, piping, pump, valve, mixer, sensor, and fitting inspection points inaccessible at the scheduled interval
needs.plain: Reduce the number of equipment inspection spots that workers cannot reach when they are due to be checked. This helps them find wear or leaks on time.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of disagreement between maintenance technicians and process engineers about whether observed deterioration requires corrective action
needs.plain: Make sure maintenance workers and process specialists agree on whether signs of wear or leaks need to be fixed. This helps ensure problems are neither ignored nor treated unnecessarily.

maintenance.name: Inspect plant components for deterioration
maintenance.statement: Inspect plant components — including mixing elements, agitators, heat-transfer surfaces, and structural parts — for signs of wear, deterioration, or damage.
maintenance.description: Performs structured visual and physical inspection of all critical plant components to detect wear, corrosion, erosion, or damage that could affect processing performance or safety, enabling timely intervention.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of critical component surfaces left uninspected because they are inaccessible or obscured

maintenance.name: Inspect skid components
maintenance.statement: Inspect skid components for deterioration.
maintenance.description: Conducting visual and tactile inspection of the skid's structural, mechanical, and fluid-path components to identify signs of wear, corrosion, or damage.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that deterioration in critical structural, mechanical, or fluid-path components remains undetected during inspection

maintenance.name: Lubricate moving components
maintenance.statement: Lubricate designated moving components at specified intervals.
maintenance.description: Proper lubrication limits friction-related wear in pumps, drives, bearings, and actuators.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of lubricant entering the sterile processing environment during component lubrication
needs.plain: Keep oil or grease from getting into the germ-free processing area while moving parts are being lubricated. This protects the product and the clean working environment.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of lubricant entering sterile or product-contact areas during maintenance
needs.plain: Keep oil or grease out of germ-free areas and places that touch the product while moving parts are being serviced.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of lubricant entering the sterile product environment during component lubrication
needs.plain: Make it unlikely that oil or grease used on moving parts will get into the clean product area. This helps keep the product free from unwanted material.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of lubricant entering sterile product-contact areas during maintenance
needs.plain: Keep lubricant from getting into clean areas that touch the product while parts are being serviced. This helps prevent the product from becoming contaminated.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of designated moving components receiving incompatible lubricant
Reduce the percentage of designated moving components exceeding their specified lubrication interval
Minimize the degree of deviation from the specified lubricant quantity for each designated moving component
Reduce the duration of sterile fill-finish production interruption beyond the planned maintenance window

maintenance.name: Manage spare parts inventory
maintenance.statement: Manage the critical spare parts inventory for the skid.
maintenance.description: Tracking stock levels, reorder points, and shelf life of critical replacement components to ensure availability when maintenance is required.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that a critical replacement component is unavailable when skid maintenance is required

maintenance.name: Monitor equipment condition
maintenance.statement: Monitor the operating condition and health of plant equipment.
maintenance.description: Tracks key operating parameters — including vibration, temperature, pressure, and output quality — and equipment condition indicators to detect early signs of degradation, enabling proactive maintenance decisions.
maintenance.userGroup: Plant operators, maintenance engineers, reliability engineers
maintenance.frequency: Continuously during operation; supplemented by scheduled checks

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of early equipment degradation remaining undetected before affecting product quality or equipment function
needs.plain: Find early signs that equipment is wearing out before the problem affects product quality or equipment operation. Early detection allows action to be taken sooner.
needs.role: Plant operators, maintenance engineers, reliability engineers

maintenance.name: Monitor plant health
maintenance.statement: Monitor the operating status and equipment condition of the processing plant through plant health indicators.
maintenance.description: Detects emerging faults, degradation trends, and anomalies to enable timely intervention before failure.
maintenance.userGroup: Maintenance engineers, plant operators, reliability teams.
maintenance.frequency: Continuously during plant operation; reviewed at defined intervals.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that a plant health indicator misrepresents the actual equipment condition
needs.role: Maintenance engineers, plant operators, reliability teams.

needs.stmt: Minimize the time between the onset of equipment degradation and its recognition by monitoring personnel
needs.role: Maintenance engineers, plant operators, reliability teams.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of plant health indicator data unavailability during plant operation
needs.role: Maintenance engineers, plant operators, reliability teams.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of false plant health alerts that trigger unnecessary maintenance action
needs.role: Maintenance engineers, plant operators, reliability teams.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of disagreement among maintenance engineers, plant operators, and reliability teams about the condition indicated for the same equipment
needs.plain: Make sure maintenance workers, machine operators, and equipment-health teams agree on what condition the same equipment is in. This supports a shared and accurate view of problems.
needs.role: Maintenance engineers, plant operators, reliability teams.

maintenance.name: Monitor system health
maintenance.statement: Monitor diagnostic alerts, component runtime, process trends, and equipment condition indicators of the batching system.
maintenance.description: Combines #83 (monitor operational health and status) with #97 (monitor diagnostic alerts, component runtime, process trends, condition indicators). #97 adds specificity to #83; both describe the same continuous monitoring job.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that displayed parameters misrepresent the batch preparation system's actual condition
Reduce the percentage of displayed parameter readings that misrepresent actual system condition
Minimize the likelihood of process trends being misinterpreted as normal equipment behavior
Minimize the likelihood that critical diagnostic alerts go unnoticed during production
Reduce the frequency of false diagnostic alerts that obscure actionable batching system faults
Reduce the percent of component runtime records containing inaccurate values during production
needs.plain: Record fewer incorrect readings about how long machine parts have been running during production. Accurate records help workers understand the machine’s condition and maintenance needs.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of equipment condition indicator unavailability during production
needs.plain: Spend less time without information showing whether the equipment is working properly during production. This helps staff notice problems and respond sooner.

maintenance.name: Optimize run performance across batches
maintenance.statement: Optimize run performance across successive batches.
maintenance.description: Analyze run data and adjust process parameters across batches to improve yield, cycle time, and process consistency.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that parameter adjustments push critical quality attributes outside approved limits across batches

maintenance.name: Perform routine service
maintenance.statement: Perform scheduled preventive service on the plant's mechanical and electrical equipment.
maintenance.description: Pumps, valves, agitators, and drives need scheduled servicing to avoid unplanned failures.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of technician exposure to hazardous energy while servicing pumps, valves, agitators, or drives
needs.plain: Technicians should have as little contact as possible with dangerous stored or active power while servicing pumps, valves, mixing equipment, or motors. This helps protect them from electric, pressure, or movement-related injuries.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of contamination being introduced into product-contact areas during service
needs.plain: Reduce the chance that dirt, germs, or other unwanted material gets into areas that touch the product during servicing. This helps protect the product from contamination.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of compromising sterile processing conditions while servicing plant equipment
needs.plain: Avoid letting servicing work make the clean conditions unsafe for sterile production.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that critical wear or deterioration is overlooked during scheduled service
needs.plain: Make sure important wear or damage is found during regular servicing. Missing it could allow a part to fail later.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that critical wear or damage remains undetected during scheduled service
Minimize the likelihood that required parts or consumables are unavailable when scheduled service begins
Reduce the time the technician waits for equipment to be released for scheduled service
