needs.plain: The technician should be able to start planned equipment service without waiting for the equipment to be made available. This helps routine work stay on schedule.

maintenance.name: Preserve idle equipment
maintenance.statement: Preserve and store plant equipment and components correctly during planned or extended shutdowns.
maintenance.description: Prevents corrosion, contamination, and degradation of plant equipment while it is not in active service.
maintenance.frequency: At each planned or extended shutdown.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of product-contact surfaces that become contaminated while the sterile liquid-processing plant is idle
needs.plain: Keep surfaces that touch the product clean while the processing equipment is not being used. This helps prevent the next product from becoming contaminated.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of corrosion on internal equipment surfaces and components during the shutdown
Reduce the number of seals and gaskets that crack or deform while the equipment is idle
Reduce the number of stored equipment components that sustain mechanical damage during the outage
Reduce the degree of ambiguity in preservation requirements or completion records for idle equipment
needs.plain: Clearly state how unused equipment must be protected and what records must be completed while it is not being used. This helps keep the equipment in known condition and shows what was done to preserve it.

maintenance.name: Preserve idle skid
maintenance.statement: Preserve the skid during extended inactivity.
maintenance.description: This limits corrosion, contamination, material degradation, and damage while the skid is stored or shut down.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of contamination entering the skid product-contact path during storage or shutdown
needs.plain: Make it less likely that dirt or other harmful substances enter the product-contact path while the processing unit is stored or shut down. This helps keep the path clean before production resumes.

maintenance.name: Protect Idle Equipment
maintenance.statement: Protect idle plant equipment during shutdown periods.
maintenance.description: Shutdown protection limits corrosion, contamination, moisture intrusion, seizure, freezing, and deformation while equipment is not operating.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of contamination reaching sterile product-contact surfaces during an extended shutdown
needs.plain: Make it as unlikely as possible for contamination to reach surfaces that touch sterile products while the equipment is shut down for a long time. This helps keep the equipment ready for safe use later.

maintenance.name: Purge and flush process lines
maintenance.statement: Purge and flush process lines to remove residual liquid product before maintenance or changeover.
maintenance.description: Displace residual liquid product from pipework and vessels using water, gas, or a displacement medium prior to maintenance access or product changeover, to ensure worker safety and prevent cross-contamination.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of contaminants entering the sterile process boundary during purging or flushing
needs.plain: The chance of unwanted dirt or germs entering the clean processing area should be as low as possible while the pipes are cleared and rinsed. This helps keep the process clean.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of purge discharge entering an active or clean process path
needs.plain: Keep material flushed out of pipes from entering a pipe route that is being used or kept clean. This prevents unwanted material from reaching the process.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of worker exposure from trapped pressure or residual product released during maintenance access
needs.plain: Prevent trapped pressure or leftover product from reaching the worker when the process lines are opened for maintenance. The lines should be emptied and rinsed safely first.

needs.stmt: Minimize the amount of residual liquid product remaining in pipework and vessels after purging and flushing
Reduce the amount of flushing medium consumed before release criteria are met
needs.plain: Use as little cleaning liquid or gas as possible before the pipes are clean enough to release. This saves materials while still meeting the required cleanliness level.

maintenance.name: Recalibrate dosing system after component change
maintenance.statement: Recalibrate the batch preparation system's dosing system following component replacement or repair.
maintenance.description: Re-establish dosing accuracy by running calibration routines after any pump, valve, meter, or control component has been replaced, adjusted, or serviced.

needs.stmt: Reduce the degree of dosing-output deviation from the specified target after recalibration
needs.plain: After replacing a part and readjusting the system, make sure it delivers amounts close to the intended target. This helps prevent too much or too little material from being given.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of verification doses outside specified tolerance after recalibration
needs.plain: Make sure test doses are within the allowed range after the dosing system is adjusted. This confirms that changing a part has not made the doses inaccurate.

maintenance.name: Repair faults
maintenance.statement: Diagnose and repair faults in the processing plant's equipment.
maintenance.description: When faults occur, damaged equipment must be repaired to restore the plant to operating condition.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of introducing contamination into sterile product-contact areas during fault repair
needs.plain: Make it very unlikely that contamination enters clean areas touching the product while a problem is being repaired. This helps keep the product safe after the repair.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of technician injury from hazardous plant conditions during fault repair
needs.plain: Reduce the chance that a technician is hurt by dangerous conditions in the facility while fixing a problem.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of incorrectly diagnosing the cause of an equipment fault
Reduce the frequency of the same equipment fault recurring after repair
Reduce the duration of repair delay caused by unavailable required components

maintenance.name: Repair faults and failures
maintenance.statement: Diagnose and repair faults, breakdowns, and failed components in the system.
maintenance.description: Faults, breakdowns, and failed components must be diagnosed and repaired to restore operation.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of introducing contamination into exposed product-contact areas during repair
needs.plain: Keep exposed areas that touch the product free from contamination while workers fix a problem.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of technician exposure to hazardous pressure, stored energy, or process liquid during diagnosis and repair
needs.plain: Protect technicians from dangerous pressure, trapped energy, or process liquid while they find and fix a problem.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of identifying the incorrect source of a batching-system fault
Reduce the number of additional batching-system components damaged during disassembly or repair
Reduce the time the batching system waits for a required replacement component after diagnosis
needs.plain: Get the needed replacement part quickly after finding the problem, so the batching system can be repaired sooner.

maintenance.name: Repair plant faults
maintenance.statement: Repair mechanical, electrical, or process faults affecting the processing plant.
maintenance.description: Diagnoses and rectifies faults in the plant's mechanical, electrical, and process systems to restore normal operating condition, covering both reactive repairs following breakdowns and corrective actions identified during inspection.
maintenance.userGroup: Maintenance technicians, plant engineers, electrical technicians

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the repaired plant fault recurs after return to production
needs.plain: After a facility problem is repaired, it should be as unlikely as possible to happen again when production restarts. This helps keep production reliable.
needs.role: Maintenance technicians, plant engineers, electrical technicians

maintenance.name: Repair skid faults
maintenance.statement: Repair faults affecting the skid.
maintenance.description: This restores intended operation after mechanical, electrical, pneumatic, instrumentation, or control failures occur.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of misidentifying the source of the skid fault before repair begins

maintenance.name: Repair system faults
maintenance.statement: Diagnose and repair mechanical, electrical, and control faults.
maintenance.description: When faults or breakdowns occur, the system must be diagnosed and repaired to restore production.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of incorrectly identifying the system fault's root cause during diagnosis
Minimize the likelihood of misidentifying the source of a mechanical, electrical, or control fault

maintenance.name: Replace consumable parts
maintenance.statement: Replace worn filters, seals, gaskets, and mixing consumables.
maintenance.description: Filters, seals, gaskets, and mixing elements wear out and must be swapped to maintain performance.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of contaminating the sterile product-contact path during consumable replacement
needs.plain: Lower the chance that germs or other unwanted material get into the parts that touch the sterile medicine when replaceable parts are changed. This helps keep the medicine safe to use.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that an installed consumable has incompatible material, dimensions, or operating limits
Minimize the likelihood of leakage from an improperly seated or damaged gasket or seal after replacement
Minimize the likelihood of damaging product-contact housings or mating surfaces while removing or installing consumables
Minimize the likelihood of installing an incorrect consumable in the batch preparation system
Minimize the likelihood of selecting a replacement consumable that does not match the system specification
Reduce the duration of plant unavailability caused by seized, obstructed, or inaccessible consumables during replacement

maintenance.name: Replace filtration elements
maintenance.statement: Replace filters, cartridges, and other consumable filtration elements in the fluid path of the batching system.
maintenance.description: Swap-out of clogged, expired, or damaged filter media and cartridges to sustain flow rate, particulate control, and fluid quality.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of contamination entering the open fluid path during element replacement
needs.plain: Replacing a filter or similar part should not let dirt or germs enter the open path carrying the liquid or material. This helps protect the product from contamination.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of technician exposure to residual pressure or hazardous fluid while opening the filter housing
needs.plain: Prevent a worker from being exposed to trapped pressure or harmful liquid when opening the filter container. This helps avoid injury or contact with the liquid.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of installing an incorrect filtration element in the batching system
Minimize the likelihood of fluid leaking from the filter housing after replacement
Reduce the duration of batching system downtime caused by an unavailable replacement element
needs.plain: Keep the equipment that prepares products in batches from being out of service for long when a replacement filter is not available. This helps work resume sooner when the filter needs changing.

maintenance.name: Replace seals and gaskets
maintenance.statement: Replace worn seals, gaskets, O-rings, diaphragms, and packing on pumps, valves, and connections within the batching system.
maintenance.description: Combines #79 (replace seals, gaskets, O-rings on pumps, valves, connections) with #96 (replace worn seals, gaskets, diaphragms, packing). Both describe the same sealing-component replacement job with overlapping component scope.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of contamination entering the opened product-contact path during sealing-component replacement
needs.plain: Keep dirt or germs from entering the opened product path while replacing seals and gaskets. This protects the product from contamination during the repair.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of releasing residual pressure or process liquid when opening a pump, valve, or connection
needs.plain: Prevent trapped pressure or process liquid from suddenly escaping when opening a pump, valve, or connection. This helps protect the worker and prevent spills.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of installing a sealing element with incompatible material or dimensions
Minimize the likelihood of installing a sealing component incompatible with the process fluid, operating conditions, or hygienic requirements
Reduce the number of serviced pumps, valves, and connections that leak after reassembly
Reduce the duration of batching-system unavailability caused by sealing-component replacement
needs.plain: Keep the equipment that prepares products in batches from being unavailable for long while worn sealing parts are replaced. This helps prevent leaks and lets production restart sooner.

maintenance.name: Replace worn or consumed plant components
maintenance.statement: Replace plant components — including consumables such as filters, screens, and pump elements, and structural components that have reached their service limits.
maintenance.description: Covers the full spectrum of component replacement: routine consumables (filters, screens, seals, pump elements) replaced on schedule, and durable components replaced when inspection or service-life criteria indicate end of serviceable life.
maintenance.frequency: At defined service intervals or on condition

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of component replacements requiring reopening because of incorrect fit, alignment, or sealing

maintenance.name: Replace worn skid components
maintenance.statement: Replace worn, degraded, or failed components — including seals, gaskets, O-rings, and other skid parts — to restore the skid to its qualified operating condition.
maintenance.description: Covers the full range of component replacement tasks during preventive and corrective maintenance, from elastomeric seals and gaskets to mechanical parts identified as degraded during inspection or service.
maintenance.userGroup: Maintenance technicians and engineering staff responsible for skid upkeep.
maintenance.frequency: As required per preventive maintenance schedule or corrective maintenance findings.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of installing a replacement component that fails skid material, dimensional, or qualification specifications
needs.role: Maintenance technicians and engineering staff responsible for skid upkeep.

maintenance.name: Requalify after maintenance
maintenance.statement: Requalify the skid after maintenance or component replacement.
maintenance.description: Matters because regulated processes require re-verification that the skid still performs to specification.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of deviation between the as-maintained skid configuration and the approved baseline

maintenance.name: Revalidate process performance after maintenance
maintenance.statement: Revalidate plant process performance following significant maintenance interventions.
maintenance.description: Run qualification batches or performance tests after major repairs, component replacements, or upgrades to confirm the plant returns to its specified process capability before resuming production.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of critical process parameters failing acceptance criteria during post-maintenance testing
needs.plain: Have as many important process settings as possible meet the required checks after repairs or servicing, so the system can be trusted to work properly again.

maintenance.name: Run clean-in-place cycle
maintenance.statement: Run the clean-in-place cycle across the plant's piping and process circuit.
maintenance.description: Automated clean-in-place cycles must be run to sanitize the wetted process circuit without disassembly.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of internal fluid pathways retaining microbial or chemical residue after the cycle
needs.plain: Remove germs and chemical residue from the inside passages that carry liquid. Leftover residue could contaminate the next product.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that cleaning solution bypasses required piping or vessels during the cycle
needs.plain: Make sure the cleaning liquid flows through every required pipe and container instead of skipping any part of the equipment.

needs.stmt: Minimize the amount of cleaning-agent residue remaining in the process circuit after the final rinse
needs.plain: Leave as little cleaning liquid as possible in the pipes and equipment after the final rinse. This helps prevent the cleaning liquid from affecting the next process.

needs.stmt: Minimize the percentage of the wetted process circuit retaining product or microbial residue after cycle completion
needs.plain: Leave as little product or germ residue as possible inside the product-carrying pipes and parts after cleaning. This helps keep the next batch clean.

needs.stmt: Minimize the duration that cleaning temperature, flow, or chemical concentration remains outside validated limits during the cycle
needs.plain: Keep cleaning heat, liquid flow, and cleaning chemical strength within their approved ranges throughout the cleaning cycle. This helps ensure the equipment is cleaned properly.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that loss of required utilities interrupts the cycle before completion
needs.plain: Make it less likely that the cleaning cycle stops before it is finished because an essential supply, such as power, water, air, or another service, is lost. This helps ensure the equipment is properly cleaned.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that cleaning or sanitizing fluid escapes from the batching system during the cycle
needs.plain: Make sure cleaning or sanitizing liquid does not leak out of the mixing equipment during cleaning. This protects people, equipment, and the surrounding area.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of deviation from validated cleaning limits during the cycle
needs.plain: Keep the cleaning process within the approved cleanliness limits from start to finish. This helps ensure the equipment is cleaned properly every time.

needs.stmt: Minimize the percentage of the intended wetted process circuit bypassed during the cycle
needs.plain: Make sure the cleaning process reaches all parts of the process path that are meant to be cleaned. This helps prevent residue or contamination from remaining in skipped sections.

needs.stmt: Reduce the frequency of clean-in-place cycles stopping before completion

maintenance.name: Sanitize product-contact surfaces for changeover
maintenance.statement: Sanitize the product-contact surfaces and vessels of the plant to hygienic standards during product changeover.
maintenance.description: Sanitize all product-contact surfaces to the required hygienic standard before introducing a new product, preventing cross-contamination.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of product-contact surfaces and vessel areas retaining prior-product residue after sanitation
needs.plain: Remove more of the previous product from surfaces that touch the product and from inside containers before switching to a new product.

maintenance.name: Schedule preventive maintenance
maintenance.statement: Schedule and plan preventive maintenance activities for the batching system using service intervals, operating hours, condition data, and component life limits.
maintenance.description: Systematic planning and calendaring of all scheduled maintenance tasks to minimize unplanned downtime and extend equipment service life.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of required preventive maintenance activities omitted from the schedule
Minimize the likelihood that preventive maintenance is scheduled after the required service interval
Reduce the number of required preventive maintenance activities omitted from the plant schedule
Reduce the number of required batching-system maintenance tasks omitted from the maintenance schedule
Minimize the likelihood that maintenance due dates are calculated from incomplete or inaccurate operating and condition data
Minimize the likelihood that a skid maintenance window conflicts with sterile fill-finish production or cleaning activities
Minimize the likelihood that scheduled batching-system maintenance conflicts with sterile fill-finish production windows
Reduce the amount of scheduled batching-system maintenance work exceeding the available outage capacity
Reduce the number of required preventive maintenance activities omitted from the plant schedule
Reduce the number of maintenance activities scheduled after their required service deadlines
Minimize the likelihood that scheduled maintenance overlaps sterile production commitments
Reduce the number of maintenance activities assigned to windows lacking required shutdown and contamination-control prerequisites
Minimize the likelihood that qualified maintenance personnel are unavailable for assigned activities
needs.plain: Make it unlikely that people with the needed maintenance skills are unavailable for the work they were assigned. This helps ensure the planned maintenance can be completed.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that required personnel, parts, or approvals are unavailable at the scheduled maintenance start
needs.plain: Make sure the needed workers, replacement parts, and approvals are ready when planned maintenance is due to begin. This helps the maintenance work start on time.

maintenance.name: Service pumps and agitators
maintenance.statement: Perform routine service, lubrication, and mechanical inspection on pumps, agitators, and mechanical drives.
maintenance.description: Combines lubrication of bearings, gearboxes, shafts, and drives (#85) with routine service and lubrication of pumps and agitators (#82). Lubrication is a sub-activity within pump/agitator servicing; they are the same Maintenance job.
maintenance.frequency: At scheduled service intervals per manufacturer recommendations.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of technician exposure to stored mechanical or process energy during service
needs.plain: Protect technicians from energy that remains stored in pumps and mixing equipment while they are being serviced. This includes pressure, moving parts, and other forces that could suddenly be released.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of introducing contaminants into product-contact areas during service
needs.plain: Prevent dirt or other unwanted material from getting into areas that touch the product while equipment is being repaired or maintained. This helps protect the product from contamination.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of failing to detect wear, leakage, or lubricant degradation during mechanical inspection
Minimize the likelihood of leaving pump or agitator components misaligned or improperly secured after reassembly
Reduce the duration of batching-system unavailability beyond the scheduled maintenance window

maintenance.name: Service pumps and valves
maintenance.statement: Service the pumps, valves, and actuated equipment on the skid.
maintenance.description: Performing scheduled lubrication, adjustment, seal replacement, and functional checks on pumps, valves, and other mechanical assemblies.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of product-contact areas becoming contaminated during pump or valve servicing
needs.plain: Keep the areas that touch the product clean while pumps or flow-control parts are being repaired or maintained. This helps prevent the product from becoming contaminated.

maintenance.name: Store system properly
maintenance.statement: Store and preserve the system when not in use.
maintenance.description: Idle or seasonal equipment must be preserved and protected from contamination and corrosion.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of microbial or particulate contamination entering the system during storage
needs.plain: Keep germs and particles from getting into the system while it is stored. Proper storage helps keep the system clean and ready for use.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of contamination entering product-contact areas during idle storage
needs.plain: Keep unwanted material from reaching the parts that touch the product while the system is stored and not in use. This helps keep the system ready for safe production.

maintenance.name: Test Protective Systems
maintenance.statement: Test the plant's protective systems for correct operation.
maintenance.description: Periodic testing confirms that interlocks, alarms, emergency stops, guards, and pressure-protection devices remain functional.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of false-pass results concealing malfunctioning protective devices
needs.plain: Make sure safety devices cannot appear to pass a test when they are actually not working. This helps ensure that faulty protection is discovered.

maintenance.name: Test fluid-path integrity
maintenance.statement: Test the integrity of the skid's fluid pathways.
maintenance.description: Performing pressure hold, bubble point, or other integrity tests on the skid's fluid paths to confirm absence of leaks or breaches before and after use.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of an actual fluid-path breach remaining undetected after integrity testing
needs.plain: Make it less likely that a real break or leak in the liquid path goes unnoticed after testing. Detecting such a problem helps prevent contamination or loss of product.

maintenance.name: Test protective functions
maintenance.statement: Test the skid's protective functions.
maintenance.description: This confirms that alarms, interlocks, emergency stops, relief devices, and shutdown functions respond as intended.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that a defective protective function is recorded as passing during periodic testing

maintenance.name: Test safety interlocks
maintenance.statement: Test plant safety interlocks under controlled conditions.
maintenance.description: Periodic testing confirms that protective functions will respond correctly during abnormal operating conditions.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of safety interlocks remaining bypassed or incorrectly configured after testing
needs.plain: Make sure the equipment’s safety locks are fully restored and set correctly after they are tested. Otherwise, the equipment could operate without the protection they are meant to provide.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of safety interlocks that fail to initiate the required protective response during controlled testing
needs.plain: Make sure each safety device triggers the needed protective action when it is tested. This helps protect people and equipment when something goes wrong.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of an unintended hazardous or contamination event during controlled safety interlock testing
needs.plain: Testing safety devices should not accidentally cause a dangerous release, movement, or contamination. The test must be controlled so people and the process remain safe.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of malfunctioning emergency stops, guards, alarms, limit switches, or shutdown interlocks incorrectly recorded as passing the functional test
needs.plain: Avoid marking safety devices as passing when they are actually not working correctly. This includes emergency shutoffs, guards, warnings, position switches, and automatic shutdowns.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of required unsafe equipment states omitted from functional testing
needs.plain: Make sure testing includes every situation in which the equipment could become unsafe. This helps confirm that the safety shutoffs work when they are needed.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of leaving a tested protective control bypassed or incorrectly reset after functional testing
needs.plain: Make sure a safety feature that was tested is turned back on and set correctly afterward. This helps ensure the machine will respond safely if a dangerous condition occurs.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of required safety interlocks omitted from the periodic test scope
needs.plain: Make sure every required safety safeguard is included in the regular testing, so none are left unchecked.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of safety interlock tests performed under conditions that differ from the specified abnormal operating conditions
needs.plain: Perform safety tests under the same unusual but specified conditions they are meant to handle. This shows whether the protections will work during a real problem.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of an interlock falsely passing under conditions that do not represent its actual trip condition
Reduce the number of installed safety interlocks omitted from functional testing
Minimize the likelihood of introducing contamination into the sterile fill-finish area during functional testing
Reduce the duration of unplanned interruption to sterile fill-finish activity caused by functional testing

maintenance.name: Update control software
maintenance.statement: Update the skid's control system software and firmware.
maintenance.description: Matters to apply patches, recipe changes, and functionality improvements to the automation layer.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of unsafe or out-of-spec process control behavior after the updated control system returns to service
needs.plain: After new control software is put back into use, prevent the process from behaving unsafely or producing results outside the required limits.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that updated process logic drives critical process parameters outside validated limits
needs.plain: Make sure software changes do not make important process conditions go outside their approved safe ranges. This helps keep the process working safely and correctly.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that updated control logic produces out-of-spec batching conditions
needs.plain: Reduce the chance that changed control software causes the production mixture to fall outside its required limits. This helps keep the finished product consistent and acceptable.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that an interrupted update leaves plant control components on inconsistent versions
needs.plain: Make sure all parts of the plant control system end up with matching software if an update is interrupted. Mismatched software can make the parts work together incorrectly.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that updated control components cannot communicate with existing controllers or field devices
needs.plain: Make sure new control parts can exchange information with the older control parts and connected devices. Otherwise, the updated system may not operate correctly.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of validated control configurations, recipes, or alarm settings lost or altered during the update
needs.plain: Prevent the update from losing or changing approved machine settings, product instructions, or warning settings.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that a software update introduces control behavior outside the validated state
Reduce the number of validated control configurations or batch recipes altered during the software update
Minimize the likelihood that validated recipes, calibration parameters, or audit records are altered during the update
Minimize the likelihood that updated control software fails required compliance validation
Reduce the number of validated recipes and control settings overwritten unintentionally during the software update
Reduce the duration of sterile fill-finish production unavailability beyond the maintenance window
Reduce the duration of sterile fill-finish production unavailability beyond the scheduled maintenance window
Reduce the number of batching devices that lose communication with the control platform after the update
needs.plain: Keep mixing equipment from losing its connection to the control system after the software is updated. This helps the equipment continue communicating and operating as expected.

maintenance.name: Upgrade plant components
maintenance.statement: Upgrade individual components or modules of the processing plant.
maintenance.description: Aging components can be upgraded to increase capacity, efficiency, or compliance without full replacement.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of contamination entering sterile product-contact areas during the component upgrade
needs.plain: Prevent harmful contamination from entering areas that touch the sterile product while equipment parts are being replaced or improved. This helps keep the product safe and clean.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the upgraded component is incompatible with existing process connections, utilities, or controls
needs.plain: Make sure the replacement part works with the existing pipes, utilities, and control system. This helps the upgraded equipment operate safely and as intended.

needs.stmt: Reduce the frequency of leaks from disturbed process joints after the upgraded module returns to service
Reduce the duration of sterile fill-finish production downtime beyond the approved shutdown window
Minimize the likelihood that incomplete upgrade records block release of the plant module for sterile production
needs.plain: Make sure all records of the upgrades are complete, so the production unit can be approved for making germ-free products.

maintenance.name: Upgrade system components
maintenance.statement: Upgrade hardware components and automation modules on the batch system.
maintenance.description: Replace or add hardware, sensors, drives, or automation modules to improve or extend the capability of the batch preparation system.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that upgraded components cause unsafe or unintended system behavior
Minimize the likelihood that upgraded components are incompatible with existing mechanical, electrical, or control interfaces

maintenance.name: Validate sanitization
maintenance.statement: Verify and validate that sanitization of the plant meets required hygiene standards.
maintenance.description: Sanitization validation confirms cleaning cycles meet hygiene standards required for liquid products.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of residual contamination on product-contact surfaces escaping detection during sanitization validation
needs.plain: Make sure any leftover contamination on surfaces that touch the product is found during cleaning checks. This helps prevent unsafe product from being accepted.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of hygiene-critical plant locations omitted from sanitization validation
needs.plain: Make sure every plant area that must be especially clean is included when the cleaning process is checked. This helps prevent contamination from being missed in an overlooked area.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of validation samples compromised during collection or handling
Reduce the number of required validation records containing missing or inconsistent evidence
Reduce the duration of plant unavailability while sanitization validation results remain pending
needs.plain: Avoid keeping the plant unavailable while waiting for cleaning checks to be confirmed. This helps the facility return to work sooner after cleaning.

maintenance.name: Verify cleaning effectiveness
maintenance.statement: Verify the effectiveness of cleaning through sampling of the skid's wetted surfaces.
maintenance.description: Collecting swab, rinse, or environmental samples from the skid after cleaning to confirm residue levels are within validated acceptance criteria.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of required skid wetted-surface locations omitted from post-cleaning sampling

maintenance.name: Verify dosing accuracy
maintenance.statement: Verify the batch preparation system's dosing accuracy.
maintenance.description: Accuracy checks reveal drift or mechanical issues affecting feeders, pumps, dispensers, and metering devices.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of dosing drift remaining undetected during periodic accuracy checks
needs.plain: Make sure the amount being added has not gradually become inaccurate without anyone noticing during routine checks. Reliable amounts help keep the process consistent.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that intermittent dosing drift remains undetected during verification

maintenance.name: Verify process safety interlocks
maintenance.statement: Verify that process safety interlocks and shutdown systems operate correctly.
maintenance.description: Periodically test and verify that all process safety interlocks — overpressure relief, over-temperature shutdown, agitator overload trip — respond correctly to protect the plant and personnel.

needs.stmt: Reduce the rate of actual interlock faults incorrectly recorded as passing results during periodic verification
needs.plain: Reduce cases where a failed safety lock is mistakenly recorded as working during checks. This helps prevent unsafe equipment from being treated as safe.

preparation.name: Anchor and level equipment
preparation.statement: Anchor and level the plant skids and vessels to their mounting points.
preparation.description: Matters to ensure mechanical stability and proper drainage of viscous media once processing begins.

needs.stmt: Reduce the degree of deviation of plant skids and vessels from the specified level tolerance

preparation.name: Anchor equipment foundations
preparation.statement: Anchor the plant equipment to its foundations and mounts.
preparation.description: Foundations and mounts must hold heavy tanks and machinery against vibration and load.

needs.stmt: Minimize the amount of anchoring dust and debris entering sterile processing areas
needs.plain: As little dust and debris as possible should enter the clean work areas while equipment is being fixed in place. This helps keep those areas clean.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of anchorage losing restraint under operating vibration and load
needs.plain: Make sure the equipment's foundation fastenings stay secure under normal shaking and weight.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of cracking the foundation during anchor installation
needs.plain: Install the anchors without cracking the foundation that supports the equipment. A sound foundation keeps the equipment secure.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of equipment misalignment after anchors are secured
Reduce the duration of anchoring delay caused by foundation concrete being below required strength at the scheduled start

preparation.name: Assemble process modules
preparation.statement: Assemble the individual process modules into the complete plant configuration.
preparation.description: Mechanically join modular plant sections, skids, and sub-assemblies into the full equipment arrangement as specified in the plant layout drawings.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of contamination of exposed product-contact surfaces during module assembly
needs.plain: Prevent exposed surfaces that will touch the product from becoming dirty or contaminated while the parts are being put together. This helps keep the product safe and clean.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of misalignment between mating module interfaces during assembly
Reduce the number of intermodule joints assembled with incomplete fastening or sealing
Reduce the duration of assembly stoppages caused by missing or incompatible joining components
Reduce the number of modules assembled in positions or orientations that conflict with the plant layout drawings

preparation.name: Assemble shipped-loose components onto the skid
preparation.statement: Assemble the skid components supplied separately for transportation onto the skid structure.
preparation.description: A mechanical installer fits and fastens instrumentation, piping spools, panels, or other components that were removed or shipped loose to meet transport constraints.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of contamination entering exposed product-contact openings during skid assembly
needs.plain: Keep dirt and germs out of openings that will touch the product while parts are being attached. This helps protect the product during later operation.

preparation.name: Assemble system modules
preparation.statement: Assemble the supplied tanks, pumps, mixers, dispensers, sensors, and support modules into the complete batching system.
preparation.description: Mechanically assemble all major and ancillary system components — including structural supports, vessels, rotating equipment, instrumentation, and dispensing hardware — into the integrated system configuration.

needs.stmt: Reduce the degree of misalignment between vessel, agitator, piping, and skid interfaces
Minimize the likelihood of connecting vessel, agitator, piping, or skid modules in the wrong configuration
Reduce the degree of misalignment among vessels, rotating equipment, and dispensing hardware after assembly
Minimize the likelihood of structural supports or modules shifting or collapsing during mechanical assembly
needs.plain: Avoid allowing supports or parts of the system to move or fall during assembly. This helps prevent injuries and damage to the system.

needs.stmt: Reduce the amount of particulate or microbial contamination introduced into product-contact areas during assembly
needs.plain: Keep dust and germs from getting onto surfaces that will touch the product while the parts are being put together. This helps protect the product from contamination.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of sterile-contact surfaces or seals damaged during handling and assembly
needs.plain: Avoid damaging surfaces or seals that must stay free of germs while putting the system parts together. This helps prevent contamination.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of supplied modules with mechanical interfaces that do not mate to adjoining modules as specified
needs.plain: Avoid receiving system parts whose connecting pieces do not fit together as planned. This helps the system go together and work properly.

preparation.name: Calibrate measurement instruments
preparation.statement: Calibrate the system's measurement instruments for accurate batch control.
preparation.description: Sensors for weight, temperature, level, and flow must be calibrated so batch accuracy meets specification.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of introducing contamination into sterile product-contact areas during calibration
needs.plain: Prevent germs from entering areas that touch the product while measuring equipment is being adjusted. This keeps the product protected during the adjustment.
needs.role: Instrumentation technicians, maintenance engineers, quality personnel.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of contaminating sterile product-contact areas during calibration
needs.plain: Adjusting the measuring tools should be as unlikely as possible to dirty clean surfaces that touch the product. This helps keep the product safe from contamination.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of residual deviation between instrument readings and reference-standard values after calibration
needs.plain: After adjustment, make sure the instrument's readings are as close as possible to trusted correct values.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of calibrating an instrument against an expired or untraceable reference standard
needs.plain: Make sure the instrument is checked against a trusted reference that is still valid and can be traced to its source.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of batch-control instruments remaining outside specified tolerance after calibration
needs.plain: Make sure the instruments used to control a production batch give readings within the allowed range after they are checked and adjusted. Accurate readings help keep the batch under control.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of measurement instruments remaining outside specified tolerance after calibration
needs.plain: Make sure measuring devices give readings within the allowed range after they are checked and adjusted. Accurate readings are important for making reliable decisions.

needs.stmt: Reduce the time the measurement device remains in service beyond its approved calibration interval
needs.role: Instrumentation technicians, maintenance engineers, quality personnel.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of calibration records assigned to the wrong measurement device or reference standard
needs.role: Instrumentation technicians, maintenance engineers, quality personnel.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of deviation from the specified tolerance after calibration
needs.role: Instrumentation technicians, maintenance engineers, quality personnel.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the required reference standard is outside its certification validity period when calibration begins
needs.role: Instrumentation technicians, maintenance engineers, quality personnel.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of instruments calibrated using incorrect range or tolerance requirements
Reduce the duration of scheduled production disruption caused by instruments remaining unavailable for calibration

preparation.name: Calibrate skid instrumentation at installation
preparation.statement: Calibrate the skid's sensors, gauges, and measurement instruments against applicable reference standards before first operation.
preparation.description: Performs initial calibration of all skid instrumentation as part of the installation and qualification sequence, establishing traceable baselines prior to IQ/OQ execution.
preparation.userGroup: Metrology, instrumentation, and quality teams executing the installation qualification.
preparation.frequency: Once per installation; repeated after any instrument replacement or major maintenance event.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of skid instruments remaining outside specified calibration tolerance before qualification testing
needs.role: Metrology, instrumentation, and quality teams executing the installation qualification.

preparation.name: Clean product-contact surfaces
preparation.statement: Clean the tanks, lines, pumps, mixers, valves, and dispensing points before introducing batch materials.
preparation.description: Pre-use cleaning removes residues or contaminants that could compromise the first subsequent batch.

needs.stmt: Minimize the amount of cleaning-agent residue remaining on product-contact surfaces after cleaning
needs.plain: As little cleaning liquid as possible should remain on surfaces that touch the product after cleaning. This helps prevent the product from being exposed to cleaning chemicals.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of recontaminating cleaned product-contact surfaces before batch materials are introduced
needs.plain: Keep cleaned surfaces that touch the product from getting dirty again before the materials are added. This helps keep the product free from unwanted contamination.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of cleaned product-contact surfaces becoming recontaminated before processing resumes
needs.plain: Keep cleaned surfaces that touch the product from becoming dirty again before work starts. This helps keep the product safe.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of cleaned product-contact surfaces with product residue or deposits remaining
needs.plain: Leave as little product buildup as possible on surfaces that touch the product after cleaning. This helps prevent old material from affecting the next batch.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of product-contact components omitted from cleaning
needs.plain: Clean every surface and part that touches the product, without leaving any out. This helps prevent leftover material or contamination from affecting the next product.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of introducing microbial contamination onto product-contact surfaces during cleaning
needs.plain: Prevent germs from getting onto surfaces that touch the product while those surfaces are being cleaned. This helps keep the product safe.

needs.stmt: Reduce the amount of residual viscous product left on product-contact surfaces and inside vessels after cleaning
needs.plain: Remove as much thick leftover product as possible from surfaces and containers after cleaning. This helps prevent old product from remaining in the equipment.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of product-contact surface area retaining previous-batch residue after cleaning
needs.plain: Remove as much leftover material from product-touching surfaces as possible, so residue from the previous batch does not affect the next one.

needs.stmt: Reduce the amount of cleaning-agent residue remaining in tanks, lines, pumps, mixers, valves, and dispensing points after cleaning
needs.plain: As little cleaning liquid as possible should remain in the tanks, pipes, pumps, mixing equipment, valves, and points where product is dispensed. This helps prevent the cleaning liquid from getting into the product.

needs.stmt: Minimize the amount of damage to product-contact surfaces caused during cleaning
needs.plain: Avoid damaging the surfaces that touch the product while cleaning them. This helps keep the equipment usable and prevents harm to the product.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of batch-start delay when product-contact surfaces do not meet pre-use acceptance criteria

preparation.name: Commission system with trial batch
preparation.statement: Run a trial batch to commission and validate the batch preparation system before production use.
preparation.description: Commissioning technician executes a qualification trial batch to verify that all system functions — mixing, temperature, dosing, control — perform within specification under representative process conditions.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of mixing, temperature, dosing, or control results outside approved specifications in the trial batch
Reduce the percentage of critical trial-batch results falling outside specification

preparation.name: Commission system with validation batch
preparation.statement: Commission the assembled system by running a validation batch to verify correct operation before production use.
preparation.description: Execute a controlled test or validation batch through the fully assembled and configured system to confirm that all components, controls, interlocks, and measurements perform to specification.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of contamination entering the product-contact path during the validation batch
Reduce the number of critical batch parameters outside approved limits during the validation batch
Minimize the degree of deviation between the executed batch sequence and the approved sequence during the validation batch
Minimize the likelihood that an interlock fails to halt the assembled system when a hazardous condition occurs during the validation batch
Reduce the number of required commissioning records missing after the validation batch

preparation.name: Commission the plant
preparation.statement: Commission and test the plant to verify readiness for production.
preparation.description: Execute the full commissioning sequence — wet trials, functional tests, performance verification — to confirm the installed plant meets design intent before handover to operations.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of contaminating product-contact surfaces or trial media during wet trials
Minimize the duration of commissioning delay caused by required plant utilities being unavailable or outside specification during scheduled testing
Reduce the number of commissioning results invalidated by missing or expired calibration records
Reduce the number of safety-critical functions that fail functional tests before plant handover
Reduce the number of specified performance acceptance criteria the installed plant fails during performance verification

preparation.name: Commission the plant with acceptance testing
preparation.statement: Commission the plant by conducting trial runs and verifying performance against defined acceptance criteria.
preparation.description: Structured commissioning activities including media trials, process function verification, and documented acceptance testing to confirm the plant meets its performance specification before handover to operations.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree to which trial-run results fall outside defined plant specifications during acceptance testing
needs.plain: Keep the plant’s trial results within the agreed limits during final testing. This helps show that the plant is working as expected before it is accepted.

preparation.name: Configure batch parameters
preparation.statement: Configure the operating parameters and control settings for the intended batch process.
preparation.description: Operating parameters such as mixing speed, temperature, dosing sequence, and batch size must be set before production.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of batch setpoints configured outside approved ranges
needs.plain: Reduce the number of batch settings entered outside the allowed ranges. This helps the batch run under the conditions that were approved for it.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of critical setpoints configured outside approved batch values
needs.plain: Fewer important machine settings should be entered outside the approved ranges for the batch. This helps the batch be made correctly and safely.

preparation.name: Configure batch recipes
preparation.statement: Configure the system-specific quantities, sequences, mixing times, temperatures, and dosing rates for each batch recipe in the control system.
preparation.description: Combines configuring batch parameters (#30 — recipes, fill volumes, mixing sequences) with configuring batch recipes (#36 — quantities, sequences, mixing times, temperatures, dosing rates). Both describe the same preparation activity at equivalent specificity.
preparation.userGroup: Process engineers, system operators, batch technicians.
preparation.frequency: Once at commissioning; repeated when new recipes are added.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of ingredient quantity values distorted by unit or scaling errors
needs.plain: Avoid entering ingredient amounts incorrectly because of confusing measurement units or changes in scale, so each batch has the right ingredients.
needs.role: Process engineers, system operators, batch technicians.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of recipe steps omitted or placed out of order in the configured sequence
needs.role: Process engineers, system operators, batch technicians.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of configured temperature, mixing, or ingredient-delivery setpoints outside validated operating limits
needs.role: Process engineers, system operators, batch technicians.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of configuring an obsolete recipe revision for production use
needs.role: Process engineers, system operators, batch technicians.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of required recipe fields left blank before recipe release
needs.role: Process engineers, system operators, batch technicians.

preparation.name: Configure control sequences
preparation.statement: Configure the plant control logic for the intended processing sequences.
preparation.description: This prepares automated equipment to execute the required startup, processing, transfer, cleaning, and shutdown steps.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of deviation between configured phase settings and approved processing requirements
needs.plain: Make sure the settings for each processing step match the approved requirements. This helps the process run as intended.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that a sequence advances before required equipment states and process conditions are satisfied
Minimize the likelihood that cleaning and processing sequences permit residue carryover into the sterile product path
Minimize the likelihood that fault handling leaves the plant unable to resume or shut down safely
Reduce the number of required sequence steps with ambiguous or conflicting requirements

preparation.name: Configure control system
preparation.statement: Configure the plant's control system and process parameters.
preparation.description: The control system governs sequencing, dosing and monitoring of the process.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the fill-finish sequence advances before required sterile conditions are established
Reduce the number of critical alarm or interlock conditions omitted or configured incorrectly
Reduce the number of process sensors or actuators assigned to the wrong control points
Reduce the number of process parameters with conflicting approved values
Reduce the percentage of dosing setpoints configured outside approved formulation ranges

preparation.name: Configure safety interlocks
preparation.statement: Configure the plant safety interlocks for the installed equipment arrangement.
preparation.description: This prepares protective responses for unsafe pressure, temperature, flow, access, level, or equipment states.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that a hazardous skid condition fails to trigger a protective interlock
needs.plain: Make sure a dangerous equipment condition automatically starts the needed protective action. This helps protect people and equipment when something goes wrong.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of deviation between configured trip thresholds and approved safety limits
needs.plain: Keep the settings that trigger automatic safety actions aligned with the approved safety limits. This helps the system react at the right time when conditions become unsafe.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of hazardous equipment states left without a configured protective response
Reduce the number of interlock bypasses remaining active at plant handover
Reduce the frequency of unintended plant trips caused by the configured interlock logic
Reduce the number of unsafe conditions linked to the wrong protective responses for the installed equipment arrangement

preparation.name: Configure skid setpoints and operating parameters
preparation.statement: Configure the skid's setpoints and operating parameters for the intended process.
preparation.description: A process engineer or operator enters the target values for temperature, pressure, flow, and other controlled variables into the skid's control system ahead of operation.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of skid setpoints configured outside validated ranges for the sterile fill-finish campaign
needs.plain: Make sure the equipment settings stay within the approved limits for the sterile production run. This helps keep the process safe and consistent.

preparation.name: Connect control networks
preparation.statement: Connect the plant control devices to the designated communications network.
preparation.description: This enables coordinated control, monitoring, diagnostics, and production-data exchange across connected equipment.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of introducing contamination into a classified sterile fill-finish area while connecting control devices
Reduce the number of plant control devices connected to an incorrect designated network segment
Reduce the percentage of connected plant control devices unable to exchange required control, monitoring, diagnostic, or production data
Reduce the rate of communication dropouts between connected control devices during project commissioning
needs.plain: Make communication interruptions between connected control devices less frequent while the project is being started and checked. This helps the devices work together reliably.

needs.stmt: Reduce the time plant control devices remain unconnected because network assignment and connection requirements are missing or conflicting
needs.plain: Spend less time leaving plant control devices disconnected because their network settings and connection instructions are missing or disagree with each other. Clear requirements help the devices connect correctly.

preparation.name: Connect electrical power
preparation.statement: Connect the processing plant to its electrical power supply.
preparation.description: Run power cables, install disconnects, and terminate all electrical power connections for plant drive motors, heaters, and control panels per electrical specifications.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that incoming electrical supply characteristics are incompatible with plant electrical specifications
Minimize the likelihood that the electrical technician contacts an energized conductor while making plant power connections
Reduce the percentage of plant power conductors terminated at incorrect terminals
Reduce the number of unresolved conflicts or omissions in electrical specifications needed to complete plant power connections
Reduce the percentage of routed plant power cables with insulation or conductor damage
needs.plain: Make sure the power cables that are installed have no damage to their protective covering or inner wires. This matters so electricity can be connected safely and reliably.

preparation.name: Connect fluid piping
preparation.statement: Connect the inlet, outlet, and recirculation piping to the batching system.
preparation.description: Fluid pathways must be joined so ingredients can be transferred through the system.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of connecting an inlet, outlet, or recirculation line to the incorrect batching-system port
Reduce the amount of unintended residual fluid trapped at piping connections after transfer or cleaning
Minimize the likelihood of fluid leaking from inlet, outlet, or recirculation piping joints during transfer
Minimize the likelihood of introducing contamination into the sterile-product fluid pathway while making piping connections
needs.plain: Keep harmful or unwanted material out of the path that carries the sterile product while the pipes are joined. This helps keep the product safe from contamination.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of damaging batching-system nozzles or piping seals while aligning and securing the piping
needs.plain: Avoid damaging the machine's pipe ends or seals while lining up and fastening the pipes. This helps prevent leaks and equipment problems.

preparation.name: Connect power and utility supplies
preparation.statement: Connect the batching system to its required electrical, pneumatic, and other utility supply sources.
preparation.description: Terminate and verify all electrical, pneumatic, and ancillary utility connections needed to energise the system, following applicable codes and specifications.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of a contaminated utility supply entering the batching system after hook-up
Reduce the number of leaks at pneumatic or ancillary utility connections identified during verification
Minimize the likelihood of personnel injury or batching-system damage during initial energization
needs.plain: Reduce the chance that people get hurt or the mixing equipment is damaged when power and other services are turned on for the first time. This helps make the startup safe.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of deviation of connected utility supplies from specified voltage, pressure, flow, and quality requirements
needs.plain: Make sure the incoming electricity and other services meet the required levels for power, pressure, amount, and cleanliness. This helps the machine run safely and reliably.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of hook-up delay caused by unavailable required utility sources or inaccessible connection points
needs.plain: Spend less time waiting to connect power and other needed services when the sources or connection points cannot be reached. This helps the equipment be set up sooner.

preparation.name: Connect process piping
preparation.statement: Connect the system to the required material inlet, transfer, recirculation, and discharge piping.
preparation.description: Install and connect all process fluid piping runs — including inlet feeds, inter-vessel transfer lines, recirculation loops, and discharge headers — between the batching system and plant infrastructure.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of unintended mechanical load transferred from misaligned piping to plant-component connections
Minimize the likelihood of connecting a batch preparation system inlet or outlet to the wrong process line
Minimize the likelihood of connecting inlet or outlet piping to the wrong plant line
Reduce the number of process piping runs connected to incorrect system or plant ports
Reduce the number of completed piping connections containing unintended crevices or stagnant pockets
Reduce the number of process lines connected to incorrect plant-component ports
Minimize the likelihood of introducing contaminants onto product-contact surfaces while connecting process piping
Reduce the number of completed process-piping joints that leak during integrity testing
Minimize the likelihood of process fluid leaking from completed piping connections during testing or startup
Reduce the amount of mechanical stress imposed on system connection points by misaligned piping
Minimize the likelihood of foreign material entering product-contact piping during installation
needs.plain: Keep dust, debris, and other unwanted material out of the product-carrying pipes while they are being installed. This helps protect the product from contamination.

needs.stmt: Reduce the time the pipefitting technician waits for specified compatible fittings, valves, or flexible connections
needs.plain: Provide the correct matching connectors, shutoff parts, and flexible tubing quickly so the pipes can be connected.

preparation.name: Connect process piping between unit operations
preparation.statement: Connect process piping, manifolds, and fittings between plant unit operations.
preparation.description: Mechanical assembly and connection of all in-process piping, manifolds, hoses, and sanitary fittings that route viscous media between the processing plant's unit operations.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of leakage from sanitary joints under specified operating conditions
needs.plain: Make it unlikely that liquid will leak from the cleanable pipe connections while the system is operating. Tight connections help prevent spills and contamination.

preparation.name: Connect utility services
preparation.statement: Connect the batch preparation system to electrical power, process fluids, compressed air, ventilation, and drainage utilities.
preparation.description: Technician establishes all required utility connections — power, fluid supply, pneumatics, ventilation, and drainage — to bring the system to an operable state.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of contaminants entering clean utility paths during connection work
needs.plain: Prevent dirt or other unwanted material from getting into clean supply lines while connections are being made. This helps keep those supplies suitable for use.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree to which supplied utility conditions deviate from plant interface requirements
needs.plain: Make sure the supplied services, such as power or air, meet the conditions required by the plant equipment.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of utility lines connected to incorrect skid interfaces during installation
Minimize the degree of mismatch between facility utility characteristics and system connection requirements
Reduce the number of utility lines connected to the wrong service or system connection point
Minimize the likelihood that a utility line is connected to the wrong plant interface during installation
Minimize the likelihood of leakage from a completed plant utility connection
Minimize the likelihood that a facility utility tie-in is incompatible with its corresponding plant interface at installation

preparation.name: Connect utility services to the plant
preparation.statement: Connect the plant to required utility services including power, steam, cooling water, and compressed air.
preparation.description: Termination and connection of all utility supply and return lines — electrical power, process steam, cooling water, and instrument air — to the plant's designated utility interfaces.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of deviation between utility supply conditions and plant interface requirements

preparation.name: Document system configuration
preparation.statement: Document the as-installed configuration, calibration records, and parameter settings of the batching system.
preparation.description: Capture and record the final installed configuration — including pipe routing, instrument calibration certificates, control parameter settings, recipe data, and access permission structures — as the baseline reference for future maintenance, audits, and regulatory review.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that final commissioning changes are omitted from the baseline documentation
Reduce the number of control parameter and recipe values recorded incorrectly in the baseline documentation
Reduce the percentage of required instrument calibration certificates missing from the closeout documentation
Reduce the number of documented pipe routes or instrument connections that differ from the physical installation
Minimize the degree of ambiguity in the documented access permission structures
needs.plain: Clearly record who is allowed to access the system and what each person is allowed to do. This helps prevent unauthorized changes or use.

preparation.name: Document the as-built plant configuration
preparation.statement: Document the as-built configuration of the installed processing plant for operational and regulatory records.
preparation.description: Creation of as-built drawings, instrument loop diagrams, equipment datasheets, and configuration records that accurately reflect the plant as physically installed and commissioned, for use in operations, maintenance, and regulatory compliance.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of documented plant components whose configuration differs from the physical installation

preparation.name: Establish cleaning-in-place or washdown procedures
preparation.statement: Establish and document the cleaning-in-place or washdown procedure for the batch preparation system prior to initial production.
preparation.description: Technician or process engineer defines, validates, and documents the step-by-step cleaning procedure — chemical selection, concentrations, contact times, temperatures, rinse cycles — appropriate to the system's materials and intended product, ensuring hygienic readiness before first use.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that prescribed cleaning parameters leave residues above acceptance limits on product-contact surfaces
needs.plain: Make sure the required cleaning process removes enough leftover material from surfaces that touch the product. This helps prevent residue from getting into the product.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of product-contact locations omitted from the documented cleaning procedure
needs.plain: Make sure every place that touches the product is included in the written cleaning instructions. Nothing that could affect the product should be missed.

preparation.name: Establish operating procedures
preparation.statement: Establish standard operating procedures and workflows for the skid.
preparation.description: Matters because regulated production requires documented, repeatable operating workflows.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of critical aseptic controls omitted from skid operating procedures

preparation.name: Establish operator access and permission levels
preparation.statement: Establish operator access credentials and permission levels within the batch preparation system's control platform.
preparation.description: Administrator creates user accounts, assigns role-based access rights, and configures authentication controls within the control system to ensure only authorized personnel can execute or modify recipes, parameters, and safety settings.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of operator accounts assigned permissions beyond approved duties
Reduce the number of operators assigned permissions beyond their approved duties

preparation.name: Establish startup and shutdown operating procedures
preparation.statement: Establish written startup and shutdown procedures for the processing plant based on its installed configuration.
preparation.description: Development and documentation of step-by-step startup and shutdown operating procedures specific to the installed plant configuration, to guide operators in safe and repeatable pre-production preparation.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of startup and shutdown steps ordered contrary to plant dependencies

preparation.name: Establish user access
preparation.statement: Establish user accounts and access permissions for the control system.
preparation.description: Access levels and user credentials control who can edit recipes and operate the system.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that authorized users cannot perform assigned control-system tasks because required permissions are missing
Minimize the likelihood of assigning recipe-editing permissions beyond approved responsibilities
Reduce the number of control-system accounts issued with shared or non-unique credentials
Reduce the duration of account setup stalled by incomplete or conflicting access approvals
needs.plain: Set up a person’s account quickly instead of letting missing or conflicting approvals hold it up. This allows the person to begin their authorized work sooner.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of newly established accounts with credentials that fail at initial sign-in
needs.plain: Make sure newly created user accounts work the first time the users sign in. This lets people begin their work without access problems.

preparation.name: Import process recipes
preparation.statement: Import approved process recipes into the plant control system.
preparation.description: This prepares recipe-driven plants with reusable parameter sets for applicable liquid products.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that an obsolete or unintended recipe revision is imported into the plant control system
Reduce the number of imported recipe parameters assigned to incorrect plant control fields
Minimize the likelihood that an approved recipe is rejected because its structure does not meet plant control system import requirements
Reduce the percentage of imported recipe parameter values that differ from the approved source values
Reduce the number of required recipe steps, phases, or parameters omitted during import

preparation.name: Import recipe data
preparation.statement: Import existing recipes and process data into the system's control software.
preparation.description: Existing formulations, batch records, and process data are imported so historical recipes are available.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of imported critical process parameters that differ from source records
needs.plain: Reduce the number of important process settings that change when they are copied into the system from the original records. This helps ensure the process runs with the intended settings.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of imported recipes containing incorrect parameter values or units
Minimize the percentage of imported formulation quantities altered by incorrect unit conversion or decimal precision handling
Minimize the likelihood that obsolete or unapproved recipe versions are designated as current after import
Reduce the number of recipe components assigned to incorrect batching-system fields during import
Reduce the number of imported recipe and product records with required formulation or product fields missing
Reduce the duration of import delay caused by source records that cannot be interpreted

preparation.name: Install and anchor system
preparation.statement: Install the batch preparation system in its production location and anchor it to the floor or structure.
preparation.description: The system must be physically positioned, leveled, and anchored within the production area before operation.

needs.stmt: Reduce the degree of deviation from level after the batch preparation system is anchored
Minimize the degree of deviation from required level tolerance after anchoring

preparation.name: Install control software
preparation.statement: Install the required control software on the batch preparation system's computing infrastructure.
preparation.description: Administrator or technician installs, licenses, and performs initial configuration of the control software platform on the system's dedicated hardware prior to recipe and parameter setup.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the control software is incompatible with dedicated computing infrastructure
Minimize the likelihood that an interrupted installation leaves control software partially deployed

preparation.name: Install process instrumentation
preparation.statement: Install and wire the plant's process instrumentation and sensors.
preparation.description: Mount, pipe, and wire all flow, temperature, pressure, level, and analytical instruments on the plant per the instrument loop diagrams.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of instrument process connections leaking into or out of the sterile process boundary
Reduce the number of instrument wiring terminations that do not match the instrument loop diagrams
Reduce the number of instruments mounted at process locations or orientations inconsistent with the plant design
Reduce the number of instruments damaged during mounting, piping, or wiring
Reduce the time installation is stalled by unavailable or incompatible instruments, fittings, or cables
needs.plain: Spend less time waiting for the right tools, parts, and cables to be available and fit properly. This helps installation work continue without avoidable stops.

preparation.name: Install single-use assemblies onto the skid
preparation.statement: Install disposable bags, tubing sets, and single-use assemblies onto the skid before operation.
preparation.description: An operator or process technician mounts and connects single-use product-contact components — including biocontainer bags, manifolds, and tubing — to the skid per the assembly drawing.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of breaching a sterile barrier while handling or mounting product-contact assemblies
needs.plain: Avoid damaging the protective germ-free covering while attaching the parts that will touch the product. This helps keep the product clean and safe.

preparation.name: Integrate control system with plant network
preparation.statement: Integrate the batch preparation system's controls and instrumentation with the plant control and data network.
preparation.description: Integration specialist configures communication links, I/O mapping, and data exchange protocols to connect the system's control architecture to the plant-wide control, historian, or MES network.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of batch records transferred to plant systems with missing or incorrect values
Minimize the likelihood that required batch data fail to reach plant control, historian, or MES destinations

preparation.name: Integrate with control system
preparation.statement: Integrate the skid controls with the facility's automation and data historian systems.
preparation.description: Matters because centralized monitoring and batch records depend on control system connectivity.
