needs.stmt: Reduce the frequency of incorrect safety-critical command and status exchanges between skid controls and facility automation

preparation.name: Integrate with external business systems
preparation.statement: Integrate the batch preparation system with designated inventory, quality, production scheduling, or enterprise resource planning systems.
preparation.description: Integration specialist establishes data connections and exchange workflows between the batch preparation system and external plant-level or enterprise-level systems to enable automated data sharing and process coordination.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of batch, material, or status fields mapped incorrectly between designated systems
Reduce the percentage of critical batch, material, or quality data mapped to incorrect external-system fields

preparation.name: Integrate with plant systems
preparation.statement: Integrate the plant with existing enterprise and supervisory systems.
preparation.description: Integrating with plant-wide systems enables coordinated data and production management.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of an external command being mapped to the wrong critical plant function
needs.plain: Prevent an outside instruction from triggering the wrong important plant action. This helps avoid unsafe or damaging operations.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of mismatch between plant data definitions and those of existing enterprise and supervisory systems
Reduce the duration of sterile fill-finish production disruption during integration cutover
Reduce the number of plant records transferred without required batch and source context
Reduce the time integration work waits for required access permissions and interface details
needs.plain: Spend less time waiting for permission to access plant systems and for the information needed to connect to them. This helps the systems work together sooner.

preparation.name: Map ingredient sources
preparation.statement: Map each ingredient source to its corresponding tank, line, pump, valve, or dosing channel.
preparation.description: Accurate source mapping prevents control commands from routing or dispensing the wrong liquid.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of ingredient sources mapped to an incorrect tank, line, pump, valve, or dosing channel
needs.plain: Make sure each ingredient source is connected to the correct equipment. This prevents an ingredient from being sent to the wrong place.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of ingredient-source routes with one or more required assets omitted
Reduce the number of ingredient-source associations that conflict across piping, equipment, and control records
Reduce the number of ingredient-source mappings left obsolete after design changes
Reduce the number of ingredient sources that cannot be uniquely identified from project records and physical connections
needs.plain: Make sure every ingredient source can be identified as one specific source using the project records and the actual connections.

preparation.name: Map ingredient sources to system inputs
preparation.statement: Map ingredient sources to the corresponding system inputs and storage locations.
preparation.description: Process engineer identifies and documents which raw material supply line, tank, or container feeds each system inlet or ingredient addition port, ensuring correct material routing before batch production.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of ingredient sources mapped to incorrect system inputs or storage locations
Reduce the number of ingredient sources assigned to the wrong system inlet or storage location

preparation.name: Perform installation and operational qualification tests
preparation.statement: Perform installation qualification and operational qualification tests on the assembled skid.
preparation.description: A validation engineer executes IQ/OQ protocols to confirm that the skid was installed correctly and operates within its specified parameters, generating documentation for regulatory submission.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of installed skid attributes that deviate from approved specifications during qualification testing

preparation.name: Perform pre-startup risk assessment
preparation.statement: Perform a pre-startup safety review of the installed skid before first operation.
preparation.description: A safety or process engineer conducts a formal pre-startup safety review (PSSR) to confirm that all installation, documentation, training, and interlock requirements are satisfied before the skid is placed into service.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that safety-critical deviations from the approved skid design remain undetected before first operation
needs.plain: Find any safety-important differences between the approved equipment design and what was actually built before starting it for the first time. Undetected differences could create unexpected hazards.

preparation.name: Position and mount equipment
preparation.statement: Position and mount the liquid batching system components at the designated installation location.
preparation.description: Place and secure tanks, pumps, metering hardware, and support structures in their specified positions, accounting for floor loading, access, and process flow.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of liquid-contact surfaces or open process connections becoming contaminated during positioning and mounting
Reduce the number of required operator or maintenance access clearances obstructed by mounted components
Minimize the likelihood that the installation foundation cannot safely support the batching system loads
needs.plain: Make sure the floor or base under the mixing system is strong and safe enough to carry its weight and operating loads. This helps prevent movement, damage, or collapse.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of tanks, pumps, and metering hardware positioned outside specified locations or orientations
needs.plain: Place tanks, pumps, and measuring equipment in the required positions and directions. This helps the equipment work correctly and safely.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of component mounting interfaces being misaligned with prepared foundation anchors
needs.plain: Make sure the equipment’s attachment points line up with the anchors already set in the foundation. Misalignment could make installation difficult or leave the equipment unstable.

preparation.name: Position plant equipment
preparation.statement: Position the processing plant equipment in its designated operating location.
preparation.description: Move and precisely locate major plant equipment items on the prepared foundation or floor area according to the layout plan.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of equipment tipping or shifting while being moved into its designated operating location
needs.plain: Move the equipment into place without letting it tip over or slide. Keeping it stable protects people, equipment, and the surrounding area.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of damage to the processing plant equipment during positioning
needs.plain: Move the equipment into place without damaging it or other parts of the processing plant. Careful positioning helps keep the plant working safely.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of encountering an obstruction or insufficient clearance while moving the equipment to its designated location
Minimize the degree to which the final equipment location or orientation deviates from the layout requirements
Minimize the likelihood that the prepared floor area cannot support the equipment load during positioning

preparation.name: Position plant modules on prepared foundations
preparation.statement: Position plant vessels, skids, and process modules on prepared foundations according to the approved installation layout.
preparation.description: Rigging and placement of processing plant vessels, skids, and modular units onto their prepared civil foundations or structural supports in accordance with the installation drawing.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of deviation between the final module position and the approved installation layout

preparation.name: Position system within operating space
preparation.statement: Position the batch preparation system within its designated operating space according to layout and access requirements.
preparation.description: Installer maneuvers and positions the system into its final location, accounting for clearance, workflow access, maintenance access, and facility layout constraints.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of required operator or maintenance access paths blocked by the final system position
Reduce the degree to which the final position violates workflow or maintenance clearances

preparation.name: Prepare installation site
preparation.statement: Prepare the installation site to receive the liquid product processing plant.
preparation.description: Site conditions determine whether the plant can be safely installed and operated.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree to which mounting surfaces and load-bearing provisions deviate from the plant installation specifications
Minimize the amount of construction debris or contamination remaining in the installation area at handover
Minimize the likelihood that the plant delivery path or installation envelope is obstructed at arrival
Reduce the number of required utility connections unavailable or outside specified conditions when installation begins
Reduce the number of required site approvals or safety clearances incomplete when installation begins

preparation.name: Prime fluid circuits
preparation.statement: Prime the required pumps, dosing lines, valves, and transfer circuits with the designated liquid.
preparation.description: Priming removes trapped air and prepares fluid-handling components for consistent delivery.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of introducing a liquid other than the designated liquid into a required circuit during priming
needs.plain: Put only the intended liquid into the required pipes and equipment when filling them for use. This helps prevent the process from being contaminated or disrupted by the wrong liquid.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of introducing microbial or particulate contamination into product-contact circuits during priming
needs.plain: Prevent germs and small particles from getting into the parts that carry the product while they are being filled with liquid. This helps keep the product clean and safe.

needs.stmt: Minimize the percentage of required circuit volume occupied by trapped air after priming
needs.plain: Make sure there is as little trapped air as possible in the fluid lines after they are filled. This helps the lines work as intended and prevents air from disrupting the process.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of liquid flow interruption caused by an obstructed or incorrectly aligned required circuit during priming
needs.plain: Restore liquid flow quickly when a required path is blocked or set up incorrectly during startup, so the system can begin working without a long delay.

needs.stmt: Reduce the amount of designated liquid released from circuit connections during priming

preparation.name: Prime ingredient feeds
preparation.statement: Prime and charge the ingredient feed lines and supply tanks.
preparation.description: Raw ingredient supply tanks and feed lines must be primed and charged before the first batch.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of charging the wrong ingredient into a supply tank or feed line
needs.plain: Make sure the correct ingredient is put into the supply tank or feed line. Using the wrong ingredient could affect the product being made.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of introducing microbial or particulate contamination into the ingredient feed lines and supply tanks during priming and charging
needs.plain: Reduce the chance that germs or unwanted particles enter the ingredient pipes and supply tanks while they are being filled and prepared for use. This helps keep the ingredients clean.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of deviation from the required ingredient charge level in the feed lines and supply tanks
needs.plain: Keep the amount of ingredient entering the supply lines and tanks as close as possible to the required amount, so the process starts with the right quantity.

needs.stmt: Reduce the degree of residual air trapped in the ingredient feed lines after priming
needs.plain: Remove as much trapped air as possible from the ingredient tubes before use. This helps ensure the right amount of ingredient moves through the tubes.

needs.stmt: Reduce the amount of raw ingredient lost through leaks while charging the feed lines and supply tanks

preparation.name: Prime pumps and lines
preparation.statement: Prime the plant's pumps and transfer lines with the viscous media.
preparation.description: Matters before production to fill high-resistance lines and prevent cavitation or dead spots at startup.

needs.stmt: Reduce the frequency of pump cavitation caused by retained air during production startup

preparation.name: Program batch recipes
preparation.statement: Program batch recipes and process sequences into the system's control platform.
preparation.description: Operator or process engineer encodes batch recipes — ingredient quantities, addition sequences, agitation profiles, timing, and endpoints — into the control system prior to production use.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of deviation between programmed recipe parameters and the approved specification
needs.plain: Make the programmed production settings match the approved instructions as closely as possible. This helps the process produce the intended result safely and consistently.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of programmed ingredient quantities differing from approved recipe values
needs.plain: Reduce mistakes in the amounts of ingredients entered into the recipe compared with the approved amounts. This helps each batch use the intended ingredients and quantities.

preparation.name: Sanitize and passivate
preparation.statement: Sanitize, passivate, and prepare product-contact surfaces before first use.
preparation.description: Matters because biopharma surfaces must be cleaned and passivated to prevent contamination and corrosion.

needs.stmt: Minimize the percentage of product-contact surfaces retaining microbial contamination after sanitization
needs.plain: Reduce the amount of harmful living contamination left on surfaces that touch the product after cleaning and sanitizing. Cleaner surfaces help protect the product from contamination.

preparation.name: Sanitize process surfaces
preparation.statement: Clean and sanitize the plant's product-contact surfaces before first production use.
preparation.description: Perform initial cleaning, rinsing, and sanitization of all product-contact surfaces to remove manufacturing residues and verify hygienic readiness before the first production run.

needs.stmt: Minimize the amount of manufacturing residue remaining on product-contact surfaces after initial cleaning
needs.plain: Remove as much manufacturing residue as possible from surfaces that touch the product during the first cleaning. This helps prepare the surfaces for sanitizing.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of disagreement among commissioning records about which product-contact surfaces require sanitation
Minimize the likelihood of sanitized product-contact surfaces becoming recontaminated between hygienic-readiness verification and first product introduction
Reduce the number of sampled product-contact locations exceeding microbial acceptance limits before first production use
Reduce the percent of product-contact surface area missed during initial cleaning and sanitization

preparation.name: Set operating limits
preparation.statement: Set the system limits for capacity, pressure, temperature, flow, timing, and dosing tolerance.
preparation.description: Operating limits constrain the equipment to ranges appropriate for its configuration and handled liquids.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree to which the dosing tolerance permits batch composition to deviate from its specified range
needs.plain: Make sure the amount of each ingredient added keeps the final mixture within its approved recipe range. This helps ensure every batch has the intended makeup.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that pressure, temperature, or flow limits exceed the equipment's configured safe envelope
Reduce the percent of operating limits outside validated ranges for the handled liquid and sterile preparation process
Minimize the likelihood that the combined capacity, flow, and timing limits make the required batch cycle infeasible
Reduce the number of operating limits based on ambiguous or mismatched units and reference bases

preparation.name: Set operating parameters
preparation.statement: Set the plant operating parameters for the planned production run.
preparation.description: This establishes equipment-specific values such as temperatures, pressures, flow rates, mixing speeds, or timing limits before use.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the parameter combination exceeds the plant's safe operating envelope during the planned production run
Reduce the number of operating parameters with conflicting approved values for the planned production run
Reduce the number of required operating parameters left unset before the planned production run
Reduce the number of set parameter values that change before the planned production run begins
Reduce the percentage of sterility-critical parameters set outside validated limits for the planned production run
needs.plain: Keep the settings that protect product sterility within the safe ranges already shown to work for the planned production run. This helps prevent the product from becoming contaminated.

preparation.name: Set up and verify safety systems
preparation.statement: Set up and verify the batch preparation system's safety interlocks and protective devices.
preparation.description: Technician programs, tests, and documents all safety interlock logic, pressure relief, emergency stop, and over-temperature protection to confirm correct protective function before commissioning.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that safety interlock logic produces an incorrect protective response to a hazardous batch condition
needs.plain: Make sure the safety controls respond correctly when a dangerous batch condition occurs. This helps the equipment take the right protective action instead of creating another hazard.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of hazardous batch sequences permitted by safety interlock logic
needs.plain: Prevent the safety system from allowing a dangerous sequence of steps. This helps stop equipment from operating in an unsafe way.

preparation.name: Set up safety systems
preparation.statement: Set up the plant's safety interlocks and protective systems.
preparation.description: Interlocks, guards and emergency stops protect operators from hazards.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of hazardous operating states omitted from interlock coverage
Minimize the likelihood that a configured safety function fails to place the plant in a safe state when demanded
Reduce the number of hazardous access points left exposed after protective guards are installed
Minimize the time required for an operator to reach an emergency stop from a hazardous work position
Reduce the number of temporary safety bypasses left active when the plant is released for operation

preparation.name: Test plant containment integrity after assembly
preparation.statement: Test the assembled plant's piping, vessels, and seals for containment integrity before commissioning.
preparation.description: Pressure or leak testing of the fully assembled plant to confirm that all vessels, piping, gaskets, and seals maintain containment integrity under operating and test conditions.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of the assembled containment boundary omitted from integrity testing
needs.plain: Test the entire assembled barrier that keeps harmful material contained, without leaving out any part. This helps confirm that the barrier will not leak.

preparation.name: Test system integrity
preparation.statement: Test assembled vessels, seals, fittings, hoses, and piping for leaks or pressure loss.
preparation.description: Integrity testing detects connection or containment defects before process liquids are handled.

needs.stmt: Minimize the amount of foreign residue introduced into sterile product-contact circuits during integrity testing
needs.plain: Keep foreign material from getting into the sealed, germ-free parts that touch the product while checking for leaks or damage. This helps keep the product clean and safe.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of an unintended pressurized release exposing personnel during integrity testing
needs.plain: Prevent a test from suddenly releasing pressure and injuring nearby people. This helps make sure the equipment is checked without creating a danger.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of leaks that remain undetected in tested process-circuit sections
Reduce the number of insecure connections that remain undetected in tested process-circuit sections
Minimize the likelihood of leaks or pressure-loss defects remaining undetected after integrity testing
Reduce the percent of assembled connections and containment sections omitted from integrity testing
Reduce the degree of ambiguity in specified test pressures and acceptance criteria for the assembled system
Reduce the percentage of required process-circuit sections omitted from integrity testing
Minimize the likelihood of contamination being introduced into sterile product-contact paths during integrity testing
needs.plain: Keep germs or other unwanted material from getting into the parts that touch the product while checking that the system is properly sealed. This helps protect the product from becoming unsafe.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of component rupture or uncontrolled pressure release during integrity testing
needs.plain: Make sure the equipment does not break or release pressure suddenly while it is being checked for leaks and strength. An uncontrolled release could injure people or damage the system.

preparation.name: Train operating personnel
preparation.statement: Train personnel to operate the viscous product processing plant safely and correctly.
preparation.description: Matters before handover so staff can run, monitor, and respond to the plant without incident.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of trained personnel unable to recognize and respond correctly to hazardous process deviations

preparation.name: Train operators
preparation.statement: Train operators to run the batch preparation system safely and correctly.
preparation.description: Operators need to understand controls, procedures, and safety practices to run the system correctly.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of safety-critical controls each operator misunderstands after training
Reduce the percentage of trained operators who execute safety-critical controls incorrectly during qualification

preparation.name: Train operators on skid
preparation.statement: Train operators and maintenance staff to run and service the skid.
preparation.description: Matters because safe, compliant operation depends on personnel understanding the specific skid.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of trained operators unable to perform required startup, shutdown, alarm-response, and cleaning procedures correctly

preparation.name: Train plant operators
preparation.statement: Train plant personnel to operate the installed liquid product processing plant.
preparation.description: Builds the operating competency needed to run the plant safely and correctly before production begins.
preparation.userGroup: Plant operators, shift supervisors, training coordinators.
preparation.frequency: Once at initial commissioning; repeated when personnel change or plant is modified.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of contamination of sterile product-contact areas during practical operator training
needs.role: Plant operators, shift supervisors, training coordinators.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of plant operators being cleared for duty despite unresolved competency gaps
needs.role: Plant operators, shift supervisors, training coordinators.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of conflicts between training instructions and the installed plant configuration or approved operating procedures
needs.role: Plant operators, shift supervisors, training coordinators.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of safety-, sterility-, or quality-critical operating scenarios omitted from operator training
needs.role: Plant operators, shift supervisors, training coordinators.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of assigned plant operators who miss required training before pre-production
needs.role: Plant operators, shift supervisors, training coordinators.

preparation.name: Train system operators
preparation.statement: Train operators to prepare, start, monitor, stop, clean, and reset the batching system.
preparation.description: Deliver structured training to process operators covering system startup, batch execution, monitoring, shutdown, cleaning, and fault response procedures.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of cleaning and reset exercises completed with contamination hazards
Reduce the percentage of critical startup, batch execution, monitoring, and shutdown steps performed incorrectly during qualification
Reduce the number of abnormal conditions and system faults handled incorrectly during qualification
Reduce the number of conflicts between training instructions, approved procedures, and the current batching system configuration
Reduce the duration of operator qualification delays caused by unavailable prerequisites, trainers, or batching system access
needs.plain: Avoid delays in preparing workers to run the system when required materials, instructors, or system access are missing. This lets operators become ready to work sooner.

preparation.name: Verify in-process integrity of single-use assembly
preparation.statement: Verify the integrity of the single-use flow path assembly during or immediately before active operation.
preparation.description: Perform a pressure hold or bubble-point test on the single-use assembly at the point of use to confirm integrity before committing process material, without referencing a specific test instrument brand.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that a single-use assembly breach remains undetected before process material is committed
needs.plain: Make sure a tear or leak in the disposable assembly is found before product is put into it. This helps prevent product from being exposed or lost.

preparation.name: Verify safety interlocks and shutdown functions
preparation.statement: Verify the configured safety interlocks, alarms, emergency stop functions, and containment sensors before system startup.
preparation.description: Combines setting up safety interlocks (#32) with verifying them before startup (#50). Verification necessarily follows and encompasses setup; at a Level-1 job granularity these are one activity.
preparation.userGroup: Safety engineers, commissioning engineers, system operators.
preparation.frequency: Once at commissioning; repeated after maintenance affecting safety systems.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of tested unsafe conditions that fail to inhibit startup or continued operation
needs.role: Safety engineers, commissioning engineers, system operators.

needs.stmt: Reduce the time from emergency-stop actuation until liquid transfer and hazardous motion cease
needs.role: Safety engineers, commissioning engineers, system operators.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percent of representative loss-of-containment conditions not detected by containment sensors
needs.role: Safety engineers, commissioning engineers, system operators.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of tested hazardous conditions producing missing or incorrect alarm indications
needs.role: Safety engineers, commissioning engineers, system operators.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of safety functions with conflicting acceptance criteria before startup
needs.role: Safety engineers, commissioning engineers, system operators.

disposal.name: Archive compliance records
disposal.statement: Archive batch, validation, and maintenance records for the retired plant.
disposal.description: Regulatory and quality records tied to the plant must be retained after it is retired.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of required validation, calibration, and compliance records omitted from the archive
Reduce the percentage of required validation, calibration, or compliance records missing from the archive
Reduce the percentage of required batch, validation, and maintenance records omitted from the retired-plant archive

disposal.name: Archive lifecycle records
disposal.statement: Archive required lifecycle, maintenance, and disposal compliance records for the retired system.
disposal.description: Combines documenting disposal activities for compliance records (#114) with archiving required lifecycle records (#103). Both describe the same record-archiving obligation at end of life.
disposal.userGroup: Quality assurance, compliance officers, records management.

needs.stmt: Minimize the percentage of required skid lifecycle records unavailable for archiving at decommissioning
Reduce the percentage of required lifecycle, maintenance, and disposal compliance records missing or incomplete when the retired batching system archive is finalized
needs.plain: Make sure the retired mixing system’s records are complete, including its use, maintenance, and proper disposal. This provides a reliable history and shows that required rules were followed.
needs.role: Quality assurance, compliance officers, records management.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that archived records become unreadable or inaccessible during the mandated retention period
needs.plain: Make sure stored records remain readable and available throughout the required storage period. This prevents important information from being lost when it is needed later.
needs.role: Quality assurance, compliance officers, records management.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of archived records attributed to the wrong retired batching system or component
needs.plain: Keep stored records from being linked to the wrong retired mixing system or part. This helps preserve an accurate history of retired equipment.
needs.role: Quality assurance, compliance officers, records management.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of archived records assigned an incorrect retention or disposition requirement
needs.plain: Keep stored records from being given the wrong rule for how long to keep them or when to dispose of them. This helps ensure records are kept and destroyed at the right times.
needs.role: Quality assurance, compliance officers, records management.

needs.stmt: Reduce the time required records remain unarchived after the decommissioning archive deadline
needs.plain: Spend less time leaving required records unfiled after the deadline for storing them has passed. Filing them promptly keeps important history available and organized.
needs.role: Quality assurance, compliance officers, records management.

disposal.name: Archive process and lifecycle records
disposal.statement: Archive batch, quality, compliance, and lifecycle records from the plant control system at end of service.
disposal.description: Preserves required documentation for regulatory, legal, and operational reference after the plant is retired.
disposal.userGroup: Plant managers, compliance officers, records management teams.

needs.stmt: Minimize the percentage of required batch, quality, compliance, and lifecycle records omitted from the archive
needs.role: Plant managers, compliance officers, records management teams.

needs.stmt: Minimize the percentage of archived records that are corrupted or unreadable after plant retirement
needs.role: Plant managers, compliance officers, records management teams.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that authorized custodians are unable to access archived records after decommissioning
needs.role: Plant managers, compliance officers, records management teams.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of archived records missing the identifiers needed to associate them with the correct batch or plant configuration
needs.plain: Make sure stored records include the information needed to link them to the correct production batch or plant setup. This prevents records from becoming impossible to identify correctly.
needs.role: Plant managers, compliance officers, records management teams.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of archived record classes assigned retention periods that conflict with applicable regulatory, legal, or organizational obligations
needs.plain: Make sure the required storage time for each type of stored record does not conflict with any relevant government rule, law, or organizational requirement. This prevents records from being deleted too soon or kept inappropriately long.
needs.role: Plant managers, compliance officers, records management teams.

disposal.name: Cancel plant services
disposal.statement: Cancel active service, licensing, and support agreements associated with the retired plant.
disposal.description: Terminates ongoing contractual obligations to avoid unnecessary costs after the plant is decommissioned.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of missing contractual cancellation deadlines that trigger renewals or termination charges
needs.plain: Cancel plant services before the contract deadlines that would cause automatic renewals or extra cancellation fees. This helps avoid unwanted costs and commitments.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of cancellation delays caused by incomplete authorization or contract documentation
needs.plain: Avoid long cancellation delays when the required approval or contract papers are incomplete. This helps plant services end when intended.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of terminating agreements before decommissioning and compliance dependencies are complete
needs.plain: Avoid ending agreements before all shutdown work and required checks are complete. Services may still be needed until those responsibilities are finished.

needs.stmt: Reduce the amount of service, licensing, and support charges billed after agreed cancellation dates
needs.plain: Prevent service, software-use, and support bills from continuing after the agreed cancellation date. This avoids paying for services that should have ended.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of active service, licensing, and support agreements omitted from cancellation at plant retirement
needs.plain: When the plant closes or retires its operations, every current service, software-use, and support agreement should be found and canceled when appropriate. This helps avoid ongoing charges for services no longer needed.

disposal.name: Cancel service contracts
disposal.statement: Cancel service, monitoring, and software contracts tied to the system.
disposal.description: Matters when maintenance, monitoring, or software service contracts must be ended.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of service, monitoring, or software contracts remaining active after the intended termination date
Reduce the number of service, monitoring, or software contracts remaining active after disposal
Reduce the number of plant-specific licenses, monitoring services, or support subscriptions left active after contract cancellation

disposal.name: Clean wetted surfaces
disposal.statement: Clean and flush wetted surfaces of tanks, pumps, and lines before disposal.
disposal.description: Perform a final clean-in-place or manual flush of all product-contact surfaces to remove residues and meet hygiene, safety, or handover standards.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of wetted surfaces retaining viscous product residue after final cleaning and flushing
needs.plain: Remove thick product residue from all surfaces that touched the product after the final cleaning and rinsing. This helps keep the next product from being contaminated.

disposal.name: Conduct end-of-life safety review
disposal.statement: Conduct a safety review of the system before initiating decommissioning activities.
disposal.description: Assess residual hazards — pressure, chemical, electrical, mechanical — present in the system to establish safe decommissioning procedures and protective measures.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of residual pressure, chemical, electrical, or mechanical hazards left unidentified before decommissioning
needs.plain: Before taking equipment out of service, find any remaining pressure, chemical, electrical, or moving-part dangers. This helps prevent injuries during removal or shutdown.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of a hazardous energy release or chemical exposure during system inspection
needs.plain: Avoid releasing stored energy or exposing people to chemicals while inspecting the system. This helps protect workers during the final safety review.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percent of hazard-bearing areas of the batching system that remain uninspected during the safety review
Minimize the likelihood that decommissioning activities begin before review findings and protective requirements are finalized
needs.plain: Make sure equipment is not taken out of service before the safety review is complete and all needed protections are decided.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of system records that are missing or inconsistent with the end-of-life configuration
needs.plain: Avoid missing records or records that do not match the system’s final setup. This helps people identify remaining safety concerns accurately.

disposal.name: Conduct final safety inspection
disposal.statement: Conduct a final safety inspection of the decommissioned plant site before handover or closure.
disposal.description: Perform a structured inspection to confirm all hazards have been eliminated, all utilities disconnected, all materials removed, and the site is safe for handover to facility management or new occupants.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that residual chemical, pharmaceutical, or biological contamination remains undetected during the final safety inspection
needs.plain: Make sure no leftover chemicals, medicines, or germs are missed during the last safety check. This helps ensure the equipment and product area are safe to use.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that an electrical, pressure, steam, gas, or liquid utility remains energized or connected at handover
needs.plain: Make sure all electricity, pressure, steam, gas, and liquid connections are shut off and disconnected before the equipment is handed over, so no hidden danger remains.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of unstable structures, unguarded openings, or sharp remnants left unidentified before handover
Reduce the percentage of safety-critical plant areas that remain inaccessible during the final inspection
Reduce the number of safety-critical conditions that cannot be verified because decommissioning or utility-isolation records are missing or conflicting

disposal.name: Decommission the plant
disposal.statement: Decommission the viscous product processing plant at end of service life.
disposal.description: Execute a formal decommissioning procedure that retires the plant from active service, covers all required safety and regulatory steps, and closes out site obligations.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of required safety and regulatory closure obligations unresolved at decommissioning closeout

disposal.name: Decommission the process skid
disposal.statement: Decommission the process skid from active production service.
disposal.description: Formally remove the skid from the active equipment register and production service.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of personnel exposure to residual hazardous material during skid decommissioning
needs.plain: Prevent workers from coming into contact with leftover dangerous material while the processing equipment is taken out of service. This protects their health during dismantling.

disposal.name: Decommission the system
disposal.statement: Decommission the batch preparation system from active service.
disposal.description: Execute all steps required to formally take the batch preparation system out of operational service.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of personnel or environmental exposure to hazardous batch residues during decommissioning
needs.plain: Prevent people or the environment from being exposed to dangerous leftover material from a production batch while the system is taken out of service. This supports safe cleanup and disposal.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of injury from stored energy or pressurized liquid released during decommissioning
needs.plain: Prevent injury from trapped pressure or stored force being released while the equipment is taken out of service. This makes dismantling safer.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that hazardous process residues expose personnel during decommissioning
needs.plain: Prevent leftover harmful material from exposing workers while the system is being taken out of service. The system should be made safe before people handle it.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of technician exposure to hazardous residues during system dismantling
needs.plain: Protect the technician from dangerous leftover material while taking the system apart. This helps prevent harmful contact during shutdown and removal.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of active sterile fill-finish production disrupted by disconnecting shared services
Reduce the percentage of decommissioned system materials assigned incorrect disposal classifications
Reduce the number of required decommissioning records left incomplete or inconsistent at project closure
needs.plain: Complete and keep consistent all records needed when the system is taken out of use at the end of the project. This prevents confusion about what was done and leaves a reliable closing record.

disposal.name: Decontaminate product-contact surfaces
disposal.statement: Decontaminate the skid's product-contact surfaces prior to removal.
disposal.description: Apply validated decontamination procedures to wetted and product-contact surfaces to achieve required release conditions before disposal handling.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of product-contact surfaces that fail release criteria after decontamination

disposal.name: Decontaminate wetted components
disposal.statement: Decontaminate tanks, lines, and mixing components before disposal.
disposal.description: Apply appropriate decontamination procedures to all wetted surfaces and internals to meet safety and regulatory requirements prior to disposal.

needs.stmt: Minimize the percentage of wetted surface area retaining hazardous residues after decontamination
needs.plain: After cleaning, as little as possible of the wet surface should still have dangerous residue on it. This helps protect people and prevent the next use from being affected.

needs.stmt: Minimize the amount of contaminated liquid released into the work area or waste stream during decontamination
needs.plain: As little contaminated liquid as possible should spill into the work area or be sent into waste during cleaning. This helps protect people and the surrounding area.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of technician exposure to residual process material while opening or draining tanks, lines, and mixing components
needs.plain: Protect technicians from contact with leftover process material when they open or drain tanks, pipes, and mixing equipment.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of internal zones left untreated because they cannot be accessed during decontamination
Reduce the number of decontamination records missing evidence required for disposal approval
needs.plain: Make sure the cleaning records include all proof needed to approve disposal. Without this evidence, the material may not be cleared for disposal.

disposal.name: Disconnect process interfaces
disposal.statement: Disconnect the system from upstream feeds, downstream lines, and utilities.
disposal.description: Safely isolate the system from all connected process streams and utility supplies before removal.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of contamination entering adjacent sterile process lines through opened interfaces
needs.plain: Keep germs or other unwanted material from entering nearby clean process lines through open connections. This protects the cleanliness of the connected systems.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of releasing pressurized or hazardous residual material during disconnection
needs.plain: Make it unlikely that trapped pressure or dangerous leftover material will escape when equipment connections are opened. This matters because the release could injure people or contaminate the area.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that an undiscovered process or utility connection remains live during disconnection
needs.plain: Avoid leaving an unnoticed pipe, cable, or other connection carrying pressure, electricity, or material when it is disconnected. This helps prevent unexpected releases or injuries.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of disruption to adjacent active equipment caused by disconnecting a shared interface
needs.plain: Disconnect a shared connection without interrupting nearby equipment that is still operating. This lets the work continue while other equipment remains in use.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of damaging facility-side piping or fittings while separating connections
needs.plain: Avoid damaging the site's pipes or connecting parts while taking the connections apart. This helps keep the site's piping usable and leak-free.

disposal.name: Disconnect utility connections
disposal.statement: Disconnect power, fluid, and process utility connections from the retired batch preparation system.
disposal.description: Safely isolate and cap all external utility feeds — electrical, water, steam, compressed air, and process lines — as part of decommissioning.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of unexpected release of residual energy, pressure, steam, or process fluid during utility disconnection
needs.plain: Prevent leftover energy, pressure, steam, or process liquid from escaping unexpectedly while service connections are removed. This protects the people doing the work.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of stored electrical energy, pressure, or heat being released during disconnection
needs.plain: Prevent stored electricity, pressure, or heat from being released suddenly while connections are being removed. This protects the person disconnecting them.

disposal.name: Dismantle plant structure
disposal.statement: Dismantle and remove the plant's structures, tanks, and piping from the site.
disposal.description: Physically break down and remove all plant equipment, vessels, piping, and structural elements from the installation site.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of an unexpected release of pressure, energy, or process material from disconnected plant systems
needs.plain: When plant equipment is taken apart, pressure, stored energy, or process material should not be released unexpectedly. This helps prevent injury, damage, or harmful exposure.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of uncontrolled collapse of partially dismantled structures, tanks, or piping
needs.plain: Prevent partly taken-apart buildings, tanks, or pipes from suddenly falling down. This protects workers from being struck or trapped.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of worker exposure to residual pharmaceutical, cleaning, or decontamination substances during dismantling
needs.plain: Make it very unlikely that workers touch or breathe leftover medicines, cleaning products, or cleaning chemicals while taking the plant apart. This helps protect their health.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of retained structures or services damaged during plant dismantling
needs.plain: Reduce the number of building parts or site services meant to remain that are damaged while the plant is taken apart. This helps preserve what is still needed after the work is finished.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of dismantled tanks or structural sections being unable to pass through planned site removal routes
needs.plain: Make sure tanks and building sections taken apart will fit through the planned ways out of the site. This helps prevent delays and the need for extra dismantling.

disposal.name: Dismantle the equipment
disposal.statement: Disconnect and dismantle the plant equipment for removal from site.
disposal.description: Mechanically disconnect, unbolt, and break down the plant into removable assemblies, facilitating transport, recycling, resale, or disposal.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of uncontrolled movement or collapse of heavy plant assemblies during dismantling
needs.plain: Reduce the chance that heavy parts move unexpectedly or fall while the equipment is being taken apart. This helps protect people and nearby equipment.

disposal.name: Dismantle the skid into handling assemblies
disposal.statement: Disassemble the process skid into manageable assemblies for final disposition.
disposal.description: Break down the skid structure into sub-assemblies of appropriate size and weight for transport, recycling, or disposal.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of stored electrical, pressure, or mechanical energy releasing unexpectedly during skid dismantling
needs.plain: Prevent trapped electricity, pressure, or movement from being released unexpectedly while the equipment frame is taken apart. This helps protect workers during dismantling.

disposal.name: Dispose of hazardous residues
disposal.statement: Dispose of hazardous solvents, lubricants, and product residues per regulation.
disposal.description: Solvents, lubricants, and product residues classed as hazardous require compliant disposal.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of incompatible hazardous residues being combined during segregation or temporary storage
needs.plain: Keep dangerous leftover materials that could react badly from being mixed while they are separated or stored temporarily.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of incompatible retained chemical residues being combined during segregation and packaging
needs.plain: Keep leftover chemicals that could react badly from being mixed while they are separated and packed for disposal.
needs.role: Environmental health and safety personnel, waste management contractors.

needs.stmt: Reduce the amount of hazardous or regulated chemical residue left in the batching system after disposal activities
needs.plain: Leave as little dangerous or legally controlled chemical residue as possible in the mixing system after it is emptied for disposal. This helps protect people and the environment.
needs.role: Environmental health and safety personnel, waste management contractors.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of hazardous residues escaping containment before final disposal
needs.plain: Keep dangerous leftover materials contained until they are finally disposed of. This protects people and the surrounding area from exposure.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of personnel exposure incidents while handling hazardous residues and contaminated materials
needs.plain: Reduce the number of incidents in which workers are exposed to dangerous leftover substances or contaminated materials. This helps protect people while waste is being handled and discarded.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of hazardous residue being released during recovery, packaging, or transfer
needs.plain: Prevent dangerous leftover material from escaping while it is collected, packed, or moved. Safe handling keeps people and the surrounding area protected.
needs.role: Environmental health and safety personnel, waste management contractors.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that hazardous residues are assigned to the wrong regulatory waste category
needs.plain: Make sure dangerous leftover materials are placed in the correct legally defined waste category. Mislabeling them could lead to unsafe disposal or rule violations.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of hazardous residues and contaminated materials assigned an incorrect regulatory classification
Reduce the number of hazardous waste shipments rejected because shipment records are incomplete or inconsistent
Reduce the percentage of retained chemical residue streams assigned an incorrect regulatory classification
needs.role: Environmental health and safety personnel, waste management contractors.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of a residue shipment being rejected because required regulatory documentation is incomplete or inconsistent
needs.plain: Reduce the chance that a shipment of leftover hazardous material is refused because required legal papers are missing or do not match. This helps the material reach an approved disposal destination.
needs.role: Environmental health and safety personnel, waste management contractors.

disposal.name: Dispose of residual waste
disposal.statement: Dispose of nonrecoverable plant waste through authorised channels.
disposal.description: Arrange compliant disposal of all nonrecoverable solid and liquid waste streams generated during plant decommissioning, including manifesting, transport, and confirmation of receipt.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that a waste stream is sent through a channel unauthorized for its composition or hazard classification
needs.plain: Make sure waste is sent through a disposal route approved for what it contains and how dangerous it is. This helps prevent unsafe handling or rule violations.

disposal.name: Document final plant condition
disposal.statement: Document the plant's physical condition and decommissioned state at the end of service.
disposal.description: Creates a formal record of the plant's final configuration, condition, and site status for handover, legal, and regulatory purposes.
disposal.userGroup: Plant managers, project engineers, compliance officers.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that residual process material or contamination is omitted from the final plant-condition record before handover
needs.role: Plant managers, project engineers, compliance officers.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of plant areas, systems, and utility connections omitted from the final-condition documentation
needs.role: Plant managers, project engineers, compliance officers.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of discrepancies between recorded decommissioned states and actual plant states at handover
needs.role: Plant managers, project engineers, compliance officers.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the plant condition changes after documentation and before handover
needs.plain: Make sure the plant stays in the same condition after it is documented and until it is handed over. This prevents disagreements about what condition it was actually in.
needs.role: Plant managers, project engineers, compliance officers.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of documented conditions lacking traceable evidence or required approval at closure
needs.plain: Make sure every condition recorded at the end has supporting evidence that can be traced back to its source and has any needed approval. This makes the final plant record trustworthy.
needs.role: Plant managers, project engineers, compliance officers.

disposal.name: Document final state
disposal.statement: Document the final condition and disposition of the skid.
disposal.description: Records the final condition, decontamination, and disposition of the skid for regulatory and audit traceability.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of equipment items whose recorded disposition differs from the actual final disposition
Reduce the percentage of required final-condition and disposition details omitted from the skid record

disposal.name: Document final system condition
disposal.statement: Record the system's physical and operational condition at retirement.
disposal.description: Capture the final state of all major components and subsystems to support asset records, warranty claims, or future reference.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree to which residue or inaccessible surfaces obscure the physical condition of product-contact components
Reduce the duration of inspection stoppage caused by hazardous stored energy in retired system components
Minimize the percentage of major components or subsystems omitted from the final condition record
needs.plain: Record the condition of every major part of the system at the end, so problems are not overlooked and others know what they are receiving.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that operational condition cannot be verified after supporting utilities are disconnected
needs.plain: Make it unlikely that the machine’s working condition cannot be checked after its supporting equipment and connections are turned off or removed. This helps create a reliable record of its final condition.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of condition entries associated with the wrong component or subsystem identifier
needs.plain: Make sure each condition note is linked to the correct part or section of the system. This prevents people from making decisions based on information about the wrong item.

disposal.name: Drain residual process fluids
disposal.statement: Drain and purge residual process and cleaning fluids from the skid.
disposal.description: Removes residual process fluids, buffers, solvents, and CIP chemicals safely from the piping and vessels.

needs.stmt: Reduce the amount of hazardous residual fluid remaining in skid piping or vessels after draining and purging
needs.plain: Remove as much dangerous leftover liquid as possible from the connected pipes and vessels after draining and flushing. This helps reduce the chance of exposure or accidental release.

disposal.name: Drain residual process liquids
disposal.statement: Drain residual process liquids from tanks, vessels, and lines.
disposal.description: Remove all remaining process fluids from the plant's storage, processing, and transfer systems prior to dismantling.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of incompatible residual process liquids mixing during drainage
needs.plain: Keep different leftover process liquids from mixing if they should not be combined while they are drained away.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of residual process liquid being released outside the intended collection boundary during drainage
needs.plain: Keep leftover liquid inside the intended collection area while it drains. This prevents spills and unwanted release into the surrounding area.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of technician exposure to hazardous residual process liquid during drainage
needs.plain: The technician should not come into contact with leftover process liquid while it is being drained. This helps prevent exposure to a potentially harmful liquid.

needs.stmt: Reduce the amount of residual process liquid remaining in tanks, vessels, and lines after drainage
Reduce the duration of drainage stoppages caused by blocked or inaccessible flow paths

disposal.name: Drain residual product
disposal.statement: Drain and evacuate residual viscous product from vessels and pipework before decommissioning.
disposal.description: Remove all residual viscous material from tanks, vessels, and interconnecting pipework to prepare the plant for cleaning, disassembly, or handover.

needs.stmt: Minimize the amount of residual viscous product remaining in vessels and pipework after evacuation
needs.plain: Leave as little thick product as possible in the tanks and pipes after they are emptied. This helps reduce waste and makes the equipment easier to clean or reuse.

disposal.name: Erase sensitive operational data
disposal.statement: Erase sensitive operational data from plant control systems before decommissioning.
disposal.description: Perform a verified erasure or sanitisation of proprietary process parameters, personnel records, and sensitive operational data stored on control-system hardware before it is retired, transferred, or scrapped.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that sensitive operational data remains recoverable after sanitization

disposal.name: Export batch data
disposal.statement: Export batch records, recipes, and process data from the control system prior to decommissioning.
disposal.description: Extract all operational data stored in the system's controllers and data historian for retention or migration.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of required batch records, recipes, and process data omitted from the export
Reduce the percentage of required batch records, recipes, or process data omitted from the export

disposal.name: Export batch records
disposal.statement: Export historical batch data and recipe records from the control system prior to decommissioning.
disposal.description: Extract all stored batch histories and production records from the system's control platform to ensure data is preserved after the system is retired.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of historical batch data and recipe records omitted from the export
needs.plain: Make sure exported records include all past production-group information and written machine instructions, so the history is not missing important details.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that batch records are corrupted during extraction or transfer
needs.plain: Make sure production records are not damaged or changed while they are being copied out or moved. The records must remain usable afterward.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of exported batch histories missing links to their recipe versions and audit trails
needs.plain: Make sure exported production records stay linked to the correct recipe version and history of changes. These links help people understand how each batch was made and what was changed.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that exported records cannot be opened or interpreted after the control platform is retired
needs.plain: Make sure the transferred production records can still be opened and understood after the machine’s control software is retired.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of misalignment between export verification and decommissioning schedules
needs.plain: Make sure the timing for checking exported batches matches the timing for taking the related equipment out of service, so records are checked before they are no longer available.

disposal.name: Export control system data
disposal.statement: Export recipes, control logic, and operational data from the plant's automation system.
disposal.description: Extract and preserve all automation programs, process recipes, historian data, and configuration files from the plant's control systems before decommissioning.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that unavailable access credentials or decryption information prevents extraction from source control systems
Minimize the likelihood that source control systems are decommissioned before all required data is extracted
Minimize the percentage of required automation programs, process recipes, historian records, and configuration files omitted from the preserved export
Minimize the degree of version inconsistency among preserved automation programs, process recipes, historian data, and configuration files
needs.plain: Keep the saved control programs, production instructions, recorded operating data, and setup files on matching versions, so they work together and describe the same system.

needs.stmt: Minimize the percentage of exported control-system files that are corrupted or unreadable
needs.plain: Reduce the share of exported files that control the equipment that are damaged or cannot be opened. This helps preserve the information needed to operate or review the system.

disposal.name: Export process data
disposal.statement: Export recipes, batch records, and control-system configurations before decommissioning.
disposal.description: Extract and transfer all operationally valuable data — process recipes, batch histories, and control-system configurations — from the plant's control systems prior to shutdown.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of required recipes, batch records, and control configurations omitted from the export

disposal.name: Isolate energy sources
disposal.statement: Isolate the plant from each connected energy source before disassembly.
disposal.description: Apply lockout/tagout or equivalent energy-isolation procedures to electrical, pneumatic, hydraulic, and thermal energy sources to ensure safe decommissioning.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of connected electrical, pneumatic, hydraulic, or thermal energy sources remaining active after isolation
needs.plain: Make sure all connected sources of electrical, air, water-powered, or heat energy are turned off after isolation. This helps prevent unexpected movement, release of energy, or injury.

disposal.name: Isolate energy sources on the skid
disposal.statement: Isolate the process skid from every connected energy source prior to decommissioning.
disposal.description: Apply lockout/tagout or equivalent energy isolation procedures to all energy sources — electrical, pneumatic, hydraulic — connected to the skid before decommissioning work begins.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of electrical, pneumatic, or hydraulic connections remaining energized after isolation is declared complete
needs.plain: After declaring the equipment safely shut off, leave as few electrical, air-powered, or liquid-powered connections active as possible, so no hidden energy remains to cause harm.

disposal.name: Migrate control configuration
disposal.statement: Transfer the retiring system's required control configuration to its replacement.
disposal.description: Extract and port control logic, parameters, and automation settings from the retiring system to ensure continuity of operations on the replacement platform.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of sterility-critical interlocks, alarms, or sequence steps that behave incorrectly after transfer
needs.plain: Avoid having important safety connections, warnings, or operating steps work incorrectly after the control settings are moved to another system. This helps the equipment keep operating safely.

needs.stmt: Minimize the percentage of required control logic, parameters, and automation settings absent from the replacement configuration at cutover
needs.plain: Put all needed operating instructions, settings, and automatic controls into the replacement system before it is put into use, so it behaves as expected.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of selecting a superseded or unapproved retiring-system configuration as the migration source
needs.plain: Avoid choosing an outdated or unapproved setup from the system being replaced as the starting point for the move. Using the wrong setup could cause errors in the new system.

needs.stmt: Minimize the percentage of transferred configuration elements lacking traceability to an approved source record required for release
needs.plain: Make sure every moved setting can be traced back to an approved record before the system is put into use. This shows where each setting came from and supports safe approval.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of sterile fill-finish production restart delay caused by configuration migration at cutover
needs.plain: Spend less time waiting to restart germ-free medicine production while the old equipment settings are moved to the replacement. This helps the replacement start with the right settings sooner.

disposal.name: Notify affected stakeholders
disposal.statement: Notify operations, quality, and safety stakeholders of the system removal.
disposal.description: Operations, quality, and safety teams need notice before the system goes offline.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of required operations, quality, and safety stakeholders omitted from the system-removal notice
needs.plain: Make sure the notice about removing the system reaches everyone responsible for operations, quality, and safety. This gives all affected people time to prepare and respond.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that stakeholders receive the notice too late to prepare for the system removal
needs.plain: Make sure the notice arrives early enough for everyone affected to prepare for the system’s removal.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of critical removal details missing from the notice
needs.plain: Avoid leaving important information about removing the equipment out of the notice. Complete instructions help everyone affected understand what will be removed and what they need to do.

needs.stmt: Reduce the degree of inconsistency in removal dates, scope, or responsibilities communicated across operations, quality, and safety teams
needs.plain: Make sure the operations, quality, and safety teams receive the same information about when equipment will be removed, what the work includes, and who is responsible.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that stakeholder receipt and understanding remain unconfirmed before removal begins
needs.plain: Make sure everyone affected has received and understood the notice before removal starts. This helps them prepare and avoid surprises.

disposal.name: Notify regulatory authorities
disposal.statement: Notify regulatory and environmental authorities of the plant's decommissioning.
disposal.description: Environmental, safety, and regulatory authorities must be informed of the plant's retirement.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of required regulatory and environmental authorities that do not receive a decommissioning notice
needs.plain: Make sure every government office responsible for safety or the environment receives notice that the equipment is being taken out of service, so required authorities are informed.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of submitting a required decommissioning notice after its mandated deadline
needs.plain: Avoid sending a required shutdown notice after the official deadline. Sending it on time helps meet legal requirements.

needs.stmt: Reduce the rate of decommissioning notices rejected for missing required plant-retirement details
needs.plain: Fewer notices about retiring plant equipment should be rejected because they leave out required details. This helps regulatory authorities receive complete information the first time.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of inconsistency in plant-retirement details reported to different authorities
needs.plain: Make sure all authorities receive the same accurate information about closing or retiring the plant. This helps prevent conflicting official records.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that receipt of a required decommissioning notice cannot be demonstrated
needs.plain: Make it unlikely that there is no proof that the required notice about taking equipment out of service was received. This helps show that the proper authorities were notified.

disposal.name: Notify stakeholders of decommissioning
disposal.statement: Notify regulators, insurers, and relevant stakeholders of the plant's decommissioning.
disposal.description: Issue formal notifications to all required parties — regulatory bodies, insurers, utility providers, and internal stakeholders — that the plant has been permanently retired from service.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of required regulators, insurers, utility providers, and internal stakeholders omitted from the notification list

disposal.name: Notify stakeholders of retirement
disposal.statement: Notify quality, regulatory, and operations stakeholders of the skid's retirement.
disposal.description: Informs regulators, quality, and downstream stakeholders that the skid is no longer in production use.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of affected stakeholder groups omitted from the batch preparation system retirement notification
Reduce the percentage of affected stakeholders omitted from the retirement notification
Minimize the percentage of required quality, regulatory, and operations stakeholders omitted from the skid-retirement notification

disposal.name: Obtain disposal permits
disposal.statement: Obtain required regulatory permits and approvals for plant decommissioning and site clearance.
disposal.description: Secure all necessary environmental, demolition, and waste-disposal permits from relevant authorities before commencing physical decommissioning activities.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that a required permit or approving authority is omitted from the decommissioning plan
needs.plain: Make sure the disposal plan includes every permit and every official approval that is required. This helps the equipment be taken out of service legally and properly.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of permit applications returned because plant decommissioning information is incomplete or inconsistent
needs.plain: Provide complete and consistent information about the plant being taken out of use when applying for disposal approval. This prevents the application from being sent back for correction.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of regulatory reviews before plant decommissioning approvals are issued
needs.plain: Spend less time waiting for government reviews before getting approval to shut down the plant. This helps the plant closure begin as planned.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that late hazardous-material or contamination findings require changes to submitted permit scopes
needs.plain: Find hazardous or contaminating materials early, so the disposal permits do not need to be changed after they are submitted.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of conflict among environmental, demolition, and waste-disposal approval conditions
needs.plain: Make sure the environmental, removal, and waste-disposal approval conditions do not contradict one another. This helps the project meet all requirements without conflicting instructions.

disposal.name: Obtain regulatory disposal approvals
disposal.statement: Obtain required regulatory approvals and permits before disposing of the system.
disposal.description: Identify and secure all permits, notifications, or regulatory clearances required by applicable environmental and safety authorities prior to decommissioning activities.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of required disposal approvals or notifications omitted from the submission plan
needs.plain: Include every required approval and notice for disposing of the equipment in the submission plan. This helps avoid delays or violations during disposal.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that approval applications are rejected for incomplete or inaccurate system-disposal information
needs.plain: Make sure disposal applications contain complete and accurate information about the system being discarded. This helps prevent the applications from being rejected.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of regulatory review beyond the planned decommissioning start date
needs.plain: Spend less time waiting for government approval to dispose of equipment after the planned shutdown date. This helps the shutdown stay on schedule.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of conflict between environmental and safety authorities over applicable disposal requirements
needs.plain: Resolve differences between environmental and safety officials about how the waste must be disposed of. This prevents delays and helps ensure the disposal is accepted by all authorities.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that an approval or permit expires before disposal activities begin
needs.plain: Make sure the required approval or permit is still valid when disposal begins. This prevents disposal work from starting without permission.

disposal.name: Physically remove the system
disposal.statement: Physically remove the batch preparation system from its installed location.
disposal.description: Coordinate rigging, disassembly, and removal of the system's structural and process assemblies from the facility floor, including any embedded or skid-mounted sub-systems.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of a structural or process assembly shifting or falling during disassembly and rigging
Minimize the likelihood of structural or process assemblies shifting or falling during removal

disposal.name: Prepare for extended storage
disposal.statement: Drain, flush, and protect the batching system for an extended period of inactivity.
disposal.description: Execution of lay-up procedures including draining fluid paths, purging residues, applying protective measures, and securing the system against deterioration during prolonged non-use.

needs.stmt: Reduce the amount of process or cleaning residue remaining in the batching system after draining and flushing
needs.plain: Remove as much leftover processing material and cleaning liquid as possible from the mixing system before storing it for a long time. This helps prevent buildup, damage, and contamination.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of trapped pressure or hazardous liquid being released unexpectedly during draining
needs.plain: Prevent trapped pressure or dangerous liquid from escaping suddenly while the system is being emptied. This protects people and nearby equipment.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of environmental contaminants entering sterile fluid paths during extended storage
Reduce the percentage of corrosion-prone batching-system surfaces left unprotected during extended inactivity
needs.plain: Protect surfaces that can easily rust on the mixing system while it is not being used for a long time, so they do not deteriorate.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of drain, isolation, or preservation states recorded incorrectly at lay-up handoff
needs.plain: Make sure the records correctly show whether drains were emptied, equipment was separated or secured, and the equipment was protected for storage. This helps prevent damage while the equipment is not being used.

disposal.name: Purge and clean process equipment
disposal.statement: Purge and clean process equipment of remaining product traces prior to removal.
disposal.description: Remove residual product traces from all process-contact equipment using appropriate flushing, purging, or cleaning methods to ensure safe handling during dismantling.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of hazardous pressure remaining in process equipment before dismantling
needs.plain: Make sure dangerous pressure has been released before taking the equipment apart, so no one is injured.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of technician exposure to hazardous product residues during purging and cleaning
needs.plain: Keep technicians from touching or breathing in harmful product leftovers while they flush and clean the equipment. This helps protect their health during cleaning.

needs.stmt: Minimize the amount of product residue released into drains or surrounding work areas during purging and cleaning
needs.plain: Release as little leftover product as possible into drains or nearby work areas while equipment is being flushed and cleaned. This reduces waste and keeps the workplace cleaner and safer.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of process-contact locations retaining detectable product traces after purging and cleaning
Reduce the duration of purge and cleaning delays caused by unavailable isolation, drainage, or utility access

disposal.name: Recover and recycle decommissioned components
disposal.statement: Salvage reusable components from the retired batch preparation system for reuse or resale and recycle eligible materials and remaining components through appropriate channels.
