disposal.description: After decommissioning, components and materials are sorted: reusable parts are tagged for internal reuse or sold, while eligible materials (metals, electronics, etc.) are directed to recycling streams in accordance with environmental and regulatory requirements.
disposal.userGroup: Facilities/asset management team, environmental health and safety, procurement.

needs.stmt: Reduce the amount of recoverable material sent to final disposal instead of reuse, resale, or recycling
needs.role: Facilities/asset management team, environmental health and safety, procurement.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of eligible materials sent through noncompliant recycling or disposal channels
needs.role: Facilities/asset management team, environmental health and safety, procurement.

disposal.name: Recover reusable components
disposal.statement: Recover serviceable components from the decommissioned plant for reuse.
disposal.description: Identify, remove, test, and catalogue mechanical components — pumps, valves, instruments, motors — that retain serviceability and can be redeployed or sold as spares.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of recoverable components damaged during removal

disposal.name: Recycle eligible skid materials
disposal.statement: Route eligible skid materials into approved recycling streams.
disposal.description: Sort and route skid materials — metals, electronics, plastics — into appropriate approved recycling streams after disassembly.

needs.stmt: Reduce the amount of eligible skid material discarded with nonrecyclable waste

disposal.name: Recycle recoverable components
disposal.statement: Recover and recycle metals, motors, and reusable components from the plant.
disposal.description: Segregate and route recoverable plant materials — structural metals, motors, instrumentation — to appropriate recycling streams.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of worker exposure to hazardous residues or stored energy during component recovery
needs.plain: Prevent workers from coming into contact with dangerous leftover material or trapped power while recovering parts. This helps them remove usable components safely.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of recoverable plant materials rejected because of residual product or hazardous contamination
Reduce the duration of onsite storage while segregated materials await acceptance or collection
Reduce the number of dismantled components lacking verified material composition and contamination status
Reduce the percentage of recoverable metals, motors, and electronic boards sent to landfill
Reduce the percentage of reusable motors and instruments damaged during removal or handling
needs.plain: Make sure reusable motors and measuring devices are not damaged while being removed or moved. This matters so they can be used again instead of being discarded.

disposal.name: Recycle system materials
disposal.statement: Route recoverable system materials into appropriate recycling streams.
disposal.description: Separate and route metals, piping, electronics, and other recyclable materials from the decommissioned system into applicable recycling channels.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that recyclable materials are rejected because of residual pharmaceutical or cleaning substances
needs.plain: Make sure recyclable materials are free of leftover medicines or cleaning products before sending them for recycling. This prevents the materials from being rejected or creating a safety problem.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of system materials assigned to incompatible recycling streams
needs.plain: Send each system material to the correct recycling category rather than putting it in the wrong one. This helps the materials get recycled properly and avoids contamination.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of components whose material composition cannot be verified before routing
needs.plain: Make sure the material each component is made from can be confirmed before it is sent for recycling. This helps ensure materials are handled and recycled correctly.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that applicable recycling channels refuse separated system materials
needs.plain: Make sure the separated materials from the system are accepted by the appropriate recycling services. This prevents recyclable materials from being refused or sent to the wrong place.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration separated recyclable materials remain onsite awaiting transfer
needs.plain: Move separated recyclable materials offsite sooner. This keeps storage areas from becoming crowded while the materials wait for pickup.

disposal.name: Remediate the site
disposal.statement: Decontaminate and remediate the plant site after equipment removal.
disposal.description: The site may require decontamination and remediation to return it to a safe, reusable condition.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of personnel exposure incidents during decontamination and remediation of the plant site
needs.plain: Reduce the number of times workers are exposed to harmful substances while cleaning up and restoring the plant site. This helps protect their health.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percent of sampled plant areas that remain above contamination clearance thresholds after remediation
Minimize the likelihood of contaminants spreading beyond the remediation boundary during work on the plant site
Reduce the percentage of remediation waste loads rejected because their contents do not meet disposal acceptance criteria
Reduce the number of previously unidentified contaminated locations discovered after remediation begins

disposal.name: Remove personal protective infrastructure
disposal.statement: Remove plant-specific safety systems and protective infrastructure prior to site handover.
disposal.description: Decommission and remove interlocks, safety showers, gas detection systems, and other plant-specific safety infrastructure that are not part of the general facility.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that plant-specific protective infrastructure is disabled while residual process hazards remain
needs.plain: Do not remove the facility’s safety protections while leftover dangers from the process are still present. This keeps people protected until the area is safe.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of personnel exposure to hazardous residues while removing plant-specific safety infrastructure
needs.plain: Protect workers from harmful leftover materials while removing the site’s safety equipment. This helps prevent exposure during the removal work.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of damaging shared facility utilities while disconnecting plant-specific safety infrastructure
Reduce the percentage of removed safety components that belong to the general facility
Reduce the number of plant-specific safety components left in place at site handover

disposal.name: Remove proprietary data
disposal.statement: Remove proprietary formulas, access credentials, and sensitive information from the system's controllers.
disposal.description: Sanitise the system's onboard storage to prevent disclosure of intellectual property or credentials after transfer or disposal.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of proprietary formula data remaining recoverable from system controllers after sanitization
Reduce the amount of proprietary formula data remaining recoverable from controller storage after sanitization

disposal.name: Remove stored proprietary data
disposal.statement: Remove stored proprietary recipes, formulations, and configuration data from the system controller.
disposal.description: Purge all confidential process data and intellectual property from onboard storage before the system is transferred, resold, or scrapped.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of proprietary recipes, formulations, and configuration data that remains recoverable from onboard storage after removal
Minimize the likelihood of proprietary process data being exposed to unauthorized parties during removal or disposition
Reduce the amount of required batch, audit, or service data unintentionally erased while removing proprietary data
needs.plain: Avoid accidentally deleting important production, inspection, or service records while removing the company's private information. This matters because the needed records must still be available afterward.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of transfer or scrapping delays caused by unresolved deletion and retention requirements
needs.plain: Shorten the delay in transferring or scrapping equipment while people are still deciding what information must be deleted and what must be kept. This helps the equipment move on sooner while handling information properly.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of removed proprietary data being repopulated on the controller before transfer, resale, or scrapping
needs.plain: Reduce the chance that deleted company information is restored to the machine before it is transferred, sold, or discarded. This helps keep private information from being exposed.

disposal.name: Remove system identification markings
disposal.statement: Remove or obliterate identification markings, asset tags, and labels from the system before transfer or disposal.
disposal.description: Strip asset tags, serial-number plates, proprietary identification, and facility-specific markings from the system to protect organisational information and comply with asset management policies.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of identification markings that remain legible after removal or obliteration
needs.plain: Make sure old identification marks are truly unreadable after they are removed, so the equipment cannot be mistaken for something else.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of required regulatory or safety markings removed unintentionally
needs.plain: Prevent legally required or safety labels from being removed by mistake. These markings help people identify the equipment and handle it safely.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of damaging system surfaces or components while stripping markings
needs.plain: Remove labels or markings without scratching or otherwise harming the equipment. This helps keep the equipment in good working condition.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of removed tags and plates remaining recoverable from the waste stream
needs.plain: Reduce the chance that removed labels and nameplates can still be recovered from the waste. This helps prevent the equipment from being identified after disposal.

needs.stmt: Reduce the amount of adhesive residue or label debris left on cleanroom-facing or product-contact surfaces
needs.plain: Leave as little glue, ink, or label material as possible on surfaces that face the clean work area or touch the product. This helps keep those surfaces clean and safe.

disposal.name: Remove the skid from the facility
disposal.statement: Rig and remove the skid from the process facility.
disposal.description: Use appropriate rigging and handling equipment to safely remove the decommissioned skid from the facility floor.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of the skid falling during lifting or transport out of the facility

disposal.name: Resell or transfer equipment
disposal.statement: Resell or transfer usable process equipment to a new owner or facility.
disposal.description: Functional equipment may be sold or transferred to another facility to extend its service life.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that equipment retains product or hazardous material at handover
needs.plain: Make sure no product or dangerous material is left inside the equipment when it is handed over or sold. This protects the people who receive or work on it.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of equipment damage during dismantling, handling, or transport
Reduce the number of required equipment history and qualification records missing or inaccurate at handover
needs.plain: Make sure the equipment’s use history and testing records are complete and accurate when it is handed over. This helps the new owner understand the equipment’s condition and past compliance.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that equipment is incompatible with the receiving facility's space, utilities, or sterile production requirements
needs.plain: Make sure the equipment will fit the new facility, work with its basic services, and meet its clean-production needs.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of postponed handover caused by unresolved ownership, regulatory, or cross-border approvals
needs.plain: Avoid delaying the handover of equipment because no one has settled who owns it, whether the transfer follows required rules, or whether it may cross a border. This helps the equipment reach its new owner sooner.

disposal.name: Resell the plant
disposal.statement: Transfer ownership of the plant to a buyer or the used-equipment market.
disposal.description: List, negotiate, and complete the sale of the decommissioned plant or its major assemblies through the secondary equipment market, recovering residual asset value.

needs.stmt: Reduce the amount of residual asset value lost in the completed plant sale

disposal.name: Return Affected Equipment
disposal.statement: Return the batch preparation system to the responsible supplier.
disposal.description: This applies when a warranty claim, recall, lease termination, or supplier take-back program requires return.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of residual hazardous or process material remaining in the system at handover
needs.plain: Make it unlikely that harmful or process-related material will still be inside the equipment when it is handed over. Removing what remains helps protect the next people who use or service it.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the supplier rejects the equipment return because decontamination status is unverifiable

disposal.name: Return eligible equipment
disposal.statement: Return eligible equipment through its designated return channel.
disposal.description: This fulfills warranty, recall, lease, rental, exchange, or supplier take-back requirements.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that residual product, cleaning agents, or hazardous material remain in the batching system at handoff
needs.plain: Make sure no product, cleaning liquid, or dangerous material is left in the mixing equipment when it is handed over. This helps protect the next person who uses it.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the batching system is routed to the wrong destination because return-channel instructions conflict
needs.plain: The batching system should be sent to the correct destination, even if different return instructions are provided. This prevents the equipment from being returned to the wrong place.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the return is refused because eligibility or authorization records are incomplete
needs.plain: The equipment should only be returned when the records show that it is allowed and properly authorized for return. This prevents the return from being refused.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of damage to the batching system during disconnection, removal, or transit
needs.plain: Prevent the batching system from being damaged while it is disconnected, removed, or transported. This helps keep the equipment usable when it is returned.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of return delay caused by misaligned site access, pickup, and receiving availability
needs.plain: Spend less time waiting to return equipment because the site, pickup service, and receiving location are not ready at the same time. Better coordination helps the equipment get back sooner.

disposal.name: Return under take-back program
disposal.statement: Return the system to the manufacturer or a designated take-back program at end of life.
disposal.description: Coordinate return logistics and documentation to comply with manufacturer take-back or extended producer responsibility requirements.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of required take-back records rejected as incomplete or inconsistent
Minimize the likelihood of take-back documentation being rejected as incomplete or inaccurate

disposal.name: Salvage reusable components
disposal.statement: Recover reusable pumps, valves, sensors, and vessels from the system.
disposal.description: Identify and extract components that retain service value for reuse in other systems or as spare parts.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of technician exposure to hazardous process residues during component extraction
needs.plain: Protect the technician from dangerous leftover material while removing parts that may be reused. This helps prevent harmful contact during the work.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of recovered components that retain unacceptable process residues
needs.plain: Make sure recovered parts do not still have unacceptable leftover material or chemicals on them. Clean parts are safer and more suitable for reuse.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of salvageable pumps, valves, sensors, and vessels damaged during extraction
needs.plain: Avoid damaging pumps, valves, sensors, and tanks that could still be used while removing them. This preserves useful equipment and reduces replacement costs.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that a recovered component has undetected degradation
needs.plain: Make sure a part recovered for reuse does not have hidden wear or damage. This helps prevent an unsafe or unreliable part from being used again.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of recovered components with missing identity or service-history records
needs.plain: Fewer recovered parts should be missing records showing what they are or how they were used and maintained. This information helps determine whether they can be safely reused.

disposal.name: Sanitize digital systems
disposal.statement: Sanitize sensitive data from the plant's controllers and storage devices.
disposal.description: Securely erase or overwrite proprietary process data, credentials, and confidential configurations from all plant digital systems prior to transfer or disposal.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that proprietary process data, credentials, or confidential configurations remain recoverable after sanitization
Reduce the number of retained digital systems unintentionally altered during sanitization
Reduce the percentage of in-scope controllers and storage devices omitted from sanitization
needs.plain: Make sure every included control device and data-storage device has its stored information safely erased, so no included device is overlooked.

needs.stmt: Reduce the time controllers and storage devices remain inaccessible for sanitization
needs.plain: Spend less time waiting for control units and storage devices to become available for secure cleaning of their stored information. This helps remove information before the devices are reused or discarded.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of sanitized controllers and storage devices lacking verifiable completion records
needs.plain: Make sure cleaned control computers and storage devices have records proving the cleaning was completed. This provides evidence that stored information was properly removed or handled.

disposal.name: Segregate hazardous materials
disposal.statement: Segregate hazardous materials removed from the plant for compliant disposal.
disposal.description: Sort and separate hazardous substances — contaminated insulation, asbestos-containing materials, process chemicals — into compliant waste streams prior to authorised disposal.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of incompatible process chemicals being combined in the same waste stream during segregation

disposal.name: Store skid components properly
disposal.statement: Store disassembled or spare skid components properly during periods of inactivity.
disposal.description: Ensuring removed assemblies, single-use components, and spare parts are stored under appropriate conditions (temperature, humidity, contamination control) to prevent degradation before reinstallation.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of removed assemblies, single-use components, or spare parts becoming contaminated during storage

disposal.name: Terminate linked services
disposal.statement: Terminate services, maintenance contracts, calibration agreements, and consumable supply arrangements linked to the retired batching system.
disposal.description: Combines cancelling specific service/calibration/consumable agreements (#101) with terminating all services linked to the retired system (#117). Both describe the same end-of-life service termination activity.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of agreements supporting active batching systems terminated by mistake during decommissioning
needs.plain: Avoid accidentally ending agreements that are still needed by systems that are actively combining or preparing batches during equipment removal. This keeps those systems working while they are still needed.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of linked service, calibration, maintenance, and consumable supply arrangements left active after system retirement
needs.plain: Make sure all connected service, testing, repair, and supply arrangements are ended when the system is retired. This prevents payments and deliveries from continuing after they are no longer needed.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of required decommissioning support unavailability after premature agreement termination
needs.plain: Keep the help needed to shut down the equipment available for longer if connected services end earlier than planned. This helps prevent delays during the shutdown.

needs.stmt: Reduce the amount of avoidable termination fees incurred when contractual notice deadlines are missed
needs.plain: Avoid unnecessary fees for ending services when required notice dates are missed. This reduces the cost of stopping services that are no longer needed.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of billing and termination disputes caused by missing or conflicting supplier confirmations
needs.plain: Resolve charges and cancellation disagreements quickly when suppliers have not confirmed the change or have given conflicting answers. This helps make sure linked services end correctly.

disposal.name: Transfer documentation package
disposal.statement: Transfer manuals, drawings, and certifications to the new owner of the plant.
disposal.description: Compile and hand over all technical documentation — operating manuals, P&IDs, as-built drawings, calibration certificates — to the incoming owner or operator.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of required manuals, drawings, or certificates omitted from the transferred documentation package

disposal.name: Transfer equipment ownership
disposal.statement: Transfer ownership of the batch preparation system to a new facility or buyer.
disposal.description: Matters when the equipment retains resale value to another facility or plant.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of required equipment-history and compliance records missing at handover
Reduce the number of required equipment history and transfer records missing at closing

disposal.name: Transfer plant ownership
disposal.statement: Transfer legal ownership of the processing plant to its next custodian.
disposal.description: Execute legal instruments — bills of sale, title transfers, asset registers — to formally convey ownership of the plant or its components to a buyer, lessee, or donee.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that undisclosed liens or third-party interests prevent clear plant title from passing
needs.plain: Reduce the chance that hidden debts or other people's ownership claims make it impossible to transfer the factory equipment with clear ownership. This helps the buyer receive undisputed ownership.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of plant components omitted from the executed transfer instruments
needs.plain: List every plant component in the documents used to transfer ownership. This makes clear exactly what equipment and parts were handed over.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that transfer instruments are executed without valid signatory authority
needs.plain: Make sure the people signing the transfer have official permission to do so. This helps ensure the ownership change is legally valid.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of plant asset identifiers that conflict across executed ownership records
needs.plain: Make sure each plant asset has the same identifying number in all ownership records. Matching records help prevent confusion about which equipment was transferred.

needs.stmt: Minimize the time between the agreed ownership-transfer date and legal completion
needs.plain: Complete the legal transfer as soon as the agreed transfer date arrives. This makes the change in ownership official without unnecessary delay.

disposal.name: Transfer processed output
disposal.statement: Transfer processed liquid from the plant to the designated receiving system.
disposal.description: Move finished or semi-finished liquid product from the plant's discharge point to downstream storage, packaging, or further processing.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of the processed liquid becoming contaminated between plant discharge and receipt
Minimize the likelihood of the processed liquid entering the wrong receiving system
Reduce the percentage of transferred liquid that falls outside release specifications during transfer
Reduce the amount of processed liquid lost through leakage or spillage during transfer
Reduce the duration of transfer stoppages caused by unavailable receiving capacity

disposal.name: Transfer system ownership
disposal.statement: Transfer or resell the system to a new owner.
disposal.description: Execute the administrative and physical steps required to transfer legal ownership of the system to another party.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of residual product or hazardous cleaning material remaining in the system at transfer
needs.plain: Make sure no product or dangerous cleaning material is left in the system when it is handed over. This helps protect the people who take responsibility for it next.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of sterile fill-finish production disruption caused by system removal and handover
Minimize the likelihood of legal ownership of the batching system remaining disputed after transfer
needs.plain: Make clear who legally owns the mixing system after it is handed over. This prevents later arguments about ownership and responsibility.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of required validation, maintenance, and safety records that are missing or inconsistent at handover
needs.plain: Make sure the new owner receives complete and consistent records about testing, upkeep, and safety. This matters so they can operate and care for the system safely after the handover.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of batching system components damaged during removal and relocation
needs.plain: Avoid damaging parts of the material-mixing system when it is taken apart and moved. This helps keep the equipment usable and reduces repair or replacement work.

disposal.name: Transition batching operations
disposal.statement: Transition batching operations to the replacement system.
disposal.description: Coordinate the handover of production workload, process parameters, and operational responsibilities from the retiring system to its replacement.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that sterile product is contaminated during the operational handover
Minimize the duration of sterile fill-finish batching interruption during the transition
Minimize the percentage of process parameters transferred to the replacement system that differ from approved settings
needs.plain: Make sure the operating settings moved to the replacement system are the same as the approved settings, so the process works as intended.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of production batches without a clear system assignment during the transition
needs.plain: Fewer production batches should be left without a clear connection to the system that made them during the changeover. This makes each batch easier to identify and track.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of operational responsibilities without clear ownership after handover
needs.plain: Make sure every ongoing work duty has a clearly named owner after the handover. This prevents important duties from being left undone.

disposal.name: Transition to replacement
disposal.statement: Transition production from the retired skid to its replacement unit.
disposal.description: Manages the changeover from the retired skid to its replacement so production continuity is maintained.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the replacement skid fails to maintain validated conditions after switchover
Reduce the percentage of batch recipes and preparation parameters transferred incorrectly to the replacement system
Minimize the likelihood that the replacement system lacks required production release at cutover
Reduce the duration of sterile fill-finish production interruption during cutover
needs.plain: Spend less time stopping germ-free medicine production while switching from the old equipment or process to its replacement. This helps the new setup begin work sooner.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of sterile product becoming contaminated during the transition
needs.plain: Avoid allowing the sterile product to become dirty or unsafe while changing to the replacement. Keeping it clean protects its quality and safety.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of critical process parameters, control limits, or batch records transferred incorrectly or omitted during cutover
needs.plain: Avoid transferring important process settings, operating limits, or production records incorrectly or leaving them out during the changeover. Accurate transfer helps the replacement system run consistently and safely.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of replacement plant output shortfall relative to sterile fill-finish demand after cutover
needs.plain: Make sure the replacement plant can produce enough sterile, ready-to-fill product after the changeover, so demand is not left unmet.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of required materials, qualified personnel, or utilities being unavailable at cutover
needs.plain: Reduce the chance that needed supplies, trained people, or basic services are unavailable when work moves to the replacement equipment. This helps the changeover happen without disruption.

disposal.name: Verify decontamination clearance before removal
disposal.statement: Confirm that the decontaminated skid meets applicable release criteria before further handling.
disposal.description: Conduct or review the decontamination verification steps required to confirm the skid is cleared for safe handling, transport, or disposal.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of residual skid contamination remaining undetected before removal clearance

usage.name: Adjust capture parameters
usage.statement: Adjust identifier capture parameters for current line conditions.
usage.description: This maintains reliable data capture when package materials, code placement, line speed, or environmental conditions vary.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of valid package identifiers not captured under current line conditions after adjustment
needs.plain: After the settings are changed, the system should still record the valid identification numbers on packages under the current working conditions. This helps prevent packages from being missed or recorded incorrectly.

usage.name: Adjust operating settings
usage.statement: Adjust scanning, marking, and rejection settings during operation to maintain capture performance.
usage.description: Modifies reader sensitivity, marking parameters, or rejection thresholds mid-run to compensate for line-speed changes, material variation, or environmental drift.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of packages with missing or unreadable marks passing the rejection point after settings adjustment
needs.plain: Reduce the chance that packages with missing or unreadable marks continue past the point where they should be removed after settings are changed. This helps ensure packages can be identified correctly.

usage.name: Aggregate packaging hierarchy
usage.statement: Aggregate individual units into cases and pallets to build the packaging hierarchy record.
usage.description: Groups scanned units into their parent packaging levels (case, pallet) to maintain an accurate hierarchical traceability record during production.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of scanned units associated with the wrong case or pallet
needs.plain: Make sure each scanned item is matched to the correct box or shipping platform, so items can be tracked and shipped correctly.

usage.name: Annotate traceability exceptions
usage.statement: Annotate a traceability exception with relevant operational details.
usage.description: This provides context for later investigation, review, or record disposition.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of linking an exception annotation to the wrong batch, lot, or packaging event

usage.name: Capture unit identifiers
usage.statement: Capture unique identifiers on each unit as it passes along the packaging line.
usage.description: Core function performed every shift as products move down the line and must be individually or batch-identified.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of passing units whose identifiers are not captured
needs.plain: Make sure the identifying numbers or codes are recorded for nearly every unit that passes inspection. This makes it possible to track each unit later.

usage.name: Coordinate multi-line operation
usage.statement: Coordinate traceability data across multiple packaging lines or sites during concurrent production.
usage.description: Manages simultaneous traceability sessions across parallel lines or geographically distributed sites, ensuring consistent identifier scoping and data integrity across all active runs.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of identifier collisions across concurrent production runs
needs.plain: Prevent different production runs happening at the same time from being given the same identifying number or name. Clear identification helps workers keep materials and records matched to the right run.

usage.name: Demonstrate system operation
usage.statement: Demonstrate traceability system operation to new operators during training runs.
usage.description: New operators need supervised runs to learn how to capture, verify, and reject correctly.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of live production traceability records incorrectly altered during a training run

usage.name: Export traceability records
usage.statement: Export selected traceability records from the system.
usage.description: This makes package histories available for external review, exchange, analysis, or evidence needs.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of selected traceability records omitted from the export

usage.name: Generate production reports
usage.statement: Generate reports on read rates, rejects, and throughput from captured traceability data.
usage.description: Produces structured operational summaries covering scanner read-rate performance, unit rejection counts, and line throughput metrics derived from the traceability data store.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of captured unit events missing required scanner, rejection, or timestamp fields
needs.plain: Make sure every recorded production event includes the required scanner information, rejection information, and time. Complete records make it possible to understand what happened during production.

usage.name: Handle duplicate identifiers
usage.statement: Resolve duplicate identifier exceptions detected during line operation.
usage.description: This prevents conflicting identity records from compromising package traceability.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of affected packages with duplicate identifiers left undiscovered during exception handling
needs.plain: Reduce the number of packages with repeated identification numbers that go unnoticed while the problem is being handled. This helps ensure each package can be tracked correctly.

usage.name: Load batch parameters
usage.statement: Load the batch, lot, and product parameters into the system before starting a run.
usage.description: Each new order or SKU requires loading the correct coding and traceability parameters before running.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that incorrect batch, lot, or product values are loaded for the scheduled order
needs.plain: Load the correct production, lot, and product information for the scheduled order. Wrong settings could cause the wrong product or batch to be made.

usage.name: Monitor line status
usage.statement: Monitor traceability system status and read rates during active production runs.
usage.description: Observes real-time system indicators, camera/scanner health, and code-read success rates to detect degradation before it affects traceability integrity.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that declining code-read success remains undetected until traceability integrity is affected
needs.plain: Make it very unlikely that a decline in the system's ability to read labels or codes goes unnoticed until product tracking is affected. This helps keep the right products linked to the right information.

usage.name: Query package genealogy
usage.statement: Query the traceability system for a package genealogy record.
usage.description: This retrieves the recorded relationships and events associated with a selected identifier.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of required package relationships and events missing from the returned genealogy
needs.plain: Make sure the returned product history includes all required links between packages and all important events involving them. This helps people trace what happened to each package.

usage.name: Reconcile missing codes
usage.statement: Reconcile units with missing or damaged codes by manually re-identifying them during use.
usage.description: Damaged codes or missed units sometimes require manual re-identification to keep records complete.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of assigning an incorrect identity to a unit during manual reconciliation
needs.plain: Avoid matching a unit to the wrong identity when its identifying code is missing. Correct matching keeps records and products connected to the right unit.

usage.name: Reconcile package counts
usage.statement: Reconcile tracked package counts against physical line processing counts.
usage.description: Compares the traceability system's unit count records with actual line counters or physical tallies to identify and resolve discrepancies in recorded versus processed quantities.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of processed packages affected by missing or duplicate traceability records
needs.plain: Reduce the number of packages missing a tracking record or having the same record more than once. Accurate records help show where each package came from and what happened to it.

usage.name: Record packaging events
usage.statement: Record each packaging event against the relevant unit identifier in the traceability system.
usage.description: Writes timestamped process events — such as filling, sealing, labelling, and inspection results — against the corresponding unit record to maintain a complete event log.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of packaging events linked to incorrect unit identifiers
needs.plain: Reduce the number of packaging records connected to the wrong product or container number. This helps keep the history of what was packed accurate and traceable.

usage.name: Record traceability data
usage.statement: Record captured identifiers and event data to the traceability repository during runs.
usage.description: Regulatory and audit requirements demand that captured serialization data be recorded and stored.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of captured identifier and event records missing from the traceability repository after a production run
needs.plain: Make sure all captured identification and event information is saved in the production tracking records after the run. Missing records make it harder to know what happened and which items were affected.

usage.name: Recover interrupted tracking
usage.statement: Recover traceability tracking after an operational interruption.
usage.description: This preserves record continuity after power loss, network failure, line stoppage, or system restart.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of permanent traceability gaps for units handled during an interruption
needs.plain: Avoid permanently losing the records that show what happened to each item when work is interrupted. This makes it possible to track every item afterward.

usage.name: Reject non-conforming units
usage.statement: Reject units that fail identification or verification checks from the packaging line.
usage.description: Non-conforming units must be automatically removed to keep untraceable product out of the flow.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of nonconforming units remaining in the packaging flow after a rejection command
needs.plain: Reduce the number of defective products that continue through the packaging line after the machine is told to remove them. This helps keep unacceptable products from reaching the next step or customers.

usage.name: Report serialization data
usage.statement: Report captured serialization and aggregation data to upstream or downstream systems.
usage.description: Transmits or exports serialization and hierarchical aggregation records to connected internal or regulatory-facing systems at the close of a production event.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of captured serialization records omitted from the production-event report

usage.name: Resolve missed reads
usage.statement: Resolve package records affected by missed identifier reads.
usage.description: This restores traceability when a package passes a capture point without a valid reading.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of associating a recovered identifier with the wrong package record
needs.plain: Make sure a recovered identification number is matched to the correct package record. A wrong match could make the package history inaccurate.

usage.name: Switch production context
usage.statement: Switch the active production context within the traceability system at changeover.
usage.description: Selects or activates a new product, batch, or line configuration profile in the system when transitioning between production runs, ensuring identifier scoping and parameters are correctly reset.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of activating a production context that does not match the scheduled run
needs.plain: Prevent the plant from starting with the wrong set of production details. This helps ensure that the planned product is made using the correct instructions.

usage.name: Trace individual units
usage.statement: Trace an individual unit or lot through the packaging line using recorded traceability data.
usage.description: Queries the recorded identifier and event data to reconstruct the full processing history of a specific unit or lot, supporting investigation or verification workflows.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of required processing events missing from the recorded unit history
needs.plain: Make sure the required steps for each individual item are recorded in its history. This helps people know what happened to each item and spot missing information.

usage.name: Track package movement
usage.statement: Track each identified package as it moves through the packaging line stations.
usage.description: Maintains a real-time position and status record for each identified package across defined line stations, enabling in-flight visibility into where each unit is in the process.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of identified packages passing line stations without position or status updates
needs.plain: Make sure every identified package receives an update about its position and current status as it moves through each station. This makes it possible to know where each package is and what has happened to it.

usage.name: Troubleshoot capture errors
usage.statement: Troubleshoot read failures and reject errors that occur during operation.
usage.description: Faults such as missed reads or jams must be diagnosed and cleared to resume traceable production.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of read-failure and reject incidents lacking complete diagnostic evidence

usage.name: Validate aggregation completeness
usage.statement: Validate that each parent packaging level contains the expected number of child units before sealing.
usage.description: Checks that the count of associated child identifiers within a case or pallet record matches the configured fill quantity prior to closing or palletising, preventing under- or over-filled shipment units from leaving the line.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of physically present child units absent from the parent record at validation
needs.plain: Make sure every physical smaller unit is included in the record for the larger unit during the check. This prevents missing equipment from being overlooked.

usage.name: Verify code readability
usage.statement: Verify that each applied code is present and machine-readable during production.
usage.description: Confirms that the code on each unit is present and readable so unmarked product does not proceed.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that a unit with a missing or machine-unreadable code is accepted during production
needs.plain: Make sure an item is not accepted if its identifying code is missing or cannot be read by the machine.

disposal.name: Archive compliance data
disposal.statement: Archive the traceability compliance data to long-term storage for the required retention period.
disposal.description: Matters when retention rules require traceability history to be preserved for a mandated period after decommissioning.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of required traceability records missing from the long-term archive
needs.plain: Keep all required records showing what happened during the work in the long-term storage system. This helps people prove what was done and review it later.

disposal.name: Cancel software licenses and associated services
disposal.statement: Cancel the traceability software licenses, service subscriptions, and any other paid services associated with the retired system.
disposal.description: Formally terminate all active software license agreements, maintenance contracts, and subscription services tied to the retired traceability system to stop ongoing charges and obligations.
disposal.frequency: Once at end of system lifecycle

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of paid licenses or associated services remaining active after decommissioning

disposal.name: Deregister serialization identifiers
disposal.statement: Deregister the system's serialization identifiers from external tracking repositories.
disposal.description: Matters when serialization data was registered with government or industry repositories that must be updated at end of life.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of retired serialization identifiers remaining active in external repositories

disposal.name: Disconnect system integrations
disposal.statement: Disconnect the traceability system from connected enterprise and line-control systems.
disposal.description: Sever all data and control interfaces between the retiring traceability system and upstream/downstream systems such as ERP, MES, and line automation controllers.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of active connections remaining after the traceability system is retired

disposal.name: Dispose of hazardous materials
disposal.statement: Dispose of hazardous consumables and components from marking and coding units according to regulations.
disposal.description: Matters when marking systems contain inks, solvents, or batteries classified as hazardous materials.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of hazardous material escaping into the controlled packaging environment during disposal

disposal.name: Document decommissioning
disposal.statement: Document the final state and decommissioning of the traceability system for audit records.
disposal.description: Matters because auditors and validation teams require documented evidence of proper decommissioning.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of required decommissioning evidence missing from the audit record

disposal.name: Notify affected stakeholders of system retirement
disposal.statement: Notify internal and external stakeholders of the traceability system retirement.
disposal.description: Communicate the planned or completed retirement of the traceability system to all parties whose operations, compliance obligations, or supply-chain visibility depend on it.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of affected internal and external stakeholders omitted from the retirement notification list

disposal.name: Purge sensitive data from retired system
disposal.statement: Purge sensitive and proprietary data from the traceability system's storage devices.
disposal.description: Remove all confidential, proprietary, and regulated data from system storage to prevent unauthorized access after the system leaves organizational control.

needs.stmt: Reduce the amount of recoverable sensitive data remaining on retired storage devices after purging

disposal.name: Recycle electronic components
disposal.statement: Recycle the electronic hardware components of the traceability system through certified channels.
disposal.description: Route recoverable circuit boards, displays, sensors, and other electronic assemblies through a certified e-waste recycling process in compliance with applicable environmental regulations.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of retired electronic assemblies routed through uncertified recycling channels

disposal.name: Remove installed hardware
disposal.statement: Remove traceability scanners, cameras, and marking hardware from the packaging line.
disposal.description: Physically detach and retrieve all hardware components of the traceability system — including readers, cameras, and coders — from their installed production locations.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of dust or debris entering the controlled packaging area during hardware removal

disposal.name: Return equipment under vendor recovery program
disposal.statement: Return eligible traceability system equipment to the original vendor through an applicable take-back or trade-in program.
disposal.description: Coordinate the logistics, documentation, and credit or disposition terms required to return end-of-life traceability hardware to the original equipment manufacturer or authorized recovery channel.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that returned equipment is refused because required decontamination clearance is absent

disposal.name: Revoke access artifacts
disposal.statement: Invalidate all authentication artifacts associated with the retired system.
disposal.description: This prevents obsolete accounts, certificates, keys, or tokens from retaining access to connected resources.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of authentication artifacts associated with the retired system omitted from the revocation scope

disposal.name: Transfer system assets to designated recipient
disposal.statement: Reassign ownership of the traceability system assets to the designated recipient.
disposal.description: Formally transfer title, documentation, and custody of traceability system hardware and associated assets to the receiving party, whether an internal facility, reseller, or third party.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that transferred hardware retains regulated production or confidential data

disposal.name: Transition traceability operations to replacement system
disposal.statement: Transition traceability operations from the retired system to its replacement.
disposal.description: Coordinate the cutover of active traceability functions — including serial number ranges, operational rules, and workflows — from the retiring system to the incoming replacement.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of packaging events not captured by the replacement system during cutover

maintenance.name: Archive historical traceability records
maintenance.statement: Archive aged traceability records and logs to maintain system performance and meet long-term retention requirements.
maintenance.description: Move historical serialization records, event logs, and audit trails to long-term storage tiers to free active system capacity while preserving regulatory access.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of historical traceability data lost or corrupted during archival

maintenance.name: Back up system configuration
maintenance.statement: Back up the traceability system configuration.
maintenance.description: Configuration backups enable recovery from hardware failure, corruption, or unintended administrative changes.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of required traceability-system configuration elements omitted from the backup

maintenance.name: Back up traceability data
maintenance.statement: Back up the serialization, aggregation, and event data captured by the traceability system according to the configured retention policy.
maintenance.description: Perform scheduled or triggered backups of all production traceability records to prevent data loss and support regulatory retention requirements.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of required serialization, aggregation, and event records omitted from the backup

maintenance.name: Calibrate vision readers
maintenance.statement: Calibrate the vision systems and code readers of the traceability line.
maintenance.description: Vision and reader accuracy drifts, requiring periodic recalibration against reference standards to maintain read reliability.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that vision readers remain outside tolerance after calibration

maintenance.name: Clean scanning components
maintenance.statement: Clean the cameras, scanners, and optical sensors of the traceability system.
maintenance.description: Remove contamination, dust, and debris from optical reading components to sustain read performance.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of scanner and camera optical surfaces retaining contamination after cleaning

maintenance.name: Document maintenance history
maintenance.statement: Document the maintenance, calibration, and validation history of the traceability system.
maintenance.description: Record completed maintenance activities, calibration results, and validation events to support regulatory audit trails and ongoing reliability tracking.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of completed maintenance, calibration, or validation events omitted from the system history

maintenance.name: Inspect hardware for wear
maintenance.statement: Inspect the conveyors, rejectors, sensors, and cabling for wear or damage.
maintenance.description: Conveyors, rejectors, sensors, and cabling wear over time and must be checked to prevent unplanned line stoppages.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of worn or damaged conveyors, rejectors, sensors, and cables missed during inspection

maintenance.name: Manage storage capacity
maintenance.statement: Manage the storage capacity allocated to traceability records, images, logs, and backups.
maintenance.description: Monitor consumed and available storage, enforce retention policies, and expand or reconfigure storage resources to prevent capacity-related system failures.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that traceability storage is exhausted before additional capacity is available

maintenance.name: Monitor system health
maintenance.statement: Monitor the operational status, performance metrics, and alert conditions of the traceability system.
maintenance.description: Continuously observe system health indicators — uptime, read rates, error counts, queue depths, and hardware status — to detect and respond to degradation before line impact.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of traceability-system degradation occurring without an alert
needs.plain: Make sure a decline in the system that tracks product history is detected and reported, rather than going unnoticed.

maintenance.name: Protect system components from environmental damage
maintenance.statement: Protect traceability system hardware from moisture, dust, vibration, and other environmental hazards present in the packaging environment.
maintenance.description: Apply protective covers, enclosures, or environmental controls to cameras, readers, controllers, and cabling to prevent premature failure from harsh production conditions.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of moisture or cleaning fluids reaching energized traceability components during packaging or sanitation

maintenance.name: Repair failed components
maintenance.statement: Repair or replace failed readers, printers, controllers, or reject mechanisms.
maintenance.description: Faulty readers, printers, or controllers must be repaired to restore full traceability coverage on the line.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of incomplete traceability coverage while a failed component is repaired or replaced
needs.plain: Keep the period as short as possible when equipment records cannot fully show what happened while a failed part is repaired or replaced. This helps maintain a clear history of the process.

maintenance.name: Replace lighting elements
maintenance.statement: Replace the illumination and lighting elements supporting code reading.
maintenance.description: Reader illumination and lighting elements degrade and require replacement to maintain consistent read quality.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of uneven or incorrectly directed illumination produced by the replacement element

maintenance.name: Replace printing consumables
maintenance.statement: Replace the ink, ribbons, or label media used by the marking and coding units.
maintenance.description: Marking and coding units consume ink, ribbons, or label stock that must be replenished to keep printing serialized codes.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of loading ink, ribbon, or label media incompatible with the marking unit or packaging application

maintenance.name: Revalidate after changes
maintenance.statement: Revalidate the traceability system following configuration or code-format changes.
maintenance.description: After changes to code formats or line configuration, the system must be revalidated to confirm accurate reads and data integrity.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of changed line configurations omitted from the revalidation scope

maintenance.name: Schedule preventive maintenance
maintenance.statement: Schedule preventive maintenance activities for the traceability line equipment.
maintenance.description: Plan and coordinate recurring preventive maintenance windows for all traceability hardware and software components to minimize unplanned downtime.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of traceability components omitted from the preventive-maintenance schedule

maintenance.name: Store system components properly
maintenance.statement: Store spare traceability system components and consumables under conditions that preserve their functionality and shelf life.
maintenance.description: Follow manufacturer and facility storage guidelines for spare readers, printers, controllers, lighting elements, and consumables to prevent degradation before deployment.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of spare-component and consumable exposure to storage conditions outside allowable ranges

maintenance.name: Synchronize system time
maintenance.statement: Synchronize traceability-system clocks with the designated time source.
maintenance.description: Accurate time synchronization preserves the sequence and correlation of packaging-line events across connected components.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of divergence between traceability-system clocks and the designated source

maintenance.name: Test recovery procedures
maintenance.statement: Test the procedures used to restore traceability services after a failure.
maintenance.description: Periodic testing verifies that backups, replacement infrastructure, and recovery instructions can return the system to operation.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of restored traceability records that are incomplete or inconsistent

maintenance.name: Update application software
maintenance.statement: Update the traceability application software to an approved release.
maintenance.description: Software updates correct defects, improve compatibility, and address security vulnerabilities.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of traceability records or configuration settings corrupted or lost during the update

maintenance.name: Update device firmware
maintenance.statement: Update firmware on supported traceability devices to an approved release.
maintenance.description: Firmware maintenance preserves device stability, interoperability, and security over the equipment lifecycle.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of a traceability device becoming inoperable after an interrupted firmware update
needs.plain: Make it less likely that a device used to track products or equipment stops working after a software update is interrupted. This helps preserve tracking information and device operation.

maintenance.name: Upgrade system components
maintenance.statement: Upgrade the hardware or software modules of the traceability system.
maintenance.description: Growing volumes, new products, or new coding standards require upgrading modules or capacity to keep pace.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that upgraded modules are incompatible with the existing traceability system

maintenance.name: Verify read performance
maintenance.statement: Verify that the traceability system reliably reads representative package identifiers under production conditions.
maintenance.description: Run structured read-rate verification tests using representative samples to confirm that code readers meet defined performance thresholds before and after maintenance or changeover.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of intermittent package-identifier read failures remaining undetected during verification
needs.plain: Make sure occasional failures to read package labels are found during checking. This prevents packages from being passed as acceptable when their labels were missed.

acquisition.name: Arrange support agreement
acquisition.statement: Arrange a service and support agreement for the traceability system.
acquisition.description: Negotiate and finalize post-sale service, maintenance, and support commitments with the traceability system supplier before or at time of purchase.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of critical service, maintenance, and regulatory-support commitments omitted from the agreement

acquisition.name: Assess integration requirements
acquisition.statement: Assess the system's integration requirements with existing plant systems.
acquisition.description: Identify data interfaces, communication protocols, and middleware needs for connecting a candidate traceability system to plant-floor and enterprise data systems.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of required plant-floor and enterprise interfaces omitted from the integration assessment

acquisition.name: Compare competing systems
acquisition.statement: Compare competing traceability systems on features, accuracy, and total cost.
acquisition.description: Evaluate multiple candidate traceability systems side-by-side using a defined set of acquisition criteria covering technical capabilities, performance, and cost.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of critical traceability requirements omitted from the side-by-side comparison

acquisition.name: Complete purchase transaction
acquisition.statement: Complete the transaction for the selected packaging line traceability system.
acquisition.description: Execute the final purchase order, payment, and documentation steps to formally acquire the selected traceability system.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of purchase-order terms conflicting with the approved commercial and technical agreement

acquisition.name: Conduct pre-installation site survey
acquisition.statement: Conduct a pre-installation site survey to verify line dimensions, utilities, and mounting surfaces meet system requirements.
acquisition.description: Assess the physical installation site before equipment arrival to confirm infrastructure readiness and identify any gaps requiring remediation.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of required line dimensions or clearances omitted or recorded inaccurately during the site survey

acquisition.name: Define system requirements
acquisition.statement: Define the requirements the packaging line traceability system must satisfy.
acquisition.description: Document functional, technical, regulatory, and integration requirements that a traceability system must meet before initiating vendor evaluation.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of required functional, technical, regulatory, and integration requirements omitted from the specification
needs.plain: The written system plan should leave out as few important needs as possible, including practical, technical, legal, and connection needs. A complete plan helps ensure the finished system does what is required.

acquisition.name: Determine total cost of ownership
acquisition.statement: Determine the total cost of ownership for the traceability system.
acquisition.description: Estimate capital, installation, integration, training, maintenance, and end-of-life costs to assess the full financial commitment of acquiring a packaging line traceability system.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of expected lifecycle costs omitted from the ownership estimate

acquisition.name: Evaluate line compatibility
acquisition.statement: Evaluate whether a traceability system fits the existing packaging line configuration.
acquisition.description: Assess mechanical, electrical, and software compatibility of candidate traceability systems against the current packaging line layout and throughput requirements.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the candidate system cannot sustain required throughput under the expected product mix
needs.plain: Make sure the proposed system can keep up with the required production speed for the expected variety of products.

acquisition.name: Negotiate purchase contract
acquisition.statement: Negotiate the purchase contract and service terms for the traceability system.
acquisition.description: Complex projects require negotiating installation, commissioning, and support terms within the purchase contract.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that negotiated data-integrity and validation responsibilities conflict with sterile fill-finish compliance obligations

acquisition.name: Obtain purchase approval
acquisition.statement: Obtain internal budget and procurement approval for the traceability system.
acquisition.description: Capital equipment of this type typically requires internal budget approval and justification.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of material benefits and risk-reduction claims lacking supporting evidence in the approval package

acquisition.name: Provision administrator access
acquisition.statement: Provision initial administrative access to the acquired packaging line traceability system.
acquisition.description: This establishes the accounts and credentials needed for subsequent system configuration, integration, and user administration.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of required administrative functions unavailable to the initial administrator account

acquisition.name: Register and license system
acquisition.statement: Register the traceability system and activate its software licenses.
acquisition.description: Software-based systems require license activation and registration before commissioning can begin.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that issued licenses do not cover purchased modules or deployment scope

acquisition.name: Request pilot or demonstration
acquisition.statement: Request a pilot or demonstration of the traceability system on sample products.
acquisition.description: A pilot or demo on representative products confirms read rates and code quality before full commitment.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree to which pilot samples differ from intended sterile fill-finish production conditions

acquisition.name: Research traceability system options
acquisition.statement: Research available packaging line traceability systems and their capabilities.
acquisition.description: Identify and gather information on traceability systems for packaging lines, including vendors, architectures, and feature sets.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of relevant packaging-line traceability systems omitted from the market scan

acquisition.name: Seek vendor references
acquisition.statement: Seek references and reviews from other users of the traceability system.
acquisition.description: Peer references and case studies reduce risk when committing to a capital traceability investment.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of references not representing comparable sterile fill-finish environments

acquisition.name: Select system configuration
acquisition.statement: Select the packaging line traceability system configuration required for the intended deployment.
acquisition.description: Determine the specific hardware, software, and integration configuration of the traceability system to be purchased based on requirements and evaluation outcomes.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the selected configuration cannot support required traceability records and data-integrity controls
needs.plain: Choose a system setup that can record who did what and preserve the accuracy of those records. This makes the information trustworthy and reviewable.

acquisition.name: Track order fulfillment
acquisition.statement: Track fulfillment of the packaging line traceability system order.
acquisition.description: This provides visibility into component availability, shipment status, license delivery, and expected receipt dates.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of ordered traceability-system components lacking current fulfillment status

acquisition.name: Verify received equipment
acquisition.statement: Verify that received traceability equipment is complete, undamaged, and conforms to the purchase order.
acquisition.description: Unpack delivered traceability system hardware, inspect all components against the packing list and purchase order, and confirm no items are missing or damaged before proceeding to installation.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of required traceability-system components missing from the delivery

acquisition.name: Verify regulatory compliance support
acquisition.statement: Verify that a traceability system supports required regulatory coding and reporting standards.
acquisition.description: Buyers must confirm the system supports the regulatory coding and reporting standards applicable to their industry.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of claimed regulatory capabilities lacking verifiable compliance evidence
needs.plain: Make sure more of the claimed legal and safety requirements have clear proof that they are being met. This makes compliance claims trustworthy.

preparation.name: Calibrate vision inspection
preparation.statement: Calibrate cameras and vision systems for accurate code reading and verification.
preparation.description: Proper calibration prevents false rejects and missed defects at production speeds.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of defective or unreadable traceability codes accepted after calibration
needs.plain: Prevent defective or unreadable tracking codes from being accepted after the camera-based inspection system is adjusted. This helps ensure products can be identified and followed through the process.

preparation.name: Configure coding parameters
preparation.statement: Configure code formats, serialization schemes, and data structures for products.
preparation.description: Regulatory and customer coding standards dictate the encoded data on each unit.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of required regulatory or customer data elements omitted from configured code structures
needs.plain: Make sure all information required by authorities or customers is included in the product codes. This helps ensure the codes are complete and meet the required expectations.

preparation.name: Configure packaging-line station topology
preparation.statement: Configure the packaging-line station layout within the traceability system.
preparation.description: Define the physical and logical layout of packaging stations so the traceability system correctly tracks product flow.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of logical station connections not matching physical product flow

preparation.name: Configure rejection thresholds and station behavior
preparation.statement: Configure reject thresholds and station behavior for non-conforming units.
preparation.description: Define the conditions under which units are rejected and the automated response at each reject station.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of nonconforming units classified as acceptable by configured reject thresholds

preparation.name: Connect to network
preparation.statement: Connect the traceability system to the plant network and data infrastructure.
preparation.description: Serialization and aggregation data must flow to upstream and downstream systems.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of serialization and aggregation messages failing to reach required upstream or downstream systems
needs.plain: Make sure the tracking messages needed by connected systems are delivered successfully. This prevents those systems from losing important information about products or packages.

preparation.name: Connect to power supply
preparation.statement: Connect the traceability equipment to the facility power supply.
preparation.description: Devices require stable power before any commissioning or configuration can occur.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that facility supply characteristics are incompatible with traceability-equipment input requirements

preparation.name: Define and configure serialization numbering pools
preparation.statement: Define and configure the serialization number pools and allocation rules used to generate unique identifiers.
preparation.description: Set up the numeric ranges, check-digit rules, and allocation logic that govern how unique serial numbers are generated and reserved to prevent duplication.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of duplicate identifiers being generated because serialization pools overlap
needs.plain: Prevent the system from creating the same identifying number more than once because separate number ranges overlap. Unique numbers help each item be tracked correctly.

preparation.name: Deploy traceability software
preparation.statement: Deploy the traceability software within the designated computing environment.
preparation.description: Deployment makes the system services and operator applications available for configuration.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that deployed traceability services fail to start in the designated environment

preparation.name: Establish exception-handling workflows
preparation.statement: Establish the system workflows used to handle traceability exceptions.
preparation.description: Define and configure the process flows the system follows when traceability errors, misreads, or out-of-sequence events occur.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of traceability exception scenarios lacking a defined handling path
needs.plain: Provide a clear way to handle every unusual situation that can make product records incomplete or inconsistent. This helps workers know what to do when normal tracking does not work.

preparation.name: Import product master data
preparation.statement: Import product, batch, and packaging master data into the traceability system.
preparation.description: Accurate product definitions are required before jobs can run against them.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of product, batch, or packaging records imported with incorrect identifiers or attributes
needs.plain: Make sure product and packaging information is entered with the right names and details. This prevents mistakes later when the products are made or tracked.

preparation.name: Integrate with enterprise systems
preparation.statement: Integrate the traceability system with ERP, MES, and warehouse management systems.
preparation.description: Serialization data must reconcile with production orders and inventory records.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of serialization transactions lost or duplicated during enterprise-system transfer
needs.plain: Prevent tracking records from being lost or recorded twice when information moves between company computer systems. This keeps product records complete and accurate.

preparation.name: Integrate with line equipment
preparation.statement: Integrate the traceability system with existing packaging line machinery and PLCs.
preparation.description: Reject stations, conveyors, and labelers must coordinate with the traceability controller.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of conveyed packages associated with incorrect traceability records
needs.plain: Reduce the number of packages whose records say the wrong thing about where they came from or what happened to them. Accurate records make it possible to follow each package through the process.

preparation.name: Load code templates
preparation.statement: Load and assign printing and marking templates for each product SKU.
preparation.description: Correct templates ensure the right layout and data print on each package.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of product SKUs assigned an incorrect printing or marking template
needs.plain: Make sure each product is assigned the correct printing or marking instructions, so packages show the right information.

preparation.name: Mount hardware at line stations
preparation.statement: Mount scanners, cameras, and marking devices onto the packaging line at the correct positions.
preparation.description: Physically position and secure traceability hardware components at designated stations along the packaging line.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of mounting traceability hardware at an incorrect position or orientation

preparation.name: Set up aggregation hierarchy
preparation.statement: Set up the parent-child aggregation hierarchy linking units to cartons and pallets.
preparation.description: Aggregation defines how individual codes roll up into cases and shipments for traceability.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of parent-child links assigning units or cartons to the wrong parent
needs.plain: Make sure each unit or carton is linked to the correct larger package, case, or pallet. Wrong links make it difficult to track where products belong.

preparation.name: Set up user access roles
preparation.statement: Set up user accounts, roles, and access permissions for system operators.
preparation.description: Access control ensures only authorized personnel change coding or override rejects.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of operator accounts receiving privileges beyond approved responsibilities

preparation.name: Synchronize system clocks
preparation.statement: Synchronize the clocks used by traceability system components.
preparation.description: Consistent timestamps are necessary to sequence events correctly across stations, devices, and connected systems.
