needs.stmt: Reduce the number of traceability components omitted from the synchronization scope

preparation.name: Train operators on system
preparation.statement: Train line operators to run, monitor, and troubleshoot the traceability system.
preparation.description: Operators must handle job changeovers, rejects, and alarms without stopping production.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of operators completing training without demonstrating correct changeover, rejection, and alarm handling

preparation.name: Validate system performance
preparation.statement: Validate the traceability system against regulatory and quality qualification protocols.
preparation.description: Regulated industries require documented IQ/OQ/PQ before production release.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of critical traceability functions failing qualification tests

usage.name: Adjust changeover configuration
usage.statement: Adjust the filling machinery configuration for a container or product changeover.
usage.description: Reconfiguring filling machine parameters, tooling, and settings to accommodate a different container format or fill product.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of compromising sterile product-contact conditions while adjusting the configuration
needs.plain: Avoid allowing germs into areas that must stay clean and protected while changing the setup for the next product or run. This helps keep the product safe from contamination.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that filling parameters are set outside the approved limits for the new product or container format
Reduce the amount of previous fill product residue remaining in the product-contact path after reconfiguration
Reduce the number of containers damaged by misaligned changeover tooling during startup
needs.plain: Avoid damaging containers when tools used to switch the machine between setups are not lined up correctly during startup. This helps the machine begin work without harming the containers.

needs.stmt: Reduce the time the filling line waits for required approved change parts after the changeover begins
needs.plain: Have the approved parts needed for the new setup ready quickly, so the filling line does not sit idle during the change.

usage.name: Adjust operating settings
usage.statement: Adjust the filling machinery settings to meet current operating conditions.
usage.description: Fine adjustments to machine settings are made while observing operation to meet running conditions.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of compromising the sterile boundary while adjusting the filling machinery settings
needs.plain: Avoid letting germs into the protected clean area while changing the machine settings. This helps keep the product safe during filling.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of setting changes causing container collisions or product spills during operation
Reduce the duration of production interruption while acceptable filling machinery settings are established
Reduce the frequency of filling machinery settings drifting outside acceptable limits as running conditions change
Reduce the percentage of filled units that remain outside specified fill limits after adjustment
needs.plain: Keep the number of filled products outside the allowed fill range as low as possible after settings are changed, so products have the right amount inside.

usage.name: Authorize corrective scope
usage.statement: Authorize changes to the maintenance scope during the intervention.
usage.description: Approval is required when diagnosis reveals work beyond the previously accepted scope.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the authorized scope omits corrective work required to restore the filling machinery
Minimize the likelihood that a sterility impact of the scope change remains unidentified at authorization
Reduce the degree of disagreement between the diagnosed work and the requested scope changes
Reduce the duration of corrective work stoppage while scope changes await authorization
Reduce the amount by which the authorized corrective scope exceeds the available maintenance budget

usage.name: Capture maintenance guidance
usage.statement: Capture operating guidance provided by maintenance personnel during a service visit.
usage.description: Recording instructions, tips, and cautions communicated by service technicians to equipment operators for future reference.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of associating captured guidance with the wrong filling machine or operating condition
needs.plain: Make sure recorded instructions are linked to the correct filling machine and the conditions under which it was running. This prevents workers from following advice meant for different equipment or settings.

needs.stmt: Reduce the degree of unresolved conflict among instructions captured from maintenance personnel during the service visit
Reduce the percentage of safety-critical cautions communicated during the service visit that are omitted from the captured guidance
needs.plain: Make sure all important safety warnings given during the service visit are written down in the guidance. This matters so future workers know about hazards and how to avoid them.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of filling machinery instructions recorded with incorrect limits, settings, or sequences
needs.plain: Make sure maintenance instructions for the filling machines correctly state the limits, settings, and order of steps. Incorrect instructions could cause poor repairs or unsafe operation.

needs.stmt: Minimize the time until guidance communicated during the service visit is available to operators for future reference
needs.plain: Make the advice given during the service visit available to operators as quickly as possible for later use. This helps them remember how to care for or use the equipment correctly.

usage.name: Communicate machine symptoms
usage.statement: Communicate observed filling-machinery symptoms to maintenance personnel.
usage.description: This gives the service team direct evidence about the condition requiring attention.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of ambiguity in the communicated symptom location and operating conditions
needs.plain: Describe clearly where the problem is and what the machine was doing when it happened. This helps others understand and investigate the problem correctly.

needs.stmt: Minimize the time between observing filling-machinery symptoms and communicating them to maintenance personnel
Reduce the frequency of conflicting filling-machinery symptom accounts across operator handoffs
Reduce the percent of observed filling-machinery symptoms omitted from the communication to maintenance personnel
needs.plain: Leave out fewer signs of problems noticed on the filling machine when telling maintenance workers about them. Complete information helps them diagnose and fix the problem.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of filling-machinery symptom details communicated inaccurately to maintenance personnel
needs.plain: Make sure information about what is wrong with the filling machine is passed accurately to the maintenance workers. Clear details help them find the problem and fix it correctly.

usage.name: Coordinate concurrent service teams
usage.statement: Coordinate access among concurrent maintenance teams working on the filling machinery.
usage.description: Coordination prevents interference when multiple specialists service interconnected machinery at the same time.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of incompatible maintenance activities being performed simultaneously on interconnected filling machinery
needs.plain: Lower the chance that maintenance tasks that interfere with each other are done at the same time on connected filling machines. This helps prevent equipment damage and unsafe conditions.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that filling machinery is energized or moved while a maintenance team is servicing it
Minimize the likelihood of maintenance personnel or materials crossing sterile-area boundaries without coordinated authorization
Minimize the likelihood that maintenance teams receive conflicting access windows or machinery-status instructions
Reduce the duration maintenance teams wait for access because work windows overlap

usage.name: Demonstrate fault behavior
usage.statement: Demonstrate the filling machinery’s fault behavior for maintenance personnel.
usage.description: A controlled demonstration is useful when a condition is intermittent or difficult to describe.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of sterile filling area contamination introduced during the fault demonstration
needs.plain: Keep the clean filling area from becoming contaminated while a machine problem is deliberately demonstrated. This helps show how the machine responds without putting the area at unnecessary risk.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of mismatch between the demonstrated fault behavior and the behavior under actual operating conditions
needs.plain: Make sure the machine responds to a simulated fault the same way it would during real operation. This shows whether the safety or warning response can be trusted.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of maintenance personnel being injured during the fault demonstration
Minimize the likelihood that the intermittent machinery fault does not recur during the demonstration
Reduce the duration of sterile fill-finish production disruption caused by the fault demonstration

usage.name: Document service performed
usage.statement: Document the service work, parts used, and readings taken during the maintenance visit.
usage.description: Recording all tasks completed, components replaced, measurements captured, and observations made during a maintenance service visit.

needs.stmt: Minimize the duration between performing the service work and documenting it
Reduce the number of completed service tasks omitted from the maintenance visit record
needs.plain: Make sure every service task that was completed is included in the visit record. This gives the customer and service team a complete account of the work.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percent of replacement components documented with incorrect traceability identifiers
needs.plain: Put fewer incorrect identifying numbers or labels on replacement parts in the service records. Correct details make it possible to tell exactly which part was used and track its history.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percent of maintenance readings transcribed incorrectly in the service record
needs.plain: Write down fewer maintenance measurements incorrectly in the service record. Accurate records help others understand what was found and what work was done.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of service observations documented without enough detail for follow-up
needs.plain: Record enough detail about each service visit or finding so someone can follow up later. This helps others understand what happened and what still needs to be done.

usage.name: Execute scheduled maintenance service
usage.statement: Execute a scheduled maintenance service on the filling machinery per the active service plan.
usage.description: Carrying out all planned maintenance tasks within a scheduled service visit to preserve machinery reliability and performance.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of introducing contamination into the sterile filling environment during the service visit
needs.plain: Make it less likely that germs or other dirt enter the clean filling area during a planned service visit. This helps protect the product while the equipment is being maintained.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of critical filling machinery components left improperly adjusted or assembled during the service visit
needs.plain: Avoid leaving important parts of the filling machines wrongly adjusted or put together after maintenance. Correctly serviced equipment is more likely to work safely and properly.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of maintenance tasks with unclear or conflicting requirements in the active service plan
needs.plain: Make sure the current service plan gives clear, consistent instructions for every maintenance task. Workers should not have to guess which requirement to follow.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of planned maintenance tasks not completed during the scheduled visit because required parts, maintenance records, or access clearances are unavailable
needs.plain: Complete planned maintenance during the scheduled visit by having the needed parts, records, and site permissions ready. This prevents the visit from ending with important work unfinished.

needs.stmt: Reduce the time the technician waits after the scheduled start before the filling machinery is released for maintenance
needs.plain: The technician should not have to wait after the planned start time for the filling machines to be made available. This helps the scheduled maintenance begin on time.

usage.name: Monitor maintenance progress
usage.statement: Monitor the maintenance service’s progress against the active work scope.
usage.description: Ongoing visibility supports operational coordination during prolonged or high-impact interventions.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of discrepancy between reported maintenance progress and actual work completed
Minimize the likelihood that an issue blocking maintenance progress remains unreported to the Maintenance Coordinator
Reduce the frequency of conflicting maintenance status reports from service personnel and sterile fill-finish stakeholders
Reduce the number of active work-scope changes not communicated to the Maintenance Coordinator before execution
needs.plain: Make sure any changes to the work being done are told to the person coordinating maintenance before the work starts. This helps prevent people from working from outdated instructions.

needs.stmt: Reduce the time between maintenance schedule slippage and the Maintenance Coordinator becoming aware of it
needs.plain: There should be less time between a maintenance job falling behind schedule and the maintenance coordinator finding out. This gives the coordinator more time to respond to the delay.

usage.name: Prioritize maintenance task sequence
usage.statement: Prioritize the sequence of maintenance tasks based on machine condition and available downtime.
usage.description: Deciding the order in which service activities are performed to maximize coverage within constrained maintenance windows and address the most critical issues first.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of unnecessary contamination exposure created by the maintenance task sequence in the sterile fill-finish area
needs.plain: Arrange repair and upkeep steps to avoid exposing the clean production area to contamination more than necessary. This helps protect the product while work is being done.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that a critical filling-machine condition is addressed after less consequential maintenance needs
Minimize the likelihood that the planned maintenance sequence exceeds the available downtime window
Reduce the frequency of maintenance sequence changes after work begins because machine-condition or production-availability information changes
Reduce the number of sequenced maintenance tasks blocked by unavailable parts, access, personnel, or approvals during the maintenance window
needs.plain: Plan maintenance tasks so they are not stopped by missing parts, unavailable workers, blocked access, or missing approval during the maintenance period. This helps the work progress in the right order and finish on time.

usage.name: Provide remote support
usage.statement: Provide remote diagnostic and troubleshooting support for the filling machinery.
usage.description: Remote diagnostics allow support staff to assess and guide fixes without an on-site visit.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that troubleshooting guidance prompts an intervention that compromises sterile conditions
Minimize the likelihood of misdiagnosing the machinery fault from incomplete or misleading diagnostic evidence
Reduce the duration of the filling-line stoppage attributable to remote troubleshooting
Reduce the frequency of remote diagnostic access becoming unavailable during the support session
needs.plain: Remote help should stay connected and available for the whole support session. This lets the support person diagnose and fix problems without being there in person.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of technician waiting for on-site personnel to provide observations or perform requested checks
needs.plain: Spend less time waiting for people at the site to share what they see or carry out requested checks. This helps the technician solve the problem sooner from a distance.

usage.name: Record service outcomes
usage.statement: Record the diagnostic conclusions and corrective actions from the maintenance intervention.
usage.description: Capturing final service outcomes, diagnoses confirmed, and corrective measures taken to create a permanent record for the equipment history.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of assigning the service record to the wrong filling machine or maintenance intervention
needs.plain: Prevent a service report from being linked to the wrong filling machine or repair job. Accurate records help people know what work was actually done and where.

needs.stmt: Minimize the time between completion of the maintenance intervention and recording its service outcomes
Reduce the percentage of confirmed diagnostic conclusions that are recorded inaccurately
needs.plain: Make sure confirmed findings about the problem are written down accurately, so people can rely on the service results.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of corrective actions recorded with missing details
needs.plain: Make sure every fix or follow-up task is recorded with all the needed details, so it can be understood and completed.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of service records with an unclear post-service equipment status
needs.plain: Make sure each service record clearly states the equipment’s condition after the work is finished. This matters so everyone knows whether it is ready to use or needs more attention.

usage.name: Replace worn components
usage.statement: Replace worn or failed components on the filling machinery during a service intervention.
usage.description: Removing and substituting degraded or failed parts to restore filling machinery to operational specification.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of introducing contamination into the sterile filling zone during component replacement
needs.plain: Avoid bringing contamination into the germ-free filling area while replacing parts. This helps keep the product safe during the repair.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of installing an incompatible replacement component on the filling machinery
needs.plain: A replacement part should fit and work correctly with the filling machine. This helps prevent breakdowns, unsafe operation, or poor filling.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of technician injury from unexpected machinery movement during component removal
Reduce the number of adjacent connections or mounting surfaces damaged during component replacement
needs.plain: Avoid damaging nearby connections or the surfaces where parts are attached while replacing worn parts. This prevents extra repairs and helps the replacement work properly.

needs.stmt: Reduce the time the filling machinery remains out of service because the required replacement component is unavailable during the service intervention
needs.plain: Have the replacement part available during the repair, so the filling machine can return to service sooner.

usage.name: Report residual faults
usage.statement: Report faults that remain visible during post-maintenance testing.
usage.description: Prompt reporting allows unresolved conditions to be addressed before the service intervention closes.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that a residual fault report does not reach maintenance personnel before the service intervention closes
Minimize the time between observing a residual fault and notifying maintenance personnel
Reduce the degree of ambiguity in descriptions of residual fault symptoms and post-maintenance test conditions
Reduce the number of visible residual faults omitted from the report after post-maintenance testing
needs.plain: Make sure every visible problem still present after maintenance testing is included in the report. This gives others an honest picture of the equipment’s condition.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of residual fault reports that identify the wrong affected filling-machine location
needs.plain: Reduce the number of reports about remaining problems that point to the wrong location on the filling machine. This helps workers find and fix the actual problem faster.

usage.name: Restore machinery to production-ready state
usage.statement: Return the filling machinery to a production-ready configuration after maintenance work is complete.
usage.description: Reassembling guards, resetting operating parameters, and confirming all service tools and materials are cleared so the machine is ready to resume production.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that service tools, loose fasteners, or maintenance materials remain in guarded or product-contact areas at release
Minimize the likelihood that machinery guards are incorrectly installed or unsecured when released for production
Minimize the likelihood of unexpected machinery movement while the production configuration is being restored
Reduce the degree to which restored operating parameters differ from approved production settings
Reduce the number of maintenance-disturbed components or connections left loose or incorrectly assembled at release
needs.plain: Before the machine is released for use, make sure every part or connection disturbed during maintenance is tight and put back together correctly. This helps prevent problems when the machine returns to production.

usage.name: Review diagnostic findings
usage.statement: Review diagnostic findings produced during the maintenance intervention.
usage.description: This allows the customer to understand the identified condition before further service work proceeds.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of overlooking a diagnostic finding that indicates a threat to sterile-product quality or operator safety
Minimize the likelihood of misinterpreting diagnostic findings when assessing the machinery condition
Reduce the percentage of diagnostic findings lacking evidence needed to verify the identified machinery condition
needs.plain: Make sure each finding about the machinery includes enough supporting proof to check whether the stated condition is real.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of conflicting conclusions about the same machinery condition within the diagnostic findings
needs.plain: Make sure the investigation reaches one consistent conclusion about the machine’s condition. Conflicting conclusions can lead to the wrong repair or safety decision.

needs.stmt: Reduce the time required for diagnostic findings to become available for review before further service work is authorized
needs.plain: Spend less time waiting for the results of checks on a problem to be ready for review. This helps people decide sooner whether more service work should be approved.

usage.name: Run supervised test cycles
usage.statement: Run supervised filling-machine test cycles during service validation.
usage.description: Controlled cycles let maintenance personnel assess performance under normal operating inputs.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of compromising sterile processing conditions during supervised filling-machine test cycles
needs.plain: Keep the conditions needed to protect the germ-free product intact while workers run supervised tests on the filling machine. Testing should not expose the product or its surroundings to contamination.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of executing an incorrect filling-machine test sequence because operator and maintenance instructions conflict
needs.plain: Avoid running the wrong test steps on the filling machine when operator and maintenance instructions disagree. Following the correct steps helps keep testing safe and useful.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of personnel injury from unexpected filling-machine movement or discharge during supervised test cycles
Reduce the percentage of supervised filling-machine test cycles run with inputs outside normal operating ranges
needs.plain: Run supervised filling-machine tests only with inputs within the machine’s normal working ranges. This makes the test results more meaningful and reduces the risk of abnormal operation.

needs.stmt: Reduce the frequency of supervised filling-machine test cycles interrupted before completion
needs.plain: Fewer supervised test runs of the filling machine should stop before they are finished. Completing each run provides a more reliable check of how the machine works.

usage.name: Supply equipment operating history
usage.statement: Supply the filling machinery's relevant operating history to maintenance personnel.
usage.description: Providing historical run data, prior fault records, and previous service summaries to technicians to support accurate diagnosis and planning.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that maintenance personnel cannot access the supplied filling machinery operating history when diagnosis begins
Minimize the time required to supply the relevant filling machinery operating history after maintenance personnel request it
Reduce the percentage of relevant historical run data, prior fault records, and previous service summaries omitted from the history supplied to maintenance personnel
needs.plain: Give maintenance workers the important records of past operation, earlier problems, and previous service work. This information helps them understand the equipment and diagnose issues more effectively.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of supplied operating-history records containing incorrect machine states, timestamps, or fault details
needs.plain: Avoid providing equipment history records that show the wrong operating condition, time, or problem details. This helps others understand what the machine actually did.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of discrepancies among the historical run data, prior fault records, and previous service summaries supplied to maintenance personnel
needs.plain: Make sure the equipment's past operating information, earlier problem reports, and previous service summaries agree. Accurate history helps maintenance workers understand what has happened before.

usage.name: Supply representative test materials
usage.statement: Supply representative production materials for maintenance testing.
usage.description: Actual fill product, containers, closures, or labels may be needed to validate machinery behavior under realistic conditions.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of exhausting supplied materials before maintenance testing is completed
Minimize the likelihood that supplied materials introduce contamination into the sterile testing environment
Minimize the likelihood that the identity or production status of supplied materials cannot be verified before maintenance testing
Reduce the percentage of supplied fill product, containers, closures, or labels that do not match current production specifications
needs.plain: Make sure the supplied product, containers, lids, and labels match the latest production requirements. This ensures that testing uses the same materials planned for actual production.

needs.stmt: Reduce the time maintenance testing waits for the required production materials
needs.plain: Spend less time waiting for the actual materials needed to test the maintenance work. Using the right materials makes the test more useful and realistic.

usage.name: Support remote diagnosis
usage.statement: Transmit requested machine evidence during a remote maintenance session.
usage.description: Remote evidence enables maintenance personnel to evaluate machinery when on-site access is unavailable or delayed.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of transmitting evidence from the wrong machine, component, or operating state
Reduce the degree to which transmitted machine evidence is unreadable or diagnostically unclear
Reduce the duration of sterile filling disruption caused by capturing requested machine evidence
Reduce the percentage of requested machine evidence omitted from the transmission
needs.plain: Send all the requested photos, readings, and other information about the machine so a remote expert can find the problem without being there in person.

needs.stmt: Reduce the frequency of interruptions before all requested machine evidence arrives
needs.plain: Avoid losing contact before all the requested machine information has been received. This helps support staff find and fix problems remotely.

usage.name: Suspend unsafe service work
usage.statement: Suspend maintenance work when an unsafe condition emerges.
usage.description: This interaction is necessary when changing site or machinery conditions create an immediate hazard.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of filling machinery remaining energized, pressurized, or capable of movement during the suspension
needs.plain: Make sure the filling machine is not still powered, under pressure, or able to move while unsafe service work is paused. This helps prevent unexpected movement or release of stored energy.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of sterile boundaries being left compromised when maintenance stops
Minimize the likelihood of maintenance resuming before the Safety Officer determines that the unsafe condition is cleared
Minimize the time from the Safety Officer recognizing the unsafe condition until all hands-on maintenance ceases
Reduce the number of affected maintenance workers who do not receive the stop instruction
needs.plain: Make sure every affected maintenance worker receives the instruction to stop. This helps prevent unsafe work from continuing.

usage.name: Test machine under load
usage.statement: Test the serviced filling machinery under operating load to verify performance.
usage.description: After service, machine performance must be checked under load to confirm the work resolved the issue.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of introducing contamination into the sterile product-contact path during the loaded test
needs.plain: Prevent germs or other unwanted material from getting into the path that touches the germ-free product while the machine is being tested with materials in it. This helps ensure the test does not spoil or endanger the product.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of deviation between loaded test conditions and approved production conditions
needs.plain: Make the test conditions as close as possible to the conditions the machine will face during real production. This makes the test results more trustworthy.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of technician injury from unexpected machine movement during the loaded test
Minimize the likelihood that required test material is unavailable when the loaded test is scheduled
Reduce the percentage of test fills outside specified volume limits during the loaded test
needs.plain: Keep test fills within the required amount range while testing the machine with a normal load. This shows whether the machine fills containers correctly during operation.

usage.name: Update control software
usage.statement: Update the control software and firmware of the filling machinery during a service visit.
usage.description: Installing current software and firmware releases on filling machinery controllers to address known issues, add features, or maintain vendor support.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the software or firmware release is incompatible with the installed controller configuration
Minimize the likelihood that a controller is rendered inoperable by an interrupted or failed installation during the service visit
Reduce the duration of filling machinery unavailability beyond the planned service window
Reduce the number of machine settings or calibration parameters lost or altered during the update
needs.plain: Make sure the machine's settings and adjustment values remain correct after the software update. Losing or changing them could make the machine operate incorrectly.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of filling machinery controllers left with mismatched software or firmware versions after the service visit
needs.plain: Make sure all machine controllers use matching versions of their control software after the service visit. Matching versions help the filling machines work consistently and avoid control problems.

usage.name: Verify restored machine function
usage.statement: Verify that the filling machinery performs as expected after maintenance.
usage.description: Customer verification confirms that the serviced machinery is ready for operational release.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of intermittent malfunctions remaining undetected during post-maintenance verification
needs.plain: Detect occasional machine problems during the checks done after maintenance instead of allowing them to go unnoticed. This helps ensure the repaired machine works reliably.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of hazardous machine behavior during post-maintenance verification
needs.plain: Prevent the machine from moving or operating dangerously while its function is checked after maintenance. This helps protect the person testing it.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree to which fill output deviates from approved specifications during post-maintenance verification
needs.plain: Keep the amount filled into each container within the approved range after maintenance. This helps confirm that the machine is working properly again.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of sterile conditions being compromised during post-maintenance verification
Reduce the percent of approved operating conditions omitted from post-maintenance verification
needs.plain: Leave out fewer approved ways of running the machine when checking it after maintenance. This helps confirm that the machine works properly in all expected situations.

usage.name: Verify safety systems
usage.statement: Verify that the safety interlocks and guards on the filling machinery function correctly.
usage.description: Testing each safety interlock, guard, and emergency-stop circuit to confirm correct operation prior to releasing the machine for production.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of compromising the sterile filling area while testing safety interlocks, guards, and emergency-stop circuits
needs.plain: Check the safety systems without allowing germs into the germ-free filling area. This protects the product while the equipment is being tested.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that hazardous machine motion continues after a guard is opened or a safety interlock is actuated during verification
Minimize the likelihood that a defective safety function is recorded as functional during verification
Minimize the likelihood of technician exposure to unexpected machine movement during safety-system testing
Reduce the percentage of installed safety interlocks, guards, and emergency-stop circuits omitted from periodic verification
needs.plain: Make sure every installed safety device, protective barrier, and emergency shutoff is checked regularly. This helps find failures before someone gets hurt.

maintenance.name: Align mechanical assemblies
maintenance.statement: Align filling heads, conveyors, guides, drive components, and container-handling assemblies to specified tolerances.
maintenance.description: This corrects positional deviations that can cause interference, uneven wear, or unreliable machine operation.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of contaminating product-contact or sterile-zone surfaces during alignment work
needs.plain: Reduce the chance that surfaces meant to touch the product, or surfaces in the germ-free area, become dirty while parts are being lined up. This helps protect the product from contamination.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of unexpected assembly movement while the technician makes alignment adjustments
Reduce the degree of residual positional deviation after adjusting the filling and container-handling assemblies
Minimize the likelihood that aligned assemblies shift after being secured and returned to service
Reduce the duration of sterile fill-finish production downtime caused by alignment work

maintenance.name: Back up machine settings
maintenance.statement: Back up the filling machinery's recipes and configuration settings.
maintenance.description: Copy and securely store control-system configurations, product recipes, calibration values, and operational parameters to enable rapid recovery after hardware failure or software update.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that backup files are corrupted or unreadable when needed for recovery
Reduce the percentage of required recipes, configurations, calibration values, and operational parameters omitted from the backup
Minimize the degree of mismatch between the backed-up settings and the approved active machine state
Minimize the likelihood that the backup is inaccessible to authorized personnel during recovery
Reduce the duration of filling-operation interruption caused by backup activity
needs.plain: Spend less time interrupting filling work to save a copy of the machine’s settings. This helps protect the settings without stopping production for long.

maintenance.name: Calibrate filling controls and dispensing accuracy
maintenance.statement: Calibrate filling controls, measuring devices, sensors, actuators, and dispensing volume against applicable references to restore target accuracy.
maintenance.description: Unifies the calibration of the filling machinery's volumetric dispensing accuracy with the calibration of its associated controls, sensors, and actuators, ensuring both dimensional and functional accuracy are addressed in a single calibration job.
maintenance.frequency: Per scheduled calibration interval or after corrective intervention

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of dispensing channels, sensors, and actuators remaining outside applicable tolerances after calibration
needs.plain: Make sure the liquid outlets, measuring devices, and moving controls stay within the allowed accuracy after adjustment. This helps each container receive the right amount.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of dispensing channels or control components assigned incorrect calibration values
needs.plain: Make sure every part that controls or measures dispensing is given the correct settings. This helps ensure the right amount is delivered.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of contaminating product-contact areas during calibration
needs.plain: Keep areas that touch the product free from contamination while workers adjust and check the filling controls.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of required calibration references being unavailable or invalid at the maintenance visit
Reduce the duration of filling-line unavailability beyond the scheduled maintenance window

maintenance.name: Diagnose machinery faults
maintenance.statement: Diagnose the source of filling machinery alarms, malfunctions, abnormal behavior, or performance deterioration.
maintenance.description: This isolates the failed or misadjusted element before corrective work begins.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of introducing contamination during diagnostic access to the sterile filling area
needs.plain: Keep the clean filling area free from contamination while opening or accessing the equipment to find a fault. This protects the product during troubleshooting.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of technician exposure to hazardous energy or process materials during diagnosis
needs.plain: The technician should be able to find a machine problem without being exposed to dangerous energy or harmful materials. This helps prevent serious injury or illness.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that intermittent fault symptoms cannot be reproduced during diagnosis
Reduce the number of fault sources incorrectly identified before corrective work begins
Reduce the duration of filling-line downtime before the fault source is isolated

maintenance.name: Document maintenance history
maintenance.statement: Document the filling machinery's maintenance and service history.
maintenance.description: Record inspections, service actions, replaced parts, measurements, faults, and outcomes to support traceability, compliance, and future maintenance planning.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of maintenance events documented with required inspection, service, parts, fault, or outcome details omitted
Reduce the percentage of recorded measurements, replaced parts, faults, or outcomes that are inaccurate
Reduce the number of maintenance records lost or altered without an attributable history
Minimize the likelihood that a service record cannot be linked to the correct filling machine, component, and maintenance event
Minimize the time between completion of maintenance work and entry of its service record
needs.plain: Enter the maintenance record as soon as the work is finished. This keeps the equipment’s service history up to date.

maintenance.name: Flush and purge fluid circuits
maintenance.statement: Flush and purge the filling machinery's fluid circuits, manifolds, and dead legs between product changeovers or extended shutdowns.
maintenance.description: Clear residual product, cleaning agents, or process fluids from internal circuits, manifolds, valves, and dead-leg zones to prevent cross-contamination, corrosion, or biological growth.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of fluid-circuit locations retaining residual product, cleaning agent, or process fluid after flushing and purging
needs.plain: Make sure little or no product, cleaning liquid, or other process liquid remains anywhere inside the fluid lines after they are rinsed and emptied. Leftover material could contaminate the next use.

needs.stmt: Reduce the amount of fluid escaping into the sterile production area during circuit flushing
needs.plain: Reduce the amount of liquid that leaks into the clean production room while the liquid lines are being emptied and rinsed. This helps keep the area clean and dry.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that incorrect valve alignment bypasses manifold branches or dead legs during flushing and purging
needs.plain: Lower the chance that valves are set incorrectly and leave parts of the fluid piping unflushed or unpurged. This helps remove unwanted material from the entire system.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of filling machinery unavailability caused by flushing and purging beyond the changeover window
Reduce the number of discrepancies between circuit documentation and actual fluid flow paths

maintenance.name: Inspect machinery components and wear parts
maintenance.statement: Inspect filling machinery components and wear parts for wear, damage, leaks, looseness, corrosion, and contamination.
maintenance.description: Combines the broad structural inspection of the filling machinery (wear, leaks, looseness, corrosion, contamination, damage) with the focused inspection of high-wear consumable parts such as nozzles, pistons, and seals, producing a unified condition assessment.
maintenance.frequency: Per maintenance event; at scheduled inspection intervals

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of introducing sterile-zone contamination while components are exposed for inspection
needs.plain: Reduce the chance that opening machine parts for inspection brings germs into the clean area. This helps protect the production area while parts are examined.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of existing component defects missed during the inspection
Minimize the likelihood of misclassifying component condition because acceptance limits are unclear
Reduce the number of components or wear parts damaged or loosened during inspection
Reduce the duration of filling-line downtime caused by inspection overruns

maintenance.name: Lubricate moving components
maintenance.statement: Lubricate designated bearings, guides, chains, gears, and other moving components.
maintenance.description: Apply specified lubricants to all designated lubrication points on the filling machinery according to the maintenance schedule and manufacturer lubrication map.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that lubricant enters the sterile product-contact environment during maintenance
needs.plain: Prevent oil or grease used on moving parts from getting into the area that touches the germ-free product. Even a small amount could contaminate the product.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of applying an incompatible lubricant to a designated moving component
needs.plain: Reduce the chance of putting the wrong lubricant on a moving part. This helps prevent damage, poor operation, or an unsafe equipment condition.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that machinery components move unexpectedly while the technician lubricates designated points
Reduce the number of designated lubrication points missed during scheduled maintenance
Minimize the degree of deviation from the specified lubricant quantity at designated lubrication points

maintenance.name: Monitor machinery health
maintenance.statement: Monitor filling machinery condition indicators, alarms, operating hours, vibration, temperature, and fault trends.
maintenance.description: Continuously track real-time and trended machine health data to detect degradation, predict failures, and trigger timely maintenance interventions.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that incipient filling machinery degradation remains undetected before functional failure
needs.plain: Find early signs that the filling machines are wearing out before they stop working. This helps prevent unexpected equipment failure.

needs.stmt: Reduce the time from a critical condition threshold breach until the alarm reaches the maintenance administrator
Reduce the percentage of expected condition records missing from continuous machinery monitoring
Reduce the rate of filling machinery alarms that indicate degradation not present
Minimize the degree of disagreement among condition indicators for the same machinery state

maintenance.name: Repair machinery defects
maintenance.statement: Repair defective mechanical, electrical, pneumatic, hydraulic, or control-system elements of filling machinery.
maintenance.description: This restores serviceability after a fault, breakdown, or damaged component is confirmed.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of introducing contamination into sterile or product-contact areas during repair
needs.plain: Prevent dirt or germs from entering sterile areas or surfaces that touch the product while repairs are being made. This helps keep the product and equipment safe during maintenance.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the confirmed machinery defect remains after repair
needs.plain: Lower the chance that the machine problem being repaired is still present afterward. This helps ensure the machine works properly when returned to use.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of technician injury from an unintended release of stored electrical, pneumatic, or hydraulic energy during repair
needs.plain: Keep technicians from being hurt if stored electricity, pressurized air, or pressurized liquid is released unexpectedly during repairs. The stored power must be made safe before work begins.

needs.stmt: Reduce the time the filling machinery remains out of service because a required replacement element is unavailable
Reduce the number of adjacent filling-machinery elements damaged during repair

maintenance.name: Replace filters and screens
maintenance.statement: Replace the filling machinery's filters and screens on schedule.
maintenance.description: Remove and replace inline filters, strainer screens, and particulate-capture media that protect product pathways and pneumatic/hydraulic circuits.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of introducing contamination into sterile product pathways during filter and screen replacement
needs.plain: Make sure replacing filters and screens does not introduce germs into the product pathways. This helps keep the product clean and safe.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of filters and screens installed with incorrect specifications or orientation
needs.plain: Reduce the number of filters and screens installed with the wrong type, size, or direction. This helps the equipment filter material properly.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of process material or utility fluid bypassing a newly installed filter or screen
needs.plain: Make sure process material or service liquid cannot flow around a newly installed filter or screen. Otherwise, unwanted material could pass through without being stopped.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of required replacement filters, screens, or seals being unavailable at the scheduled maintenance start
Reduce the duration of filling-line shutdown beyond the allocated maintenance window

maintenance.name: Replace scheduled maintenance consumables
maintenance.statement: Replace scheduled maintenance consumables — excluding filters and screens — such as lubricants, belts, and drive components.
maintenance.description: Swap out consumable maintenance items (lubricants, drive belts, timing components, etc.) at prescribed intervals to prevent unplanned downtime; filters and screens are addressed under a separate job.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of introducing contamination into the sterile filling area while replacing scheduled consumables
needs.plain: Prevent germs or other unwanted material from entering the sterile filling area while scheduled parts and supplies are replaced. This protects the product and the clean work area.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of technician injury from unreleased hazardous energy during scheduled consumable replacement
needs.plain: Make sure dangerous power or pressure has been released before replacing parts that are changed on a regular schedule. This helps prevent injuries during maintenance.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of replacement drive or timing components left misaligned or incorrectly tensioned
Reduce the number of scheduled consumables replaced with an incorrect type or specification
Reduce the duration of maintenance delay caused by required consumables being unavailable for the scheduled maintenance window

maintenance.name: Replace seals and gaskets
maintenance.statement: Replace worn seals, gaskets, and O-rings on the filling machinery.
maintenance.description: Seals, gaskets, and O-rings degrade over time and must be swapped to prevent leaks and drips.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that sterile product-contact surfaces are contaminated during replacement work
needs.plain: Prevent germs or other contamination from reaching surfaces that touch the sterile product while seals or gaskets are replaced.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that an installed seal, gasket, or O-ring is incompatible with the filling machinery or process materials
needs.plain: Make sure the new seal or rubber ring works with the filling equipment and the materials being handled, so it does not fail or cause problems.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that sealing surfaces are damaged while removing worn seals, gaskets, or O-rings
needs.plain: Worn seals should be removed without damaging the surfaces they press against. This helps the replacement seals work properly and prevent leaks.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that residual pressure or hazardous product is released during disassembly
needs.plain: Prevent trapped pressure or dangerous material from escaping when parts are taken apart. This protects people replacing worn seals and gaskets.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that a replacement seal, gasket, or O-ring is twisted, pinched, or mis-seated during installation
needs.plain: Make it unlikely that a replacement seal or rubber ring is twisted, pinched, or placed incorrectly during installation. This helps prevent leaks or other problems.

maintenance.name: Run clean-in-place cycle
maintenance.statement: Run a clean-in-place sanitation cycle on the filling machinery.
maintenance.description: Execute an automated or manual clean-in-place (CIP) cycle to sanitize internal fluid pathways, tanks, and product-contact circuits without disassembly.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of viable contamination remaining in product-contact circuits after the cycle
needs.plain: Make sure no living germs or leftover contamination remain in the product-contact pipes and equipment after cleaning. This helps keep the product safe.

needs.stmt: Minimize the percentage of required product-contact pathways bypassed during the cycle
needs.plain: Make sure the cleaning liquid reaches nearly all of the product-contact paths that need cleaning. Any path it misses may still contain leftover material or contamination.

needs.stmt: Reduce the amount of cleaning-agent residue remaining in product-contact circuits after the final rinse
needs.plain: Leave as little cleaning chemical as possible inside the product-carrying pipes after the final rinse, so the next product is not contaminated.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of the sanitation cycle stopping before the required sequence is complete
needs.plain: Prevent the cleaning process from stopping before every required step is finished. This helps ensure the equipment is properly cleaned before it is used again.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of cleaning chemicals escaping into the production area during the cycle
needs.plain: Keep cleaning chemicals from leaking into the production area while the equipment cleans itself. Escaping chemicals could contaminate the area or harm people and equipment.

maintenance.name: Schedule preventive service
maintenance.statement: Schedule preventive maintenance for the filling machinery.
maintenance.description: Plan and assign preventive maintenance tasks for filling machinery based on operating hours, calendar intervals, or condition triggers.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of required preventive maintenance tasks omitted from the filling machinery schedule
Minimize the likelihood that required parts, production access, or approvals are unavailable on the scheduled service date
Reduce the percentage of preventive service events scheduled after the applicable operating-hour, calendar-interval, or condition-trigger deadline
Reduce the duration of sterile fill-finish production disrupted by scheduled preventive maintenance
Minimize the likelihood that assigned maintenance personnel are unavailable for the scheduled filling machinery service window

maintenance.name: Store machinery properly
maintenance.statement: Store and preserve the filling machinery when not in use.
maintenance.description: When idle or between campaigns the machine must be stored and preserved against corrosion and contamination.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of contamination entering product-contact areas while the filling machinery is stored
needs.plain: Keep unwanted material from getting into areas that will touch the product while the filling machine is stored. Proper storage helps keep the machine ready for safe use.

needs.stmt: Reduce the degree of corrosion developing on product-contact and mechanical surfaces during idle storage
Minimize the likelihood of seals and other nonmetallic components degrading during prolonged storage
Minimize the likelihood of incomplete or conflicting preservation-status information when the machinery is returned to service
Reduce the number of detached machine parts or accessories lost or misidentified during storage
needs.plain: Keep removed machine parts and accessories from being lost or confused with one another while they are stored. Clear storage makes it easier to find and return the right parts.

maintenance.name: Test electrical and pneumatic connections
maintenance.statement: Test and reseat electrical connectors, terminal blocks, and pneumatic fittings on the filling machinery.
maintenance.description: Inspect and functionally test wiring harness connectors, terminal-block connections, and pneumatic push-in or threaded fittings for looseness, corrosion, or leakage that may cause intermittent faults.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of contaminating the sterile production area during connection maintenance
needs.plain: Make it less likely that germs or other dirt enter the clean production room while electrical and air connections are being serviced. This helps protect the product and the room.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of technician exposure to hazardous electrical or pneumatic energy during testing
needs.plain: Keep the technician from coming into contact with dangerous electricity or compressed air while checking the connections. This helps prevent serious injury during testing.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of an intermittent electrical or pneumatic connection fault remaining undetected after testing
Reduce the number of electrical connections reseated in incorrect terminal positions
Reduce the number of connectors or fittings damaged during inspection and reseating

maintenance.name: Test safety devices
maintenance.statement: Test guards, interlocks, emergency stops, protective sensors, and shutdown devices for correct operation.
maintenance.description: This confirms that machinery safeguards remain functional after service or during preventive inspections.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that a malfunctioning guard, interlock, emergency stop, protective sensor, or shutdown device is classified as operational
needs.plain: Make sure a broken protective device is not mistakenly treated as working. These devices must work correctly to protect people and equipment.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that a tested safety device remains bypassed or disabled when the filling machinery is returned to service
needs.plain: Make sure a safety device that was temporarily turned off for testing is turned back on before the filling equipment is used again. This helps ensure the equipment is protected during normal operation.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that safety-device testing exposes the technician to hazardous machinery motion or stored energy
needs.plain: Test protective devices without exposing the technician to moving machinery or trapped energy, so the test itself does not cause injury.

needs.stmt: Reduce the degree of ambiguity in deciding whether each safety device meets its acceptance criteria
Minimize the likelihood that safety-device testing compromises sterile fill-finish conditions
needs.plain: Make sure checking the safety devices does not make the sterile manufacturing area unsafe or unclean.

maintenance.name: Tighten and torque fasteners
maintenance.statement: Inspect and re-torque fasteners, clamps, and fittings on the filling machinery to specified values.
maintenance.description: Check and re-tighten bolted joints, tri-clamp fittings, nozzle retainers, and frame fasteners that may loosen due to vibration and thermal cycling during production.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of introducing contamination through a hygienic joint disturbed during inspection and re-torquing
needs.plain: Avoid letting dirt or germs enter through a clean connection when it is opened for inspection and tightened again. This helps keep the equipment clean and safe to use.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of technician injury from residual pressure or unexpected machinery movement while accessing joints
needs.plain: The technician should be able to tighten the connections without being hurt by trapped pressure or sudden machine movement. This helps prevent unexpected injuries while working on the machine.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of fasteners, clamps, and fittings tightened outside specified torque values
Reduce the percent of required fasteners, clamps, and fittings left unchecked or not re-torqued
Minimize the likelihood of damaging threads, seals, or mating surfaces during tightening

maintenance.name: Upgrade components
maintenance.statement: Upgrade the filling machinery's components and modules.
maintenance.description: Aging or high-wear components can be upgraded to extend service life or improve performance.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of introducing contamination into sterile product-contact areas during component upgrades
needs.plain: Avoid bringing germs or other unwanted material into areas that touch the product when replacing or improving parts. This helps keep the product safe and clean.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of upgraded components installed incorrectly in the filling machinery
Minimize the likelihood that upgraded components are incompatible with existing machinery interfaces
Reduce the number of adjacent modules damaged while replacing filling machinery components
Reduce the duration of filling machinery unavailability caused by component upgrade work

maintenance.name: Verify sensors and controls
maintenance.statement: Verify and adjust the filling machinery's sensors and control systems.
maintenance.description: Check proximity sensors, level sensors, pressure transducers, and control-system outputs for correct response and adjust or replace out-of-tolerance devices.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of contaminating the sterile production area during sensor and control verification
needs.plain: Reduce the chance that checking measuring devices and controls brings germs into the clean production area. This helps keep the area suitable for making clean products.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of accepting an out-of-tolerance sensor or control response as compliant
needs.plain: Do not accept a sensor or control response as acceptable when it is outside the allowed range. This helps prevent faulty readings or actions from being treated as safe.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of using incorrect acceptance limits to evaluate sensor and control responses
needs.plain: Make sure the correct pass-and-fail limits are used when checking whether sensors and controls respond properly. This prevents working equipment from being wrongly rejected or unsafe equipment from being accepted.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of unintended filling-machinery actuation during sensor and control verification
needs.plain: Reduce the chance that the filling machine starts or moves unexpectedly while its sensors and controls are being checked. This helps prevent injury and equipment damage.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of filling-operation disruption caused by sensor and control verification

disposal.name: Archive maintenance records
disposal.statement: Archive the accumulated filling machinery maintenance and inspection records.
disposal.description: Matters when the maintenance relationship ends and accumulated equipment history must be retained by the owner.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of accumulated filling machinery maintenance and inspection records missing from the final owner archive
needs.plain: Have fewer machine-care and inspection records missing from the final archive given to the owner. This ensures the owner has a complete history of the machine.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of archived records linked to the wrong filling machine, component, or service event
needs.plain: Keep stored records from being linked to the wrong filling machine, part, or repair event. This helps people trace the correct maintenance history.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of archived records that are unreadable or corrupted when retrieved during the owner's retention period
needs.plain: Have fewer saved records that cannot be read or opened when the owner needs them during the required storage period. This keeps the records usable over time.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of archived records being destroyed before applicable retention obligations expire
needs.plain: Protect stored maintenance records from being deleted or destroyed before the required storage period ends. These records may be needed later to show how the equipment was maintained.

needs.stmt: Reduce the time required to locate and retrieve a requested maintenance or inspection record from the owner's archive
needs.plain: Spend less time finding and retrieving a requested maintenance or inspection record from the owner’s stored files. Easy access helps people make decisions using the equipment’s past history.

disposal.name: Cancel maintenance service agreement
disposal.statement: Cancel the filling machinery maintenance service agreement.
disposal.description: Formally terminate the contractual relationship with the maintenance service provider, ensuring all notice periods and exit clauses are fulfilled.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the cancellation notice is submitted after the contractual deadline
needs.plain: The cancellation notice should be sent by the deadline in the agreement. This helps ensure the maintenance service can be ended without extra charges or other problems.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the provider rejects the cancellation notice for unmet contractual submission requirements
needs.plain: Make sure the cancellation notice follows all contract rules for how and when it must be sent. This helps the provider accept the cancellation.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of required filling machinery maintenance records inaccessible after cancellation
needs.plain: Keep access to all required maintenance records for the filling machines after the service agreement ends. This preserves the information needed to maintain the machines and understand their past work.

needs.stmt: Minimize the duration of the required maintenance coverage gap before provider transition or machinery decommissioning
needs.plain: Avoid a long gap in the maintenance support that is still needed before responsibility changes or the machinery is taken out of use. This helps prevent the machinery from being left without needed care.

disposal.name: Disconnect service integrations
disposal.statement: Disconnect maintenance-service integrations from filling machinery systems and business applications.
disposal.description: This stops obsolete data exchanges, automated notifications, or remote connections after the service ends.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of unauthorized remote access to filling machinery systems through residual service credentials after disconnection
needs.plain: Prevent former service accounts or passwords from allowing remote access to the filling machines after the service connection is removed. This helps stop people without permission from controlling or viewing the systems.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of obsolete maintenance-service integration endpoints that continue exchanging data or notifications after service termination
needs.plain: Stop old service connections from sending messages or information after the service has ended. This prevents outdated connections from continuing to affect current work.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of sterile fill-finish production disruption caused by disconnecting a still-needed dependency
needs.plain: Spend less time stopping germ-free medicine production when a connection to another needed service is removed. This helps the equipment keep working with the services it still needs.

needs.stmt: Reduce the time spent waiting for administrative access or authorization needed to disconnect maintenance-service integrations
needs.plain: Spend less time waiting for the permission and account access needed to disconnect maintenance-related connections. This helps the work begin sooner and avoids unauthorized changes.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of maintenance-service integrations whose disconnected state cannot be verified
needs.plain: Make sure it can be confirmed that every maintenance connection has been fully turned off. This prevents unwanted access or continued activity after the connection is ended.

disposal.name: Dispose of maintenance waste materials
disposal.statement: Dispose of used lubricants, solvents, and worn parts generated by filling machinery maintenance activities.
disposal.description: Ensures regulated and non-regulated waste produced during maintenance is disposed of through appropriate channels, including hazardous-material handling as required.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of used lubricants or solvents leaking or spilling before removal from the maintenance area
needs.plain: Make it unlikely that used oil, grease, or cleaning liquids will leak or spill before they leave the maintenance area. Preventing spills protects people, equipment, and the surrounding area.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of personnel exposure to used lubricants or solvents during disposal handling
needs.plain: Prevent workers from coming into contact with used lubricants or cleaning liquids while throwing them away. This helps protect their health.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of regulated maintenance waste being transferred through a channel not authorized for its waste category
Reduce the percentage of used lubricants, solvents, and worn parts assigned to an incorrect waste category
Reduce the percentage of maintenance-waste consignments rejected because required disposal records are incomplete or inconsistent
needs.plain: Make sure all required disposal paperwork for maintenance waste is complete and agrees across records. Otherwise, the waste shipment may be refused.

disposal.name: Export maintenance history and condition-monitoring data
disposal.statement: Export filling machinery maintenance history and condition-monitoring data from the service system prior to termination.
disposal.description: Ensures the customer retains a portable copy of all maintenance records and sensor/condition data for regulatory compliance, warranty, or handover to a successor provider.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that service-system access ends before the export is completed
needs.plain: Make sure access to the service system stays available until the data export is finished, so no maintenance or condition information is lost.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of maintenance and condition-monitoring records omitted from the exported dataset
needs.plain: Make sure the exported file includes all maintenance records and equipment-condition information. Missing records can leave an incomplete history and make problems harder to investigate.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of exported records containing corrupted values or unreadable attachments
needs.plain: Make sure exported records contain accurate values and attachments that can be opened, so important maintenance information is not lost or misunderstood.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of exported records that cannot be associated with the correct filling machine or component
needs.plain: Make sure each exported record can be linked to the correct filling machine or part, so the information is not assigned to the wrong equipment.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of exported files that cannot be interpreted outside the terminated service environment
needs.plain: Make exported files usable after the original service ends, so people can still open and understand the maintenance information.

disposal.name: Liquidate spare parts inventory
disposal.statement: Liquidate the remaining spare parts inventory held for filling machinery maintenance.
disposal.description: Matters when leftover spare parts inventory dedicated to the maintenance program must be disposed of or resold.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of transferring restricted, expired, or nonconforming spare parts to an ineligible buyer
Reduce the percentage of spare parts lacking required identity, condition, or traceability records
needs.plain: Make sure each spare part has records showing what it is, what condition it is in, and where it came from or has been kept track of. This helps people sell or handle the parts with confidence.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percent of spare parts inventory left unsold at the planned liquidation close
needs.plain: Have fewer spare parts left without a buyer when the planned sale ends, so less inventory goes to waste or must be dealt with later.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of spare parts rendered unsalable by damage or contamination during liquidation
needs.plain: Prevent spare parts from being damaged or contaminated while they are being prepared for sale. Parts in poor condition may no longer be safe or usable to sell.

needs.stmt: Reduce the amount by which handling, storage, transaction, and disposal costs exceed liquidation proceeds
needs.plain: Keep the costs of handling, storing, selling, and getting rid of spare parts from being higher than the money earned from selling them. This limits the loss on unwanted stock.

disposal.name: Notify stakeholders of maintenance service termination
disposal.statement: Notify affected internal stakeholders and regulators that the filling machinery maintenance service has ended.
disposal.description: Ensures all parties dependent on the maintenance service — including operations, compliance, and regulatory bodies — are formally informed of the service end date and implications.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of affected internal stakeholders and regulators omitted from the termination notification
needs.plain: Make sure every affected employee inside the organization and every relevant government official is told when a maintenance service is ending. This prevents people from being caught unaware or missing required actions.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of delay in notifying affected internal stakeholders and regulators of service termination
needs.plain: Tell affected coworkers and government authorities promptly when maintenance service ends. This gives everyone who needs to know time to respond or make other arrangements.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of inaccuracies in the communicated service end date and implications
needs.plain: Make sure people are told the correct date when the maintenance service ends and what that ending means for them.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of termination notifications that fail to reach intended recipients
needs.plain: Avoid having messages about ending maintenance service fail to reach the people who need them. This helps everyone know when the service has stopped.

needs.stmt: Reduce the degree of inconsistency among termination notices sent to operations, compliance, and regulatory bodies
needs.plain: Make sure everyone receives the same information when the maintenance service is being ended, including the operations team and the relevant oversight groups.

disposal.name: Recover customer-owned materials
disposal.statement: Recover customer-owned parts, documentation, or materials held through the maintenance service.
disposal.description: This restores custody of retained spares or other customer property before the service relationship closes.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that retained customer-owned product-contact parts become contaminated before custody transfer
needs.plain: Keep the customer’s parts that touch the product clean until they are handed back. Contamination before the handover could make them unsuitable for use.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of customer-owned property being transferred to an unauthorized recipient during recovery
Minimize the percentage of customer-owned parts, documentation, or materials that cannot be located when recovery is requested
needs.plain: Reduce the share of customer-owned parts, documents, or supplies that cannot be found when the customer asks for them back. This helps ensure the customer's property is returned.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of retained items with missing or conflicting ownership and custody records at service closure
needs.plain: Reduce the number of items still on hand whose ownership or custody records are missing or disagree at the end of service. This helps ensure items are returned to the right owner and responsibility is clear.

needs.stmt: Reduce the time customer-owned property remains in maintenance service custody after recovery is requested
needs.plain: Spend less time waiting for customer-owned items to be returned after the customer asks for them back. This helps the customer regain control of their property sooner.

disposal.name: Redeploy reusable service assets
disposal.statement: Redeploy reusable maintenance assets to another filling line or facility.
disposal.description: Matters when reusable service assets can be redeployed to another site rather than discarded.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that redeployed maintenance assets introduce contaminants into the receiving sterile fill-finish environment
needs.plain: Prevent reused maintenance equipment from bringing dirt or germs into the clean production area. This helps protect the cleanliness of the receiving area.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that maintenance assets selected for redeployment are incompatible with the destination filling line or facility requirements
needs.plain: Make sure reused maintenance equipment will work with the new filling line and meet the needs of its new site.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of redeployed maintenance assets arriving with incomplete or conflicting identity, traceability, or service records
needs.plain: Make sure reused maintenance equipment arrives with complete and consistent records showing what it is, where it came from, and how it was serviced. Reliable records help the next user operate and maintain it safely.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of redeployed maintenance assets damaged during transfer between sites
needs.plain: Fewer reusable maintenance items should be damaged while being moved from one site to another. Protecting them helps ensure they can be used at the new site.

needs.stmt: Reduce the time redeployed maintenance assets wait between release from the originating site and availability at the destination
needs.plain: Spend less time waiting for reusable maintenance equipment to move from one site to another and become ready for use. This makes the equipment available where it is needed sooner.

disposal.name: Request final service inspection
disposal.statement: Request a final inspection of the maintained filling machinery before service termination.
disposal.description: This provides a defined closing assessment when machinery condition or service completion must be verified.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that service termination proceeds before the final inspection is confirmed
needs.plain: Make sure the service is not ended until the final inspection has been completed and confirmed.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of required machinery and maintenance details missing from the final inspection request
needs.plain: Make sure the final inspection request includes all important details about the machines and their maintenance. This helps the inspector prepare and carry out a complete check.

needs.stmt: Reduce the degree of ambiguity in the final inspection scope and acceptance criteria
needs.plain: Clearly define what the final inspection will cover and what the equipment must pass. This helps determine whether the service work is complete and acceptable.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that required maintenance records are unavailable when the final inspection request is reviewed
