needs.plain: Make sure all repair records are available when the request for the last inspection is reviewed. This gives the reviewer the information needed to check the work.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the requested final inspection window conflicts with sterile fill-finish production or site-access constraints
needs.plain: The final inspection should be scheduled for a time when production is not running and inspectors can enter the site. This prevents the inspection from being disrupted or blocked.

disposal.name: Request service data deletion
disposal.statement: Request deletion of confidential data retained by the maintenance service.
disposal.description: This limits continued retention of machinery, facility, operational, or personnel information after termination.

needs.stmt: Minimize the percentage of confidential service data omitted from the deletion request
needs.plain: Reduce the share of private service information left out of a request to delete it. This helps ensure that all requested information is removed.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of the deletion request being rejected because the Data Administrator's authority cannot be verified
needs.plain: Avoid having a request to delete service records rejected because the person responsible for the data cannot prove they are authorized. The request should come with clear proof of authority.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of the deletion request reaching a party without authority to act
needs.plain: Avoid sending the request to delete service records to someone who is not allowed to handle it. The request must reach a person with the proper authority.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of unresolved conflict between the requested deletion scope and mandatory retention obligations
needs.plain: Resolve disagreements quickly about which service data can be deleted and which data must legally be kept. This helps the person requesting deletion know when the request is complete.

needs.stmt: Reduce the time the Data Administrator waits for acknowledgment that the deletion request was received
needs.plain: Spend less time waiting for confirmation that a request to erase service data was received. This lets the person who manages the data know sooner that the request is being handled.

disposal.name: Return provider-owned tooling and equipment
disposal.statement: Return maintenance tooling, diagnostic devices, and loaned equipment to the service provider upon service termination.
disposal.description: Ensures all provider-owned assets used during the maintenance service are collected and returned, closing out asset accountability at service end.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that provider-owned equipment retains hazardous residues when released from the sterile fill-finish area
needs.plain: Make sure provider-owned tools and equipment do not still contain dangerous leftover material when they leave the sterile production area. This protects people and other work areas.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of provider-owned tools or devices left on-site after service termination
needs.plain: Fewer provider-owned tools or devices should remain at the site after the service ends. This helps ensure the provider gets its property back and the site is left organized.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of damage to provider-owned assets during removal or return transit
needs.plain: Protect the provider's tools and equipment from being damaged while they are removed or sent back. This helps avoid repair costs and disputes about their condition.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of returned assets whose identifiers do not match provider accountability records
needs.plain: Reduce the number of returned tools and equipment whose labels do not match the provider’s records. This helps prove that all provider-owned items were returned and accounted for.

needs.stmt: Reduce the time provider-owned assets remain on-site after service termination awaiting authorization for removal
needs.plain: Spend less time leaving the provider’s tools and equipment at the site after the service ends while waiting for permission to take them away. Quick approval helps clear the site sooner.

disposal.name: Revoke provider access
disposal.statement: Revoke the maintenance provider's access to facilities and control systems.
disposal.description: Matters when access credentials or system integrations granted to the maintenance provider must be revoked.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that access cutoff interrupts an active sterile filling batch
needs.plain: Make it unlikely that removing a service provider’s system access will interrupt a batch of medicine being filled under germ-free conditions. This matters because stopping the batch could affect the work and the product.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of facility and control-system access paths that remain active after revocation is declared complete
needs.plain: Make sure all ways for the service provider to enter the facility or its control systems are turned off after access is withdrawn. Any remaining access could allow unwanted entry.

needs.stmt: Reduce the time the revocation request waits for authorization from facility or control-system owners
needs.plain: A request to remove a provider’s access should be approved quickly by the people responsible for the site or system. This helps prevent access from lasting longer than necessary.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that revoking provider access also disables access required by administrators or filling machinery
needs.plain: Remove the outside provider’s access without accidentally blocking access still needed by administrators or by the equipment that fills containers.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that unresolved maintenance obligations require provider access to be restored after revocation
needs.plain: Reduce the chance that unfinished repair or upkeep duties mean the provider must regain access after its access has been withdrawn. This helps ensure access can be ended cleanly.

disposal.name: Settle final maintenance service charges
disposal.statement: Settle the final charges and close the financial account associated with the filling machinery maintenance service.
disposal.description: Ensures all outstanding invoices, credit notes, and financial obligations related to the maintenance service are resolved before contract closure.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of charges on the final invoice that conflict with the maintenance agreement or completed service records
needs.plain: Fewer items on the final bill should disagree with the maintenance contract or the work that was actually completed. This helps ensure the customer pays only the correct amount.

needs.stmt: Reduce the amount of approved credit omitted from the final settlement
needs.plain: Make sure the final bill includes as much of the approved discount or account credit as possible. This keeps the amount paid from being higher than it should be.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of final charges lacking documentation needed for financial validation
needs.plain: Make sure every final maintenance bill has the documents needed to confirm that the charges are correct.

needs.stmt: Minimize the time the final settlement waits for required internal authorization
needs.plain: Get final payment approval as quickly as possible. This allows the maintenance provider to be paid without unnecessary delay.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that additional maintenance service charges are submitted after financial account closure
needs.plain: Make sure no new maintenance bills are submitted after the account has been closed. This prevents unexpected charges after the final payment.

disposal.name: Transfer maintenance responsibility
disposal.statement: Transfer maintenance responsibility and equipment documentation to a successor service provider.
disposal.description: Matters when the current maintenance provider is replaced and continuity of care must pass to a successor.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of required filling machinery documentation that is missing or inaccurate at handover
needs.plain: Make sure the documents for the filling machines are complete and accurate when maintenance responsibility changes hands. This helps the new maintenance team care for the machines correctly.

needs.stmt: Minimize the duration of the maintenance coverage gap during the provider transition
needs.plain: Avoid a long gap in repair coverage while responsibility moves from one provider to another. This helps keep the machinery supported during the change.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of open maintenance issues and known equipment risks omitted from the handover
needs.plain: Make sure all unresolved maintenance problems and known equipment dangers are included when responsibility changes hands. This helps the new team understand what still needs attention.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the successor lacks required site and equipment access at takeover
needs.plain: The person taking over maintenance should have the required access to the site and equipment on the takeover date. This allows them to begin the work without delays.

needs.stmt: Reduce the degree of ambiguity about maintenance ownership after the transfer
needs.plain: Make it clear who is responsible for maintaining the equipment after it changes hands. This prevents disagreements or neglected repairs later.

disposal.name: Transfer open work orders
disposal.statement: Transfer unresolved filling machinery work orders to the responsible recipient.
disposal.description: This prevents active maintenance needs from being abandoned during a provider or ownership transition.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of unresolved filling machinery work orders omitted from the transition handoff
needs.plain: Make sure all unfinished repair and service jobs for the filling machines are included when responsibility changes hands. This helps the new team know what still needs to be done.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that an unresolved filling machinery work order is transferred to a recipient who does not hold responsibility after the transition
needs.plain: Make sure an unfinished filling-machine job is handed to the person who will actually be responsible after the changeover. This prevents the job from being left without an owner.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration unresolved filling machinery work orders remain without confirmed ownership during the transition
needs.plain: Make sure each open job for the filling machines has a clearly named person responsible for it during the handover. This prevents work from being left unattended.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of transferred filling machinery work orders missing fault history, current status, priority, or sterility-related constraints
needs.plain: Make sure transferred work orders include the problem history, current progress, importance, and any requirements for keeping the equipment sterile. This helps the next team understand what still needs to be done and handle it safely.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the responsible recipient cannot access supporting maintenance records after the work-order transfer
needs.plain: The person taking over the unfinished work should still be able to see the related maintenance records. This helps them continue the work safely and correctly.

disposal.name: Verify maintenance waste disposition
disposal.statement: Verify the disposition of regulated waste generated through the filling machinery maintenance service.
disposal.description: This provides evidence that removed lubricants, chemicals, contaminated parts, or similar materials received appropriate final handling.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of regulated maintenance waste receiving final handling at an unauthorized destination
Minimize the likelihood that the documented waste classification differs from the removed material's actual classification
Reduce the percentage of regulated maintenance waste consignments lacking evidence of final handling
Reduce the number of custody gaps documented between waste removal and final handling
Reduce the time between final handling and receipt of disposition evidence by the Sustainability Officer
needs.plain: There should be less time between finishing the handling of maintenance waste and giving the environmental manager proof of what happened to it. This helps confirm that the waste was handled as required.

preparation.name: Arrange site access
preparation.statement: Arrange site access for authorized maintenance personnel.
preparation.description: Access preparation prevents security or facility controls from delaying the scheduled service.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of mandatory sterile-area entry prerequisites that remain incomplete at scheduled arrival
Minimize the likelihood that site access authorization remains pending at scheduled arrival
Reduce the number of discrepancies between maintenance personnel details and approved site-access records
Minimize the likelihood that the required site escort is unavailable when maintenance personnel arrive
Reduce the duration of scheduled maintenance access lost to conflicts with sterile fill-finish production or decontamination activities

preparation.name: Authorize work permits
preparation.statement: Authorize the permits required for the planned maintenance activities.
preparation.description: Review and formally approve the work permits — including hot-work, confined-space, or lockout/tagout authorisations — necessary before maintenance personnel begin servicing the filling machinery.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of approving a maintenance permit before required hazard controls are verified
needs.plain: Do not approve maintenance work until all required safety protections have been checked. This helps prevent people from starting work while hazards remain uncontrolled.

needs.stmt: Reduce the time spent waiting for missing hazard assessments or prerequisite approvals before a permit can be authorized
needs.plain: Reduce waiting for safety reviews and other required approvals before allowing work to begin. This helps work permits be approved without unnecessary delay.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of maintenance permits that omit applicable contamination restrictions for work in the sterile filling area
Reduce the percentage of authorized permits whose scope does not match the planned maintenance activities
Minimize the likelihood of overlapping maintenance permits imposing conflicting isolation or access requirements

preparation.name: Configure service notifications
preparation.statement: Configure maintenance service notifications for designated personnel.
preparation.description: Set up automated alerts, escalation rules, and communication channels so that relevant stakeholders are notified of upcoming, in-progress, or completed maintenance events.

needs.stmt: Reduce the degree of conflict among designated personnel regarding maintenance notification recipients, timing, and escalation responsibilities
needs.plain: Make sure the responsible people agree on who receives maintenance alerts, when they receive them, and who must be contacted if a problem gets worse. This helps maintenance issues reach the right person quickly.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of designated personnel records containing missing or incorrect contact and role details
needs.plain: Make sure staff records contain the correct names, contact details, and job responsibilities, so service alerts reach the right people.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of maintenance event statuses mapped to the wrong recipients or escalation rules
needs.plain: Make sure each maintenance status sends alerts to the right people and follows the right rules for raising urgent issues. This helps the right person respond when action is needed.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that required communication channels are unavailable during configuration
needs.plain: Make sure the ways people need to communicate are working during setup. This ensures that notifications and configuration questions can reach the right people.

needs.stmt: Reduce the time spent waiting for authorization to activate required communication channels
needs.plain: Spend less time waiting for permission to turn on the communication methods that are needed. This helps service messages start working sooner.

preparation.name: Define maintenance scope
preparation.statement: Define the maintenance activities authorised for each filling machinery asset.
preparation.description: Establish and document the boundaries of maintenance tasks — including permissible interventions, exclusions, and escalation thresholds — for each filling machine prior to service.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the maintenance scope authorises sterile-boundary interventions without required escalation
needs.plain: Prevent the maintenance plan from allowing work on the sterile barrier without getting the required higher-level review or approval. This helps protect the sterile process during repairs.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of required maintenance activities omitted from the authorised scope for each filling machinery asset
Minimize the degree of ambiguity in the documented boundaries between permissible and excluded maintenance interventions
Reduce the number of foreseeable filling machine conditions without documented escalation thresholds
Reduce the percentage of filling machinery assets whose scope is defined from incomplete or outdated configuration records

preparation.name: Disassemble machine guards
preparation.statement: Disassemble the guards and covers to access serviceable components.
preparation.description: Provides physical access to internal mechanisms that require servicing.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of stored energy or pressure being released during guard removal
needs.plain: Release or control trapped pressure and stored energy before removing the guards. Otherwise, parts could move suddenly or forcefully.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of contamination entering the sterile filling area while internal mechanisms are exposed
needs.plain: Keep germs from entering the germ-free filling area while parts inside the machine are uncovered. This protects the product during maintenance.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of attached interlocks, seals, or cables damaged during disassembly
Reduce the time required to release seized or inaccessible guard fasteners
Reduce the amount of unnecessary disassembly caused by incorrect guard or cover identification

preparation.name: Drain residual product
preparation.statement: Drain and purge residual product from the filling system before servicing.
preparation.description: Removes residual product to allow safe and clean access to internal components.

needs.stmt: Minimize the amount of residual product escaping into the sterile processing area during draining and purging
needs.plain: Keep as little leftover product as possible from escaping into the clean processing room while the equipment is emptied and cleared. This helps protect the room from contamination.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of technician exposure to residual product during draining and purging
needs.plain: Make it unlikely that a technician will come into contact with product left in the equipment while it is emptied and cleared. This helps protect the technician from exposure.

needs.stmt: Minimize the amount of residual product remaining trapped in internal filling-system components after draining and purging
Minimize the likelihood of drained product entering an incompatible waste stream
Reduce the duration of the servicing delay caused by draining and purging residual product
needs.plain: Shorten the servicing delay caused by removing leftover product from the equipment and lines. This allows workers to begin servicing sooner.

preparation.name: Establish a parts quarantine area
preparation.statement: Establish a designated quarantine area for removed components awaiting inspection or disposal.
preparation.description: Identify and mark a physical area near the work site where disassembled or replaced components are segregated from serviceable parts, preventing accidental reinstallation and enabling traceability.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that removed components are mixed with serviceable parts in or around the quarantine area
needs.plain: Keep removed parts separate from usable parts in and around the holding area. This prevents parts that should not be used from being mistaken for serviceable ones.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that removed components expose surrounding controlled areas to contamination while quarantined
needs.plain: Keep removed parts from spreading dirt or harmful material into nearby clean work areas while they are being held. This protects the surrounding areas from contamination.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of quarantined components lacking a matching traceability identifier
needs.plain: Fewer parts being held aside should be missing a matching identification number that links them to their records. This helps people know exactly where each part came from and what happened to it.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that removed components exceed the available quarantine space during maintenance
needs.plain: Make sure the holding area has enough room for all parts removed during maintenance. This prevents parts from being left outside the area or stored unsafely.

needs.stmt: Reduce the time spent waiting for required site or quality approval of the quarantine area
needs.plain: Spend less time waiting for the required approval that the area for holding questionable parts is suitable. This allows the area to be set up and used sooner.

preparation.name: Gather spare parts
preparation.statement: Gather the spare parts, seals, and consumables required for the maintenance task.
preparation.description: Prevents delays by confirming all consumables and replacement parts are available prior to service.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that gathered seals or consumables are contaminated before use in the sterile fill-finish area
needs.plain: Keep spare seals and supplies clean before they are used in the clean production area. This helps prevent unwanted contamination from reaching the product or equipment.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that a required spare part is unavailable at the scheduled maintenance start
Reduce the number of gathered parts that are incorrect or incompatible with the filling machinery
Minimize the likelihood that the approved maintenance scope conflicts with the parts requirements used by the technician
Reduce the number of gathered parts lacking required identity or release records

preparation.name: Inspect filling machine condition
preparation.statement: Inspect the filling machinery to assess wear, damage, and maintenance requirements.
preparation.description: Establishes the baseline condition and scope of work before maintenance begins.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that filling machinery inspection activities compromise sterile-area conditions
needs.plain: Checking the filling machine should not bring dirt or germs into the clean working area. The inspection must protect the conditions needed to keep the product clean.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of technician exposure to hazardous energy or process residues during the inspection
needs.plain: Protect the technician from dangerous stored power and leftover process material during the inspection. This helps prevent injury or harmful contact.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percent of wear or damage sites missed during the filling machinery inspection
Reduce the duration of inspection delay caused by unavailable machine access or prerequisites
needs.plain: Spend less time waiting to inspect the filling machine because the machine or something needed for the inspection is unavailable. This helps staff confirm its condition sooner.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of maintenance needs assigned the wrong scope or urgency after inspection
needs.plain: After checking the machine, assign each maintenance need the correct area of work and level of urgency. This helps workers address the right problems in the right order.

preparation.name: Isolate power sources
preparation.statement: Isolate and lock out the machine's electrical, pneumatic, and hydraulic power sources.
preparation.description: Protects personnel from hazardous energy during maintenance on filling equipment.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of isolated power sources being re-energized while maintenance is underway
needs.plain: Prevent anyone from turning the power back on while maintenance is in progress. This helps protect the people working on the equipment.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of maintenance beginning before the machine's zero-energy state is confirmed
needs.plain: Make it less likely that maintenance starts before all energy sources to the machine are confirmed to be shut off and unable to restart it. This helps protect the person doing the work.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of electrical, pneumatic, or hydraulic power sources left energized after lockout
needs.plain: After shutting down and locking the equipment, make sure no electrical power, air pressure, or fluid pressure is still active.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of hazardous pneumatic or hydraulic pressure remaining trapped after source isolation
needs.plain: As little dangerous air, liquid, or stored pressure as possible should remain trapped after the power source is shut off. This helps prevent unexpected release of pressure during the work.

needs.stmt: Reduce the time spent waiting for access or authorization to isolate all power sources

preparation.name: Measure component wear
preparation.statement: Measure component wear and tolerances to determine maintenance scope.
preparation.description: Confirms wear-level data to prioritize which components require attention first.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of introducing contamination into product-contact areas while measuring component wear and tolerances
Reduce the percentage of wear and tolerance readings outside acceptable accuracy
Reduce the percentage of required component wear points left unmeasured during the assessment
Minimize the degree of disagreement among applicable wear and tolerance limits
Minimize the time the technician waits for machinery access before measuring wear and tolerances
needs.plain: Give the technician access to the machinery as quickly as possible so they can check how worn the parts are. This helps the condition of the machinery be assessed sooner.

preparation.name: Record baseline settings
preparation.statement: Record the machine's baseline settings and control parameters before adjustment.
preparation.description: Captures pre-service settings so the machine can be restored to production spec afterward.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of required baseline settings and control parameters omitted from the pre-service record
Reduce the percentage of recorded baseline values that differ from the machine's pre-adjustment values
Minimize the likelihood that machine settings are adjusted before baseline capture is complete
Minimize the likelihood that the baseline record is unavailable when production settings must be restored
Reduce the number of recorded values that cannot be unambiguously matched to their corresponding controls
needs.plain: Fewer recorded measurements or settings should be unclear about which control they belong to. Clear links help people know exactly what each value describes.

preparation.name: Register filling machinery assets
preparation.statement: Register each filling machinery asset in the maintenance service record.
preparation.description: Formally enrol each covered filling machine into the maintenance service programme, capturing asset identifiers, location, and configuration baseline for ongoing service tracking.

needs.stmt: Minimize the percentage of covered filling machinery assets omitted from the maintenance service record
needs.plain: Reduce the share of covered filling machines missing from the upkeep service records. This helps ensure every covered machine receives and can be tracked for its required service.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of registered filling machinery assets assigned an incorrect asset identifier
needs.plain: Make sure each filling machine is given the correct identifying number when it is recorded. This helps people find and manage the right machine.

needs.stmt: Minimize the percentage of registered filling machinery assets with missing or incorrect configuration details
needs.plain: Record complete and correct setup details for every filling machine, so the equipment list accurately describes what is installed.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of registered filling machinery assets linked to the wrong facility location
needs.plain: Make sure each recorded filling machine is connected to the correct site or building. This helps staff know where the machine is located.

needs.stmt: Reduce the time an asset registration waits for conflicting source information to be resolved
needs.plain: Registering a machine should take less time when different records contain conflicting information. This helps the machine be added to the records accurately and quickly.

preparation.name: Retrieve diagnostic data
preparation.statement: Retrieve diagnostic and fault log data from the machine's control system.
preparation.description: Enables condition-based servicing by extracting operational and fault data from the machine.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of retrieved diagnostic and fault log data that is corrupted or truncated
Reduce the percentage of required historical fault records overwritten before retrieval
Minimize the duration of sterile filling interruption caused by diagnostic data retrieval
needs.plain: Keep the interruption to sterile filling as short as possible when retrieving fault information. This helps production resume quickly.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that required access authorization is unavailable to the technician when retrieval begins
needs.plain: Make sure the technician has the required permission to enter the system when data retrieval begins. This prevents delays in collecting the diagnostic information.

needs.stmt: Minimize the duration of machine control system unavailability during the diagnostic data retrieval window
needs.plain: Keep the machine’s control system unavailable for as little time as possible while retrieving its fault information. This helps reduce disruption to work with the machine.

preparation.name: Review maintenance documentation
preparation.statement: Review the machine's service manuals and maintenance history before starting work.
preparation.description: Ensures the correct manufacturer procedures and specifications are on hand before servicing.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that a critical sterile-boundary or personnel-safety requirement is overlooked before servicing the filling machine
needs.plain: Make sure no important germ-control or worker-safety requirement is missed before the filling machine is serviced. This helps maintenance begin only after the necessary precautions are understood.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of using service documentation that does not match the filling machine's current configuration or revision
Minimize the likelihood that required service manuals or specifications are unavailable before work begins on the filling machine
Reduce the percent of relevant prior maintenance events missing from the filling machine's history
Reduce the number of unresolved conflicts among manufacturer procedures, machine specifications, and maintenance records before servicing

preparation.name: Sanitize work area
preparation.statement: Sanitize the machine surfaces and surrounding work area prior to maintenance.
preparation.description: Establishes a hygienic work zone required when servicing food, beverage, or pharmaceutical fillers.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of contamination spreading from the maintenance work zone into adjacent sterile areas during sanitation
needs.plain: Keep germs and other unwanted material from spreading from the area being cleaned into nearby areas that must stay germ-free. This helps protect products and equipment from contamination.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of residual contamination remaining on machine surfaces and in the surrounding maintenance area after sanitation
needs.plain: Leave as little leftover contamination as possible on the machine and in the nearby service area after cleaning. This helps keep the equipment and work space safe for the next task.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that sanitized machine surfaces become recontaminated before maintenance begins
needs.plain: Surfaces that have been cleaned and disinfected should stay clean until maintenance starts. This helps prevent germs or other dirt from getting back onto the machine.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of the technician being exposed to hazardous residues while sanitizing the filling machinery and work area
needs.plain: The person sanitizing the filling equipment and work area should avoid contact with harmful leftover substances. This protects them while they clean and disinfect the area.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of waiting for hygienic release of the work zone before maintenance begins
needs.plain: Release the work area for maintenance as soon as it has been properly cleaned and made safe. This prevents maintenance workers from having to wait unnecessarily.

preparation.name: Schedule maintenance window
preparation.statement: Schedule the maintenance window to align with production downtime.
preparation.description: Plan and coordinate a maintenance time slot that minimises disruption to filling line production schedules.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that required maintenance personnel are unavailable during planned production downtime
Reduce the frequency of production schedule changes that displace the confirmed maintenance window
Minimize the amount by which expected maintenance work exceeds the available production downtime
Reduce the number of required sterile-line shutdown prerequisites omitted when selecting the maintenance window
Reduce the duration of approval delays for the maintenance window from production and quality stakeholders

preparation.name: Set service intervals
preparation.statement: Set the required maintenance interval for each filling machinery asset.
preparation.description: Determine and document the frequency of scheduled maintenance events for each filling machine based on operational parameters, manufacturer guidance, and regulatory requirements.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of filling machinery assets assigned maintenance intervals without accounting for sterile fill-finish criticality
Minimize the likelihood of using obsolete maintenance guidance or regulatory criteria when setting an asset interval
Reduce the number of filling machinery assets with unresolved interval conflicts among manufacturer guidance, operating conditions, and regulatory requirements
Reduce the percentage of filling machinery assets with missing operating parameters needed to set maintenance intervals
Reduce the percentage of maintenance interval records lacking decision rationale and approval traceability
needs.plain: Make sure each service schedule explains why the timing was chosen and shows who approved it. This makes the schedule understandable and accountable.

preparation.name: Stage tools, instruments, and replacement parts
preparation.statement: Stage the tools, calibration instruments, and replacement parts required for the planned maintenance work.
preparation.description: Covers the physical assembly and staging of all service tooling, calibration equipment, and replacement components needed before maintenance begins, ensuring technicians have everything on hand at the point of work.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of staged replacement parts becoming contaminated before installation
needs.plain: Keep replacement parts and the tools prepared for the repair clean until they are installed.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of required tools, calibration instruments, or replacement parts omitted from the staged set
Minimize the likelihood of staging calibration instruments with expired or unverified calibration status
Reduce the number of staged replacement parts that do not match the filling machinery configuration
Reduce the duration of maintenance delay caused by staged items arriving after the scheduled start
needs.plain: Have the tools, equipment, and replacement parts ready before maintenance is due to begin. This prevents the maintenance team from waiting for items to arrive.

preparation.name: Train service technicians on maintenance procedures
preparation.statement: Train service technicians on the maintenance procedures for filling machinery.
preparation.description: Deliver structured instruction to maintenance personnel covering filling machinery service methods, safety protocols, and equipment-specific procedures before they perform work.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of breaching sterile-area contamination controls during practical filling machinery instruction
needs.plain: Prevent training exercises from allowing harmful contamination into the area where clean product is handled. This protects the product while technicians learn how to maintain the filling equipment.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of trainees or instructors being injured during practical filling machinery maintenance exercises
needs.plain: Prevent trainees and instructors from being hurt during hands-on maintenance practice on filling machines. This helps everyone learn the work safely.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of maintenance procedures taught inconsistently with the applicable filling machinery configuration or service requirements
needs.plain: Teach service workers the correct maintenance steps for the specific filling machine and its service needs. Consistent training helps them perform the work safely and correctly.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percent of technicians unable to demonstrate critical filling machinery maintenance procedures correctly before field work
needs.plain: Have fewer technicians who cannot correctly show the important machine-care tasks before they begin work at a customer site. This helps prevent mistakes during field service.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of scheduled instruction lost because required machinery, documentation, or authorized area access is unavailable
needs.plain: Lose as little scheduled teaching time as possible when the needed machines, instructions, or approved work areas are unavailable. This helps service workers learn how to maintain the equipment on schedule.

preparation.name: Verify calibration status of measurement instruments
preparation.statement: Verify the calibration status of measurement instruments before using them during maintenance.
preparation.description: Confirm that gauges, flow meters, torque wrenches, and other measurement tools are within their calibration validity period and traceable to applicable standards before the maintenance task begins.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that an instrument with expired calibration is cleared for maintenance use
Minimize the likelihood that an instrument's calibration expires before its maintenance measurements are completed
Reduce the percentage of instruments lacking calibration traceability evidence for applicable standards
Reduce the number of instruments whose identifiers do not match their calibration records
Reduce the time spent waiting for calibration records before maintenance can begin
needs.plain: Spend less time waiting for records showing that measuring tools were checked and adjusted before starting repairs. This lets maintenance begin sooner and helps ensure the tools give dependable readings.

acquisition.name: Assign service contacts
acquisition.statement: Assign the operational contacts responsible for maintenance coordination.
acquisition.description: Named contacts give technicians clear points of communication for approvals, access, and escalation.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of required approval, access, or escalation roles left without assigned contacts
needs.plain: Give a named contact to every role responsible for approvals, access, or handling problems. This helps people know whom to reach when action or a decision is needed.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that assigned contacts lack the authority needed to approve maintenance work or arrange sterile-area access
needs.plain: Make sure the assigned contacts have permission to approve the needed work and arrange entry into restricted clean areas. Otherwise, maintenance could be delayed.

needs.stmt: Reduce the degree of ambiguity in responsibility for maintenance approvals, facility access, and escalation among assigned contacts
needs.plain: Clearly assign who approves maintenance, who arranges access to the site, and who is contacted when a problem needs to be raised. This prevents delays and confusion about who must act.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that assigned contacts are unavailable when maintenance coordination is required
needs.plain: Make sure the people assigned to handle maintenance can be reached when their help is needed. This prevents delays in arranging repairs or upkeep.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of assigned contacts whose details are incorrect or outdated when provided to technicians
needs.plain: Make sure technicians receive the correct, up-to-date contact details for the people assigned to help them. This helps technicians reach the right person without delay.

acquisition.name: Compare service proposals
acquisition.statement: Compare filling machinery maintenance service proposals against documented requirements.
acquisition.description: Proposal comparison distinguishes differences in coverage, service levels, pricing, parts treatment, response commitments, and exclusions.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of documented maintenance-service requirements lacking corresponding proposal information
needs.plain: Make sure every maintenance requirement in the records is matched with information in the proposed service plans, so the customer can compare what is being offered.

needs.stmt: Reduce the degree of ambiguity in documented maintenance-service requirements used for comparison
needs.plain: Make the maintenance requirements clear enough that different service offers can be compared fairly. This helps people choose the right service without guessing what each offer includes.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of comparing proposal prices built on inconsistent scope, parts, or labor assumptions
needs.plain: Compare service prices using the same work, replacement parts, and labor assumptions. Otherwise, a cheaper price may simply leave out work or costs included in another proposal.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of comparing superseded or mismatched proposal revisions
needs.plain: Make sure all offers being compared are the correct and most recent versions. Using an old or mismatched version could lead to the wrong choice or price comparison.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of internally conflicting coverage, service-level, response, or exclusion terms within proposals
needs.plain: Make sure the proposals do not contradict themselves about what is covered, what service is promised, how quickly help will come, or what is excluded. Clear and consistent terms make it easier to compare the offers and know what to expect.

acquisition.name: Complete filling machinery maintenance service purchase
acquisition.statement: Complete the transaction for the selected filling machinery maintenance service.
acquisition.description: Finalise payment or purchase-order issuance to secure the maintenance service agreement.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of negotiations over unresolved service scope, site-access conditions, or contractual responsibilities
needs.plain: Spend less time settling disagreements about what service includes, how workers may enter the site, and who is responsible for what. This helps the maintenance work get agreed and started sooner.

needs.stmt: Reduce the amount by which final charges for the selected filling machinery maintenance service exceed approved funding
needs.plain: Keep the final price of the filling-machine maintenance service within the approved budget. This prevents the purchase from costing more than the amount set aside for it.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of delay in obtaining final authorization for payment or purchase-order issuance
needs.plain: Get the final approval needed to pay for the service or issue the purchase order without delay. This allows the purchase to be completed and the supplier to be paid or engaged.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of discrepancies between transaction details and the selected filling machinery maintenance service quotation
needs.plain: Make sure the purchase details match the chosen offer for maintaining the machines that fill products. This helps ensure the right service is bought at the agreed terms.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that missing supplier or compliance records block payment or purchase-order issuance
needs.plain: Make sure all supplier and required compliance records are complete, so the order can be issued and the supplier can be paid.

acquisition.name: Evaluate in-house versus outsourced maintenance options
acquisition.statement: Evaluate whether to outsource filling machinery maintenance or perform it in-house.
acquisition.description: Assess the operational, financial, and compliance trade-offs between self-performing maintenance and engaging a third-party service provider.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of unresolved evidence gaps concerning sterile fill-finish compliance capability for each option
Minimize the degree of error in estimates of production downtime exposure under each maintenance option
Reduce the amount of lifecycle maintenance cost omitted or allocated inconsistently between the in-house and outsourced options
needs.plain: Count ongoing repair and upkeep costs in the same way for both the company’s own team and an outside provider. This makes the two maintenance choices easier to compare fairly.

needs.stmt: Reduce the degree of mismatch in maintenance scope, response coverage, and exclusions used to compare the in-house and outsourced options
needs.plain: Compare the internal and outside maintenance choices using the same information about what work is covered, how quickly help is available, and what is left out.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of evaluation delays caused by conflicting stakeholder input or missing approvals
needs.plain: Spend less time waiting to decide whether maintenance should be done by the company or an outside provider when people disagree or approvals are missing. This helps the maintenance plan move forward.

acquisition.name: Evaluate service fit for installed machinery
acquisition.statement: Evaluate whether the maintenance service fits the installed filling machinery.
acquisition.description: Assess the compatibility and suitability of a provider's service scope against the specific filling machinery in the facility.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percent of applicable sterile fill-finish requirements omitted from the service-fit evaluation
Reduce the number of critical maintenance activities excluded from the service scope but missed during evaluation
Minimize the likelihood of judging the maintenance service compatible with an unsupported configuration of the installed filling machinery
needs.plain: Avoid deciding that a maintenance service will work with a machine setup that the service does not support. This prevents wasted time, cost, and possible equipment problems.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of installed machinery configuration details that are missing or outdated when fit is assessed
needs.plain: Keep the installed machinery information complete and current when deciding whether a service will work with it, so the decision is based on the equipment that is really there.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of discrepancy between the provider's and facility's interpretations of service responsibilities and exclusions
needs.plain: Make sure the service provider and the facility agree on who is responsible for each service task and what is not included. This helps prevent disputes or missed work.

acquisition.name: Negotiate service contract
acquisition.statement: Negotiate the maintenance service contract scope and pricing.
acquisition.description: Negotiating scope and pricing aligns the service level with budget and operational needs.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of mismatch between proposed response and restoration commitments and sterile fill-finish needs
Reduce the number of sterile-area maintenance and documentation requirements omitted from the proposed contract scope
Reduce the degree of ambiguity in responsibility for maintenance tasks and exclusions
needs.plain: Clearly state who is responsible for each maintenance job and what is not included. This prevents disagreements about who must do the work or pay for it.

needs.stmt: Reduce the amount by which proposed contract pricing exceeds the approved maintenance budget
needs.plain: Keep the proposed service price within the amount approved for maintenance. This helps avoid spending more than planned.

needs.stmt: Minimize the time required to resolve scope and pricing disagreements before contract completion
needs.plain: Settle differences about what the work includes and what it will cost as quickly as possible before finishing the agreement. This helps the contract be completed sooner.

acquisition.name: Obtain compliance documentation
acquisition.statement: Obtain the provider's insurance and compliance documentation.
acquisition.description: Insurance and compliance documentation must be obtained before allowing service on site.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of required insurance and compliance documents absent from the provider submission
Reduce the percentage of submitted insurance and compliance documents that are expired or fail site requirements
Minimize the likelihood that submitted insurance and compliance documentation cannot be authenticated with the issuing source
Reduce the number of discrepancies in provider identity, coverage, work scope, or validity details across submitted documents
needs.plain: Make sure all submitted documents agree about who the provider is, what it covers, what work it will do, and how long the information remains valid. This helps confirm that the provider meets the requirements.

needs.stmt: Reduce the time from requesting the documentation to receiving a complete provider package
needs.plain: Spend less time between asking for required records and receiving the service company’s complete set of documents. This helps the customer show that the work meets its obligations.

acquisition.name: Request service quote
acquisition.statement: Request a quote for the filling machinery maintenance service.
acquisition.description: A formal quote clarifies scope, labor rates, parts, and terms before commitment.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of mismatch between the quoted maintenance scope and the requested work
Reduce the number of required machinery configuration and maintenance details missing from the quote request
needs.plain: Include all the needed machine settings and maintenance information in the request for a price, so the service provider can give an accurate quote.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of required cost components and commercial terms omitted from the service quote
needs.plain: List every required charge and business term in the service price offer, so the customer can understand the full cost and conditions.

needs.stmt: Reduce the time the purchaser waits between submitting the request and receiving the service quote
needs.plain: The buyer should receive the service price and details soon after asking for them. This helps the buyer decide and arrange the service sooner.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the service quote expires before the purchaser completes internal approval
needs.plain: The service price offer should remain valid until the buyer finishes its internal approval. This prevents the buyer from having to request the offer again.

acquisition.name: Research maintenance service providers
acquisition.statement: Research available filling machinery maintenance service providers.
acquisition.description: Identify and gather information on providers offering filling machinery maintenance services prior to evaluation.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of relevant available filling machinery maintenance service providers omitted from the research findings
needs.plain: Include all important companies that can maintain the available filling machines. This gives the buyer a more complete view of possible maintenance support.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of provider profiles missing information on machinery coverage, sterile fill-finish experience, qualifications, or geographic availability
needs.plain: Keep fewer service-provider profiles missing information about the equipment they support, their experience with sterile drug filling and packaging, their qualifications, or where they can work. This makes it easier to choose a suitable provider.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that collected provider claims about qualifications and service capabilities are outdated or inaccurate
needs.plain: Make sure information collected about service companies’ training, experience, and abilities is current and correct. Outdated or wrong information could lead to choosing a provider that cannot do the work.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of ambiguity in the boundaries between providers' preventive, corrective, and emergency maintenance offerings
needs.plain: Clearly show where each provider’s routine, repair, and emergency maintenance services begin and end. This prevents two providers from assuming the other one is responsible.

needs.stmt: Reduce the time spent waiting for providers to supply requested service and qualification information
needs.plain: Spend less time waiting for providers to send information about their services and qualifications. This makes it faster to compare providers for maintenance work.

acquisition.name: Review service agreement terms
acquisition.statement: Review the maintenance service agreement terms and conditions.
acquisition.description: Contract terms, SLAs, and liability clauses must be reviewed to protect operations.

needs.stmt: Reduce the amount of contamination-related loss excluded from provider liability by the agreement terms
Reduce the degree of ambiguity in SLA response and service-restoration obligations for the sterile fill-finish line
Minimize the likelihood of overlooking terms that permit maintenance activity to disrupt sterile fill-finish operations without defined notice or coordination obligations
Reduce the number of referenced SLA exhibits or service schedules unavailable to the administrator during review
needs.plain: Fewer supporting attachments or service schedules referred to in the service agreement should be unavailable to the person reviewing it. Having them available helps the reviewer understand the full agreement.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of unresolved conflicts between the main agreement, SLA, and service schedules at the end of the administrator's review
needs.plain: Resolve differences among the main contract, the service commitments, and the detailed service schedules before the administrator finishes reviewing them. This ensures the agreement says the same thing everywhere.

acquisition.name: Schedule initial service visit
acquisition.statement: Schedule the initial filling machinery maintenance service visit.
acquisition.description: Arrange the timing and logistics for the first on-site maintenance visit following contract execution.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the scheduled visit conflicts with sterile fill-finish production, cleaning, or validation activities
Minimize the likelihood that qualified service personnel are unavailable during the required maintenance window
needs.plain: Make it unlikely that people with the needed service skills are unavailable during the period when maintenance must be done. This helps ensure the first service visit can take place on time.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of site-entry prerequisites remaining incomplete by the scheduled visit date
needs.plain: Complete all requirements for entering the site before the planned visit date. This helps ensure the visiting workers can get in and begin their work.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of discrepancy between the maintenance planner's and service provider's understanding of the machinery, service scope, and expected on-site work period
needs.plain: Make sure the maintenance planner and service provider agree about the machine, the work to be done, and how long the visit should take. This helps the first service visit run smoothly.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of visit-confirmation delay caused by unresolved travel and site logistics
needs.plain: Confirm the visit quickly by settling travel plans and details about getting to the site. This helps the first service visit happen without unnecessary delay.

acquisition.name: Seek references and reviews
acquisition.statement: Seek references and reviews for the maintenance service provider.
acquisition.description: References and reviews reveal a provider's reliability and quality of past maintenance work.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percent of available references based on maintenance work outside comparable sterile fill-finish environments
needs.plain: Rely less on information from maintenance work done in places that are not similar sterile production facilities. More relevant examples can lead to better decisions.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that maintenance results claimed in references cannot be verified
needs.plain: Make sure claims about maintenance results from other customers can be checked, so they can be trusted.

needs.stmt: Reduce the degree to which reviews omit maintenance failures and unresolved service issues
needs.plain: Make sure reviews include serious maintenance problems and service issues that have not been fixed. This helps give a more complete picture of the equipment.

needs.stmt: Reduce the degree of disagreement among references about maintenance quality and reliability
needs.plain: Make sure customer references give a consistent picture of the supplier’s maintenance work and how dependable its equipment is. This helps the buyer assess the quality of ongoing support.

needs.stmt: Minimize the time spent waiting for reference customers to respond to purchaser inquiries
needs.plain: Get answers from the supplier’s past customers as quickly as possible. Their feedback helps the buyer judge whether the supplier is dependable.

acquisition.name: Select filling machinery maintenance service provider
acquisition.statement: Select the filling machinery maintenance service provider that satisfies the purchase criteria.
acquisition.description: Make the final supplier selection decision based on evaluated proposals, credentials, pricing, and coverage fit.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of required maintenance scope for sterile fill-finish machinery omitted from proposals
Reduce the number of provider credentials and qualifications that cannot be verified before selection
Reduce the amount of foreseeable service charges excluded from quoted pricing
needs.plain: Make sure the quoted price includes service charges that are reasonably likely to occur. This helps prevent unexpected maintenance bills later.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of conflict in proposal information across service scope, coverage, and commercial terms
needs.plain: Make sure the supplier’s offer is consistent about the work covered, the level of service, and the business terms. This helps the buyer choose the right maintenance provider.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of supplier selection beyond the required maintenance decision date
needs.plain: Choose the company that will maintain the filling equipment before the required decision date. This prevents maintenance planning from being held up.

acquisition.name: Verify activation of purchased maintenance service
acquisition.statement: Verify that the purchased filling machinery maintenance service has been activated.
acquisition.description: Confirm with the provider that the service agreement is live, access is provisioned, and the first service cycle is scheduled.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the purchased filling machinery maintenance service remains inactive when verification is performed
needs.plain: Make sure the purchased maintenance service is active when someone checks it. This confirms that the buyer can actually use the service when needed.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of authorized maintenance personnel without provisioned access to the purchased maintenance service
needs.plain: Fewer approved repair workers should be unable to use the maintenance service that was purchased. This helps them get support when they need it.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the first maintenance service cycle remains unscheduled after activation
needs.plain: Make sure the first scheduled maintenance visit is set up after the service begins. This helps prevent the equipment from going without its planned first check.

needs.stmt: Reduce the time from purchase acceptance to receipt of verifiable activation confirmation
needs.plain: Spend less time between accepting a purchase and receiving confirmation that the service has really been turned on. This gives the buyer proof that the purchased maintenance service is ready to use.

acquisition.name: Verify provider credentials and technician qualifications
acquisition.statement: Verify the maintenance provider's credentials and technician qualifications.
acquisition.description: Confirm that the service provider holds appropriate certifications and that assigned technicians meet required qualification standards.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that technicians lacking required sterile fill-finish qualifications are approved for the maintenance project
needs.plain: Make sure only technicians with the required sterile production training and approval are assigned to the maintenance work. This helps prevent mistakes and protects the product and work area.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that expired or invalid maintenance provider credentials are accepted as current
Reduce the degree of mismatch between the provider credential scope and the planned filling machinery maintenance work
Reduce the percent of required provider credential and technician qualification evidence missing when verification begins
Reduce the time required to confirm the authenticity of submitted provider credentials and technician qualification records
needs.plain: Spend less time checking whether a provider’s submitted documents and workers’ training records are genuine. Fast confirmation helps ensure qualified people are approved to do the work.

usage.name: Adjust dose parameters mid-delivery
usage.statement: Modify a permitted dose rate or quantity parameter on the dosing system while delivery is underway.
usage.description: Authorized operator makes an in-session adjustment to dosing parameters without stopping the current delivery cycle.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of an adjusted parameter differing from the authorized target during delivery
needs.plain: When a setting is changed during delivery, it should match the approved target. This helps ensure that the right amount or condition is delivered.

usage.name: Deliver a bolus dose
usage.statement: Administer a rapid supplemental dose through the dosing system on demand during an active session.
usage.description: Covers the operator action of commanding the dosing system to deliver a discrete supplemental quantity above the ongoing programmed rate, as authorised and configured within the system.
usage.frequency: On demand during active dosing sessions; frequency varies by clinical need

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of deviation between delivered bolus and authorized quantity
needs.plain: Make the delivered single dose as close as possible to the amount that was approved. This helps ensure the person receives neither too much nor too little.

usage.name: Deliver dose in transit
usage.statement: Operate the dosing system while the patient is being transported or moving.
usage.description: Sustains uninterrupted precision dosing in mobile or field conditions.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of dose-output deviation caused by movement during transport
needs.plain: Keep the delivered amount from changing because the load moves while it is being transported. This helps ensure the intended amount reaches its destination.

usage.name: Diagnose active dosing fault
usage.statement: Use the dosing system's operational data to identify the cause of an active fault during a dosing session.
usage.description: Technician or operator reviews system-reported fault information to determine root cause and appropriate corrective action without stopping operation if possible.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of active dosing faults assigned an incorrect cause

usage.name: Handle dosing system input variation
usage.statement: Adjust dosing system operation to accommodate variation in the physical or chemical properties of the substance being delivered.
usage.description: Operator modifies settings or technique when the substance's viscosity, concentration, temperature, or other properties fall outside standard parameters during active delivery.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of delivered doses outside specification when formulation properties vary
needs.plain: Keep the number of doses that are the wrong strength or otherwise outside the required limits as low as possible when the mixture's properties change. This helps ensure people receive the intended dose.

usage.name: Initiate dosing cycle
usage.statement: Start the configured dosing cycle on the dosing system to begin active delivery.
usage.description: Covers the operator action of commanding the dosing system to commence a programmed delivery cycle, transitioning the system from a ready/configured state to active dosing.
usage.userGroup: Clinical staff, pharmacists, process operators, manufacturing technicians
usage.frequency: Once per dosing session or batch run

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of the wrong configured dosing cycle starting
needs.plain: Make sure the correct set of dosing steps starts, rather than the wrong one. This helps ensure the right amount of material is delivered in the right way.
needs.role: Clinical staff, pharmacists, process operators, manufacturing technicians

usage.name: Load Pharmaceutical Source
usage.statement: Load the pharmaceutical source container into the dosing system.
usage.description: This places the required medication, formulation, ingredient, or solution into the system before dosing begins.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of loading the wrong pharmaceutical source
needs.plain: Make sure the correct pharmaceutical material is loaded. Using the wrong source could result in the wrong product being made.

usage.name: Manage dosing system output
usage.statement: Manage the routed output of the dosing system to ensure delivered substance reaches the intended target pathway.
usage.description: Operator oversees or adjusts the output routing — tubing, port, channel assignment — of the dosing system during active delivery to confirm substance is directed correctly.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of delivered formulation entering an unintended output pathway
needs.plain: Make sure the prepared product goes only through the intended outlet and not through the wrong path.

usage.name: Monitor active delivery status
usage.statement: Observe the dosing system's real-time delivery status during active administration.
usage.description: Operator continuously tracks delivery progress indicators on the dosing system throughout a live dosing session.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of displayed delivery status differing from the actual dosing state
needs.plain: Make sure the status shown on the screen matches what the system is actually doing with the material. Incorrect information could lead workers to make the wrong decision.

usage.name: Monitor system status
usage.statement: Monitor the operating status and health of the system.
usage.description: Real-time monitoring of dose accuracy, pressure, and error states enables early detection of drift or failure.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of a critical dosing fault remaining undetected during sterile filling
needs.plain: Make sure a serious dosing problem is detected during the clean filling process instead of going unnoticed.

usage.name: Operate concurrent dosing channels
usage.statement: Run multiple simultaneous dose deliveries across parallel channels on the dosing system.
usage.description: Operator manages several independent delivery channels running in parallel on a multi-channel dosing system.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of doses delivered through channels assigned to the wrong product
needs.plain: Send as few doses as possible through a delivery path meant for a different product, so each product reaches the correct place.

usage.name: Operate dosing system in training mode
usage.statement: Use the dosing system's simulation or training mode to demonstrate or rehearse dosing procedures without delivering actual substance.
usage.description: Covers operating the dosing system in a non-delivery simulation mode for the purpose of training, competency assessment, or procedure rehearsal by operators or trainees.
usage.userGroup: Clinical educators, training coordinators, operators-in-training, biomedical engineers conducting demonstrations
usage.frequency: Periodically; during onboarding and competency re-assessment cycles

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of actual formulation being delivered during simulation
needs.role: Clinical educators, training coordinators, operators-in-training, biomedical engineers conducting demonstrations

usage.name: Record active delivery events
usage.statement: Capture the dosing system's administration events and delivered volumes during an active session.
usage.description: Operator or system logs real-time delivery data including timestamps, quantities, and delivery events for audit and review.

needs.stmt: Minimize the percentage of administration events missing from the active-session record

usage.name: Respond to active delivery alarm
usage.statement: Acknowledge and act on an alarm raised by the dosing system during active operation.
usage.description: Operator reviews alarm information and takes corrective or confirmatory action to address the alert condition.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of taking incorrect corrective action for the active alarm

usage.name: Resume dose delivery
usage.statement: Resume a previously paused dose delivery cycle.
usage.description: This continues dosing from a controlled interruption without initiating an unrelated cycle.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of resumed doses outside fill-quantity tolerance
needs.plain: Reduce the number of doses that are the wrong amount after delivery is restarted. This helps ensure each dose contains the intended quantity.

usage.name: Run repeated-dose sequence
usage.statement: Execute a configured sequence of repeated dosing cycles on the dosing system.
usage.description: Operator initiates and oversees an automated sequence of multiple consecutive dosing cycles as defined by the delivery protocol.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of sequence doses outside acceptance limits
needs.plain: Make sure repeated doses stay within the allowed amount. This matters so each dose is consistent and correct.

usage.name: Select dosing mode
usage.statement: Select the intended operating mode on the dosing system for the upcoming delivery cycle.
usage.description: Operator chooses among available dosing modes (e.g., continuous, intermittent, bolus) to match the required delivery profile.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of the selected dosing mode not matching the required delivery profile
needs.plain: Choose a way of dispensing the product that matches how much must be delivered and how it must be delivered. This helps ensure each container receives the intended amount in the right manner.

usage.name: Self-administer scheduled dose
usage.statement: Operate the dosing system to receive one's own medication at prescribed intervals.
usage.description: Supports patient self-dosing outside clinical supervision.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of deviation between received dose and prescribed dose
needs.plain: Make the amount of medicine received as close as possible to the amount prescribed. This helps the person take the intended amount.

usage.name: Switch medication reservoir
usage.statement: Replace or change the medication source feeding the dosing system during use.
usage.description: Maintains continuous dosing across different agents or refills mid-therapy.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of connecting an incorrect medication source during reservoir change
needs.plain: Avoid connecting the wrong medicine supply when replacing the medicine container. This helps ensure the person receives the intended medicine.

usage.name: Terminate dosing session
usage.statement: Stop and close out the dosing system's active delivery at the end of or before the configured endpoint of an administration session.
usage.description: Covers the operator action of ending an active dosing session, whether at the planned endpoint or earlier than configured, and transitioning the system to a stopped/safe state.
usage.userGroup: Clinical staff, pharmacists, process operators, manufacturing technicians

needs.stmt: Minimize the amount of pharmaceutical delivered after termination is initiated
needs.plain: Once stopping the dose has begun, deliver as little additional medicine as possible. This helps prevent giving more medicine than intended.
needs.role: Clinical staff, pharmacists, process operators, manufacturing technicians

usage.name: Verify delivered dose quantity
usage.statement: Verify that the quantity delivered by the dosing system matches the programmed target value.
usage.description: Operator cross-checks the dosing system's reported output against the intended dose to confirm accuracy.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of an inaccurate delivered quantity being accepted as matching the target
needs.plain: Avoid accepting an incorrect amount as if it matched the intended amount. The amount delivered should be checked against the target.

disposal.name: Archive device history records
disposal.statement: Archive the complete device history record of the retired system for the required regulatory retention period.
disposal.description: Compile and store all maintenance logs, calibration records, repair history, and usage data in a durable archive that satisfies applicable device-record retention requirements after the system is decommissioned.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of required device history records missing from the archive

disposal.name: Cancel connected services
disposal.statement: Cancel software subscriptions, calibration contracts, and support services linked to the retired dosing system.
disposal.description: Covers the administrative actions required to terminate all ongoing service relationships — software licenses, calibration service agreements, remote monitoring subscriptions, and technical support contracts — associated with a dosing system being decommissioned.
disposal.userGroup: Procurement staff, IT administrators, biomedical engineers, facilities managers

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of connected services remaining active after system retirement
needs.role: Procurement staff, IT administrators, biomedical engineers, facilities managers

disposal.name: Decommission the dosing system
disposal.statement: Withdraw the precision dosing system from active service at end of its service life.
disposal.description: Formally remove the system from operational use, completing all required administrative and physical steps to retire the unit.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of stored energy being released during system disconnection

disposal.name: Decontaminate the dosing system
disposal.statement: Decontaminate the system to render it safe for handling, transport, and downstream disposal.
disposal.description: Apply validated decontamination procedures to all external and internal surfaces of the unit before it is moved or handed over for disposal.

needs.stmt: Minimize the percentage of system surfaces retaining hazardous residue after decontamination

disposal.name: Deregister the asset
disposal.statement: Remove the system from the device inventory, asset registry, and regulatory device listings.
disposal.description: Update all internal asset management systems and, where required, notify regulatory bodies to remove the unit from the active device register.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of required asset listings retaining the retired dosing unit

disposal.name: Discard biohazardous disposables
disposal.statement: Place contaminated dosing disposables into an approved biohazard waste stream.
disposal.description: This controls exposure from used reservoirs, tubing, cartridges, needles, or other medication-contact components.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of contaminated dosing disposables entering the wrong waste stream

disposal.name: Dispose of pharmaceutical waste
disposal.statement: Dispose of expired or unused medication associated with the system through regulated channels.
disposal.description: Matters because expired or unused pharmaceutical stock associated with the system requires regulated destruction.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of pharmaceutical waste entering an incorrect regulated waste stream

disposal.name: Document disposal for compliance
disposal.statement: Document the final state and disposal method of the system for regulatory and audit records.
disposal.description: Create and retain records covering the system's condition at retirement, the disposal pathway taken, and any certificates of destruction or recycling as required by regulation.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of required disposal evidence items missing from compliance records

disposal.name: Export dosing records
disposal.statement: Export stored dosing and event records from the system prior to disposal.
disposal.description: Extract all historical dosing logs and event data from the system's storage to an external medium or system for retention and audit purposes.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of stored dosing records omitted from the retirement export

disposal.name: Notify stakeholders of system retirement
disposal.statement: Notify affected stakeholders that the dosing system is being taken out of service.
