disposal.description: Communicate the planned or completed retirement of the unit to all relevant internal and external parties, including clinical staff, procurement, and service providers.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of affected stakeholders omitted from the retirement notice

disposal.name: Obtain certificate of destruction or disposal
disposal.statement: Obtain a formal certificate of destruction or disposal from the authorised disposal vendor.
disposal.description: Secure written confirmation from the third-party recycler, scrapper, or disposal facility that the unit has been processed in accordance with applicable regulations, for use as compliance evidence.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of mandatory compliance details missing from the disposal certificate

disposal.name: Purge patient data
disposal.statement: Remove all stored patient and configuration data from the system's memory prior to disposal.
disposal.description: Erase protected personal and clinical data held in the device to comply with privacy regulations before the unit leaves the operator's custody.

needs.stmt: Reduce the amount of protected patient data remaining after sanitization

disposal.name: Recycle recoverable components
disposal.statement: Segregate and route recoverable materials from the retired system to approved recycling streams.
disposal.description: Identify reusable or recyclable parts of the decommissioned unit and direct them to appropriate recycling or refurbishment channels in compliance with applicable regulations.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of recoverable components entering disposal streams

disposal.name: Remove hazardous components
disposal.statement: Remove batteries and other hazardous components from the system for separate regulated disposal.
disposal.description: Disassemble the unit to extract batteries, capacitors, or other hazardous materials that require separate handling under waste regulations.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of hazardous components remaining inside the disassembled system

disposal.name: Remove residual medication
disposal.statement: Remove residual pharmaceutical material from the dosing system's reservoirs and fluid pathways prior to decommissioning.
disposal.description: Covers the emptying and flushing of all pharmaceutical substance from reservoirs, tubing, cartridges, and fluid-contact pathways to prepare the system for safe handling, decontamination, and downstream disposal processing.
disposal.userGroup: Clinical staff, pharmacists, biomedical engineers, decommissioning technicians

needs.stmt: Reduce the amount of pharmaceutical residue remaining in reservoirs or fluid pathways after removal
needs.role: Clinical staff, pharmacists, biomedical engineers, decommissioning technicians

disposal.name: Segregate disposable components
disposal.statement: Separate single-use and disposable system components into their designated waste streams.
disposal.description: Sort consumable and single-use parts of the system (e.g., tubing sets, filters, cartridges) into the correct regulated waste categories prior to collection.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of product-contact disposables assigned to incorrect waste categories

disposal.name: Transfer ownership
disposal.statement: Transfer ownership of the functional system to a new operator or facility.
disposal.description: Matters when a still-functional system is transferred to another facility or resold on the secondary market.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of required qualification and maintenance records missing at handover

disposal.name: Transition dosing operations to replacement system
disposal.statement: Transition dosing operations from the retired system to its designated replacement.
disposal.description: Plan and execute the operational handover so that dosing continuity is maintained while the old system is retired and the replacement becomes the active unit.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of sterile dosing interruption during operational handover

preparation.name: Assemble dosing system components
preparation.statement: Assemble the pump, reservoir, delivery, and ancillary components into a complete, operational dosing unit.
preparation.description: Covers mechanical assembly of all sub-components into the required operating configuration prior to installation.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of product-contact components becoming contaminated during assembly
needs.plain: Keep parts that touch the product clean while they are being put together. This helps prevent contamination of the product.

preparation.name: Calibrate dosing mechanism
preparation.statement: Calibrate the dosing mechanism against a traceable measurement reference to verify delivered volumes meet specified tolerances.
preparation.description: Confirms metrological accuracy of the dosing system prior to use or after maintenance, using an appropriate reference standard.

needs.stmt: Reduce the frequency of calibration doses falling outside specified tolerances
needs.plain: Make sure the amounts delivered by the dosing mechanism stay within the allowed limits more consistently. Accurate amounts help the process produce the intended result.

preparation.name: Configure dosing parameters
preparation.statement: Configure dose limits, delivery rates, and operating protocols within the system settings.
preparation.description: Establishes the core dosing parameters governing how the system delivers pharmaceutical material during operation.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of dose parameters configured outside approved specifications
needs.plain: Make sure the settings that control each dose stay within the approved limits. This helps ensure that each dose is the right amount.

preparation.name: Connect fluid pathways
preparation.statement: Connect tubing, cartridges, and fluid lines between the dosing system and the intended delivery point.
preparation.description: Establishes the complete fluid circuit required for pharmaceutical material to flow from source to delivery endpoint.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of sterile fluid-path connections becoming contaminated during setup
needs.plain: Keep the clean liquid-handling connections free from germs and other contamination during setup. This protects the cleanliness of the product.

preparation.name: Connect power supply
preparation.statement: Connect the dosing system to its power source and backup power provisions.
preparation.description: The system needs stable power and, where applicable, backup power for uninterrupted dosing.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of connecting a power source incompatible with dosing-system requirements

preparation.name: Establish documentation and record-keeping baseline
preparation.statement: Establish the required installation, calibration, and configuration records for the dosing system in the applicable quality or compliance documentation system.
preparation.description: Creates the foundational documentation package — installation qualification records, calibration certificates, configuration logs — required before the system enters service in a regulated environment.

needs.stmt: Minimize the percentage of required setup records missing from the documentation baseline

preparation.name: Establish operator accounts and access controls
preparation.statement: Provision operator accounts, roles, and access permissions for authorised dosing system users.
preparation.description: Covers creating user credentials, assigning role-based access levels, and configuring lockout or override permissions within the dosing system prior to initial operation.
preparation.userGroup: IT administrators, biomedical engineers, pharmacy informatics staff, department managers
preparation.frequency: Once at initial setup; repeated when user roles change

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of operator accounts receiving permissions beyond approved responsibilities
needs.role: IT administrators, biomedical engineers, pharmacy informatics staff, department managers

preparation.name: Install and mount unit
preparation.statement: Install and secure the dosing system in its designated operating location.
preparation.description: The unit must be physically mounted or positioned in its operating location for safe operation.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of the mounted dosing system shifting during operation

preparation.name: Integrate with clinical systems
preparation.statement: Integrate the dosing system with pharmacy, dispensing, and electronic health record systems.
preparation.description: Integration with pharmacy, EHR, or dispensing systems enables closed-loop medication workflows.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of clinical data fields mapped incorrectly between connected systems

preparation.name: Learn system operation
preparation.statement: Learn to operate the dosing system through self-guided instruction, documentation, or on-device guidance.
preparation.description: Enables individual operators to acquire the knowledge and skills needed to set up, operate, and respond to the system independently.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of the operator completing onboarding with undetected knowledge gaps

preparation.name: Load approved drug library
preparation.statement: Import approved drug definitions, concentration profiles, formulary limits, and dosing rules into the dosing system.
preparation.description: Populates the system with the facility's approved pharmaceutical data set to enable safe dose-limit enforcement.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of an obsolete drug-library version being loaded into the dosing system

preparation.name: Perform installation qualification
preparation.statement: Execute a formal installation qualification protocol to verify that the dosing system is installed according to manufacturer specifications and site requirements.
preparation.description: Systematic, documented verification that all physical installation criteria — location, utilities, environmental conditions, ancillary connections — meet defined acceptance criteria before operational qualification proceeds.

needs.stmt: Reduce the amount of required installation evidence missing from the qualification record

preparation.name: Prime dosing pathway
preparation.statement: Prime the dosing system to purge air and establish continuous pharmaceutical fluid flow through the delivery pathway.
preparation.description: Ensures the fluid path is fully charged with liquid and free of air before dosing commences.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of the delivery pathway occupied by residual air after priming
needs.plain: Remove as much trapped air as possible from the delivery tubing before giving a dose. This helps the system deliver the right amount.

preparation.name: Register the dosing system
preparation.statement: Register the pharmaceutical precision dosing system with the manufacturer or applicable authority to activate warranty and support entitlements.
preparation.description: Complete product registration requirements with the manufacturer, regulatory body, or support portal following receipt.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of system identifiers conflicting with receiving and quality records

preparation.name: Set safety controls and dose limits
preparation.statement: Set dose limits, alarm thresholds, lockout conditions, and override permissions within the dosing system.
preparation.description: Defines the safety envelope within which the system operates, preventing unsafe dosing events through configured constraints.

needs.stmt: Reduce the degree to which dose limits deviate from validated dosing boundaries
needs.plain: Keep the allowed dose limits as close as possible to the proven safe dosing range, so the amount given stays safe and effective.

preparation.name: Train staff on use
preparation.statement: Train clinical staff to operate the dosing system according to established protocols.
preparation.description: Trainers must instruct staff on safe operation and protocol compliance before clinical use.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of trained staff unable to execute critical dosing tasks correctly

preparation.name: Unbox and inspect received system
preparation.statement: Unpack the precision dosing system and inspect all components for shipping damage, missing parts, and specification conformity.
preparation.description: Covers unpacking, visual inspection, and verification of delivered contents against order documentation.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of shipping-damaged components overlooked during receipt inspection

preparation.name: Validate system setup readiness
preparation.statement: Validate the fully configured dosing system by executing required pre-use functional tests before commencing operation.
preparation.description: Confirms that assembly, connections, calibration, and configuration are correct and that the system is safe and ready for use.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of functional tests falsely accepting an incorrect system configuration

acquisition.name: Assess integration compatibility
acquisition.statement: Assess how a candidate dosing system integrates with existing production lines, control systems, and facility infrastructure.
acquisition.description: Evaluate communication protocols, physical interfaces, utility requirements, and software compatibility of the dosing system relative to the buyer's existing environment.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of control-interface mismatches left unidentified before purchase

acquisition.name: Assess pharmaceutical compatibility
acquisition.statement: Assess each candidate system's compatibility with the intended substances, delivery formats, and process conditions.
acquisition.description: Evaluate whether the dosing system materials, contact surfaces, and operating parameters are compatible with the specific compounds or formulations it will handle.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of overlooking formulation-contact material interactions during assessment

acquisition.name: Complete purchase transaction
acquisition.statement: Complete the purchase transaction and contract terms for the dosing system.
acquisition.description: Matters at the point of commitment to finalize price, warranty, and service terms.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of discrepancies between the agreed dosing-system scope and the signed contract

acquisition.name: Evaluate regulatory compliance fit
acquisition.statement: Evaluate whether a candidate dosing system meets applicable regulatory and validation requirements for the intended operating environment.
acquisition.description: Review system documentation, certifications, and validation readiness against the regulatory framework governing the buyer's facility or application.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of required compliance evidence items missing from candidate documentation

acquisition.name: Evaluate service and support capabilities
acquisition.statement: Evaluate the service, maintenance support, and spare-parts availability offered by each candidate supplier.
acquisition.description: Assess post-sale support structures including response times, field service coverage, training offerings, and parts supply chain to inform supplier selection for a long-lifecycle capital asset.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of critical spare parts lacking lifecycle availability commitments

acquisition.name: Evaluate total cost of ownership
acquisition.statement: Evaluate the total cost of ownership of candidate dosing systems over their expected operational life.
acquisition.description: Estimate and compare acquisition price, installation, consumables, maintenance, calibration, and end-of-life costs across candidate systems to support a financially sound purchasing decision.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of required lifecycle costs omitted from candidate estimates

acquisition.name: Obtain reference site visits or demonstrations
acquisition.statement: Obtain access to reference installations or live demonstrations of candidate dosing systems.
acquisition.description: Arrange visits to existing customer sites or request hands-on demonstrations to observe system performance, usability, and reliability under real or simulated operating conditions.

needs.stmt: Reduce the degree to which demonstration conditions differ from intended sterile fill-finish use

acquisition.name: Qualify the supplier
acquisition.statement: Qualify the supplier's quality systems and audit history before commitment.
acquisition.description: Matters in regulated environments where supplier qualification is a prerequisite for purchase.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of critical supplier quality-system deficiencies remaining undetected before approval

acquisition.name: Request supplier quotations
acquisition.statement: Request price and delivery quotations from qualified dosing system suppliers.
acquisition.description: Matters when moving from evaluation toward budgeting and vendor selection.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of critical sterile fill-finish requirements omitted from quotation requests

acquisition.name: Research dosing system options
acquisition.statement: Research available pharmaceutical precision dosing systems and their capabilities.
acquisition.description: Identify and gather information on precision dosing systems on the market, including technical specifications and supplier landscape.

needs.stmt: Minimize the percentage of relevant pharmaceutical dosing systems overlooked during research

acquisition.name: Secure procurement approval
acquisition.statement: Secure internal approval to procure the selected dosing system.
acquisition.description: Navigate financial, legal, and governance approval workflows required to authorise the capital purchase.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of required approval documents missing from the procurement package

acquisition.name: Track equipment shipment
acquisition.statement: Track the shipment and delivery status of the ordered dosing system.
acquisition.description: Matters between order and receipt to coordinate installation resources and downtime.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of discrepancy between reported shipment state and actual delivery state

acquisition.name: Verify dosing performance evidence
acquisition.statement: Verify the dosing accuracy and performance evidence provided for each candidate system.
acquisition.description: Review test data, validation reports, and performance claims to confirm they substantiate the stated accuracy and reliability of candidate dosing systems.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree to which test conditions differ from intended sterile fill-finish use

maintenance.name: Back up system data
maintenance.statement: Back up the dosing system's configuration, calibration, dosing records, and maintenance data to a secure location.
maintenance.description: Covers the periodic export and secure storage of all system data — including dosing event records, configuration settings, calibration data, and maintenance logs — to protect against data loss and support audit and requalification activities.
maintenance.userGroup: Biomedical engineers, IT administrators, pharmacy informatics staff, quality assurance personnel
maintenance.frequency: Periodically per site data-management schedule; before any system change

needs.stmt: Minimize the percentage of required configuration and dosing data omitted from the backup
needs.role: Biomedical engineers, IT administrators, pharmacy informatics staff, quality assurance personnel

maintenance.name: Document maintenance history
maintenance.statement: Document each maintenance action in the dosing system's service record.
maintenance.description: Records completed maintenance, calibration, and repair activities with date, performer, and findings to support regulatory traceability and equipment qualification.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of completed maintenance actions omitted from the service record

maintenance.name: Inspect wetted parts and seals
maintenance.statement: Inspect the wetted parts and seals of the dosing system for wear or damage.
maintenance.description: Visually and tactilely examines fluid-contact components and sealing elements to detect degradation, cracking, or deformation that could compromise dosing integrity or sterility.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of wetted-part wear remaining undetected during inspection

maintenance.name: Protect system during storage
maintenance.statement: Store and protect the dosing system under controlled environmental conditions during periods of non-use.
maintenance.description: Covers preparing the system for storage (e.g., cleaning, powering down, applying protective covers) and maintaining it in controlled conditions to prevent environmental degradation, contamination, or damage during idle periods.
maintenance.userGroup: Biomedical engineers, equipment custodians, facilities staff
maintenance.frequency: As required during periods of planned non-use

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of product-contact surfaces becoming contaminated during storage
needs.role: Biomedical engineers, equipment custodians, facilities staff

maintenance.name: Repair faulty components
maintenance.statement: Repair faulty components to restore the dosing system to operational specification.
maintenance.description: Diagnoses and repairs failed or out-of-spec components — mechanical, electrical, or software — to return the dosing system to its qualified operating state.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of a repaired component failing qualified operating specifications
needs.plain: Make sure a repaired part continues to work within the required limits. This helps prevent the same equipment problem from returning.

maintenance.name: Replace consumable components
maintenance.statement: Replace worn or depleted consumable components of the system.
maintenance.description: Consumable components such as seals, filters, tubing, and cartridges degrade with use and must be swapped to preserve performance.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of the sterile product-contact path becoming contaminated during replacement
needs.plain: Lower the chance that germs or other unwanted material get into the parts that touch the sterile medicine when replaceable parts are changed. This helps keep the medicine safe to use.

maintenance.name: Replace filtration elements
maintenance.statement: Replace the filtration elements in the dosing system's fluid or air circuits.
maintenance.description: Removes and replaces inline filters, membrane filters, or air filters in the system's fluid-path or environmental control circuits to maintain flow performance and prevent contamination.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of unfiltered flow bypassing the replacement filtration element

maintenance.name: Replace power components
maintenance.statement: Replace depleted or degraded power-storage components.
maintenance.description: Battery replacement preserves portable operation, clock retention, alarm availability, or safe shutdown capability.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of retained dosing configuration values lost during power-component replacement

maintenance.name: Requalify system performance
maintenance.statement: Requalify the dosing system against its validated performance criteria after maintenance or change.
maintenance.description: Executes a formal requalification protocol to confirm that the dosing system continues to meet its validated performance specifications following maintenance, repair, or component change.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of qualification doses outside validated tolerances
needs.plain: Make sure doses remain within the tested limits when the system’s performance is checked again. This confirms the system still works as required.

maintenance.name: Schedule preventive service
maintenance.statement: Schedule preventive maintenance service for the system.
maintenance.description: Scheduled preventive service reduces unplanned downtime and keeps the system within qualified operating parameters.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of preventive service being scheduled after the required maintenance interval

maintenance.name: Service mechanical assemblies
maintenance.statement: Service the dosing system's mechanical drive assemblies.
maintenance.description: Specified servicing limits friction, binding, backlash, or wear that could reduce dosing consistency.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of mechanical drive misalignment after reassembly

maintenance.name: Sterilize product-contact surfaces
maintenance.statement: Sterilize the product-contact surfaces of the dosing system.
maintenance.description: Applies an approved sterilization method to all surfaces that contact the pharmaceutical product, ensuring microbial control between production runs or after maintenance.

needs.stmt: Minimize the percentage of product-contact surface area retaining viable microorganisms after sterilization
needs.plain: Remove as many living germs as possible from every surface that touches the product. This helps prevent the product from becoming contaminated.

maintenance.name: Test safety functions
maintenance.statement: Test the dosing system's alarms, interlocks, and shutdown functions.
maintenance.description: Functional testing confirms that protective controls respond correctly to abnormal operating conditions.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of the shutdown function failing under a specified abnormal condition
needs.plain: Make sure the shutdown feature works when the expected fault or unusual situation occurs. This helps stop the equipment safely when something goes wrong.

maintenance.name: Update control software
maintenance.statement: Update the control software and firmware of the dosing system.
maintenance.description: Installs approved software and firmware releases on the dosing system's control platform to incorporate patches, regulatory compliance changes, or new functionality.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of updated controls delivering doses outside approved specifications
needs.plain: Prevent updated computer controls from giving doses that are too high, too low, or otherwise outside the approved limits. This helps ensure each product receives the correct amount.

maintenance.name: Upgrade system components
maintenance.statement: Upgrade the modular components of the system.
maintenance.description: Upgrading modules or components extends capability and keeps the system current with evolving process requirements.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of post-upgrade doses outside validated tolerances
needs.plain: Make sure doses remain within the tested limits after machine parts or computer controls are upgraded. This confirms the upgrade has not harmed performance.

maintenance.name: Verify dosing accuracy
maintenance.statement: Verify the dosing system's delivered quantity against a traceable reference.
maintenance.description: Performs a check — without mechanical adjustment — to confirm that the dosing system delivers within its specified accuracy limits, generating a record for quality and regulatory purposes.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of assessing delivered quantity against incorrect accuracy limits

usage.name: Adjust mixing parameters
usage.statement: Adjust mixing parameters during the batch to achieve target consistency.
usage.description: Tuning speed, temperature, or timing during use accommodates ingredient behavior.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that a parameter adjustment compromises the sterile state of the batch
needs.plain: Reduce the chance that changing mixing settings makes the batch no longer germ-free. This helps protect the batch while achieving the desired mixing.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that adjusted settings exceed validated operating limits during the batch
needs.plain: Keep changed mixing settings within the tested and approved operating range during production. Settings outside that range could affect the product.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of deviation from target consistency after a parameter adjustment
needs.plain: Keep the mixture as close as possible to the desired evenness after changing the mixing settings. This helps maintain consistent product quality.

needs.stmt: Minimize the time between detecting a consistency deviation and applying the corresponding parameter adjustment
Reduce the frequency of consistency oscillations caused by successive parameter adjustments during the batch

usage.name: Adjust process setpoints
usage.statement: Adjust an active process setpoint — including temperature, flow, timing, and dosing parameters — through the batching system controls.
usage.description: Combines controlling batch temperature (#56) with adjusting process setpoints (#74). Temperature control is a specific instance of setpoint adjustment; #74 is the correct Level-1 framing that subsumes #56.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that a setpoint adjustment moves a critical parameter outside validated operating limits during an active batch
needs.plain: Lower the chance that changing a machine setting during production pushes an important condition outside its tested safe range. This helps keep the batch within safe and approved conditions.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of selecting the wrong batch phase, parameter, or target value when adjusting an active process setpoint
needs.plain: Avoid choosing the wrong step, setting, or desired value when changing an active process. This helps prevent the process from running incorrectly.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that an entered setpoint fails to take effect in the active batch
needs.plain: Make sure a new target setting actually takes effect in the batch that is running. Otherwise, the process may continue using the old setting.

needs.stmt: Reduce the time between recognizing a process deviation and completing the required setpoint correction
Reduce the frequency of process-parameter oscillation caused by competing setpoint changes during an active batch

usage.name: Coordinate multi-operator use
usage.statement: Coordinate batching system use across multiple operators during production.
usage.description: Manage task handoffs, access, and sequencing when more than one operator is working with the batching system simultaneously or across shifts.

needs.stmt: Reduce the frequency of sterile-boundary breaches caused by overlapping operator activity
needs.plain: Two or more operators working at the same time should not cause a break in the clean boundary around the process. This helps keep the product protected from contamination.

needs.stmt: Reduce the frequency of batching tasks performed out of the required sequence during multi-operator production
Minimize the likelihood of conflicting operator actions altering the same batch simultaneously
Reduce the number of shift handoffs that omit current batch status or pending actions
Reduce the duration of production delays caused by operator access conflicts

usage.name: Diagnose process deviations
usage.statement: Diagnose a process deviation from the system's operational data.
usage.description: This helps isolate abnormal ingredient delivery, vessel conditions, control responses, or equipment behavior during use.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that measurement faults are mistaken for actual batch conditions
Minimize the likelihood of assigning the wrong initiating event when responses cascade through the batching system
Reduce the number of batch-affecting manual interventions absent from the operational record
Minimize the percentage of critical operational records missing from the deviation interval
Minimize the degree of timestamp misalignment across ingredient delivery, vessel, control, and equipment records

usage.name: Discharge finished batch
usage.statement: Discharge the finished batch from the system to downstream processes.
usage.description: Operate the system's discharge or transfer function to move the completed liquid batch to the next process step, storage, or filling line.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the finished batch becomes contaminated during discharge
needs.plain: Keep the completed batch from becoming contaminated while it is being emptied or transferred. This protects the quality of the finished product.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the finished batch is transferred to the wrong downstream destination
Reduce the amount of finished batch released outside the intended transfer path during discharge
Reduce the amount of finished batch remaining in the batching system after discharge
Reduce the duration of finished-batch hold while the downstream destination is unavailable

usage.name: Draw in-process sample
usage.statement: Draw an in-process sample from the system's designated sampling point.
usage.description: This supports intermediate evaluation of the liquid before the batch cycle is completed.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of introducing microbial or particulate contamination into the batch during the sample draw
needs.plain: Taking a sample should not introduce germs or particles into the batch. This helps keep the batch clean and trustworthy.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of drawing the in-process sample from the wrong batch or sampling point
needs.plain: Make sure the sample comes from the correct batch and the correct place, so its test results describe the product being made.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of difference between the sample composition and the liquid at the designated sampling point
Minimize the likelihood that the designated sampling point is inaccessible or obstructed when the sample is required
Minimize the likelihood of an uncontrolled liquid release during the sample draw

usage.name: Execute addition sequence
usage.statement: Execute the batching system's programmed ingredient-addition sequence.
usage.description: Combines operating the agitation function (#55) with executing the ingredient-addition sequence (#73). Agitation is an embedded function within the addition sequence execution; both are sub-activities of running a batch cycle, with #73 being the correct Level-1 framing.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of adding an ingredient not specified for the current programmed step
needs.plain: Add only the ingredient called for in the current step. This prevents the wrong ingredient from being added to the batch.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of breaching the sterile boundary during ingredient addition
needs.plain: Keep the germ-free area sealed while ingredients are added. This prevents outside contamination from entering the product.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of deviation between the specified and actual ingredient quantity added at a programmed step
needs.plain: Make the amount of ingredient added at each planned step match the required amount as closely as possible. This helps ensure the final product has the intended recipe.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of programmed ingredient-addition steps skipped during a batch run
Reduce the duration of interrupted agitation while the ingredient-addition sequence is active

usage.name: Handle input variations
usage.statement: Handle variations in incoming ingredient properties during batching.
usage.description: Accommodating changes in ingredient viscosity, temperature, or concentration keeps batches on-target.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that operator intervention needed to accommodate an ingredient property variation compromises the sterile boundary during batching
needs.plain: Let the operator handle differences in an ingredient without breaking the protected clean barrier around the batch. This helps keep the batch free from contamination.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of batches with composition outside target because incoming ingredient concentration differs from its expected value
needs.plain: Reduce the number of batches whose mixture is off target because an ingredient arrives stronger or weaker than expected. This helps keep each batch’s composition consistent.

needs.stmt: Reduce the degree of batch nonuniformity caused by variation in incoming ingredient viscosity
needs.plain: Keep each production run as consistent as possible when the incoming ingredient is thicker or thinner than usual. This helps the finished product remain uniform.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that incoming ingredient temperature variation causes degradation before batching is complete
Reduce the duration of batching beyond the allowable processing window while the operator evaluates and responds to unexpected ingredient properties

usage.name: Load ingredient components
usage.statement: Load ingredient components into the batching system for a new batch.
usage.description: Physically or automatically load raw liquid and/or dry ingredients into the system's intake for a new production batch.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of loading an incorrect ingredient component or lot into the new batch
needs.plain: Make sure the new batch receives the correct ingredient and batch lot. This prevents the wrong material from being used in the product.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of introducing microbial, particulate, or cross-product contamination while loading ingredient components
needs.plain: Prevent germs, dust, particles, or material from another product from getting into the ingredients while they are being loaded. This helps keep the ingredients clean and prevents products from being mixed accidentally.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of deviation between the ingredient quantity loaded and the batch target
needs.plain: Load an ingredient amount that is as close as possible to the amount required for the batch. This helps keep the finished product consistent.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of ingredient loading interruptions caused by an intake blockage
Minimize the likelihood of operator exposure to hazardous ingredient material during loading
needs.plain: Protect workers from breathing in or touching dangerous ingredient material while loading it. This helps prevent harm to their health.

usage.name: Monitor batch progress
usage.statement: Monitor batch progress and system status during operation.
usage.description: Observe live system indicators, alarms, and process data to track batch status and detect anomalies throughout the batching cycle.

needs.stmt: Reduce the time between onset of a critical batch anomaly and operator recognition
Minimize the likelihood that a critical alarm is obscured by simultaneous noncritical alarms
Minimize the degree of discrepancy between indicated batch status and the actual process state
Minimize the duration of unavailable or frozen live process data during a batching cycle
Minimize the duration of gaps in active batch monitoring during operator handovers

usage.name: Operate system in manual mode
usage.statement: Operate the batching system in manual mode to complete a batch step.
usage.description: Bypass automated sequencing and control individual system functions (pumps, valves, agitators) manually to complete a batch step during trials, failures, or non-standard conditions.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of nonsterile or unintended material entering the batch through a manually established flow path
needs.plain: Make sure unwanted or unclean material cannot enter the product through a path set up by hand. This helps protect the product when the equipment is being operated manually.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree to which critical process conditions deviate from approved limits during manual control
Reduce the number of required pump, valve, or agitator actions omitted or performed out of sequence during the batch step
Minimize the likelihood of exceeding the allowable batch hold limit while completing the step manually
Minimize the likelihood of loss of liquid containment while independently actuating system functions

usage.name: Pause batch cycle
usage.statement: Pause the active batch cycle through the system controls.
usage.description: This limits further processing when an input, equipment, safety, or quality issue requires intervention.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the active batch cycle continues after the operator issues the pause command
Minimize the likelihood that pausing the batch cycle leaves the system outside safe pressure or temperature limits
Minimize the likelihood that the operator pauses the wrong batch cycle during an urgent intervention
Reduce the degree of uncertainty about whether the active batch cycle has fully paused
Minimize the likelihood that the operator cannot access the pause controls because of the current control mode or access authority

usage.name: Record batch data
usage.statement: Record batch data for each production run performed on the system.
usage.description: Capture and store process data, timestamps, and batch identifiers generated during a production run for traceability and reporting.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of process data being linked to an incorrect batch identifier for a production run
Reduce the percentage of required process data absent from the batch record for a production run
Minimize the likelihood of a stored batch record becoming inaccessible when needed for traceability or reporting
Minimize the degree of mismatch between recorded timestamps and actual production events
Reduce the time from production-run completion until the complete batch record is available for traceability and reporting

usage.name: Recover interrupted batch
usage.statement: Resume an interrupted batch from an approved system state.
usage.description: This enables controlled continuation after a stoppage without restarting the entire run.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of sterile batch contamination during the interruption or resumption
needs.plain: Prevent the medicine from becoming contaminated when production is stopped and then started again. The process must be brought back safely without exposing the batch to unwanted material.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of resuming the interrupted batch from an incorrectly identified system state
Minimize the degree of deviation from approved batch-condition limits during the interruption
Minimize the likelihood that the approved interruption hold limit is exceeded before batch resumption
Reduce the time the interrupted batch waits for required utilities after resumption is initiated

usage.name: Rinse between batches
usage.statement: Rinse the batching system's wetted surfaces between consecutive batch runs.
usage.description: Flush or rinse product-contact surfaces, lines, and vessels between batches of the same or different formulations to prevent cross-contamination or carry-over.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of introducing microbial or particulate contaminants to product-contact surfaces during rinsing
needs.plain: Keep germs and bits of unwanted material from getting onto product-touching surfaces while they are rinsed between batches.

needs.stmt: Minimize the percent of prior-batch residue remaining on wetted surfaces after rinsing
Minimize the amount of rinse fluid remaining in the batching system when the next batch begins
Reduce the duration of batching system unavailability caused by between-batch rinsing
needs.plain: Keep the equipment that prepares products in batches from being unavailable for long while it is rinsed between batches. This helps the next batch start sooner.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of rinse-fluid leaks or spills during between-batch rinsing
needs.plain: Avoid spilling or leaking rinse liquid while cleaning between production runs. This helps keep people, equipment, and the work area safe and clean.

usage.name: Scale batch size
usage.statement: Scale the batch size to match the required production volume.
usage.description: Adjust the system's batch quantity and associated parameters to produce a larger or smaller volume than the default recipe specifies.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that ingredient proportions are incorrect after scaling the batch quantity
needs.plain: Make sure the ingredient amounts stay in the correct proportions when the batch size is changed. This helps produce the intended mixture.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of composition nonuniformity within the scaled batch
needs.plain: Make the materials in the larger batch as evenly mixed as possible. This helps ensure that each portion has the intended composition.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the scaled batch quantity falls outside the system's validated operating range
Minimize the likelihood that the scaled batch exceeds validated sterile hold limits before filling
Minimize the likelihood that required ingredients are unavailable in sufficient quantities for the enlarged batch

usage.name: Start batching cycle
usage.statement: Start the batching cycle to begin preparing the liquid batch.
usage.description: Initiate the automated or manual batching cycle sequence after recipe and quantity parameters are confirmed.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of initiating the batching cycle with parameters that differ from the confirmed recipe or quantity
needs.plain: Reduce the chance of starting a mixing or production run with settings or an amount different from the approved instructions. This helps ensure the run makes the intended product in the intended quantity.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of initiating the batching cycle while sterile boundary conditions are compromised
needs.plain: Do not start mixing and batching while the barriers that keep the process clean are damaged, open, or otherwise not working properly. This helps prevent contamination of the batch.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the batching cycle initiates before the operator's intentional start command
Minimize the likelihood that unresolved process or safety interlocks block cycle initiation
Reduce the duration of waiting for the batching system to accept the start command after all start conditions are satisfied
needs.plain: Have the mixing system begin its work quickly once every required condition is ready. This avoids wasting time after the operator has given the start instruction.

usage.name: Switch between recipes
usage.statement: Switch the batching system between different batch recipes during production.
usage.description: Combines setting batch recipe parameters before a run (#62) with switching between recipes during production (#67). Setting parameters before a run is a sub-step performed when switching between recipes; both address the same usage-phase recipe-selection activity.
usage.frequency: Per recipe change event during production.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of residual material from the previous formulation contaminating the next batch during the recipe change
needs.plain: Keep leftover material from the previous product out of the next batch when changing recipes. This prevents the new batch from being mixed with the wrong product.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of incorrect recipe parameters being activated for the next batch
needs.plain: Reduce the chance that the next batch starts with the wrong instructions or settings. This helps prevent the batch from being made incorrectly.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of selecting an unapproved or obsolete recipe version for the next batch
needs.plain: Reduce the chance that someone chooses a recipe that has not been approved or is no longer current for the next batch. This helps ensure the batch is made using the correct instructions.

needs.stmt: Reduce the amount of batch material discarded after an incorrect recipe change
Reduce the duration of production interruption while switching the batching system to the next recipe

usage.name: Transfer batch to intermediate vessel
usage.statement: Transfer the prepared batch to an intermediate holding or surge vessel.
usage.description: Operate the system's transfer function to move a completed batch into a buffer tank or intermediate vessel when direct downstream processing is not immediately available.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of batch contamination during transfer to the intermediate vessel
needs.plain: Prevent germs or other unwanted material from getting into the batch while it is moved into another container. This protects the batch from being spoiled or made unsafe.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of routing the prepared batch to the wrong intermediate vessel
Minimize the likelihood of exceeding the approved hold limit while awaiting intermediate vessel availability
Reduce the percent of the prepared batch remaining in the source vessel after transfer
Reduce the amount of batch lost through leakage or overflow during transfer

usage.name: Troubleshoot batching faults
usage.statement: Troubleshoot batching faults that occur during a production run.
usage.description: Identify and resolve faults or alarms that interrupt or degrade the batching process while the system is in operation.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of compromising sterile conditions while troubleshooting the batching fault
Minimize the likelihood that the technician misidentifies the underlying cause of the batching fault
Minimize the likelihood of altering a critical batch parameter while testing the suspected fault
Minimize the likelihood of the batch exceeding allowable hold limits before the batching fault is resolved
Reduce the degree of ambiguity in the alarm and batch-state indications available to the technician

usage.name: Verify batch accuracy
usage.statement: Verify that the batching system delivers the correct quantity and composition of each batch within specified tolerances.
usage.description: Periodic end-to-end accuracy verification of batch outputs against target recipes to confirm that cumulative instrument drift or mechanical wear has not degraded dosing performance.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that offsetting ingredient deviations conceal an out-of-tolerance dose during end-to-end verification
needs.plain: Make it very unlikely that one ingredient being too high hides another ingredient being too low during a full batch check. Each ingredient amount should be correct on its own.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that a verification sample changes composition through contamination before analysis
Minimize the likelihood that verification results are biased by out-of-tolerance measurement references
Reduce the number of recipe ingredient pathways not exercised during accuracy verification
Reduce the number of recipe targets or tolerance limits that conflict across verification records

usage.name: Verify batch endpoint
usage.statement: Verify the batch endpoint from the batching system's process readings.
usage.description: Confirm that the batch has reached its defined endpoint criteria using process readings available on the system before authorising discharge.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of batches confirmed at endpoint while defined criteria remain unmet
Minimize the degree of disagreement among process readings used to verify the batch endpoint
Minimize the likelihood that endpoint criteria cannot be matched unambiguously to the available process readings
Reduce the number of required process readings unavailable during endpoint verification
Reduce the duration that process readings lag behind the batch's actual condition during endpoint verification

usage.name: Verify batch quality
usage.statement: Verify batch quality against specification during preparation.
usage.description: Sampling or in-line measurement during use confirms the batch meets specification.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of deviation between reported and actual batch quality attributes
needs.plain: Make sure the reported product quality matches the product’s actual quality. This matters because decisions should be based on accurate results.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of introducing contamination into the sterile batch during sampling
needs.plain: Prevent germs or other unwanted material from getting into the sterile batch while a sample is taken. This helps keep the batch safe and usable.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that a collected sample does not represent the batch as a whole
needs.plain: Make sure the collected sample accurately reflects the entire lot, rather than just one part of it. This makes the quality decision more reliable.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that batch results are evaluated against an incorrect or obsolete specification
needs.plain: Make it unlikely that a batch is judged using quality limits that are wrong or no longer current. This matters because the decision about the batch could be incorrect.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of the batch hold while awaiting quality verification results
needs.plain: Spend less time keeping a medicine batch on hold while waiting for quality test results. This helps the batch be released or addressed sooner.

disposal.name: Archive lifecycle records
disposal.statement: Archive required lifecycle, maintenance, and disposal compliance records for the retired system.
disposal.description: Combines documenting disposal activities for compliance records (#114) with archiving required lifecycle records (#103). Both describe the same record-archiving obligation at end of life.
disposal.userGroup: Quality assurance, compliance officers, records management.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of required lifecycle, maintenance, and disposal compliance records missing or incomplete when the retired batching system archive is finalized
needs.plain: Make sure the retired mixing system’s records are complete, including its use, maintenance, and proper disposal. This provides a reliable history and shows that required rules were followed.
needs.role: Quality assurance, compliance officers, records management.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that archived records become unreadable or inaccessible during the mandated retention period
needs.plain: Make sure stored records remain readable and available throughout the required storage period. This prevents important information from being lost when it is needed later.
needs.role: Quality assurance, compliance officers, records management.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of archived records attributed to the wrong retired batching system or component
needs.plain: Keep stored records from being linked to the wrong retired mixing system or part. This helps preserve an accurate history of retired equipment.
needs.role: Quality assurance, compliance officers, records management.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of archived records assigned an incorrect retention or disposition requirement
needs.plain: Keep stored records from being given the wrong rule for how long to keep them or when to dispose of them. This helps ensure records are kept and destroyed at the right times.
needs.role: Quality assurance, compliance officers, records management.

needs.stmt: Reduce the time required records remain unarchived after the decommissioning archive deadline
needs.plain: Spend less time leaving required records unfiled after the deadline for storing them has passed. Filing them promptly keeps important history available and organized.
needs.role: Quality assurance, compliance officers, records management.

disposal.name: Conduct end-of-life safety review
disposal.statement: Conduct a safety review of the system before initiating decommissioning activities.
disposal.description: Assess residual hazards — pressure, chemical, electrical, mechanical — present in the system to establish safe decommissioning procedures and protective measures.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of residual pressure, chemical, electrical, or mechanical hazards left unidentified before decommissioning
needs.plain: Before taking equipment out of service, find any remaining pressure, chemical, electrical, or moving-part dangers. This helps prevent injuries during removal or shutdown.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of a hazardous energy release or chemical exposure during system inspection
needs.plain: Avoid releasing stored energy or exposing people to chemicals while inspecting the system. This helps protect workers during the final safety review.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percent of hazard-bearing areas of the batching system that remain uninspected during the safety review
Minimize the likelihood that decommissioning activities begin before review findings and protective requirements are finalized
needs.plain: Make sure equipment is not taken out of service before the safety review is complete and all needed protections are decided.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of system records that are missing or inconsistent with the end-of-life configuration
needs.plain: Avoid missing records or records that do not match the system’s final setup. This helps people identify remaining safety concerns accurately.

disposal.name: Decommission the system
disposal.statement: Decommission the liquid batching and preparation system at end of service life.
disposal.description: Execute all steps required to permanently take the system out of service.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of injury from stored energy or pressurized liquid released during decommissioning
needs.plain: Prevent injury from trapped pressure or stored force being released while the equipment is taken out of service. This makes dismantling safer.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of technician exposure to hazardous residues during system dismantling
needs.plain: Protect the technician from dangerous leftover material while taking the system apart. This helps prevent harmful contact during shutdown and removal.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of active sterile fill-finish production disrupted by disconnecting shared services
Reduce the percentage of decommissioned system materials assigned incorrect disposal classifications
Reduce the number of required decommissioning records left incomplete or inconsistent at project closure
needs.plain: Complete and keep consistent all records needed when the system is taken out of use at the end of the project. This prevents confusion about what was done and leaves a reliable closing record.

disposal.name: Decontaminate wetted components
disposal.statement: Decontaminate tanks, lines, and mixing components before disposal.
disposal.description: Apply appropriate decontamination procedures to all wetted surfaces and internals to meet safety and regulatory requirements prior to disposal.

needs.stmt: Minimize the percentage of wetted surface area retaining hazardous residues after decontamination
needs.plain: After cleaning, as little as possible of the wet surface should still have dangerous residue on it. This helps protect people and prevent the next use from being affected.

needs.stmt: Minimize the amount of contaminated liquid released into the work area or waste stream during decontamination
needs.plain: As little contaminated liquid as possible should spill into the work area or be sent into waste during cleaning. This helps protect people and the surrounding area.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of technician exposure to residual process material while opening or draining tanks, lines, and mixing components
needs.plain: Protect technicians from contact with leftover process material when they open or drain tanks, pipes, and mixing equipment.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of internal zones left untreated because they cannot be accessed during decontamination
Reduce the number of decontamination records missing evidence required for disposal approval
needs.plain: Make sure the cleaning records include all proof needed to approve disposal. Without this evidence, the material may not be cleared for disposal.

disposal.name: Disconnect process interfaces
disposal.statement: Disconnect the system from upstream feeds, downstream lines, and utilities.
disposal.description: Safely isolate the system from all connected process streams and utility supplies before removal.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of contamination entering adjacent sterile process lines through opened interfaces
needs.plain: Keep germs or other unwanted material from entering nearby clean process lines through open connections. This protects the cleanliness of the connected systems.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of releasing pressurized or hazardous residual material during disconnection
needs.plain: Make it unlikely that trapped pressure or dangerous leftover material will escape when equipment connections are opened. This matters because the release could injure people or contaminate the area.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that an undiscovered process or utility connection remains live during disconnection
needs.plain: Avoid leaving an unnoticed pipe, cable, or other connection carrying pressure, electricity, or material when it is disconnected. This helps prevent unexpected releases or injuries.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of disruption to adjacent active equipment caused by disconnecting a shared interface
needs.plain: Disconnect a shared connection without interrupting nearby equipment that is still operating. This lets the work continue while other equipment remains in use.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of damaging facility-side piping or fittings while separating connections
needs.plain: Avoid damaging the site's pipes or connecting parts while taking the connections apart. This helps keep the site's piping usable and leak-free.

disposal.name: Dispose of hazardous residues
disposal.statement: Dispose of hazardous and regulated chemical residues retained by the batching system according to applicable regulations.
disposal.description: Combines disposing of hazardous chemical residues (#100) with disposing of regulated residues retained by the system (#119). Both describe the same regulatory disposal obligation for chemical residues.
disposal.userGroup: Environmental health and safety personnel, waste management contractors.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of incompatible retained chemical residues being combined during segregation and packaging
needs.plain: Keep leftover chemicals that could react badly from being mixed while they are separated and packed for disposal.
needs.role: Environmental health and safety personnel, waste management contractors.

needs.stmt: Reduce the amount of hazardous or regulated chemical residue left in the batching system after disposal activities
needs.plain: Leave as little dangerous or legally controlled chemical residue as possible in the mixing system after it is emptied for disposal. This helps protect people and the environment.
needs.role: Environmental health and safety personnel, waste management contractors.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of hazardous residue being released during recovery, packaging, or transfer
needs.plain: Prevent dangerous leftover material from escaping while it is collected, packed, or moved. Safe handling keeps people and the surrounding area protected.
needs.role: Environmental health and safety personnel, waste management contractors.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of retained chemical residue streams assigned an incorrect regulatory classification
needs.role: Environmental health and safety personnel, waste management contractors.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of a residue shipment being rejected because required regulatory documentation is incomplete or inconsistent
needs.plain: Reduce the chance that a shipment of leftover hazardous material is refused because required legal papers are missing or do not match. This helps the material reach an approved disposal destination.
needs.role: Environmental health and safety personnel, waste management contractors.

disposal.name: Document final system condition
disposal.statement: Record the system's physical and operational condition at retirement.
disposal.description: Capture the final state of all major components and subsystems to support asset records, warranty claims, or future reference.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree to which residue or inaccessible surfaces obscure the physical condition of product-contact components
Reduce the duration of inspection stoppage caused by hazardous stored energy in retired system components
Minimize the percentage of major components or subsystems omitted from the final condition record
needs.plain: Record the condition of every major part of the system at the end, so problems are not overlooked and others know what they are receiving.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that operational condition cannot be verified after supporting utilities are disconnected
needs.plain: Make it unlikely that the machine’s working condition cannot be checked after its supporting equipment and connections are turned off or removed. This helps create a reliable record of its final condition.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of condition entries associated with the wrong component or subsystem identifier
needs.plain: Make sure each condition note is linked to the correct part or section of the system. This prevents people from making decisions based on information about the wrong item.

disposal.name: Export batch records
disposal.statement: Export historical batch data and recipe records from the control system prior to decommissioning.
disposal.description: Extract all stored batch histories and production records from the system's control platform to ensure data is preserved after the system is retired.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of historical batch data and recipe records omitted from the export
needs.plain: Make sure exported records include all past production-group information and written machine instructions, so the history is not missing important details.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that batch records are corrupted during extraction or transfer
needs.plain: Make sure production records are not damaged or changed while they are being copied out or moved. The records must remain usable afterward.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of exported batch histories missing links to their recipe versions and audit trails
needs.plain: Make sure exported production records stay linked to the correct recipe version and history of changes. These links help people understand how each batch was made and what was changed.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that exported records cannot be opened or interpreted after the control platform is retired
needs.plain: Make sure the transferred production records can still be opened and understood after the machine’s control software is retired.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of misalignment between export verification and decommissioning schedules
needs.plain: Make sure the timing for checking exported batches matches the timing for taking the related equipment out of service, so records are checked before they are no longer available.

disposal.name: Migrate control configuration
disposal.statement: Transfer the retiring system's required control configuration to its replacement.
disposal.description: Extract and port control logic, parameters, and automation settings from the retiring system to ensure continuity of operations on the replacement platform.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of sterility-critical interlocks, alarms, or sequence steps that behave incorrectly after transfer
needs.plain: Avoid having important safety connections, warnings, or operating steps work incorrectly after the control settings are moved to another system. This helps the equipment keep operating safely.

needs.stmt: Minimize the percentage of required control logic, parameters, and automation settings absent from the replacement configuration at cutover
needs.plain: Put all needed operating instructions, settings, and automatic controls into the replacement system before it is put into use, so it behaves as expected.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of selecting a superseded or unapproved retiring-system configuration as the migration source
needs.plain: Avoid choosing an outdated or unapproved setup from the system being replaced as the starting point for the move. Using the wrong setup could cause errors in the new system.

needs.stmt: Minimize the percentage of transferred configuration elements lacking traceability to an approved source record required for release
needs.plain: Make sure every moved setting can be traced back to an approved record before the system is put into use. This shows where each setting came from and supports safe approval.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of sterile fill-finish production restart delay caused by configuration migration at cutover
needs.plain: Spend less time waiting to restart germ-free medicine production while the old equipment settings are moved to the replacement. This helps the replacement start with the right settings sooner.

disposal.name: Notify affected stakeholders
disposal.statement: Notify operations, quality, and safety stakeholders of the system removal.
disposal.description: Operations, quality, and safety teams need notice before the system goes offline.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of required operations, quality, and safety stakeholders omitted from the system-removal notice
needs.plain: Make sure the notice about removing the system reaches everyone responsible for operations, quality, and safety. This gives all affected people time to prepare and respond.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that stakeholders receive the notice too late to prepare for the system removal
needs.plain: Make sure the notice arrives early enough for everyone affected to prepare for the system’s removal.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of critical removal details missing from the notice
needs.plain: Avoid leaving important information about removing the equipment out of the notice. Complete instructions help everyone affected understand what will be removed and what they need to do.

needs.stmt: Reduce the degree of inconsistency in removal dates, scope, or responsibilities communicated across operations, quality, and safety teams
needs.plain: Make sure the operations, quality, and safety teams receive the same information about when equipment will be removed, what the work includes, and who is responsible.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that stakeholder receipt and understanding remain unconfirmed before removal begins
needs.plain: Make sure everyone affected has received and understood the notice before removal starts. This helps them prepare and avoid surprises.

disposal.name: Obtain regulatory disposal approvals
disposal.statement: Obtain required regulatory approvals and permits before disposing of the system.
disposal.description: Identify and secure all permits, notifications, or regulatory clearances required by applicable environmental and safety authorities prior to decommissioning activities.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of required disposal approvals or notifications omitted from the submission plan
needs.plain: Include every required approval and notice for disposing of the equipment in the submission plan. This helps avoid delays or violations during disposal.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that approval applications are rejected for incomplete or inaccurate system-disposal information
needs.plain: Make sure disposal applications contain complete and accurate information about the system being discarded. This helps prevent the applications from being rejected.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of regulatory review beyond the planned decommissioning start date
needs.plain: Spend less time waiting for government approval to dispose of equipment after the planned shutdown date. This helps the shutdown stay on schedule.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of conflict between environmental and safety authorities over applicable disposal requirements
needs.plain: Resolve differences between environmental and safety officials about how the waste must be disposed of. This prevents delays and helps ensure the disposal is accepted by all authorities.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that an approval or permit expires before disposal activities begin
needs.plain: Make sure the required approval or permit is still valid when disposal begins. This prevents disposal work from starting without permission.

disposal.name: Prepare for extended storage
disposal.statement: Drain, flush, and protect the batching system for an extended period of inactivity.
disposal.description: Execution of lay-up procedures including draining fluid paths, purging residues, applying protective measures, and securing the system against deterioration during prolonged non-use.

needs.stmt: Reduce the amount of process or cleaning residue remaining in the batching system after draining and flushing
needs.plain: Remove as much leftover processing material and cleaning liquid as possible from the mixing system before storing it for a long time. This helps prevent buildup, damage, and contamination.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of trapped pressure or hazardous liquid being released unexpectedly during draining
needs.plain: Prevent trapped pressure or dangerous liquid from escaping suddenly while the system is being emptied. This protects people and nearby equipment.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of environmental contaminants entering sterile fluid paths during extended storage
Reduce the percentage of corrosion-prone batching-system surfaces left unprotected during extended inactivity
needs.plain: Protect surfaces that can easily rust on the mixing system while it is not being used for a long time, so they do not deteriorate.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of drain, isolation, or preservation states recorded incorrectly at lay-up handoff
needs.plain: Make sure the records correctly show whether drains were emptied, equipment was separated or secured, and the equipment was protected for storage. This helps prevent damage while the equipment is not being used.

disposal.name: Recycle system materials
disposal.statement: Route recoverable system materials into appropriate recycling streams.
disposal.description: Separate and route metals, piping, electronics, and other recyclable materials from the decommissioned system into applicable recycling channels.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that recyclable materials are rejected because of residual pharmaceutical or cleaning substances
needs.plain: Make sure recyclable materials are free of leftover medicines or cleaning products before sending them for recycling. This prevents the materials from being rejected or creating a safety problem.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of system materials assigned to incompatible recycling streams
needs.plain: Send each system material to the correct recycling category rather than putting it in the wrong one. This helps the materials get recycled properly and avoids contamination.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of components whose material composition cannot be verified before routing
needs.plain: Make sure the material each component is made from can be confirmed before it is sent for recycling. This helps ensure materials are handled and recycled correctly.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that applicable recycling channels refuse separated system materials
needs.plain: Make sure the separated materials from the system are accepted by the appropriate recycling services. This prevents recyclable materials from being refused or sent to the wrong place.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration separated recyclable materials remain onsite awaiting transfer
needs.plain: Move separated recyclable materials offsite sooner. This keeps storage areas from becoming crowded while the materials wait for pickup.

disposal.name: Remove stored proprietary data
disposal.statement: Remove stored proprietary recipes, formulations, and configuration data from the system controller.
disposal.description: Purge all confidential process data and intellectual property from onboard storage before the system is transferred, resold, or scrapped.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of proprietary process data being exposed to unauthorized parties during removal or disposition
Reduce the percentage of proprietary recipes, formulations, and configuration data that remains recoverable from onboard storage after removal
Reduce the amount of required batch, audit, or service data unintentionally erased while removing proprietary data
needs.plain: Avoid accidentally deleting important production, inspection, or service records while removing the company's private information. This matters because the needed records must still be available afterward.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of transfer or scrapping delays caused by unresolved deletion and retention requirements
needs.plain: Shorten the delay in transferring or scrapping equipment while people are still deciding what information must be deleted and what must be kept. This helps the equipment move on sooner while handling information properly.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of removed proprietary data being repopulated on the controller before transfer, resale, or scrapping
needs.plain: Reduce the chance that deleted company information is restored to the machine before it is transferred, sold, or discarded. This helps keep private information from being exposed.

disposal.name: Remove system identification markings
disposal.statement: Remove or obliterate identification markings, asset tags, and labels from the system before transfer or disposal.
disposal.description: Strip asset tags, serial-number plates, proprietary identification, and facility-specific markings from the system to protect organisational information and comply with asset management policies.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of identification markings that remain legible after removal or obliteration
needs.plain: Make sure old identification marks are truly unreadable after they are removed, so the equipment cannot be mistaken for something else.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of required regulatory or safety markings removed unintentionally
needs.plain: Prevent legally required or safety labels from being removed by mistake. These markings help people identify the equipment and handle it safely.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of damaging system surfaces or components while stripping markings
needs.plain: Remove labels or markings without scratching or otherwise harming the equipment. This helps keep the equipment in good working condition.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of removed tags and plates remaining recoverable from the waste stream
needs.plain: Reduce the chance that removed labels and nameplates can still be recovered from the waste. This helps prevent the equipment from being identified after disposal.

needs.stmt: Reduce the amount of adhesive residue or label debris left on cleanroom-facing or product-contact surfaces
needs.plain: Leave as little glue, ink, or label material as possible on surfaces that face the clean work area or touch the product. This helps keep those surfaces clean and safe.

disposal.name: Return eligible equipment
disposal.statement: Return eligible equipment through its designated return channel.
disposal.description: This fulfills warranty, recall, lease, rental, exchange, or supplier take-back requirements.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that residual product, cleaning agents, or hazardous material remain in the batching system at handoff
