needs.plain: Make sure no product, cleaning liquid, or dangerous material is left in the mixing equipment when it is handed over. This helps protect the next person who uses it.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the batching system is routed to the wrong destination because return-channel instructions conflict
needs.plain: The batching system should be sent to the correct destination, even if different return instructions are provided. This prevents the equipment from being returned to the wrong place.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the return is refused because eligibility or authorization records are incomplete
needs.plain: The equipment should only be returned when the records show that it is allowed and properly authorized for return. This prevents the return from being refused.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of damage to the batching system during disconnection, removal, or transit
needs.plain: Prevent the batching system from being damaged while it is disconnected, removed, or transported. This helps keep the equipment usable when it is returned.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of return delay caused by misaligned site access, pickup, and receiving availability
needs.plain: Spend less time waiting to return equipment because the site, pickup service, and receiving location are not ready at the same time. Better coordination helps the equipment get back sooner.

disposal.name: Salvage reusable components
disposal.statement: Recover reusable pumps, valves, sensors, and vessels from the system.
disposal.description: Identify and extract components that retain service value for reuse in other systems or as spare parts.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of technician exposure to hazardous process residues during component extraction
needs.plain: Protect the technician from dangerous leftover material while removing parts that may be reused. This helps prevent harmful contact during the work.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of recovered components that retain unacceptable process residues
needs.plain: Make sure recovered parts do not still have unacceptable leftover material or chemicals on them. Clean parts are safer and more suitable for reuse.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of salvageable pumps, valves, sensors, and vessels damaged during extraction
needs.plain: Avoid damaging pumps, valves, sensors, and tanks that could still be used while removing them. This preserves useful equipment and reduces replacement costs.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that a recovered component has undetected degradation
needs.plain: Make sure a part recovered for reuse does not have hidden wear or damage. This helps prevent an unsafe or unreliable part from being used again.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of recovered components with missing identity or service-history records
needs.plain: Fewer recovered parts should be missing records showing what they are or how they were used and maintained. This information helps determine whether they can be safely reused.

disposal.name: Terminate linked services
disposal.statement: Terminate services, maintenance contracts, calibration agreements, and consumable supply arrangements linked to the retired batching system.
disposal.description: Combines cancelling specific service/calibration/consumable agreements (#101) with terminating all services linked to the retired system (#117). Both describe the same end-of-life service termination activity.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of agreements supporting active batching systems terminated by mistake during decommissioning
needs.plain: Avoid accidentally ending agreements that are still needed by systems that are actively combining or preparing batches during equipment removal. This keeps those systems working while they are still needed.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of linked service, calibration, maintenance, and consumable supply arrangements left active after system retirement
needs.plain: Make sure all connected service, testing, repair, and supply arrangements are ended when the system is retired. This prevents payments and deliveries from continuing after they are no longer needed.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of required decommissioning support unavailability after premature agreement termination
needs.plain: Keep the help needed to shut down the equipment available for longer if connected services end earlier than planned. This helps prevent delays during the shutdown.

needs.stmt: Reduce the amount of avoidable termination fees incurred when contractual notice deadlines are missed
needs.plain: Avoid unnecessary fees for ending services when required notice dates are missed. This reduces the cost of stopping services that are no longer needed.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of billing and termination disputes caused by missing or conflicting supplier confirmations
needs.plain: Resolve charges and cancellation disagreements quickly when suppliers have not confirmed the change or have given conflicting answers. This helps make sure linked services end correctly.

disposal.name: Transfer system ownership
disposal.statement: Transfer or resell the system to a new owner.
disposal.description: Execute the administrative and physical steps required to transfer legal ownership of the system to another party.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of residual product or hazardous cleaning material remaining in the system at transfer
needs.plain: Make sure no product or dangerous cleaning material is left in the system when it is handed over. This helps protect the people who take responsibility for it next.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of sterile fill-finish production disruption caused by system removal and handover
Minimize the likelihood of legal ownership of the batching system remaining disputed after transfer
needs.plain: Make clear who legally owns the mixing system after it is handed over. This prevents later arguments about ownership and responsibility.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of required validation, maintenance, and safety records that are missing or inconsistent at handover
needs.plain: Make sure the new owner receives complete and consistent records about testing, upkeep, and safety. This matters so they can operate and care for the system safely after the handover.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of batching system components damaged during removal and relocation
needs.plain: Avoid damaging parts of the material-mixing system when it is taken apart and moved. This helps keep the equipment usable and reduces repair or replacement work.

disposal.name: Transition batching operations
disposal.statement: Transition batching operations to the replacement system.
disposal.description: Coordinate the handover of production workload, process parameters, and operational responsibilities from the retiring system to its replacement.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that sterile product is contaminated during the operational handover
Minimize the duration of sterile fill-finish batching interruption during the transition
Minimize the percentage of process parameters transferred to the replacement system that differ from approved settings
needs.plain: Make sure the operating settings moved to the replacement system are the same as the approved settings, so the process works as intended.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of production batches without a clear system assignment during the transition
needs.plain: Fewer production batches should be left without a clear connection to the system that made them during the changeover. This makes each batch easier to identify and track.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of operational responsibilities without clear ownership after handover
needs.plain: Make sure every ongoing work duty has a clearly named owner after the handover. This prevents important duties from being left undone.

acquisition.name: Arrange delivery logistics
acquisition.statement: Arrange transport, rigging, and site-delivery scheduling for the batching system.
acquisition.description: Coordinate with freight and rigging contractors to schedule transport of the system from the supplier or warehouse to the installation site, accounting for equipment size and weight.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the planned site access route cannot accommodate the batching system's dimensions or weight
needs.plain: Make sure the route into the site has enough space and can support the batching system's weight. This helps prevent problems when bringing the equipment to the site.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree to which available transport and rigging capacity falls below the batching system's size and weight requirements
needs.plain: Make sure the available vehicles, lifting equipment, and handling support can safely move the production system, given its size and weight.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of delivery delay caused by conflicting availability among the site, carrier, and rigging contractor
needs.plain: Coordinate the location, transport company, and lifting contractor so the delivery does not have to wait. This helps the equipment arrive when everyone is ready to receive and move it.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of batching system damage during loading, transport, unloading, or rigging
needs.plain: Protect the mixing system from damage while it is loaded, moved, unloaded, or lifted into place. Damage during delivery could delay installation or make the system unsafe to use.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that delivery is held because required transport permits or site-entry clearances are missing
needs.plain: Make sure the delivery is not delayed because required travel permissions or permission to enter the site are missing.

acquisition.name: Assess process and material compatibility
acquisition.statement: Assess each candidate system's wetted materials, process conditions, and fluid chemistry suitability against the intended application.
acquisition.description: Combines wetted-material compatibility verification (#2) with process-condition and fluid-chemistry compatibility assessment (#21), which are inseparable aspects of the same compatibility evaluation activity.
acquisition.userGroup: Process engineers, materials engineers, procurement leads.
acquisition.frequency: Once per acquisition event, per candidate system evaluated.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of judging chemically incompatible wetted materials suitable for the intended application
needs.plain: Make sure the materials that touch the process liquid will not react badly with it or make the process unsafe. This helps ensure the equipment is suitable for its intended use.
needs.role: Process engineers, materials engineers, procurement leads.

needs.stmt: Minimize the percentage of wetted components whose material identity is unverified
needs.role: Process engineers, materials engineers, procurement leads.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of intended fluid chemistry and process requirements missing from the compatibility evaluation basis
needs.role: Process engineers, materials engineers, procurement leads.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of ambiguity in each candidate system's allowable temperature, pressure, and chemical exposure limits
needs.plain: Clearly state the highest and lowest temperatures, pressures, and chemical exposures each possible system can safely handle. This helps the buyer choose equipment that will work safely in the intended setting.
needs.role: Process engineers, materials engineers, procurement leads.

needs.stmt: Minimize the time spent waiting for missing supplier compatibility information during candidate evaluation
needs.plain: Get the missing information needed to check whether the supplier’s option will work with the existing process and materials. This keeps the evaluation from being held up.
needs.role: Process engineers, materials engineers, procurement leads.

acquisition.name: Compare vendor proposals
acquisition.statement: Compare competing suppliers' specifications, pricing, terms, and technical documentation for the batching system.
acquisition.description: Requesting technical documentation (#19) is a step performed within vendor proposal comparison (#4). The output — a comparative assessment of suppliers — is identical. Retaining #4's framing, enriched to include documentation.
acquisition.userGroup: Procurement leads, process engineers, project managers.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of overlooking critical sterile fill-finish requirements during proposal comparison
needs.role: Procurement leads, process engineers, project managers.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of ambiguity created by inconsistent scopes and assumptions across supplier proposals
needs.plain: Make the different supplier offers clear and consistent about what work is included and what each supplier assumes. This makes the offers fair and easy to compare.
needs.role: Procurement leads, process engineers, project managers.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of required technical documents missing from supplier proposals
needs.plain: Reduce the number of supplier proposals that are missing required technical documents. This makes it easier to compare what each supplier is offering.
needs.role: Procurement leads, process engineers, project managers.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of conflicting specifications, pricing, or terms within supplier submissions
needs.plain: Make sure each supplier’s offer gives consistent information about what is included, how much it costs, and the terms. This makes it easier to compare offers fairly.
needs.role: Procurement leads, process engineers, project managers.

needs.stmt: Minimize the amount of acquisition cost omitted from proposal pricing
needs.plain: Leave out as little of the purchase cost as possible from the prices shown in the sellers' offers.
needs.role: Procurement leads, process engineers, project managers.

acquisition.name: Complete purchase transaction
acquisition.statement: Complete the purchase contract, payment, and warranty terms for the system.
acquisition.description: The buyer completes contract terms, payment, and warranty arrangements to finalize the purchase.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of ambiguity in the contract's acceptance obligations for sterility-critical batching performance
Minimize the amount of purchaser payment due before agreed delivery and acceptance obligations are fulfilled
needs.plain: Avoid paying before the supplier has met the agreed delivery and acceptance requirements. This protects the buyer from paying for equipment that has not yet been properly delivered or approved.

needs.stmt: Minimize the percent of critical system components and performance obligations excluded from warranty coverage
needs.plain: Reduce the share of important system parts and promised performance that the warranty does not cover. This gives the buyer broader protection if something fails or does not perform as promised.

needs.stmt: Minimize the amount of required project cost omitted from the agreed purchase price
needs.plain: Make sure the agreed purchase price includes every project cost that is needed. This prevents unexpected charges later.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of contract execution delay caused by unresolved purchaser and supplier approvals
needs.plain: Avoid delaying the purchase because the buyer and supplier have not finished approving it. This helps the agreement be signed and carried out sooner.

acquisition.name: Confirm lead time and availability
acquisition.statement: Confirm the supplier's manufacturing lead time and component availability for the specified system configuration.
acquisition.description: Obtain a firm lead-time commitment and confirm that long-lead components (e.g., specialised instrumentation, custom vessels) are available to meet the project schedule.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the supplier's committed completion date falls after the required site-delivery date
needs.plain: Make sure the supplier can finish the order early enough for it to arrive at the work site by the required date. This helps keep the work on schedule.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that critical custom components are unavailable when manufacturing must begin
needs.plain: Make sure important specially made parts will be available when manufacturing needs to start.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of specified configuration requirements omitted from the supplier's commitment
needs.plain: Make sure the supplier agrees to every required setup or feature. This prevents important requirements from being left out of the supplier's promise.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of waiting for the supplier to provide a firm manufacturing and component-availability commitment
needs.plain: Get a definite promise from the supplier about when the item can be made and whether its parts are available. This helps the buyer know when the order can realistically be delivered.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that confirmed component availability changes before purchase terms are finalized
needs.plain: Make sure the parts that are currently available will still be available when the purchase agreement is completed. A change could delay the order.

acquisition.name: Define system requirements
acquisition.statement: Define the functional, capacity, and process requirements that the liquid batching and preparation system must satisfy.
acquisition.description: Research of available system types (#15) is a sub-activity performed while defining requirements (#18). The output of both is the same: a defined requirements baseline. Retaining #18's more precise framing.
acquisition.userGroup: Process engineers, project managers, procurement leads.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of critical sterile fill-finish requirements omitted from the requirements baseline
needs.role: Process engineers, project managers, procurement leads.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of mismatch between specified batching capacity and projected production demand
needs.role: Process engineers, project managers, procurement leads.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of unresolved conflicts among quality, production, engineering, and procurement requirements
needs.plain: Resolve disagreements among quality, production, engineering, and purchasing needs when describing the system. This helps prevent conflicting instructions and costly changes later.
needs.role: Process engineers, project managers, procurement leads.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of ambiguity in requirements for material transfers, utilities, cleaning, and environmental interfaces
needs.plain: Clearly state what is needed for moving materials, supplying services such as water or electricity, cleaning, and connecting to the surrounding environment. This prevents important requirements from being missed.
needs.role: Process engineers, project managers, procurement leads.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that specified requirements conflict with available space, utilities, or material-flow constraints
needs.plain: Make sure the requested system features fit the available room, power and other services, and the way materials move through the site.
needs.role: Process engineers, project managers, procurement leads.

acquisition.name: Evaluate capacity and specification fit
acquisition.statement: Evaluate whether candidate systems' capacity, throughput, and technical specifications match defined production requirements.
acquisition.description: Combines evaluation of batch capacity and throughput (#1) with comparison of technical specifications against requirements (#20), which are performed in the same decision activity and overlap in scope.
acquisition.userGroup: Process engineers, procurement leads, project managers.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of overlooking a required batch-size or operating-range mismatch during evaluation
needs.role: Process engineers, procurement leads, project managers.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of critical sterile fill-finish specifications evaluated without supporting supplier evidence
needs.role: Process engineers, procurement leads, project managers.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that candidate throughput is overstated by excluding cleaning, changeover, and planned downtime
needs.plain: Make sure the expected output includes time for cleaning, switching between products or tasks, and planned stoppages. Otherwise, the actual output may be less than promised.
needs.role: Process engineers, procurement leads, project managers.

needs.stmt: Reduce the degree of inconsistency in how engineering, procurement, and project stakeholders interpret production requirements
needs.plain: Make sure the engineering, purchasing, and project teams all understand the production needs in the same way before choosing equipment.
needs.role: Process engineers, procurement leads, project managers.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of supplier specifications presented on incompatible operating bases or units
needs.plain: Avoid comparing supplier information when it uses different conditions or measurement units. This makes it possible to compare the options fairly and accurately.
needs.role: Process engineers, procurement leads, project managers.

acquisition.name: Evaluate regulatory compliance evidence
acquisition.statement: Evaluate compliance documentation, certifications, and regulatory evidence supplied by candidate system suppliers.
acquisition.description: Combines confirming the system meets certification standards (#3) with evaluating the compliance documentation and evidence supplied (#23). Both jobs occur in the same evaluation step and address the same compliance gate.
acquisition.userGroup: Procurement leads, quality engineers, regulatory affairs personnel.
acquisition.frequency: Once per acquisition event, per candidate system evaluated.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that submitted certifications are expired, invalid, or outside the proposed batching system's scope
needs.role: Procurement leads, quality engineers, regulatory affairs personnel.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of mismatch between compliance evidence and the proposed batching system configuration
needs.role: Procurement leads, quality engineers, regulatory affairs personnel.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of applicable sterile fill-finish requirements unsupported by supplier evidence
needs.role: Procurement leads, quality engineers, regulatory affairs personnel.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of material contradictions among compliance documents, certifications, and supplier claims
needs.plain: Make sure compliance documents, certificates, and supplier statements do not significantly contradict one another. Consistent evidence makes it easier to judge whether the product or supplier meets the required rules.
needs.role: Procurement leads, quality engineers, regulatory affairs personnel.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of the compliance-gate delay caused by late supplier clarifications
needs.plain: Spend less time waiting at the required compliance checkpoint because the supplier provides explanations late. This helps the work move forward sooner.
needs.role: Procurement leads, quality engineers, regulatory affairs personnel.

acquisition.name: Evaluate rental options
acquisition.statement: Evaluate rental or lease arrangements for temporary or trial batching capacity.
acquisition.description: Some batching needs are temporary or trial, making rental or lease options relevant.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of rental candidates missing qualification, cleaning, or material-compatibility records required for sterile fill-finish use
Minimize the likelihood of finding no rental or lease arrangement that meets the required batching capacity, sterile-processing requirements, and production window
needs.plain: Find a rental or lease option that can handle the needed amount of production, meet cleanliness requirements, and be available during the planned production period. This helps ensure the temporary equipment will actually meet the project's needs.

needs.stmt: Minimize the amount by which the total rental or lease expense exceeds the project budget because required charges were omitted
needs.plain: Include every required charge so the total rental or lease cost stays as close as possible to, and no higher than, the project budget.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of accepting lease terms that conflict with the trial schedule, decontamination obligations, or return conditions
needs.plain: Choose rental terms that fit the testing timetable, cleaning responsibilities, and requirements for returning the equipment. Conflicting terms could cause delays, extra work, or unexpected costs.

needs.stmt: Minimize the time required to obtain the technical, commercial, and availability information needed to approve a rental arrangement
needs.plain: Gather the price, practical details, and availability needed to approve renting something as quickly as possible. This helps the buyer choose and approve a rental without delay.

acquisition.name: Inspect received equipment
acquisition.statement: Inspect and verify the delivered system against the order for completeness, transit damage, missing components, and contamination.
acquisition.description: Combines [Acquisition] #17 (inspect delivered system for completeness and damage) with [Preparation] #41 (inspect for transit damage, missing components, contamination). Both describe the same receive-and-inspect activity; it belongs in Acquisition as it occurs at point of delivery before preparation begins.
acquisition.userGroup: Receiving personnel, project managers, commissioning engineers.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of missing ordered components incorrectly recorded as received during inspection
needs.plain: Make sure every part that was ordered is actually present before marking it as received. This prevents missing parts from being overlooked.
needs.role: Receiving personnel, project managers, commissioning engineers.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of inconsistency between delivered-system identifiers and approved order documentation
needs.plain: Make sure the equipment delivered has the same identifying information as the equipment approved in the order. This helps confirm that the correct equipment arrived.
needs.role: Receiving personnel, project managers, commissioning engineers.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of inspection delay caused by inaccessible or unsafe inspection areas after delivery
needs.plain: Spend less time waiting to inspect delivered equipment when the inspection areas are hard to reach or not safe to enter. This helps staff check the equipment safely and sooner.
needs.role: Receiving personnel, project managers, commissioning engineers.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of transit damage remaining undetected before delivery acceptance
needs.plain: Make sure any damage from shipping is found before the delivered equipment is formally accepted. This protects the recipient from accepting equipment that arrived damaged.
needs.role: Receiving personnel, project managers, commissioning engineers.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of contamination remaining undetected on delivered system surfaces during receiving inspection
needs.plain: Make sure unwanted material left on the delivered equipment is noticed during the incoming inspection. This helps prevent dirty equipment from being accepted and used.
needs.role: Receiving personnel, project managers, commissioning engineers.

acquisition.name: Place system order
acquisition.statement: Place the purchase order for the approved liquid batching and preparation system.
acquisition.description: Issue the formal purchase order to the selected supplier, confirming configuration, price, delivery schedule, and contractual terms.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of required sterile fill-finish quality and validation deliverables omitted from the purchase order
Minimize the likelihood that the purchase order specifies a system configuration different from the approved configuration
needs.plain: Make sure the purchase order describes the same system setup that was approved. This helps prevent the wrong equipment or features from being ordered.

needs.stmt: Reduce the time between final supplier selection and purchase order issuance
needs.plain: There should be less time between choosing the final supplier and sending the official purchase order. This helps the supplier start providing the requested system sooner.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of discrepancies between the purchase order and the approved supplier offer for price, currency, taxes, and payment terms
needs.plain: Make sure the order matches the supplier's approved offer for the price, type of money used, taxes, and payment schedule. This prevents unexpected costs or payment disputes.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of unresolved contractual conflicts between the purchase order and the final supplier agreement
needs.plain: Resolve any contract differences between the purchase order and the final agreement with the supplier. This helps ensure both sides are committed to the same deal.

acquisition.name: Qualify prospective suppliers
acquisition.statement: Qualify prospective suppliers of liquid batching and preparation systems against organisational procurement criteria.
acquisition.description: Assess supplier financial stability, quality management systems, manufacturing capability, references, and compliance with procurement standards before approving them for competitive evaluation.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of approving a prospective supplier whose quality management system fails sterile fill-finish requirements
needs.plain: Avoid approving a new supplier whose quality controls do not meet the needs for making sterile products. This helps ensure that supplied materials and services are dependable.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that material financial instability in a prospective supplier remains undetected before approval
needs.plain: Check whether a possible supplier is financially stable before approving it, so financial trouble does not disrupt the purchase.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree to which a prospective supplier's manufacturing capability claims are unsupported for the required batching systems
needs.plain: Make sure a possible supplier can prove its claims about making the required batch-production systems.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of required supplier qualification evidence that is missing, outdated, or unverifiable during assessment
needs.plain: Make sure suppliers provide complete, current, and checkable proof that they meet the required standards. This matters so the business can confidently decide whether a supplier is suitable.

needs.stmt: Reduce the frequency of conflicting assessments of the same prospective supplier against organisational procurement criteria
needs.plain: Make sure different people reach the same conclusion when judging a possible supplier against the organisation’s buying rules. This supports fair and reliable supplier decisions.

acquisition.name: Register the system
acquisition.statement: Register the delivered system with the manufacturer for warranty activation and support entitlement.
acquisition.description: Submit serial numbers, installation site, and owner information to the manufacturer to activate warranty coverage and establish eligibility for technical support services.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of delivered-system identifiers rejected as invalid or mismatched
needs.plain: Make sure the system’s identifying information is correct and matches the delivery, so the system can be registered without errors.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that required delivery-acceptance evidence is unavailable when registration is submitted
needs.plain: Make sure all required proof that the delivery was accepted is available before registering the system. This prevents the registration from being delayed or rejected.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of required registration fields with conflicting owner or installation-site details
needs.plain: Make sure the owner and installation location are recorded consistently, so the system registration does not contain conflicting information.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of the gap between delivery acceptance and manufacturer confirmation of registration
needs.plain: Spend less time waiting between accepting the delivered system and the maker confirming that it has been officially recorded. This helps the system become fully recognized sooner.

acquisition.name: Request supplier quotation
acquisition.statement: Request a quotation for the selected liquid batching and preparation system configuration.
acquisition.description: This obtains pricing, scope, options, exclusions, lead time, payment terms, warranty coverage, and delivery conditions for procurement review.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the quotation scope differs from the selected liquid batching and preparation system configuration
needs.plain: Make sure the price offer covers the exact liquid mixing and preparation system that was chosen. This helps prevent missing equipment, features, or costs.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of required commercial and delivery details omitted from the quotation
needs.plain: Include all important business and delivery details in the supplier's price proposal. This helps the buyer compare the offer and understand exactly what the supplier is proposing.

needs.stmt: Minimize the time from submitting a complete quotation request to receiving the supplier quotation
needs.plain: Get the supplier’s price offer as quickly as possible after sending a complete request. This helps the buyer decide and move forward sooner.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the supplier cannot access configuration specifications or request attachments
needs.plain: Make sure the supplier can see the detailed requirements and files needed to prepare a price quote. This helps them quote the right product or service.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the quotation expires before procurement review is completed
needs.plain: Make sure the supplier's price offer remains valid until the purchasing review is finished. Otherwise, the supplier may require a new offer or different terms.

acquisition.name: Review acquisition scope
acquisition.statement: Review the quoted acquisition scope for the liquid batching and preparation system.
acquisition.description: This identifies omissions, substitutions, assumptions, interfaces, optional components, documentation deliverables, and commercial limitations before commitment.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that required sterile fill-finish capabilities omitted from the quoted scope remain undetected
Reduce the number of quoted substitutions whose equivalence to acquisition requirements cannot be verified
needs.plain: Fewer alternative products should be quoted when no one can confirm that they meet the buying requirements. This helps prevent the purchase of something that does not do the job needed.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of ambiguity in responsibility for utility, controls, and facility interfaces
needs.plain: Clearly assign who is responsible for electricity and other services, control systems, and connections to the building. This prevents gaps between the equipment and the facility.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of required qualification and compliance documentation deliverables missing from the quoted scope
needs.plain: Make sure the price proposal includes all required proof that the equipment meets standards and has been properly tested, so the buyer knows what is included.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that commercial exclusions or optional components are mistaken for included scope
needs.plain: Make sure items left out of the price and optional parts are not mistaken for things that are included. This prevents unexpected costs and missing equipment.

acquisition.name: Secure purchase approval
acquisition.statement: Obtain budget authorization and complete the procurement approval process for the system.
acquisition.description: Large capital equipment purchases require formal budgeting, approval, and procurement processes.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the capital request is rejected because its financial and production justification is insufficient
needs.plain: The request to buy the equipment should clearly explain its cost and how it will support production. This improves the chance that the purchase will be approved.

needs.stmt: Reduce the time the purchase request remains pending with required approvers
needs.plain: The purchase request should be approved quickly by everyone who needs to review it. This helps the buyer get what is needed without unnecessary delay.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of required quality, validation, or supplier-qualification documents missing from the approval package
needs.plain: Include all needed quality, testing, and supplier records in the approval package, so the purchase can be reviewed and approved with enough evidence.

needs.stmt: Reduce the degree of disagreement among engineering, quality, finance, and procurement stakeholders on purchase requirements
needs.plain: Make sure engineering, quality, finance, and purchasing agree on what the purchase must include. This reduces the chance of approving equipment that does not meet important needs.

needs.stmt: Reduce the amount by which the evaluated acquisition cost exceeds the proposed capital budget
needs.plain: Keep the full expected purchase cost within the approved amount for new equipment or other long-term purchases. This makes it easier to get approval.

acquisition.name: Seek peer references
acquisition.statement: Seek references from existing users of candidate liquid batching and preparation systems.
acquisition.description: Contact reference customers provided by shortlisted suppliers, or peer networks, to gather first-hand operational experience with the system before committing to purchase.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of reference requests unanswered before the supplier selection decision
needs.plain: Get answers to all requested user references before choosing a supplier. This gives the buyer useful feedback from people who have already used the supplier.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of reference users whose operating settings are not comparable to the intended sterile fill-finish environment
needs.plain: Make sure reference users work in conditions similar to the planned sterile drug-filling operation. Their experience is more useful when their equipment and work setting are comparable.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree to which reference accounts are biased by supplier screening or participation
needs.plain: Make sure other customers’ opinions are not shaped only by which customers the supplier chose or allowed to take part.

needs.stmt: Minimize the amount of decision-critical operating detail withheld because of confidentiality restrictions
needs.plain: Get as much important information about how the equipment operates as possible, even when people cannot share some details because they are confidential.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of evaluation criteria with conflicting peer accounts of system performance in sterile fill-finish use
needs.plain: Make sure people giving references agree about how well the system performs when used to fill and seal sterile products. Conflicting accounts make it harder to judge whether the system is suitable.

acquisition.name: Specify system configuration
acquisition.statement: Specify the required components, options, and configuration for the batching system order.
acquisition.description: Custom-engineered systems require specification of options, materials, and configuration before ordering.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that specified wetted materials are incompatible with the formulation or sanitation requirements
needs.plain: Make sure the materials that touch the product work safely with the product and can meet cleaning and sanitation needs. This helps prevent damage, contamination, or ineffective cleaning.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree to which the specified batching system configuration falls short of the required batch-volume range
Reduce the number of required components or options omitted from the batching system order specification
needs.plain: Make sure the order form for the production system includes all the parts and choices that are needed. This helps prevent delays or an unusable system.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of discrepancies between specified facility interfaces and installation-site conditions
needs.plain: Make sure the system design matches the conditions at the place where it will be installed. This helps prevent problems during setup.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of unresolved conflicts among process, quality, engineering, and procurement requirements in the order specification
needs.plain: Resolve disagreements among process, quality, engineering, and purchasing needs before writing the equipment order. This helps ensure the supplier receives one clear set of instructions.

acquisition.name: Track system shipment
acquisition.statement: Track the shipment of the liquid batching and preparation system.
acquisition.description: This allows receiving personnel to prepare for arrival while responding to delays, split shipments, route changes, or special handling events.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the system shipment becomes untraceable because its tracking identifiers are missing or inconsistent
needs.plain: Make sure the shipment has complete and matching identification numbers so it can be followed during delivery. This helps prevent the shipment from becoming impossible to locate.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration that the shipment status remains outdated after a transit event
needs.plain: Update the shipment status soon after something happens during transport. This helps people see where the shipment stands without relying on old information.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of discrepancy between the reported arrival estimate and the actual arrival of the system shipment
needs.plain: Make the reported delivery date for the system match the date it actually arrives. This helps the receiving team plan for the shipment.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of split-shipment consignments with unidentified contents or receiving destinations
needs.plain: Clearly identify what is in each shipment sent separately and where it must be delivered. This helps receiving staff send every part to the right place.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that a route change or special-handling event affecting the system shipment goes unreported
needs.plain: Make sure changes to the delivery route or special handling of the shipment are reported. This helps the recipient know where the system is and about any delivery risks.

acquisition.name: Verify site compatibility
acquisition.statement: Verify each candidate system's dimensions, utility requirements, and connectivity against site constraints and installation conditions.
acquisition.description: Combines assessing footprint, utility, and connectivity requirements against the site (#5) with verifying dimensions, utilities, and connectivity against site constraints (#22). Identical evaluation activity stated twice.
acquisition.userGroup: Facilities engineers, process engineers, project managers.
acquisition.frequency: Once per acquisition event, per candidate site/system combination.

needs.stmt: Minimize the percentage of required candidate-system dimensional, utility, and connectivity specifications that are missing or unverified during technical evaluation
needs.plain: Check and confirm that a possible replacement system will fit the site, have the needed services, and connect properly before choosing it.
needs.role: Facilities engineers, process engineers, project managers.

needs.stmt: Reduce the degree of discrepancy between documented site constraints and actual installation conditions
needs.plain: Make sure the written description of the site matches the conditions that are actually there, so the equipment will fit and work properly.
needs.role: Facilities engineers, process engineers, project managers.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of required site utilities whose available capacity or quality falls below candidate-system requirements
needs.plain: Make sure the site’s water, power, air, and other needed services have enough capacity and suitable quality for the equipment. This helps prevent operating problems after installation.
needs.role: Facilities engineers, process engineers, project managers.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the candidate system or its transport modules cannot traverse site access paths or fit within the installation envelope
needs.plain: The proposed system and the parts used to move it should fit through the site’s entrances and travel routes and fit in the planned installation space. This prevents the equipment from being impossible to deliver or install.
needs.role: Facilities engineers, process engineers, project managers.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of required process, control, and data connections that are incompatible with site interfaces
needs.plain: Make sure the equipment’s needed processes, controls, and information connections work with the systems already at the site, so everything can connect and operate together.
needs.role: Facilities engineers, process engineers, project managers.

acquisition.name: Witness system demonstration
acquisition.statement: Witness a demonstration of a candidate liquid batching and preparation system.
acquisition.description: This provides direct evidence of system operation, control behavior, batching performance, accessibility, and operator interaction before purchase.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of mismatch between the demonstrated configuration and conditions and the intended sterile fill-finish application
needs.plain: Make sure the demonstration setup and conditions closely match how the system is meant to be used to fill and finish germ-free products.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of critical batching or control functions that fail during the demonstration
needs.plain: Avoid having important mixing, measuring, or control functions fail during the demonstration. The demonstration should show that the system can perform its essential jobs.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of purchase-critical acceptance criteria left without observable evidence after the demonstration
needs.plain: Leave fewer important purchase requirements without clear proof after the equipment demonstration. This helps confirm that the equipment actually meets what was promised.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the scheduled demonstration is postponed because required materials, utilities, records, or qualified personnel are unavailable
needs.plain: The demonstration should take place as planned, with all needed supplies, services, records, and trained people available. This prevents the demonstration from being delayed.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of restrictions on observation, component access, or operator interaction during the demonstration
needs.plain: Keep the system open for viewing, hands-on access, and operator participation during the demonstration. This lets people properly examine and try the system.

preparation.name: Assemble system modules
preparation.statement: Assemble the supplied tanks, pumps, mixers, dispensers, sensors, and support modules into the complete batching system.
preparation.description: Mechanically assemble all major and ancillary system components — including structural supports, vessels, rotating equipment, instrumentation, and dispensing hardware — into the integrated system configuration.

needs.stmt: Reduce the degree of misalignment among vessels, rotating equipment, and dispensing hardware after assembly
Minimize the likelihood of structural supports or modules shifting or collapsing during mechanical assembly
needs.plain: Avoid allowing supports or parts of the system to move or fall during assembly. This helps prevent injuries and damage to the system.

needs.stmt: Reduce the amount of particulate or microbial contamination introduced into product-contact areas during assembly
needs.plain: Keep dust and germs from getting onto surfaces that will touch the product while the parts are being put together. This helps protect the product from contamination.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of sterile-contact surfaces or seals damaged during handling and assembly
needs.plain: Avoid damaging surfaces or seals that must stay free of germs while putting the system parts together. This helps prevent contamination.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of supplied modules with mechanical interfaces that do not mate to adjoining modules as specified
needs.plain: Avoid receiving system parts whose connecting pieces do not fit together as planned. This helps the system go together and work properly.

preparation.name: Clean product-contact surfaces
preparation.statement: Clean the tanks, lines, pumps, mixers, valves, and dispensing points before introducing batch materials.
preparation.description: Pre-use cleaning removes residues or contaminants that could compromise the first subsequent batch.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of recontaminating cleaned product-contact surfaces before batch materials are introduced
needs.plain: Keep cleaned surfaces that touch the product from getting dirty again before the materials are added. This helps keep the product free from unwanted contamination.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of introducing microbial contamination onto product-contact surfaces during cleaning
needs.plain: Prevent germs from getting onto surfaces that touch the product while those surfaces are being cleaned. This helps keep the product safe.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of product-contact surface area retaining previous-batch residue after cleaning
needs.plain: Remove as much leftover material from product-touching surfaces as possible, so residue from the previous batch does not affect the next one.

needs.stmt: Reduce the amount of cleaning-agent residue remaining in tanks, lines, pumps, mixers, valves, and dispensing points after cleaning
needs.plain: As little cleaning liquid as possible should remain in the tanks, pipes, pumps, mixing equipment, valves, and points where product is dispensed. This helps prevent the cleaning liquid from getting into the product.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of batch-start delay when product-contact surfaces do not meet pre-use acceptance criteria

preparation.name: Commission system with validation batch
preparation.statement: Commission the assembled system by running a validation batch to verify correct operation before production use.
preparation.description: Execute a controlled test or validation batch through the fully assembled and configured system to confirm that all components, controls, interlocks, and measurements perform to specification.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of contamination entering the product-contact path during the validation batch
Reduce the number of critical batch parameters outside approved limits during the validation batch
Minimize the degree of deviation between the executed batch sequence and the approved sequence during the validation batch
Minimize the likelihood that an interlock fails to halt the assembled system when a hazardous condition occurs during the validation batch
Reduce the number of required commissioning records missing after the validation batch

preparation.name: Configure batch recipes
preparation.statement: Configure the system-specific quantities, sequences, mixing times, temperatures, and dosing rates for each batch recipe in the control system.
preparation.description: Combines configuring batch parameters (#30 — recipes, fill volumes, mixing sequences) with configuring batch recipes (#36 — quantities, sequences, mixing times, temperatures, dosing rates). Both describe the same preparation activity at equivalent specificity.
preparation.userGroup: Process engineers, system operators, batch technicians.
preparation.frequency: Once at commissioning; repeated when new recipes are added.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of ingredient quantity values distorted by unit or scaling errors
needs.plain: Avoid entering ingredient amounts incorrectly because of confusing measurement units or changes in scale, so each batch has the right ingredients.
needs.role: Process engineers, system operators, batch technicians.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of recipe steps omitted or placed out of order in the configured sequence
needs.role: Process engineers, system operators, batch technicians.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of configured temperature, mixing, or ingredient-delivery setpoints outside validated operating limits
needs.role: Process engineers, system operators, batch technicians.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of configuring an obsolete recipe revision for production use
needs.role: Process engineers, system operators, batch technicians.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of required recipe fields left blank before recipe release
needs.role: Process engineers, system operators, batch technicians.

preparation.name: Connect fluid piping
preparation.statement: Connect the inlet, outlet, and recirculation piping to the batching system.
preparation.description: Fluid pathways must be joined so ingredients can be transferred through the system.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of connecting an inlet, outlet, or recirculation line to the incorrect batching-system port
Reduce the amount of unintended residual fluid trapped at piping connections after transfer or cleaning
Minimize the likelihood of fluid leaking from inlet, outlet, or recirculation piping joints during transfer
Minimize the likelihood of introducing contamination into the sterile-product fluid pathway while making piping connections
needs.plain: Keep harmful or unwanted material out of the path that carries the sterile product while the pipes are joined. This helps keep the product safe from contamination.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of damaging batching-system nozzles or piping seals while aligning and securing the piping
needs.plain: Avoid damaging the machine's pipe ends or seals while lining up and fastening the pipes. This helps prevent leaks and equipment problems.

preparation.name: Connect power and utility supplies
preparation.statement: Connect the batching system to its required electrical, pneumatic, and other utility supply sources.
preparation.description: Terminate and verify all electrical, pneumatic, and ancillary utility connections needed to energise the system, following applicable codes and specifications.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of a contaminated utility supply entering the batching system after hook-up
Reduce the number of leaks at pneumatic or ancillary utility connections identified during verification
Minimize the likelihood of personnel injury or batching-system damage during initial energization
needs.plain: Reduce the chance that people get hurt or the mixing equipment is damaged when power and other services are turned on for the first time. This helps make the startup safe.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of deviation of connected utility supplies from specified voltage, pressure, flow, and quality requirements
needs.plain: Make sure the incoming electricity and other services meet the required levels for power, pressure, amount, and cleanliness. This helps the machine run safely and reliably.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of hook-up delay caused by unavailable required utility sources or inaccessible connection points
needs.plain: Spend less time waiting to connect power and other needed services when the sources or connection points cannot be reached. This helps the equipment be set up sooner.

preparation.name: Connect process piping
preparation.statement: Connect the system to the required material inlet, transfer, recirculation, and discharge piping.
preparation.description: Install and connect all process fluid piping runs — including inlet feeds, inter-vessel transfer lines, recirculation loops, and discharge headers — between the batching system and plant infrastructure.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of process piping runs connected to incorrect system or plant ports
Reduce the number of completed piping connections containing unintended crevices or stagnant pockets
Minimize the likelihood of process fluid leaking from completed piping connections during testing or startup
Reduce the amount of mechanical stress imposed on system connection points by misaligned piping
Minimize the likelihood of foreign material entering product-contact piping during installation
needs.plain: Keep dust, debris, and other unwanted material out of the product-carrying pipes while they are being installed. This helps protect the product from contamination.

preparation.name: Document system configuration
preparation.statement: Document the as-installed configuration, calibration records, and parameter settings of the batching system.
preparation.description: Capture and record the final installed configuration — including pipe routing, instrument calibration certificates, control parameter settings, recipe data, and access permission structures — as the baseline reference for future maintenance, audits, and regulatory review.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that final commissioning changes are omitted from the baseline documentation
Reduce the number of control parameter and recipe values recorded incorrectly in the baseline documentation
Reduce the percentage of required instrument calibration certificates missing from the closeout documentation
Reduce the number of documented pipe routes or instrument connections that differ from the physical installation
Minimize the degree of ambiguity in the documented access permission structures
needs.plain: Clearly record who is allowed to access the system and what each person is allowed to do. This helps prevent unauthorized changes or use.

preparation.name: Establish user access
preparation.statement: Establish user accounts and access permissions for the control system.
preparation.description: Access levels and user credentials control who can edit recipes and operate the system.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that authorized users cannot perform assigned control-system tasks because required permissions are missing
Minimize the likelihood of assigning recipe-editing permissions beyond approved responsibilities
Reduce the number of control-system accounts issued with shared or non-unique credentials
Reduce the duration of account setup stalled by incomplete or conflicting access approvals
needs.plain: Set up a person’s account quickly instead of letting missing or conflicting approvals hold it up. This allows the person to begin their authorized work sooner.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of newly established accounts with credentials that fail at initial sign-in
needs.plain: Make sure newly created user accounts work the first time the users sign in. This lets people begin their work without access problems.

preparation.name: Import recipe data
preparation.statement: Import existing recipe formulations and product data into the batching system.
preparation.description: Recipe libraries, formulations, and historical data may need to be loaded from existing systems.

needs.stmt: Minimize the percentage of imported formulation quantities altered by incorrect unit conversion or decimal precision handling
Minimize the likelihood that obsolete or unapproved recipe versions are designated as current after import
Reduce the number of recipe components assigned to incorrect batching-system fields during import
Reduce the number of imported recipe and product records with required formulation or product fields missing
Reduce the duration of import delay caused by source records that cannot be interpreted

preparation.name: Map ingredient sources
preparation.statement: Map each ingredient source to its corresponding tank, line, pump, valve, or dosing channel.
preparation.description: Accurate source mapping prevents control commands from routing or dispensing the wrong liquid.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of ingredient sources mapped to an incorrect tank, line, pump, valve, or dosing channel
needs.plain: Make sure each ingredient source is connected to the correct equipment. This prevents an ingredient from being sent to the wrong place.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of ingredient-source routes with one or more required assets omitted
Reduce the number of ingredient-source associations that conflict across piping, equipment, and control records
Reduce the number of ingredient-source mappings left obsolete after design changes
Reduce the number of ingredient sources that cannot be uniquely identified from project records and physical connections
needs.plain: Make sure every ingredient source can be identified as one specific source using the project records and the actual connections.

preparation.name: Position and mount equipment
preparation.statement: Position and mount the liquid batching system components at the designated installation location.
preparation.description: Place and secure tanks, pumps, metering hardware, and support structures in their specified positions, accounting for floor loading, access, and process flow.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of liquid-contact surfaces or open process connections becoming contaminated during positioning and mounting
Reduce the number of required operator or maintenance access clearances obstructed by mounted components
Minimize the likelihood that the installation foundation cannot safely support the batching system loads
needs.plain: Make sure the floor or base under the mixing system is strong and safe enough to carry its weight and operating loads. This helps prevent movement, damage, or collapse.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of tanks, pumps, and metering hardware positioned outside specified locations or orientations
needs.plain: Place tanks, pumps, and measuring equipment in the required positions and directions. This helps the equipment work correctly and safely.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of component mounting interfaces being misaligned with prepared foundation anchors
needs.plain: Make sure the equipment’s attachment points line up with the anchors already set in the foundation. Misalignment could make installation difficult or leave the equipment unstable.

preparation.name: Prime fluid circuits
preparation.statement: Prime the required pumps, dosing lines, valves, and transfer circuits with the designated liquid.
preparation.description: Priming removes trapped air and prepares fluid-handling components for consistent delivery.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of introducing a liquid other than the designated liquid into a required circuit during priming
needs.plain: Put only the intended liquid into the required pipes and equipment when filling them for use. This helps prevent the process from being contaminated or disrupted by the wrong liquid.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of introducing microbial or particulate contamination into product-contact circuits during priming
needs.plain: Prevent germs and small particles from getting into the parts that carry the product while they are being filled with liquid. This helps keep the product clean and safe.

needs.stmt: Minimize the percentage of required circuit volume occupied by trapped air after priming
needs.plain: Make sure there is as little trapped air as possible in the fluid lines after they are filled. This helps the lines work as intended and prevents air from disrupting the process.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of liquid flow interruption caused by an obstructed or incorrectly aligned required circuit during priming
needs.plain: Restore liquid flow quickly when a required path is blocked or set up incorrectly during startup, so the system can begin working without a long delay.

needs.stmt: Reduce the amount of designated liquid released from circuit connections during priming

preparation.name: Prime ingredient feeds
preparation.statement: Prime and charge the ingredient feed lines and supply tanks.
preparation.description: Raw ingredient supply tanks and feed lines must be primed and charged before the first batch.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of charging the wrong ingredient into a supply tank or feed line
needs.plain: Make sure the correct ingredient is put into the supply tank or feed line. Using the wrong ingredient could affect the product being made.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of introducing microbial or particulate contamination into the ingredient feed lines and supply tanks during priming and charging
needs.plain: Reduce the chance that germs or unwanted particles enter the ingredient pipes and supply tanks while they are being filled and prepared for use. This helps keep the ingredients clean.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of deviation from the required ingredient charge level in the feed lines and supply tanks
needs.plain: Keep the amount of ingredient entering the supply lines and tanks as close as possible to the required amount, so the process starts with the right quantity.

needs.stmt: Reduce the degree of residual air trapped in the ingredient feed lines after priming
needs.plain: Remove as much trapped air as possible from the ingredient tubes before use. This helps ensure the right amount of ingredient moves through the tubes.

needs.stmt: Reduce the amount of raw ingredient lost through leaks while charging the feed lines and supply tanks

preparation.name: Set operating limits
preparation.statement: Set the system limits for capacity, pressure, temperature, flow, timing, and dosing tolerance.
preparation.description: Operating limits constrain the equipment to ranges appropriate for its configuration and handled liquids.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree to which the dosing tolerance permits batch composition to deviate from its specified range
needs.plain: Make sure the amount of each ingredient added keeps the final mixture within its approved recipe range. This helps ensure every batch has the intended makeup.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that pressure, temperature, or flow limits exceed the equipment's configured safe envelope
Reduce the percent of operating limits outside validated ranges for the handled liquid and sterile preparation process
Minimize the likelihood that the combined capacity, flow, and timing limits make the required batch cycle infeasible
Reduce the number of operating limits based on ambiguous or mismatched units and reference bases

preparation.name: Test system integrity
preparation.statement: Test assembled vessels, seals, fittings, hoses, and piping for leaks or pressure loss.
preparation.description: Integrity testing detects connection or containment defects before process liquids are handled.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of leaks or pressure-loss defects remaining undetected after integrity testing
Reduce the percent of assembled connections and containment sections omitted from integrity testing
Reduce the degree of ambiguity in specified test pressures and acceptance criteria for the assembled system
Minimize the likelihood of contamination being introduced into sterile product-contact paths during integrity testing
needs.plain: Keep germs or other unwanted material from getting into the parts that touch the product while checking that the system is properly sealed. This helps protect the product from becoming unsafe.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of component rupture or uncontrolled pressure release during integrity testing
needs.plain: Make sure the equipment does not break or release pressure suddenly while it is being checked for leaks and strength. An uncontrolled release could injure people or damage the system.

preparation.name: Train system operators
preparation.statement: Train operators to prepare, start, monitor, stop, clean, and reset the batching system.
preparation.description: Deliver structured training to process operators covering system startup, batch execution, monitoring, shutdown, cleaning, and fault response procedures.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of cleaning and reset exercises completed with contamination hazards
Reduce the percentage of critical startup, batch execution, monitoring, and shutdown steps performed incorrectly during qualification
Reduce the number of abnormal conditions and system faults handled incorrectly during qualification
Reduce the number of conflicts between training instructions, approved procedures, and the current batching system configuration
Reduce the duration of operator qualification delays caused by unavailable prerequisites, trainers, or batching system access
needs.plain: Avoid delays in preparing workers to run the system when required materials, instructors, or system access are missing. This lets operators become ready to work sooner.

preparation.name: Verify safety interlocks and shutdown functions
preparation.statement: Verify the configured safety interlocks, alarms, emergency stop functions, and containment sensors before system startup.
preparation.description: Combines setting up safety interlocks (#32) with verifying them before startup (#50). Verification necessarily follows and encompasses setup; at a Level-1 job granularity these are one activity.
preparation.userGroup: Safety engineers, commissioning engineers, system operators.
preparation.frequency: Once at commissioning; repeated after maintenance affecting safety systems.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of tested unsafe conditions that fail to inhibit startup or continued operation
needs.role: Safety engineers, commissioning engineers, system operators.

needs.stmt: Reduce the time from emergency-stop actuation until liquid transfer and hazardous motion cease
needs.role: Safety engineers, commissioning engineers, system operators.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percent of representative loss-of-containment conditions not detected by containment sensors
needs.role: Safety engineers, commissioning engineers, system operators.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of tested hazardous conditions producing missing or incorrect alarm indications
needs.role: Safety engineers, commissioning engineers, system operators.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of safety functions with conflicting acceptance criteria before startup
needs.role: Safety engineers, commissioning engineers, system operators.

maintenance.name: Back up control and recipe data
maintenance.statement: Back up batch recipes, calibration records, controller settings, user configurations, and event logs from the batching system's control platform.
maintenance.description: Periodic export and secure storage of all critical process data and system configurations to enable recovery from data loss, hardware failure, or software corruption.

needs.stmt: Minimize the percentage of required batch recipes, calibration records, controller settings, user configurations, and event logs omitted from each backup
needs.plain: Include all needed production instructions, equipment adjustment records, operating settings, user choices, and activity histories in every backup. This helps restore operations if the original information is lost.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that exported backup files are corrupted or unreadable
needs.plain: Make sure saved copies of the machine’s operating settings and product instructions are complete and can be opened.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that related recipes, calibration records, controller settings, and user configurations are captured from inconsistent system states
needs.plain: Make sure recipes, measurement records, machine settings, and user preferences are saved from one consistent version of the system. This helps prevent missing or conflicting information during recovery or transfer.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of unauthorized access to sensitive process data during export and storage
needs.plain: Protect sensitive information about how the process is run when it is copied and stored. This prevents people without permission from accessing it.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that completed backups cannot be located or retrieved when recovery is required
needs.plain: Make sure saved copies of control and recipe information can be found and opened when needed to restore it. A backup is not useful if no one can locate or retrieve it.

maintenance.name: Calibrate measurement instruments
maintenance.statement: Calibrate the flow meters, load cells, level sensors, temperature probes, and dosing devices to their reference standards to maintain measurement accuracy.
maintenance.description: Combines initial calibration at commissioning (#44, Preparation) with periodic recalibration during service life (#80, Maintenance). Periodic recalibration is the recurring Maintenance job; initial calibration at commissioning is a sub-step of Preparation commissioning covered by #46. Homing the canonical job in Maintenance.
maintenance.userGroup: Instrumentation technicians, maintenance engineers, quality personnel.
maintenance.frequency: At scheduled calibration intervals (typically annually or per quality plan).

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of introducing contamination into sterile product-contact areas during calibration
needs.plain: Prevent germs from entering areas that touch the product while measuring equipment is being adjusted. This keeps the product protected during the adjustment.
needs.role: Instrumentation technicians, maintenance engineers, quality personnel.

needs.stmt: Reduce the time the measurement device remains in service beyond its approved calibration interval
needs.role: Instrumentation technicians, maintenance engineers, quality personnel.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of calibration records assigned to the wrong measurement device or reference standard
needs.role: Instrumentation technicians, maintenance engineers, quality personnel.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of deviation from the specified tolerance after calibration
needs.role: Instrumentation technicians, maintenance engineers, quality personnel.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the required reference standard is outside its certification validity period when calibration begins
needs.role: Instrumentation technicians, maintenance engineers, quality personnel.

maintenance.name: Document maintenance history
maintenance.statement: Document inspections, calibration results, replacements, repairs, updates, and service dates for the batching system.
maintenance.description: Ongoing recording of all maintenance activities, calibration certificates, and service events to support compliance, audit trails, and equipment lifecycle management.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of completed inspections, calibrations, replacements, repairs, updates, and service events omitted from the batching-system history
needs.plain: Fewer completed checks, adjustments, part changes, repairs, updates, and other service work should be missing from the production system's history. This provides a complete record of what was done.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of maintenance records containing incorrect inspection findings, calibration results, replacement details, repair details, update details, or service dates
needs.plain: Keep maintenance records accurate about inspection results, equipment checks, replaced parts, repairs, changes, and service dates. Reliable details help workers know the machine’s true condition and service history.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of calibration and service records missing required certificates, approvals, or sign-offs
needs.plain: Fewer adjustment and repair records should be missing required certificates, approvals, or signatures. This helps show that the work was properly completed and accepted.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of maintenance events represented by conflicting records in the batching-system history
needs.plain: Make sure the batching system does not show conflicting versions of the same maintenance event. One consistent record prevents confusion about what work happened.

needs.stmt: Reduce the time between completion of a maintenance activity and its entry into the batching-system history
needs.plain: There should be less time between finishing maintenance work and recording it in the production system’s history. This keeps the work record up to date.

maintenance.name: Inspect electrical and control connections
maintenance.statement: Inspect electrical wiring, terminal connections, conduit, and control panel components of the batching system for integrity and safety.
maintenance.description: Periodic physical inspection of all electrical infrastructure associated with the batching system to detect loose connections, insulation degradation, corrosion, and potential failure points before they cause downtime or safety incidents.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of contaminants entering controlled production areas while batching-system electrical infrastructure is accessed
needs.plain: Keep dirt and other unwanted material out of the controlled production area while electrical parts of the mixing system are being accessed. This protects the cleanliness of the area.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of unexpected energization of electrical circuits while the technician accesses batching-system connections
needs.plain: Prevent electricity from unexpectedly turning on while a technician reaches the machine's mixing-system connections. This helps protect the technician from electric shock or injury.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of loose, degraded, or corroded batching-system connections remaining undetected after inspection
Minimize the percentage of required wiring and terminal connection points that remain inaccessible or obscured during inspection
needs.plain: Make all needed wires and connection points visible and reachable during inspection, so workers can check them properly and find problems.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of batching-system electrical components misidentified because records conflict with installed wiring
needs.plain: Avoid wrongly identifying electrical parts because the paperwork does not match the wires and parts that are actually installed. Accurate identification helps people inspect, repair, and operate the system safely.

maintenance.name: Inspect for wear and leaks
maintenance.statement: Inspect the batching system's tanks, piping, pumps, valves, mixers, sensors, and fittings for leaks, corrosion, deterioration, and component wear.
maintenance.description: Combines #81 (inspect for leaks, corrosion, wear) with #95 (inspect tanks, piping, pumps, valves, mixers, sensors, fittings for deterioration, wear, damage). Both describe the same periodic inspection job; #95 adds component specificity to #81.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that accessing product-contact areas during inspection introduces contamination
needs.plain: Avoid bringing dirt or germs onto surfaces that touch the product while checking for wear and leaks. This helps keep the product safe.

needs.stmt: Minimize the percentage of existing leaks, corrosion, deterioration, and component wear not detected during the inspection
