Minimize the likelihood that accessing tanks, piping, pumps, valves, mixers, sensors, or fittings for inspection causes damage or misalignment
Reduce the number of required tank, piping, pump, valve, mixer, sensor, and fitting inspection points inaccessible at the scheduled interval
needs.plain: Reduce the number of equipment inspection spots that workers cannot reach when they are due to be checked. This helps them find wear or leaks on time.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of disagreement between maintenance technicians and process engineers about whether observed deterioration requires corrective action
needs.plain: Make sure maintenance workers and process specialists agree on whether signs of wear or leaks need to be fixed. This helps ensure problems are neither ignored nor treated unnecessarily.

maintenance.name: Monitor system health
maintenance.statement: Monitor diagnostic alerts, component runtime, process trends, and equipment condition indicators of the batching system.
maintenance.description: Combines #83 (monitor operational health and status) with #97 (monitor diagnostic alerts, component runtime, process trends, condition indicators). #97 adds specificity to #83; both describe the same continuous monitoring job.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that critical diagnostic alerts go unnoticed during production
Reduce the frequency of false diagnostic alerts that obscure actionable batching system faults
Minimize the likelihood of process trends being misinterpreted as normal equipment behavior
Reduce the duration of equipment condition indicator unavailability during production
needs.plain: Spend less time without information showing whether the equipment is working properly during production. This helps staff notice problems and respond sooner.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percent of component runtime records containing inaccurate values during production
needs.plain: Record fewer incorrect readings about how long machine parts have been running during production. Accurate records help workers understand the machine’s condition and maintenance needs.

maintenance.name: Repair faults and failures
maintenance.statement: Diagnose and repair faults, breakdowns, and failed components in the system.
maintenance.description: Faults, breakdowns, and failed components must be diagnosed and repaired to restore operation.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of introducing contamination into exposed product-contact areas during repair
needs.plain: Keep exposed areas that touch the product free from contamination while workers fix a problem.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of technician exposure to hazardous pressure, stored energy, or process liquid during diagnosis and repair
needs.plain: Protect technicians from dangerous pressure, trapped energy, or process liquid while they find and fix a problem.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of identifying the incorrect source of a batching-system fault
Reduce the number of additional batching-system components damaged during disassembly or repair
Reduce the time the batching system waits for a required replacement component after diagnosis
needs.plain: Get the needed replacement part quickly after finding the problem, so the batching system can be repaired sooner.

maintenance.name: Replace filtration elements
maintenance.statement: Replace filters, cartridges, and other consumable filtration elements in the fluid path of the batching system.
maintenance.description: Swap-out of clogged, expired, or damaged filter media and cartridges to sustain flow rate, particulate control, and fluid quality.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of contamination entering the open fluid path during element replacement
needs.plain: Replacing a filter or similar part should not let dirt or germs enter the open path carrying the liquid or material. This helps protect the product from contamination.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of technician exposure to residual pressure or hazardous fluid while opening the filter housing
needs.plain: Prevent a worker from being exposed to trapped pressure or harmful liquid when opening the filter container. This helps avoid injury or contact with the liquid.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of installing an incorrect filtration element in the batching system
Minimize the likelihood of fluid leaking from the filter housing after replacement
Reduce the duration of batching system downtime caused by an unavailable replacement element
needs.plain: Keep the equipment that prepares products in batches from being out of service for long when a replacement filter is not available. This helps work resume sooner when the filter needs changing.

maintenance.name: Replace seals and gaskets
maintenance.statement: Replace worn seals, gaskets, O-rings, diaphragms, and packing on pumps, valves, and connections within the batching system.
maintenance.description: Combines #79 (replace seals, gaskets, O-rings on pumps, valves, connections) with #96 (replace worn seals, gaskets, diaphragms, packing). Both describe the same sealing-component replacement job with overlapping component scope.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of contamination entering the opened product-contact path during sealing-component replacement
needs.plain: Keep dirt or germs from entering the opened product path while replacing seals and gaskets. This protects the product from contamination during the repair.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of releasing residual pressure or process liquid when opening a pump, valve, or connection
needs.plain: Prevent trapped pressure or process liquid from suddenly escaping when opening a pump, valve, or connection. This helps protect the worker and prevent spills.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of installing a sealing component incompatible with the process fluid, operating conditions, or hygienic requirements
Reduce the number of serviced pumps, valves, and connections that leak after reassembly
Reduce the duration of batching-system unavailability caused by sealing-component replacement
needs.plain: Keep the equipment that prepares products in batches from being unavailable for long while worn sealing parts are replaced. This helps prevent leaks and lets production restart sooner.

maintenance.name: Run clean-in-place cycle
maintenance.statement: Run the clean-in-place or sanitization cycle through the piping and vessels of the batching system.
maintenance.description: Execution of an in-situ cleaning or sanitization sequence through the system's internal fluid pathways to remove microbial and chemical residue without disassembly.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of internal fluid pathways retaining microbial or chemical residue after the cycle
needs.plain: Remove germs and chemical residue from the inside passages that carry liquid. Leftover residue could contaminate the next product.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that cleaning solution bypasses required piping or vessels during the cycle
needs.plain: Make sure the cleaning liquid flows through every required pipe and container instead of skipping any part of the equipment.

needs.stmt: Minimize the duration that cleaning temperature, flow, or chemical concentration remains outside validated limits during the cycle
needs.plain: Keep cleaning heat, liquid flow, and cleaning chemical strength within their approved ranges throughout the cleaning cycle. This helps ensure the equipment is cleaned properly.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that loss of required utilities interrupts the cycle before completion
needs.plain: Make it less likely that the cleaning cycle stops before it is finished because an essential supply, such as power, water, air, or another service, is lost. This helps ensure the equipment is properly cleaned.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that cleaning or sanitizing fluid escapes from the batching system during the cycle
needs.plain: Make sure cleaning or sanitizing liquid does not leak out of the mixing equipment during cleaning. This protects people, equipment, and the surrounding area.

maintenance.name: Schedule preventive maintenance
maintenance.statement: Schedule and plan preventive maintenance activities for the batching system using service intervals, operating hours, condition data, and component life limits.
maintenance.description: Systematic planning and calendaring of all scheduled maintenance tasks to minimize unplanned downtime and extend equipment service life.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of required batching-system maintenance tasks omitted from the maintenance schedule
Minimize the likelihood that maintenance due dates are calculated from incomplete or inaccurate operating and condition data
Minimize the likelihood that scheduled batching-system maintenance conflicts with sterile fill-finish production windows
Reduce the amount of scheduled batching-system maintenance work exceeding the available outage capacity
Minimize the likelihood that required personnel, parts, or approvals are unavailable at the scheduled maintenance start
needs.plain: Make sure the needed workers, replacement parts, and approvals are ready when planned maintenance is due to begin. This helps the maintenance work start on time.

maintenance.name: Service pumps and agitators
maintenance.statement: Perform routine service, lubrication, and mechanical inspection on pumps, agitators, and mechanical drives.
maintenance.description: Combines lubrication of bearings, gearboxes, shafts, and drives (#85) with routine service and lubrication of pumps and agitators (#82). Lubrication is a sub-activity within pump/agitator servicing; they are the same Maintenance job.
maintenance.frequency: At scheduled service intervals per manufacturer recommendations.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of technician exposure to stored mechanical or process energy during service
needs.plain: Protect technicians from energy that remains stored in pumps and mixing equipment while they are being serviced. This includes pressure, moving parts, and other forces that could suddenly be released.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of introducing contaminants into product-contact areas during service
needs.plain: Prevent dirt or other unwanted material from getting into areas that touch the product while equipment is being repaired or maintained. This helps protect the product from contamination.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of failing to detect wear, leakage, or lubricant degradation during mechanical inspection
Minimize the likelihood of leaving pump or agitator components misaligned or improperly secured after reassembly
Reduce the duration of batching-system unavailability beyond the scheduled maintenance window

maintenance.name: Test safety interlocks
maintenance.statement: Test emergency stops, guards, alarms, limit switches, and shutdown interlocks.
maintenance.description: Functional testing confirms that protective controls respond correctly when unsafe equipment states occur.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of malfunctioning emergency stops, guards, alarms, limit switches, or shutdown interlocks incorrectly recorded as passing the functional test
needs.plain: Avoid marking safety devices as passing when they are actually not working correctly. This includes emergency shutoffs, guards, warnings, position switches, and automatic shutdowns.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of required unsafe equipment states omitted from functional testing
needs.plain: Make sure testing includes every situation in which the equipment could become unsafe. This helps confirm that the safety shutoffs work when they are needed.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of leaving a tested protective control bypassed or incorrectly reset after functional testing
needs.plain: Make sure a safety feature that was tested is turned back on and set correctly afterward. This helps ensure the machine will respond safely if a dangerous condition occurs.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of introducing contamination into the sterile fill-finish area during functional testing
Reduce the duration of unplanned interruption to sterile fill-finish activity caused by functional testing

maintenance.name: Update control software
maintenance.statement: Update the control system software and firmware for the batching equipment.
maintenance.description: Application of software patches, firmware revisions, and driver updates to the batching system's control platform to maintain security, compliance, and functionality.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that updated control logic produces out-of-spec batching conditions
needs.plain: Reduce the chance that changed control software causes the production mixture to fall outside its required limits. This helps keep the finished product consistent and acceptable.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that validated recipes, calibration parameters, or audit records are altered during the update
Minimize the likelihood that updated control software fails required compliance validation
Reduce the duration of sterile fill-finish production unavailability beyond the maintenance window
Reduce the number of batching devices that lose communication with the control platform after the update
needs.plain: Keep mixing equipment from losing its connection to the control system after the software is updated. This helps the equipment continue communicating and operating as expected.

usage.name: Align and engage docking interface
usage.statement: Align the mating interfaces of the containment isolator docking system and engage the docking connection between the container and the isolator port.
usage.description: Combines aligning mating interfaces prior to engagement (67) with engaging the docking connection (59). Alignment is an immediate precursor to engagement and the two form one operational action at Level-1.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of containment loss at the mating interface during docking engagement
needs.plain: Keep harmful material contained where two sealed equipment parts join together. This helps prevent leaks while the connection is being made.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the docking connection remains incompletely engaged after alignment
needs.plain: Make sure the parts are fully locked together after they are lined up. This helps prevent a loose connection during use.

usage.name: Disengage docking connection
usage.statement: Disengage the docking connection while retaining the containment seal on each side.
usage.description: Undocking the container while retaining the split-barrier seal keeps both sides contained.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of the containment seal losing integrity during docking disengagement
needs.plain: Keep the protective seal intact while the connected equipment is separated. This prevents leaks or contamination from entering or escaping.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of the transfer container shifting or dropping during docking disengagement
needs.plain: Make it unlikely that a transfer container will move unexpectedly or fall when its connection is released. This matters because people or equipment could be injured or damaged.

usage.name: Document transfer operation
usage.statement: Document the details of each docked transfer operation for traceability.
usage.description: Record transfer event data including time, materials, and operator details to satisfy traceability requirements.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of docked-transfer records missing required material, operator, or event details
Minimize the likelihood that a completed docked-transfer record is unavailable for traceability review

usage.name: Equalize pressure across docked transfer interface
usage.statement: Equalize pressure across the docked transfer interface prior to barrier opening.
usage.description: Balance pressure differentials at the docking interface to prevent contamination or material dispersion when the barrier is opened.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of an acceptable pressure status being indicated while the differential remains outside limits
needs.plain: Make sure the equipment does not show a safe pressure status while the pressure difference is still outside the allowed range.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of residual pressure imbalance across the docked interface before barrier opening
needs.plain: Make the air pressure on both sides of the connected transfer point as equal as possible before opening the barrier. This helps prevent a sudden pressure change from moving contaminants or materials between the two areas.

usage.name: Handle high-volume docking cycles
usage.statement: Handle repeated high-volume docking cycles during intensive operation.
usage.description: Sustain system performance and containment integrity across a high number of consecutive docking cycles.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of containment loss during consecutive docking cycles
needs.plain: Keep the contents safely contained through repeated connections and disconnections, even when they happen many times in a row.

needs.stmt: Reduce the frequency of unplanned stoppages caused by docking-interface wear during intensive use

usage.name: Manage transfer rate through docking interface
usage.statement: Manage the rate at which material passes through the docking interface during transfer.
usage.description: Control or adjust transfer speed or flow to maintain containment integrity and meet process requirements.

needs.stmt: Reduce the frequency of material blockages at the docking interface during transfer
Minimize the degree of fluctuation in material flow through the docking interface during transfer

usage.name: Manipulate contents via gloves
usage.statement: Manipulate the docked contents through the isolator glove ports.
usage.description: Handling items inside the isolator through glove ports is required for hands-on tasks during transfer.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of glove damage compromising containment while manipulating docked contents
needs.plain: Avoid damaging the gloves while handling materials inside the sealed enclosure, because a damaged glove could let harmful material escape or enter the work area.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of unintended contacts between gloves and critical sterile surfaces during manipulation
needs.plain: Avoid touching important sterile surfaces with gloves during handling. This helps keep those surfaces clean and free from contamination.

usage.name: Monitor containment pressure
usage.statement: Monitor the containment pressure differential during the docked operation.
usage.description: Watching pressure differentials during use ensures the containment boundary is not breached.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of a containment pressure excursion remaining undetected during docked transfer
needs.plain: Make sure an unsafe change in pressure inside the enclosure is noticed during a connected transfer. This allows the problem to be addressed before containment is lost.

needs.stmt: Reduce the degree of deviation between indicated and actual containment pressure during docked transfer
needs.plain: Make sure the pressure shown during a transfer is close to the actual pressure inside the sealed area. This helps workers detect unsafe conditions and keep the transfer contained.

usage.name: Operate docking system controls
usage.statement: Operate the docking system controls to sequence each stage of the transfer cycle.
usage.description: Use the control interface to initiate, advance, pause, or halt the docking cycle stages in the correct operational sequence.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of being unable to halt the docking cycle when an unsafe condition arises
Minimize the degree of discrepancy between the displayed and actual docking-cycle stage

usage.name: Transfer material through docking interface
usage.statement: Transfer material through the docked interface between the two enclosures.
usage.description: Move material from one enclosure to the other once the docking connection is established and barriers are open.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of sterile material becoming contaminated while passing through the docked interface
needs.plain: Prevent sterile material from becoming dirty or unsafe while it passes through the connected transfer opening.

needs.stmt: Minimize the amount of material spilled into the docking interface during transfer
needs.plain: Prevent material from spilling into the connection area while it is being moved.

usage.name: Verify barrier position
usage.statement: Verify the indicated position of each docking barrier during operation.
usage.description: Confirm that each containment barrier is in its correct indicated position (open or closed) at each stage of the docking cycle.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the indicated barrier position differs from its physical position during docking
needs.plain: Make sure the position shown for the barrier matches where the barrier actually is while the systems are being connected. This helps prevent unsafe or incorrect connections.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration that the barrier position indication remains stale after movement

usage.name: Verify seal integrity
usage.statement: Verify the integrity of the seal formed at the docking connection before opening the transfer barrier.
usage.description: Confirm that the docking seal meets containment requirements prior to proceeding with barrier opening.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that a compromised docking seal is accepted as meeting containment requirements
needs.plain: Make sure a damaged connection seal is not mistakenly accepted as safe enough to contain the material. This helps prevent leaks or contamination.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that residue or condensation obscures the docking seal during verification

acquisition.name: Arrange specialized delivery
acquisition.statement: Arrange delivery suitable for the docking system's dimensions, weight, handling limits, and destination access.
acquisition.description: This reduces the risk of transport damage, unsafe handling, or failed delivery for specialized equipment.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that access clearances or load limits prevent the docking system from reaching the installation area
Reduce the degree of physical damage sustained by the docking system during transport or unloading

acquisition.name: Assess installation constraints
acquisition.statement: Assess the candidate docking system against the intended installation site constraints.
acquisition.description: Evaluate physical, structural, utility, and access constraints at the installation site to confirm the candidate system can be accommodated.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that an incompatibility between required interfaces and available site utilities is overlooked during assessment
Reduce the degree of discrepancy between recorded and actual installation-space dimensions

acquisition.name: Compare supplier proposals and references
acquisition.statement: Compare formal supplier proposals for the containment isolator docking system and seek references from existing users to support supplier selection.
acquisition.description: Combines comparing commercial proposals across candidate suppliers (11) with seeking user references and recommendations (3). Both jobs occur at the same decision point in supplier selection.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of critical containment and integration requirements left unaddressed across supplier proposals
Reduce the number of material conflicts between proposal claims and comparable-user accounts of containment, integration, and support

acquisition.name: Confirm regulatory compliance
acquisition.statement: Confirm the docking system meets applicable regulatory and industry standards.
acquisition.description: Regulated environments require documented compliance with GMP, ISO, or pharmacopeia standards prior to procurement.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of applicable regulatory requirements omitted from the docking-system compliance assessment
Minimize the likelihood that compliance evidence excludes the procured docking-system configuration or intended use

acquisition.name: Define system requirements
acquisition.statement: Define the required specifications for the containment isolator docking system prior to procurement.
acquisition.description: Establish the technical, regulatory, and operational requirements that the docking system must satisfy, forming the basis for supplier solicitation.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of docking specifications that conflict with isolator, transfer-container, or facility interfaces
needs.plain: Avoid conflicting requirements for how equipment connects to the sealed enclosure, transfer container, or building, so the parts can fit and work together.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of critical containment, sterility, or decontamination requirements omitted from the procurement specification

acquisition.name: Evaluate and compare docking system options
acquisition.statement: Evaluate available containment isolator docking system technologies, configurations, and technical documentation against containment performance, isolator compatibility, and facility exposure limits.
acquisition.description: Covers researching available options (18), reviewing technical documentation (10), comparing containment performance (20), verifying isolator/transfer-port compatibility (19), and comparing systems across containment performance, port size, and specifications (1). Consolidated into one pre-selection evaluation job.
acquisition.userGroup: Process engineer, procurement specialist, EHS/containment engineer

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of overlooking incompatibility between a candidate docking interface and the isolator transfer port
needs.role: Process engineer, procurement specialist, EHS/containment engineer

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of noncomparability among candidate containment claims produced under differing test conditions
needs.role: Process engineer, procurement specialist, EHS/containment engineer

acquisition.name: Identify eligible suppliers
acquisition.statement: Identify suppliers capable of providing the specified containment isolator docking system.
acquisition.description: This creates a viable supplier pool for a specialized item requiring relevant technical capability.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of classifying a supplier as eligible without verified sterile fill-finish capability
Reduce the number of capable docking-system suppliers omitted from the viable supplier pool

acquisition.name: Navigate procurement process
acquisition.statement: Navigate the internal procurement and approval process for the docking system.
acquisition.description: Enterprise procurement of capital equipment must move through defined approval and purchasing workflows.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the docking-system approval package is rejected because required documentation is incomplete
Minimize the degree of ambiguity about ownership and sequence of internal approvals for the docking-system purchase

acquisition.name: Obtain qualification documentation
acquisition.statement: Obtain the certification, qualification, and regulatory documentation supplied with the docking system.
acquisition.description: Collect factory test records, material certificates, declarations of conformity, and other qualification documents delivered with or shortly after the system.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of required qualification documents absent from the supplied package
Reduce the number of supplied documents whose identifiers do not match the delivered docking-system configuration

acquisition.name: Place purchase order and complete transaction
acquisition.statement: Place the approved purchase order for the selected docking system configuration and complete the associated purchase transaction.
acquisition.description: Combines placing the formal purchase order (13) with completing the purchase transaction (6). These are sequential sub-steps of the same commercial closing event and are indistinct at Level-1.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of discrepancies between purchase-order line items and the approved docking-system configuration
Reduce the amount by which the final docking-system transaction cost exceeds approved funding

acquisition.name: Qualify selected supplier
acquisition.statement: Qualify the selected docking system supplier under the applicable procurement controls.
acquisition.description: This verifies that the supplier can consistently meet technical, quality, documentation, delivery, and support obligations.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of technical, quality, delivery, and support obligations the selected supplier cannot substantiate
Reduce the number of required supplier qualification records that are missing, incomplete, or unapproved

acquisition.name: Register acquired system
acquisition.statement: Register the acquired docking system with the supplier when registration is required.
acquisition.description: This establishes the equipment record needed for warranty coverage, technical support, service notifications, or controlled document access.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of required docking-system identifiers entered incorrectly during registration
Reduce the duration that supplier confirmation of an active equipment record remains outstanding after registration

acquisition.name: Track system shipment
acquisition.statement: Track the docking system shipment through delivery completion.
acquisition.description: This enables coordination of receiving personnel, unloading equipment, controlled access, and schedule changes.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of a partial docking-system delivery being recorded as complete
Reduce the frequency of gaps in docking-system shipment location updates during transit

acquisition.name: Unbox, inspect, and receive docking system
acquisition.statement: Unbox and inspect the containment isolator docking system components for completeness and transit damage, and verify receipt against the purchase order and shipping documentation.
acquisition.description: Combines unboxing and inspecting components on arrival (32) with receiving and verifying the order against purchase and shipping documents (21). These occur at the same physical receiving event.

needs.stmt: Minimize the percentage of docking-system components with transit damage upon unboxing
Minimize the likelihood of contamination-sensitive docking interfaces being exposed by breached packaging before installation

acquisition.name: Witness factory acceptance testing
acquisition.statement: Witness factory acceptance testing for the purchased docking system.
acquisition.description: This provides pre-shipment evidence that the manufactured system matches the approved configuration and defined acceptance criteria.

needs.stmt: Minimize the percentage of defined acceptance criteria not demonstrated during factory acceptance testing
Minimize the likelihood that factory acceptance testing is conducted on a docking system that differs from the approved configuration

maintenance.name: Back up docking system configuration data
maintenance.statement: Back up the configuration and calibration data stored in the docking system controller.
maintenance.description: Export and securely store configuration parameters, calibration records, and operational settings from the docking system controller to enable recovery after a hardware failure or software update.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the stored controller backup is corrupted or unreadable when recovery is required
Reduce the number of required configuration parameters, calibration records, or controller settings omitted from the backup

maintenance.name: Calibrate monitoring sensors
maintenance.statement: Calibrate the pressure and seal-monitoring sensors on the containment isolator docking system.
maintenance.description: Restores accurate readings on pressure, alignment, and seal-force indicators used to confirm a valid dock.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of monitoring-sensor readings outside acceptance limits after calibration
Minimize the likelihood that monitoring-sensor calibration uses an expired or out-of-tolerance reference

maintenance.name: Document maintenance history
maintenance.statement: Document the maintenance and service history of the containment isolator docking system.
maintenance.description: Records inspections, seal replacements, and service events to support compliance and traceability requirements.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of inspections, seal replacements, or service events omitted from the docking-system maintenance history
Minimize the likelihood that a maintenance event is attributed to the wrong docking system or component

maintenance.name: Lubricate moving mechanisms
maintenance.statement: Lubricate the moving clamp and collar mechanisms of the containment isolator docking system.
maintenance.description: Keeps the moving latch, clamp, and rotating collar mechanisms operating smoothly to ensure reliable sealing.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of lubricant entering the docking-system containment boundary during maintenance
Minimize the likelihood of applying lubricant incompatible with docking-system seals or moving mechanisms

maintenance.name: Protect idle ports
maintenance.statement: Cover and protect the docking ports when the containment isolator docking system is not in use.
maintenance.description: Protects idle ports from contamination and mechanical damage while the docking system is not in use.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of contamination entering an idle docking port while the system is not in use
Reduce the duration the idle docking port remains unprotected after undocking

maintenance.name: Realign docking guides
maintenance.statement: Adjust and realign the docking alignment guides on the containment isolator docking system.
maintenance.description: Confirms the alignment guides hold the two halves in correct registration to ensure repeatable safe docking.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of misregistration between docking halves after guide realignment
Minimize the likelihood of realigned docking guides shifting during subsequent docking cycles

maintenance.name: Repair damaged components
maintenance.statement: Repair or replace damaged docking components on the containment isolator docking system.
maintenance.description: Removes and replaces worn or damaged docking components to return the system to service.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the repaired docking interface fails containment verification before return to service
needs.plain: The repaired connection should pass checks showing that nothing can leak out before it is used again. This helps prevent unsafe leaks during operation.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of introducing contamination into the docking interface during component repair

maintenance.name: Replace port seals
maintenance.statement: Replace the seals and gaskets on the docking ports of the containment isolator docking system.
maintenance.description: Remove worn or degraded seals and gaskets from docking ports and install compatible replacements to restore leak-tight containment at the docking interface.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the docking interface fails leak-tightness verification after seal replacement
needs.plain: Make sure the connection remains completely sealed after its seals are replaced. This prevents leaks and helps keep the equipment and product protected.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of installed docking-port seals that are twisted, pinched, or incompletely seated
needs.plain: Make sure the seals installed on the connection openings are flat, undamaged, and fully in place. This helps prevent leaks.

maintenance.name: Schedule preventive maintenance
maintenance.statement: Schedule preventive maintenance for each docking system.
maintenance.description: Plan and schedule recurring preventive maintenance activities for each containment isolator docking system unit to ensure continued performance and compliance.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of in-service docking systems omitted from the preventive maintenance schedule
Minimize the likelihood that preventive maintenance is scheduled after the docking system's compliance due date

maintenance.name: Test dock seal integrity
maintenance.statement: Test the seal integrity of the docked connection on the containment isolator docking system.
maintenance.description: Confirms the mating flanges and locking mechanism achieve and hold a leak-tight connection when docked.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of a docking-seal breach remaining undetected during integrity testing
needs.plain: Make sure a broken seal where equipment joins together cannot go unnoticed during the leak check. Finding a damaged seal helps prevent unwanted material from entering or escaping.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of failing to establish prescribed integrity-test conditions at the docked connection
needs.plain: Make it unlikely that the connection will be tested under the required conditions. This helps ensure the docked seal is working properly and will not leak.

maintenance.name: Update control software
maintenance.statement: Update the control software and firmware of the containment isolator docking system.
maintenance.description: Keeps embedded control software and firmware current to maintain compatibility and correct known issues.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of a containment breach caused by incorrect docking control after a software update
needs.plain: Prevent a software update from making the equipment connect incorrectly and allowing protection to fail. This helps keep harmful material contained.

needs.stmt: Reduce the amount of validated docking-system configuration lost or overwritten during the software update

maintenance.name: Validate communication interface with connected isolator
maintenance.statement: Validate the communication interface between the docking system and the connected isolator unit.
maintenance.description: Test the electrical and data communication paths between the docking system controller and the mated isolator to confirm signals, interlocks, and status data are transmitted correctly.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that communication cannot be established between the docking controller and connected isolator
Reduce the number of isolator interlock state changes received incorrectly by the docking controller during validation

maintenance.name: Verify interlock function
maintenance.statement: Verify the operation of the safety interlock and locking mechanism on the containment isolator docking system.
maintenance.description: Verifies the interlock and locking mechanism engages and disengages correctly to prevent unsafe undocking.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of an interlock or locking-mechanism fault remaining undetected after verification
needs.plain: Make sure a problem with the safety lock does not go unnoticed after it has been checked. This helps prevent the equipment from operating when it is not safely secured.

needs.stmt: Reduce the degree of ambiguity in determining interlock operation from conflicting mechanical and control indications
needs.plain: Make it easier to tell whether a safety feature is working when the physical signs and system messages disagree. This matters because people need to know clearly whether the equipment is safely blocked from moving or operating.

maintenance.name: Verify safety interlock override controls
maintenance.statement: Verify that manual override controls for docking safety interlocks function correctly and return to normal operation.
maintenance.description: Test manual override mechanisms on docking safety interlocks to confirm they engage and disengage correctly and that the system returns to its default safety state after override release.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of the docking safety interlock remaining overridden after manual-override release
needs.plain: Make sure the safety protection turns back on after a manual override is released. This helps prevent the equipment from operating without the required safeguard.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of the manual override failing to engage under valid test conditions
needs.plain: Make sure the manual safety override works when it is tested under the correct conditions. This helps ensure the safety control can be used when it is needed.

preparation.name: Calibrate docking sensors against approved references
preparation.statement: Calibrate docking system sensors against approved reference standards prior to operation.
preparation.description: The calibration technician verifies and adjusts docking sensors — such as position, pressure, or seal-engagement sensors — against traceable reference standards to ensure accurate system feedback and safe operation.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of docking sensors remaining outside approved tolerance after calibration
needs.plain: After checking and adjusting the docking sensors, make sure as few as possible remain outside the approved accuracy range. This helps the sensors detect connections correctly.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of applying an incorrect reference to a docking sensor during calibration

preparation.name: Decontaminate product-contact surfaces before first use
preparation.statement: Decontaminate the docking system's product-contact and transfer-path surfaces before initial operation.
preparation.description: The operator or technician applies an approved decontamination procedure to all surfaces that will contact product or be exposed during transfer operations, ensuring the system is free from contamination prior to first use.
preparation.frequency: One-time (pre-first-use); periodic thereafter per maintenance schedule

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that decontaminated product-contact or transfer-path surfaces become recontaminated before first use
needs.plain: Make it very unlikely that surfaces cleaned to be free of germs become contaminated again before they are used. This helps keep the product and its path through the equipment clean.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of treated product-contact and transfer-path surfaces failing contamination criteria before first use
needs.plain: Make sure surfaces that touch the product or carry it are clean enough before they are used for the first time. This helps prevent the product from becoming contaminated.

preparation.name: Install interface seals at docking interface
preparation.statement: Install approved seals at the docking interface prior to system commissioning.
preparation.description: The technician fits and seats approved seal components at all docking interface points, confirming correct installation before the system is leak-tested and commissioned.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of docking-interface points fitted with an incorrect or unapproved seal
needs.plain: As few connection points as possible should have the wrong seal or a seal that has not been approved. The right seal helps prevent leaks and keeps the connection safe.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of docking-interface points with an incompletely seated or twisted seal
needs.plain: Make sure each seal is fully in place and not twisted where two parts connect. This helps prevent leaks or loss of protection.

preparation.name: Record commissioning baseline configuration
preparation.statement: Record the approved commissioning baseline configuration of the docking system for reference and regulatory documentation.
preparation.description: The validation specialist documents all verified settings, test results, and as-built configuration data at the point of commissioning approval, establishing a baseline record for ongoing compliance, change control, and audit purposes.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of baseline values that differ from the verified as-built docking-system configuration
Reduce the percentage of required settings, test results, or as-built data omitted from the commissioning baseline record

disposal.name: Cancel associated service contracts
disposal.statement: Cancel service, calibration, and maintenance contracts associated with the decommissioned docking system.
disposal.description: Formally terminate all active third-party service agreements, calibration schedules, and maintenance contracts tied to the retired docking system to avoid continued charges and obligations.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of active service, calibration, or maintenance agreements overlooked during retirement closure
Reduce the amount of charges incurred after an agreement tied to the retired docking system is terminated

disposal.name: Decommission the docking system
disposal.statement: Decommission the containment isolator docking system at end of service life.
disposal.description: Execute formal end-of-service decommissioning procedures for the docking system, including all required sign-offs and regulatory steps.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of personnel exposure to residual hazardous material during docking-system decommissioning
needs.plain: Prevent workers from coming into contact with dangerous material left in the equipment while the docking system is taken out of service. This protects them during the shutdown and removal work.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of an unexpected release of stored energy or hazardous utilities during docking-system disconnection
needs.plain: Prevent stored pressure, electricity, or other dangerous supplies from being released unexpectedly while the docking equipment is disconnected. This helps protect people during shutdown and removal.

disposal.name: Discard consumable seals
disposal.statement: Discard the docking system's spent single-use seals and rings.
disposal.description: Single-use alpha rings, bags, or elastomer seals must be discarded according to their material class.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of operator exposure to hazardous residue while handling spent seals for disposal
Reduce the number of reusable docking components discarded with spent seals

disposal.name: Dismantle the docking system
disposal.statement: Dismantle the containment isolator docking system into removable sub-assemblies.
disposal.description: Systematically disassemble the docking system following approved procedures to reduce it to transportable components for waste segregation, recycling, or disposal.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of damaging the containment boundary while separating docking-system components
needs.plain: Avoid damaging the sealed barrier while taking apart the connected equipment. This helps keep the contents protected from the outside environment.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of a docking-system subassembly shifting or falling during dismantling
needs.plain: Prevent parts of the docking equipment from moving or falling while it is being taken apart. This helps protect people and nearby equipment.

disposal.name: Dispose of hazardous components
disposal.statement: Dispose of the docking system's hazardous and contaminated components through an authorized waste stream.
disposal.description: Route removed hazardous components — including contaminated materials and regulated waste — through applicable authorised disposal channels.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of contaminants being released while hazardous docking-system components are handled or transferred
needs.plain: Prevent contaminants from escaping while hazardous equipment parts are handled or moved. This protects people and the surrounding area.

needs.stmt: Minimize the percentage of hazardous docking-system components assigned an incorrect waste classification

disposal.name: Document and archive decommissioning records
disposal.statement: Document the docking system's final condition at decommissioning including decontamination status and disassembly configuration, and archive all decommissioning, decontamination, and disposal documentation.
disposal.description: Combines documenting the final state at decommissioning (115) with archiving decommissioning and disposal records (114). Documentation and archiving are performed as part of the same records-closure activity.
disposal.frequency: Once at end of service life

needs.stmt: Minimize the percentage of required decommissioning, decontamination, or disposal records absent at closure
Minimize the likelihood of archived docking-system decommissioning records being unavailable or unreadable when requested

disposal.name: Notify stakeholders of retirement
disposal.statement: Notify relevant stakeholders that the docking system has been retired.
disposal.description: Manufacturers, safety officers, and quality teams need notification when a containment asset is retired.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of required stakeholders omitted from the docking-system retirement notification
Minimize the time between retirement authorization and notification of required docking-system stakeholders

disposal.name: Recycle recoverable materials
disposal.statement: Recycle the docking system's recoverable metal and polymer components.
disposal.description: Identify and route recoverable materials — metals, polymers, and other recyclable parts — from the decommissioned docking system to appropriate recycling channels.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of docking-system components retaining hazardous residue when routed for recycling
Reduce the percentage of recoverable docking-system materials discarded as nonrecoverable waste

disposal.name: Remove from asset register
disposal.statement: Remove the retired docking system from the asset and maintenance registry.
disposal.description: Retired units must be struck from asset registers, calibration schedules, and maintenance systems.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that an active docking-system record is removed instead of the retired unit record
Reduce the number of asset, calibration, or maintenance records that remain active after docking-system retirement

disposal.name: Remove residual process materials
disposal.statement: Remove residual process materials from the docking system prior to decontamination.
disposal.description: Clear all residual powders, liquids, or other process materials retained within the docking system's cavities, ports, and transfer zones before decontamination steps begin.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of operator exposure to hazardous residual process material during docking-system clearance
needs.plain: Reduce the chance that workers come into contact with dangerous material left in the equipment while clearing the connection system. This helps protect workers during cleanup.

needs.stmt: Minimize the amount of residual process material remaining in docking-system cavities, ports, or transfer zones after clearance
needs.plain: Leave as little leftover process material as possible in the spaces, openings, and transfer areas of the connection system after cleaning or clearing it. This matters because leftover material could contaminate later work or create a safety problem.

disposal.name: Return under warranty
disposal.statement: Return the docking system to the manufacturer under warranty or recall.
disposal.description: Units with defects covered under warranty or subject to recall may be returned to the manufacturer.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of damage to the docking system during return transit
Minimize the likelihood that the manufacturer rejects the return because eligibility or authorization records are incomplete

disposal.name: Transfer ownership of the docking system
disposal.statement: Transfer ownership of the docking system and its associated records to a new owner or facility.
disposal.description: Execute the formal ownership transfer of the docking system, including documentation of asset history and regulatory status, to the receiving party.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that custody passes to the receiving party without documented docking-system contamination status
Reduce the percentage of required asset-history and ownership-chain records missing at handoff

disposal.name: Transition to replacement system
disposal.statement: Transition the containment line from the decommissioned docking system to its replacement unit.
disposal.description: Plan and execute the operational handover from the retired docking system to the replacement, ensuring continuity of containment processes.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of a containment breach while disconnecting the retired docking system and connecting its replacement
needs.plain: Keep the enclosed system from opening or leaking while the old connection is removed and the replacement is attached. This prevents material from escaping or outside contamination from entering.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of discovering incompatible containment-line interfaces during replacement-system cutover

disposal.name: Verify disposal clearance
disposal.statement: Verify the docking system's release status against applicable disposal criteria.
disposal.description: This confirms whether the equipment may be handled as reusable equipment, recyclable material, controlled waste, or hazardous waste.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of hazardous residue remaining undetected when docking-system disposal clearance is verified
Reduce the number of required contamination, material, or service records unavailable for disposal-clearance review

usage.name: Align and engage docking interface
usage.statement: Align the mating interfaces of the containment isolator docking system and engage the docking connection between the container and the isolator port.
usage.description: Combines aligning mating interfaces prior to engagement (67) with engaging the docking connection (59). Alignment is an immediate precursor to engagement and the two form one operational action at Level-1.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of containment loss at the mating interface during docking engagement
needs.plain: Keep harmful material contained where two sealed equipment parts join together. This helps prevent leaks while the connection is being made.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the docking connection remains incompletely engaged after alignment
needs.plain: Make sure the parts are fully locked together after they are lined up. This helps prevent a loose connection during use.

usage.name: Disengage docking connection
usage.statement: Disengage the docking connection while retaining the containment seal on each side.
usage.description: Undocking the container while retaining the split-barrier seal keeps both sides contained.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of the containment seal losing integrity during docking disengagement
needs.plain: Keep the protective seal intact while the connected equipment is separated. This prevents leaks or contamination from entering or escaping.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of the transfer container shifting or dropping during docking disengagement
needs.plain: Make it unlikely that a transfer container will move unexpectedly or fall when its connection is released. This matters because people or equipment could be injured or damaged.

usage.name: Document transfer operation
usage.statement: Document the details of each docked transfer operation for traceability.
usage.description: Record transfer event data including time, materials, and operator details to satisfy traceability requirements.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of docked-transfer records missing required material, operator, or event details
Minimize the likelihood that a completed docked-transfer record is unavailable for traceability review

usage.name: Equalize pressure across docked transfer interface
usage.statement: Equalize pressure across the docked transfer interface prior to barrier opening.
usage.description: Balance pressure differentials at the docking interface to prevent contamination or material dispersion when the barrier is opened.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of an acceptable pressure status being indicated while the differential remains outside limits
needs.plain: Make sure the equipment does not show a safe pressure status while the pressure difference is still outside the allowed range.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of residual pressure imbalance across the docked interface before barrier opening
needs.plain: Make the air pressure on both sides of the connected transfer point as equal as possible before opening the barrier. This helps prevent a sudden pressure change from moving contaminants or materials between the two areas.

usage.name: Handle high-volume docking cycles
usage.statement: Handle repeated high-volume docking cycles during intensive operation.
usage.description: Sustain system performance and containment integrity across a high number of consecutive docking cycles.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of containment loss during consecutive docking cycles
needs.plain: Keep the contents safely contained through repeated connections and disconnections, even when they happen many times in a row.

needs.stmt: Reduce the frequency of unplanned stoppages caused by docking-interface wear during intensive use

usage.name: Manage transfer rate through docking interface
usage.statement: Manage the rate at which material passes through the docking interface during transfer.
usage.description: Control or adjust transfer speed or flow to maintain containment integrity and meet process requirements.

needs.stmt: Reduce the frequency of material blockages at the docking interface during transfer
Minimize the degree of fluctuation in material flow through the docking interface during transfer

usage.name: Manipulate contents via gloves
usage.statement: Manipulate the docked contents through the isolator glove ports.
usage.description: Handling items inside the isolator through glove ports is required for hands-on tasks during transfer.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of glove damage compromising containment while manipulating docked contents
needs.plain: Avoid damaging the gloves while handling materials inside the sealed enclosure, because a damaged glove could let harmful material escape or enter the work area.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of unintended contacts between gloves and critical sterile surfaces during manipulation
needs.plain: Avoid touching important sterile surfaces with gloves during handling. This helps keep those surfaces clean and free from contamination.

usage.name: Monitor containment pressure
usage.statement: Monitor the containment pressure differential during the docked operation.
usage.description: Watching pressure differentials during use ensures the containment boundary is not breached.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of a containment pressure excursion remaining undetected during docked transfer
needs.plain: Make sure an unsafe change in pressure inside the enclosure is noticed during a connected transfer. This allows the problem to be addressed before containment is lost.

needs.stmt: Reduce the degree of deviation between indicated and actual containment pressure during docked transfer
needs.plain: Make sure the pressure shown during a transfer is close to the actual pressure inside the sealed area. This helps workers detect unsafe conditions and keep the transfer contained.

usage.name: Operate docking system controls
usage.statement: Operate the docking system controls to sequence each stage of the transfer cycle.
usage.description: Use the control interface to initiate, advance, pause, or halt the docking cycle stages in the correct operational sequence.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of being unable to halt the docking cycle when an unsafe condition arises
Minimize the degree of discrepancy between the displayed and actual docking-cycle stage

usage.name: Transfer material through docking interface
usage.statement: Transfer material through the docked interface between the two enclosures.
usage.description: Move material from one enclosure to the other once the docking connection is established and barriers are open.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of sterile material becoming contaminated while passing through the docked interface
needs.plain: Prevent sterile material from becoming dirty or unsafe while it passes through the connected transfer opening.

needs.stmt: Minimize the amount of material spilled into the docking interface during transfer
needs.plain: Prevent material from spilling into the connection area while it is being moved.

usage.name: Verify barrier position
usage.statement: Verify the indicated position of each docking barrier during operation.
usage.description: Confirm that each containment barrier is in its correct indicated position (open or closed) at each stage of the docking cycle.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the indicated barrier position differs from its physical position during docking
needs.plain: Make sure the position shown for the barrier matches where the barrier actually is while the systems are being connected. This helps prevent unsafe or incorrect connections.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration that the barrier position indication remains stale after movement

usage.name: Verify seal integrity
usage.statement: Verify the integrity of the seal formed at the docking connection before opening the transfer barrier.
usage.description: Confirm that the docking seal meets containment requirements prior to proceeding with barrier opening.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that a compromised docking seal is accepted as meeting containment requirements
needs.plain: Make sure a damaged connection seal is not mistakenly accepted as safe enough to contain the material. This helps prevent leaks or contamination.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that residue or condensation obscures the docking seal during verification

acquisition.name: Arrange specialized delivery
acquisition.statement: Arrange delivery suitable for the docking system's dimensions, weight, handling limits, and destination access.
acquisition.description: This reduces the risk of transport damage, unsafe handling, or failed delivery for specialized equipment.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that access clearances or load limits prevent the docking system from reaching the installation area
Reduce the degree of physical damage sustained by the docking system during transport or unloading

acquisition.name: Assess installation constraints
acquisition.statement: Assess the candidate docking system against the intended installation site constraints.
acquisition.description: Evaluate physical, structural, utility, and access constraints at the installation site to confirm the candidate system can be accommodated.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that an incompatibility between required interfaces and available site utilities is overlooked during assessment
Reduce the degree of discrepancy between recorded and actual installation-space dimensions

acquisition.name: Compare supplier proposals and references
acquisition.statement: Compare formal supplier proposals for the containment isolator docking system and seek references from existing users to support supplier selection.
acquisition.description: Combines comparing commercial proposals across candidate suppliers (11) with seeking user references and recommendations (3). Both jobs occur at the same decision point in supplier selection.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of critical containment and integration requirements left unaddressed across supplier proposals
Reduce the number of material conflicts between proposal claims and comparable-user accounts of containment, integration, and support

acquisition.name: Confirm regulatory compliance
acquisition.statement: Confirm the docking system meets applicable regulatory and industry standards.
acquisition.description: Regulated environments require documented compliance with GMP, ISO, or pharmacopeia standards prior to procurement.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of applicable regulatory requirements omitted from the docking-system compliance assessment
Minimize the likelihood that compliance evidence excludes the procured docking-system configuration or intended use

acquisition.name: Define system requirements
acquisition.statement: Define the required specifications for the containment isolator docking system prior to procurement.
acquisition.description: Establish the technical, regulatory, and operational requirements that the docking system must satisfy, forming the basis for supplier solicitation.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of docking specifications that conflict with isolator, transfer-container, or facility interfaces
needs.plain: Avoid conflicting requirements for how equipment connects to the sealed enclosure, transfer container, or building, so the parts can fit and work together.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of critical containment, sterility, or decontamination requirements omitted from the procurement specification

acquisition.name: Evaluate and compare docking system options
acquisition.statement: Evaluate available containment isolator docking system technologies, configurations, and technical documentation against containment performance, isolator compatibility, and facility exposure limits.
acquisition.description: Covers researching available options (18), reviewing technical documentation (10), comparing containment performance (20), verifying isolator/transfer-port compatibility (19), and comparing systems across containment performance, port size, and specifications (1). Consolidated into one pre-selection evaluation job.
acquisition.userGroup: Process engineer, procurement specialist, EHS/containment engineer

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of overlooking incompatibility between a candidate docking interface and the isolator transfer port
needs.role: Process engineer, procurement specialist, EHS/containment engineer

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of noncomparability among candidate containment claims produced under differing test conditions
needs.role: Process engineer, procurement specialist, EHS/containment engineer

acquisition.name: Identify eligible suppliers
acquisition.statement: Identify suppliers capable of providing the specified containment isolator docking system.
acquisition.description: This creates a viable supplier pool for a specialized item requiring relevant technical capability.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of classifying a supplier as eligible without verified sterile fill-finish capability
Reduce the number of capable docking-system suppliers omitted from the viable supplier pool

acquisition.name: Navigate procurement process
acquisition.statement: Navigate the internal procurement and approval process for the docking system.
acquisition.description: Enterprise procurement of capital equipment must move through defined approval and purchasing workflows.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the docking-system approval package is rejected because required documentation is incomplete
Minimize the degree of ambiguity about ownership and sequence of internal approvals for the docking-system purchase

acquisition.name: Obtain qualification documentation
acquisition.statement: Obtain the certification, qualification, and regulatory documentation supplied with the docking system.
acquisition.description: Collect factory test records, material certificates, declarations of conformity, and other qualification documents delivered with or shortly after the system.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of required qualification documents absent from the supplied package
Reduce the number of supplied documents whose identifiers do not match the delivered docking-system configuration

acquisition.name: Place purchase order and complete transaction
acquisition.statement: Place the approved purchase order for the selected docking system configuration and complete the associated purchase transaction.
acquisition.description: Combines placing the formal purchase order (13) with completing the purchase transaction (6). These are sequential sub-steps of the same commercial closing event and are indistinct at Level-1.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of discrepancies between purchase-order line items and the approved docking-system configuration
Reduce the amount by which the final docking-system transaction cost exceeds approved funding

acquisition.name: Qualify selected supplier
acquisition.statement: Qualify the selected docking system supplier under the applicable procurement controls.
acquisition.description: This verifies that the supplier can consistently meet technical, quality, documentation, delivery, and support obligations.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of technical, quality, delivery, and support obligations the selected supplier cannot substantiate
Reduce the number of required supplier qualification records that are missing, incomplete, or unapproved

acquisition.name: Register acquired system
acquisition.statement: Register the acquired docking system with the supplier when registration is required.
acquisition.description: This establishes the equipment record needed for warranty coverage, technical support, service notifications, or controlled document access.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of required docking-system identifiers entered incorrectly during registration
Reduce the duration that supplier confirmation of an active equipment record remains outstanding after registration

acquisition.name: Track system shipment
acquisition.statement: Track the docking system shipment through delivery completion.
acquisition.description: This enables coordination of receiving personnel, unloading equipment, controlled access, and schedule changes.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of a partial docking-system delivery being recorded as complete
Reduce the frequency of gaps in docking-system shipment location updates during transit

acquisition.name: Unbox, inspect, and receive docking system
acquisition.statement: Unbox and inspect the containment isolator docking system components for completeness and transit damage, and verify receipt against the purchase order and shipping documentation.
acquisition.description: Combines unboxing and inspecting components on arrival (32) with receiving and verifying the order against purchase and shipping documents (21). These occur at the same physical receiving event.

needs.stmt: Minimize the percentage of docking-system components with transit damage upon unboxing
Minimize the likelihood of contamination-sensitive docking interfaces being exposed by breached packaging before installation

acquisition.name: Witness factory acceptance testing
acquisition.statement: Witness factory acceptance testing for the purchased docking system.
acquisition.description: This provides pre-shipment evidence that the manufactured system matches the approved configuration and defined acceptance criteria.

needs.stmt: Minimize the percentage of defined acceptance criteria not demonstrated during factory acceptance testing
Minimize the likelihood that factory acceptance testing is conducted on a docking system that differs from the approved configuration

maintenance.name: Back up docking system configuration data
maintenance.statement: Back up the configuration and calibration data stored in the docking system controller.
maintenance.description: Export and securely store configuration parameters, calibration records, and operational settings from the docking system controller to enable recovery after a hardware failure or software update.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the stored controller backup is corrupted or unreadable when recovery is required
Reduce the number of required configuration parameters, calibration records, or controller settings omitted from the backup

maintenance.name: Calibrate monitoring sensors
maintenance.statement: Calibrate the pressure and seal-monitoring sensors on the containment isolator docking system.
maintenance.description: Restores accurate readings on pressure, alignment, and seal-force indicators used to confirm a valid dock.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of monitoring-sensor readings outside acceptance limits after calibration
Minimize the likelihood that monitoring-sensor calibration uses an expired or out-of-tolerance reference

maintenance.name: Document maintenance history
maintenance.statement: Document the maintenance and service history of the containment isolator docking system.
maintenance.description: Records inspections, seal replacements, and service events to support compliance and traceability requirements.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of inspections, seal replacements, or service events omitted from the docking-system maintenance history
Minimize the likelihood that a maintenance event is attributed to the wrong docking system or component

maintenance.name: Lubricate moving mechanisms
maintenance.statement: Lubricate the moving clamp and collar mechanisms of the containment isolator docking system.
maintenance.description: Keeps the moving latch, clamp, and rotating collar mechanisms operating smoothly to ensure reliable sealing.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of lubricant entering the docking-system containment boundary during maintenance
Minimize the likelihood of applying lubricant incompatible with docking-system seals or moving mechanisms

maintenance.name: Protect idle ports
maintenance.statement: Cover and protect the docking ports when the containment isolator docking system is not in use.
maintenance.description: Protects idle ports from contamination and mechanical damage while the docking system is not in use.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of contamination entering an idle docking port while the system is not in use
Reduce the duration the idle docking port remains unprotected after undocking

maintenance.name: Realign docking guides
maintenance.statement: Adjust and realign the docking alignment guides on the containment isolator docking system.
maintenance.description: Confirms the alignment guides hold the two halves in correct registration to ensure repeatable safe docking.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of misregistration between docking halves after guide realignment
Minimize the likelihood of realigned docking guides shifting during subsequent docking cycles

maintenance.name: Repair damaged components
maintenance.statement: Repair or replace damaged docking components on the containment isolator docking system.
maintenance.description: Removes and replaces worn or damaged docking components to return the system to service.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the repaired docking interface fails containment verification before return to service
needs.plain: The repaired connection should pass checks showing that nothing can leak out before it is used again. This helps prevent unsafe leaks during operation.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of introducing contamination into the docking interface during component repair

maintenance.name: Replace port seals
maintenance.statement: Replace the seals and gaskets on the docking ports of the containment isolator docking system.
maintenance.description: Remove worn or degraded seals and gaskets from docking ports and install compatible replacements to restore leak-tight containment at the docking interface.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the docking interface fails leak-tightness verification after seal replacement
needs.plain: Make sure the connection remains completely sealed after its seals are replaced. This prevents leaks and helps keep the equipment and product protected.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of installed docking-port seals that are twisted, pinched, or incompletely seated
needs.plain: Make sure the seals installed on the connection openings are flat, undamaged, and fully in place. This helps prevent leaks.

maintenance.name: Schedule preventive maintenance
maintenance.statement: Schedule preventive maintenance for each docking system.
maintenance.description: Plan and schedule recurring preventive maintenance activities for each containment isolator docking system unit to ensure continued performance and compliance.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of in-service docking systems omitted from the preventive maintenance schedule
Minimize the likelihood that preventive maintenance is scheduled after the docking system's compliance due date

maintenance.name: Test dock seal integrity
maintenance.statement: Test the seal integrity of the docked connection on the containment isolator docking system.
maintenance.description: Confirms the mating flanges and locking mechanism achieve and hold a leak-tight connection when docked.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of a docking-seal breach remaining undetected during integrity testing
needs.plain: Make sure a broken seal where equipment joins together cannot go unnoticed during the leak check. Finding a damaged seal helps prevent unwanted material from entering or escaping.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of failing to establish prescribed integrity-test conditions at the docked connection
needs.plain: Make it unlikely that the connection will be tested under the required conditions. This helps ensure the docked seal is working properly and will not leak.

maintenance.name: Update control software
maintenance.statement: Update the control software and firmware of the containment isolator docking system.
maintenance.description: Keeps embedded control software and firmware current to maintain compatibility and correct known issues.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of a containment breach caused by incorrect docking control after a software update
needs.plain: Prevent a software update from making the equipment connect incorrectly and allowing protection to fail. This helps keep harmful material contained.

needs.stmt: Reduce the amount of validated docking-system configuration lost or overwritten during the software update

maintenance.name: Validate communication interface with connected isolator
maintenance.statement: Validate the communication interface between the docking system and the connected isolator unit.
maintenance.description: Test the electrical and data communication paths between the docking system controller and the mated isolator to confirm signals, interlocks, and status data are transmitted correctly.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that communication cannot be established between the docking controller and connected isolator
Reduce the number of isolator interlock state changes received incorrectly by the docking controller during validation

maintenance.name: Verify interlock function
maintenance.statement: Verify the operation of the safety interlock and locking mechanism on the containment isolator docking system.
maintenance.description: Verifies the interlock and locking mechanism engages and disengages correctly to prevent unsafe undocking.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of an interlock or locking-mechanism fault remaining undetected after verification
needs.plain: Make sure a problem with the safety lock does not go unnoticed after it has been checked. This helps prevent the equipment from operating when it is not safely secured.

needs.stmt: Reduce the degree of ambiguity in determining interlock operation from conflicting mechanical and control indications
