needs.plain: Make it easier to tell whether a safety feature is working when the physical signs and system messages disagree. This matters because people need to know clearly whether the equipment is safely blocked from moving or operating.

maintenance.name: Verify safety interlock override controls
maintenance.statement: Verify that manual override controls for docking safety interlocks function correctly and return to normal operation.
maintenance.description: Test manual override mechanisms on docking safety interlocks to confirm they engage and disengage correctly and that the system returns to its default safety state after override release.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of the docking safety interlock remaining overridden after manual-override release
needs.plain: Make sure the safety protection turns back on after a manual override is released. This helps prevent the equipment from operating without the required safeguard.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of the manual override failing to engage under valid test conditions
needs.plain: Make sure the manual safety override works when it is tested under the correct conditions. This helps ensure the safety control can be used when it is needed.

preparation.name: Calibrate docking sensors against approved references
preparation.statement: Calibrate docking system sensors against approved reference standards prior to operation.
preparation.description: The calibration technician verifies and adjusts docking sensors — such as position, pressure, or seal-engagement sensors — against traceable reference standards to ensure accurate system feedback and safe operation.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of docking sensors remaining outside approved tolerance after calibration
needs.plain: After checking and adjusting the docking sensors, make sure as few as possible remain outside the approved accuracy range. This helps the sensors detect connections correctly.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of applying an incorrect reference to a docking sensor during calibration

preparation.name: Decontaminate product-contact surfaces before first use
preparation.statement: Decontaminate the docking system's product-contact and transfer-path surfaces before initial operation.
preparation.description: The operator or technician applies an approved decontamination procedure to all surfaces that will contact product or be exposed during transfer operations, ensuring the system is free from contamination prior to first use.
preparation.frequency: One-time (pre-first-use); periodic thereafter per maintenance schedule

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that decontaminated product-contact or transfer-path surfaces become recontaminated before first use
needs.plain: Make it very unlikely that surfaces cleaned to be free of germs become contaminated again before they are used. This helps keep the product and its path through the equipment clean.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of treated product-contact and transfer-path surfaces failing contamination criteria before first use
needs.plain: Make sure surfaces that touch the product or carry it are clean enough before they are used for the first time. This helps prevent the product from becoming contaminated.

preparation.name: Install interface seals at docking interface
preparation.statement: Install approved seals at the docking interface prior to system commissioning.
preparation.description: The technician fits and seats approved seal components at all docking interface points, confirming correct installation before the system is leak-tested and commissioned.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of docking-interface points fitted with an incorrect or unapproved seal
needs.plain: As few connection points as possible should have the wrong seal or a seal that has not been approved. The right seal helps prevent leaks and keeps the connection safe.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of docking-interface points with an incompletely seated or twisted seal
needs.plain: Make sure each seal is fully in place and not twisted where two parts connect. This helps prevent leaks or loss of protection.

preparation.name: Record commissioning baseline configuration
preparation.statement: Record the approved commissioning baseline configuration of the docking system for reference and regulatory documentation.
preparation.description: The validation specialist documents all verified settings, test results, and as-built configuration data at the point of commissioning approval, establishing a baseline record for ongoing compliance, change control, and audit purposes.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of baseline values that differ from the verified as-built docking-system configuration
Reduce the percentage of required settings, test results, or as-built data omitted from the commissioning baseline record

disposal.name: Cancel associated service contracts
disposal.statement: Cancel service, calibration, and maintenance contracts associated with the decommissioned docking system.
disposal.description: Formally terminate all active third-party service agreements, calibration schedules, and maintenance contracts tied to the retired docking system to avoid continued charges and obligations.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of active service, calibration, or maintenance agreements overlooked during retirement closure
Reduce the amount of charges incurred after an agreement tied to the retired docking system is terminated

disposal.name: Decommission the docking system
disposal.statement: Decommission the containment isolator docking system at end of service life.
disposal.description: Execute formal end-of-service decommissioning procedures for the docking system, including all required sign-offs and regulatory steps.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of personnel exposure to residual hazardous material during docking-system decommissioning
needs.plain: Prevent workers from coming into contact with dangerous material left in the equipment while the docking system is taken out of service. This protects them during the shutdown and removal work.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of an unexpected release of stored energy or hazardous utilities during docking-system disconnection
needs.plain: Prevent stored pressure, electricity, or other dangerous supplies from being released unexpectedly while the docking equipment is disconnected. This helps protect people during shutdown and removal.

disposal.name: Discard consumable seals
disposal.statement: Discard the docking system's spent single-use seals and rings.
disposal.description: Single-use alpha rings, bags, or elastomer seals must be discarded according to their material class.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of operator exposure to hazardous residue while handling spent seals for disposal
Reduce the number of reusable docking components discarded with spent seals

disposal.name: Dismantle the docking system
disposal.statement: Dismantle the containment isolator docking system into removable sub-assemblies.
disposal.description: Systematically disassemble the docking system following approved procedures to reduce it to transportable components for waste segregation, recycling, or disposal.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of damaging the containment boundary while separating docking-system components
needs.plain: Avoid damaging the sealed barrier while taking apart the connected equipment. This helps keep the contents protected from the outside environment.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of a docking-system subassembly shifting or falling during dismantling
needs.plain: Prevent parts of the docking equipment from moving or falling while it is being taken apart. This helps protect people and nearby equipment.

disposal.name: Dispose of hazardous components
disposal.statement: Dispose of the docking system's hazardous and contaminated components through an authorized waste stream.
disposal.description: Route removed hazardous components — including contaminated materials and regulated waste — through applicable authorised disposal channels.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of contaminants being released while hazardous docking-system components are handled or transferred
needs.plain: Prevent contaminants from escaping while hazardous equipment parts are handled or moved. This protects people and the surrounding area.

needs.stmt: Minimize the percentage of hazardous docking-system components assigned an incorrect waste classification

disposal.name: Document and archive decommissioning records
disposal.statement: Document the docking system's final condition at decommissioning including decontamination status and disassembly configuration, and archive all decommissioning, decontamination, and disposal documentation.
disposal.description: Combines documenting the final state at decommissioning (115) with archiving decommissioning and disposal records (114). Documentation and archiving are performed as part of the same records-closure activity.
disposal.frequency: Once at end of service life

needs.stmt: Minimize the percentage of required decommissioning, decontamination, or disposal records absent at closure
Minimize the likelihood of archived docking-system decommissioning records being unavailable or unreadable when requested

disposal.name: Notify stakeholders of retirement
disposal.statement: Notify relevant stakeholders that the docking system has been retired.
disposal.description: Manufacturers, safety officers, and quality teams need notification when a containment asset is retired.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of required stakeholders omitted from the docking-system retirement notification
Minimize the time between retirement authorization and notification of required docking-system stakeholders

disposal.name: Recycle recoverable materials
disposal.statement: Recycle the docking system's recoverable metal and polymer components.
disposal.description: Identify and route recoverable materials — metals, polymers, and other recyclable parts — from the decommissioned docking system to appropriate recycling channels.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of docking-system components retaining hazardous residue when routed for recycling
Reduce the percentage of recoverable docking-system materials discarded as nonrecoverable waste

disposal.name: Remove from asset register
disposal.statement: Remove the retired docking system from the asset and maintenance registry.
disposal.description: Retired units must be struck from asset registers, calibration schedules, and maintenance systems.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that an active docking-system record is removed instead of the retired unit record
Reduce the number of asset, calibration, or maintenance records that remain active after docking-system retirement

disposal.name: Remove residual process materials
disposal.statement: Remove residual process materials from the docking system prior to decontamination.
disposal.description: Clear all residual powders, liquids, or other process materials retained within the docking system's cavities, ports, and transfer zones before decontamination steps begin.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of operator exposure to hazardous residual process material during docking-system clearance
needs.plain: Reduce the chance that workers come into contact with dangerous material left in the equipment while clearing the connection system. This helps protect workers during cleanup.

needs.stmt: Minimize the amount of residual process material remaining in docking-system cavities, ports, or transfer zones after clearance
needs.plain: Leave as little leftover process material as possible in the spaces, openings, and transfer areas of the connection system after cleaning or clearing it. This matters because leftover material could contaminate later work or create a safety problem.

disposal.name: Return under warranty
disposal.statement: Return the docking system to the manufacturer under warranty or recall.
disposal.description: Units with defects covered under warranty or subject to recall may be returned to the manufacturer.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of damage to the docking system during return transit
Minimize the likelihood that the manufacturer rejects the return because eligibility or authorization records are incomplete

disposal.name: Transfer ownership of the docking system
disposal.statement: Transfer ownership of the docking system and its associated records to a new owner or facility.
disposal.description: Execute the formal ownership transfer of the docking system, including documentation of asset history and regulatory status, to the receiving party.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that custody passes to the receiving party without documented docking-system contamination status
Reduce the percentage of required asset-history and ownership-chain records missing at handoff

disposal.name: Transition to replacement system
disposal.statement: Transition the containment line from the decommissioned docking system to its replacement unit.
disposal.description: Plan and execute the operational handover from the retired docking system to the replacement, ensuring continuity of containment processes.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of a containment breach while disconnecting the retired docking system and connecting its replacement
needs.plain: Keep the enclosed system from opening or leaking while the old connection is removed and the replacement is attached. This prevents material from escaping or outside contamination from entering.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of discovering incompatible containment-line interfaces during replacement-system cutover

disposal.name: Verify disposal clearance
disposal.statement: Verify the docking system's release status against applicable disposal criteria.
disposal.description: This confirms whether the equipment may be handled as reusable equipment, recyclable material, controlled waste, or hazardous waste.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of hazardous residue remaining undetected when docking-system disposal clearance is verified
Reduce the number of required contamination, material, or service records unavailable for disposal-clearance review

preparation.name: Align docking mechanism
preparation.statement: Align and adjust the port's docking and locking mechanism to the mating component.
preparation.description: Aligning the docking mechanism ensures the mating port or container connects reliably without breach.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of positional or angular offset between the docking mechanism and mating component after alignment

preparation.name: Assemble port components
preparation.statement: Assemble the transfer port components in the required configuration prior to installation.
preparation.description: Some transfer port designs are supplied in sub-assemblies that must be combined before the unit is installed at the process boundary.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of sealing elements being damaged or incorrectly seated during transfer-port assembly
needs.plain: Prevent sealing parts from being damaged or placed incorrectly when a transfer opening is put together. Good seals help keep the protected area closed and clean.

preparation.name: Clean and sanitize port contact surfaces
preparation.statement: Clean and sanitize the port's transfer-contact surfaces to the required readiness condition prior to first use.
preparation.description: Removing particulates and biological burden from contact surfaces ensures the port is ready for contained transfer without cross-contamination.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of viable microorganisms remaining on port contact surfaces before first use

preparation.name: Conduct interface compatibility trial
preparation.statement: Conduct a representative trial of the candidate port with mating components before final selection.
preparation.description: Test or process engineer performs a physical fit and function trial of the candidate port against actual mating equipment to validate interface compatibility prior to committing to a purchase.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree to which the port trial configuration differs from the intended production configuration

preparation.name: Connect utilities and power
preparation.statement: Connect the port to required power, pressure, or control utilities.
preparation.description: Where powered or automated ports exist, connecting utilities is needed before operation.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of connecting the port to a utility outside specified operating limits

preparation.name: Install seals and gaskets
preparation.statement: Install the sealing rings, gaskets, and interlock components on the port.
preparation.description: Correct gasket and sealing element placement is essential to maintain containment integrity.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of port seals being installed in the wrong position or orientation

preparation.name: Mount port to enclosure
preparation.statement: Mount the transfer port into the opening of the containment enclosure.
preparation.description: Physically fitting the port into the isolator or enclosure wall is required before it can function.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of leakage at the mounted port-to-enclosure interface
needs.plain: Make sure the connection between the opening and its surrounding case does not leak. A tight connection keeps liquid or air from escaping or entering.

acquisition.name: Approve Port Supplier
acquisition.statement: Approve the proposed port supplier through the applicable procurement process.
acquisition.description: This verifies that the supplier satisfies required commercial, quality, traceability, support, and continuity controls.

needs.stmt: Minimize the percentage of applicable sterile fill-finish quality controls the proposed port supplier does not satisfy

acquisition.name: Arrange controlled delivery conditions
acquisition.statement: Arrange delivery under the specified handling and transport conditions for the port.
acquisition.description: Logistics coordinator specifies and arranges transport conditions (e.g., orientation, temperature, vibration limits) required to protect the port during shipment.

needs.stmt: Minimize the percent of shipment legs and handoffs lacking confirmed control of specified transport conditions

acquisition.name: Assess decontamination process compatibility
acquisition.statement: Assess the port's compatibility with the specified cleaning, sterilization, or decontamination process.
acquisition.description: Validation or process engineer determines whether the port materials, seals, and construction can withstand the cleaning or decontamination regime that will be applied in the intended environment.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that cumulative degradation of port materials seals or joints remains undetected across repeated decontamination cycles

acquisition.name: Confirm regulatory and certification compliance
acquisition.statement: Confirm the port meets applicable regulatory, cleanroom, and material-certification standards for the intended use.
acquisition.description: Quality or compliance specialist verifies that candidate ports carry the necessary regulatory approvals and certifications before approving them for procurement.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of required port approvals or certificates that are missing or expired

acquisition.name: Establish supply agreement
acquisition.statement: Establish a supply or framework agreement to ensure ongoing availability of matched port components.
acquisition.description: For phased or ongoing production, buyers negotiate framework agreements to secure consistent supply of matched port halves.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that agreement specifications permit incompatible port halves to be supplied

acquisition.name: Evaluate and compare candidate port solutions
acquisition.statement: Evaluate candidate contained material transfer port solutions against selection criteria, containment performance requirements, and supplier references.
acquisition.description: Covers comparing supplier offerings on features, materials, and validation documentation; seeking reference installations; evaluating stated containment performance and operator exposure ratings; and comparing eligible port configurations against approved selection criteria. Collapses four overlapping pre-selection evaluation jobs into one canonical entry.
acquisition.userGroup: Procurement engineers, process engineers, EHS/containment specialists
acquisition.frequency: Once per new port selection event or when re-qualifying suppliers

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that candidate containment claims cannot be verified against required operator exposure limits
needs.plain: Make sure claims about how well each possible opening or connection protects workers can be checked against the allowed amount of exposure. This helps people choose a safer design.
needs.role: Procurement engineers, process engineers, EHS/containment specialists

acquisition.name: Obtain supplier quotation
acquisition.statement: Obtain a quotation for the selected port configuration.
acquisition.description: Procurement specialist requests formal pricing, lead time, and commercial terms from the chosen supplier for the specified port configuration.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the quoted port configuration differs from the selected configuration

acquisition.name: Place port purchase order
acquisition.statement: Place an order for the approved contained material transfer port configuration.
acquisition.description: Purchaser or procurement specialist issues a formal purchase order for the selected and approved port configuration to the chosen supplier.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of purchase-order configuration fields that differ from the approved port configuration

acquisition.name: Research transfer port technologies
acquisition.statement: Research the available contained material transfer port technologies and their containment mechanisms.
acquisition.description: Buyer or specifier investigates the range of transfer port types, operating principles, and containment approaches available on the market before defining requirements.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of relevant contained-transfer technologies omitted from the market scan

acquisition.name: Review Qualification Evidence
acquisition.statement: Review the supplier-provided qualification evidence for the contained material transfer port.
acquisition.description: This establishes whether test reports, certificates, drawings, declarations, and validation support satisfy governance requirements.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of required port qualification artifacts missing from the supplier package

acquisition.name: Verify equipment compatibility
acquisition.statement: Verify that the transfer port is compatible with the existing enclosure, vessel, and mating port interfaces.
acquisition.description: Ports must physically and functionally mate with existing isolators, glove boxes, drums, or process vessels, so compatibility drives every purchase.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the transfer port and existing mating interface fail containment requirements after mating
needs.plain: Make sure the transfer opening and the equipment it connects to fit together tightly and keep the process sealed. This helps prevent leaks and contamination after they are joined.

acquisition.name: Verify received port traceability documentation
acquisition.statement: Verify the received port's traceability package against the purchase order requirements.
acquisition.description: Quality specialist checks that the traceability documents accompanying the delivered port (e.g., certificates of conformance, batch records) match what was specified in the purchase order.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of identifier conflicts among the delivered port traceability documents and purchase order

disposal.name: Archive lifecycle records
disposal.statement: Archive the qualification, validation, and decommissioning records for the retired contained material transfer port.
disposal.description: Consolidate and store all lifecycle documentation — including qualification, validation, and decommissioning records — in a retrievable archive.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of required port lifecycle records absent or incomplete in the archive

disposal.name: Cancel assigned services
disposal.statement: Cancel service coverage assigned to the retired contained material transfer port.
disposal.description: This prevents continued charges or scheduled support after the port leaves service.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of service coverage remaining active after transfer-port retirement

disposal.name: Decommission the transfer port
disposal.statement: Decommission the contained material transfer port from the process system.
disposal.description: Formally remove the transfer port from active service within the process system, completing all required decommissioning steps.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of residual process material escaping from the transfer port during disconnection
needs.plain: Prevent leftover process material from leaking out of the transfer opening when equipment is disconnected. This helps protect people, the surroundings, and other materials from spills or exposure.

disposal.name: Decontaminate the transfer port
disposal.statement: Decontaminate the contained material transfer port surfaces after removal from service.
disposal.description: Apply approved decontamination procedures to all internal and external surfaces of the transfer port to render it safe for handling, disposal, or transfer.

needs.stmt: Minimize the amount of hazardous residue remaining on internal or external transfer-port surfaces after decontamination
needs.plain: Leave as little dangerous leftover material as possible on the inside and outside surfaces of the transfer opening after cleaning. This helps protect people and materials from harmful traces.

disposal.name: Dispose of regulated waste components
disposal.statement: Dispose of contaminated contained material transfer port components through the applicable regulated waste stream.
disposal.description: Route components that cannot be decontaminated or recycled through the correct regulated waste disposal channel in compliance with applicable requirements.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that contaminated port components are assigned to an inapplicable regulated waste stream

disposal.name: Document final condition
disposal.statement: Document the final condition and disposition of the contained material transfer port.
disposal.description: Record the physical state, any defects, contamination status, and final disposition decision for the transfer port at end of life.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the transfer port contamination status is recorded incorrectly at end of life

disposal.name: Notify affected stakeholders of removal
disposal.statement: Notify affected stakeholders of the contained material transfer port's removal from service.
disposal.description: Communicate the retirement and removal of the transfer port to all parties whose processes, safety, or compliance are affected.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of affected stakeholders omitted from the port-removal notice

disposal.name: Purge residual material
disposal.statement: Purge residual contained material from the transfer port before disposal.
disposal.description: Matters before removal because residual contained material inside the port could pose contamination or exposure risk.

needs.stmt: Minimize the amount of contained material remaining inside the transfer port after purging
needs.plain: Leave as little material as possible inside the transfer opening after it has been flushed out. This helps prevent leftover material from being carried into the next operation.

disposal.name: Recycle recoverable components
disposal.statement: Separate and recycle the recoverable components of the transfer port.
disposal.description: Matters when port components are made of recoverable materials such as stainless steel or elastomers.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of recoverable component mass contaminated by pharmaceutical residue

disposal.name: Recycle uncontaminated materials
disposal.statement: Route uncontaminated contained material transfer port materials into an approved recycling stream.
disposal.description: Identify and divert clean, recyclable materials from the retired transfer port into the appropriate recycling channel, diverting them from landfill.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of port materials classified as uncontaminated that retain pharmaceutical or cleaning residue

disposal.name: Return defective port
disposal.statement: Return the defective transfer port to the supplier under warranty or recall.
disposal.description: Matters when a defective port is returned under warranty or a manufacturer recall.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of containment loss from the defective port during return transit

disposal.name: Seal the vacated interface
disposal.statement: Seal the enclosure interface left by the removed contained material transfer port.
disposal.description: Fit an approved blanking plate, cap, or seal to the opening left in the enclosure after the transfer port has been removed, maintaining containment integrity.

needs.stmt: Reduce the rate of containment checks that fail after the vacated enclosure interface is sealed

disposal.name: Segregate contaminated components
disposal.statement: Segregate contaminated contained material transfer port components from clean components prior to disposal.
disposal.description: Physically separate components that have contacted process materials from uncontaminated components to enable compliant disposal routing.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of contaminated port components incorrectly placed in the clean disposal stream

disposal.name: Transfer port ownership
disposal.statement: Transfer ownership of the serviceable contained material transfer port to another user or site.
disposal.description: Execute the legal and administrative steps required to transfer ownership of a retired or surplus transfer port to a new recipient.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of required ownership maintenance compliance or decontamination records missing from the port transfer package

disposal.name: Transition process to replacement port
disposal.statement: Transition the process from the retired contained material transfer port to its replacement.
disposal.description: Manage the changeover of process operations from the decommissioned transfer port to its successor, ensuring continuity and compliance.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of contamination entering the sterile process boundary during port changeover

disposal.name: Verify decontamination status
disposal.statement: Verify the decontamination status of the contained material transfer port before release from service.
disposal.description: Confirm through inspection or testing that the transfer port meets the required decontamination standard before it is released for disposal, transfer, or recycling.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of residual contamination remaining undetected during transfer-port verification
needs.plain: Make sure leftover contamination is not missed when checking the opening used to move materials between areas. This helps prevent contamination from being carried to another area.

maintenance.name: Back up port configuration data
maintenance.statement: Back up configuration and calibration data stored in electronically controlled transfer port units.
maintenance.description: Export and securely store parameter sets, calibration records, and interlock configuration data from ports with embedded electronic controls, enabling recovery after component replacement or firmware failure.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that backed-up port configuration and calibration data are corrupted or unreadable during recovery

maintenance.name: Calibrate interlock and alignment mechanisms
maintenance.statement: Calibrate and verify the port's interlock and alignment mechanisms to confirm they operate within specification.
maintenance.description: Perform dimensional and functional calibration of locking, alignment, and engagement mechanisms to ensure correct mating and interlock operation during transfers.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of an out-of-spec interlock or alignment condition remaining undetected during calibration verification

maintenance.name: Calibrate port instrumentation
maintenance.statement: Calibrate instrumentation integrated into the transfer port.
maintenance.description: This maintains the accuracy of equipped pressure, position, or status-monitoring devices.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of integrated port devices remaining outside specified tolerance after calibration

maintenance.name: Calibrate transfer port monitoring devices
maintenance.statement: Calibrate the transfer port's monitoring devices against applicable reference standards before first use.
maintenance.description: Calibrating sensors and gauges integrated into the port ensures measurement accuracy and compliance with process and regulatory requirements.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of deviation between port monitoring-device readings and applicable references during calibration

maintenance.name: Decontaminate transfer port contact surfaces
maintenance.statement: Decontaminate the port's contact surfaces to neutralise hazardous or biological residue following material transfer.
maintenance.description: Apply a validated decontamination procedure to all product-contact surfaces of the port to eliminate residual hazardous, toxic, or biological material and restore safe handling status.

needs.stmt: Reduce the amount of hazardous or biological residue remaining on port contact surfaces after decontamination
needs.plain: Remove as much dangerous or biological residue as possible from the surfaces where the transfer connection touches after cleaning. This helps prevent residue from being carried into the next process.

maintenance.name: Document maintenance history
maintenance.statement: Record all cleaning, inspection, and repair activities in the port's maintenance log.
maintenance.description: Regulated and validated processes require an auditable record of every maintenance action on containment equipment.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of completed port maintenance activities omitted from the maintenance log

maintenance.name: Inspect coupling mechanisms
maintenance.statement: Inspect the port's coupling mechanism for wear that could affect engagement.
maintenance.description: This detects deterioration in the components that connect mating port sections.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that coupling wear capable of impairing engagement remains undetected during inspection

maintenance.name: Lubricate moving components
maintenance.statement: Apply approved lubricant to the port's moving latch and rotation components.
maintenance.description: Moving latch and rotation surfaces need lubrication to operate smoothly without shedding particles.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of lubricant entering the contained transfer path during lubrication

maintenance.name: Monitor port operational status
maintenance.statement: Monitor the port's seal, interlock, and pressure status for signs of degradation or impending failure.
maintenance.description: Continuously observe available indicators of port condition — including seal state, interlock engagement, and differential pressure — to detect deterioration before a containment event occurs.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of a normal indication when the actual port seal interlock or pressure condition is unsafe
needs.plain: Make sure the equipment does not show a safe status when the connection lock or pressure is actually unsafe. This helps prevent operation under dangerous conditions.

maintenance.name: Protect idle port openings
maintenance.statement: Protect exposed port openings while the transfer port is not in use.
maintenance.description: Fit dust caps, blanking plates, or protective covers to open port interfaces when the port is not engaged in a transfer, preventing ingress of contamination and damage to sealing surfaces.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of contamination entering the transfer port while its idle opening is exposed

maintenance.name: Repair damaged port mechanisms
maintenance.statement: Repair or replace damaged latches, rings, or actuators to restore specified port function.
maintenance.description: Diagnose and remedy mechanical failures in port components such as latches, actuators, or coupling rings that prevent the port from achieving its specified engagement or containment function.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of repaired port mechanisms that fail specified engagement or containment verification

maintenance.name: Replace consumable transfer components
maintenance.statement: Replace disposable liners, sleeves, or single-use transfer components between uses.
maintenance.description: Swap out single-use or limited-life consumable elements within the port assembly to maintain specified hygiene and containment performance between transfers.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of installing a consumable that is incompatible with the port assembly

maintenance.name: Replenish port consumable stock
maintenance.statement: Replenish the inventory of port seals, liners, and single-use transfer consumables to maintain operational readiness.
maintenance.description: Track and reorder consumable port components — including seals, gaskets, and single-use liners — before stock falls below the level needed to sustain scheduled maintenance and transfer operations.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of reordered port consumables that do not match installed-port requirements

maintenance.name: Schedule preventive maintenance
maintenance.statement: Schedule and perform preventive maintenance on the transfer port assembly.
maintenance.description: Scheduled service prevents unplanned failures that would breach containment during operation.

needs.stmt: Reduce the frequency of scheduled port maintenance being displaced by sterile fill-finish production demands

maintenance.name: Store port properly
maintenance.statement: Store idle port components in protective covers to preserve sealing surfaces.
maintenance.description: Proper storage protects sealing surfaces from contamination and damage when the port is idle or in reserve.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of port sealing surfaces being scratched dented or deformed during storage

maintenance.name: Tighten mounting hardware
maintenance.statement: Tighten port mounting hardware to its specified condition.
maintenance.description: This prevents movement that could compromise alignment or sealing performance.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percent of port mounting hardware remaining below specified tightness after maintenance

maintenance.name: Update control firmware
maintenance.statement: Update the firmware of an electronically controlled transfer port.
maintenance.description: This maintains supported functionality in ports equipped with electronic controls.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of unintended change to validated port parameters or interlock behavior after the firmware update

maintenance.name: Verify closure operation
maintenance.statement: Verify that the port's closure mechanism completes its specified motion.
maintenance.description: This confirms that the port can return to its contained state after use.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of accepting incomplete port closure as complete during verification
needs.plain: Make sure a port that is only partly closed is not mistakenly accepted as fully closed during the check.

maintenance.name: Verify safety interlocks
maintenance.statement: Verify the operation of each safety interlock fitted to the port.
maintenance.description: This ensures equipped ports prevent unintended opening or incorrect transfer sequences.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of an intermittent port interlock fault remaining undetected during verification

usage.name: Clear obstruction from port passage
usage.statement: Clear a blockage or obstruction from the port passage to restore unobstructed material flow during operation.
usage.description: Removing material clogs, bridging, or foreign objects from inside the port passage while maintaining containment, to allow transfer to continue.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of containment loss while clearing the port passage
needs.plain: Prevent the barrier that keeps material contained from being lost while clearing a blockage from an opening or passage. This helps keep people and the surrounding area safe.

usage.name: Close transfer passage
usage.statement: Close the port passage after material transfer is complete.
usage.description: Actuating the internal door, valve, or disc of the port assembly to fully close the transfer pathway before undocking, restoring containment integrity on both sides.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of the port pathway remaining partially open after closure actuation
needs.plain: Make it less likely that the passage for moving liquid or product remains partly open after it is supposed to close. This helps prevent unwanted flow or contamination.

usage.name: Coordinate transfer between operators
usage.statement: Coordinate the transfer with an operator on the opposite side of the port.
usage.description: Two-sided transfers require synchronized action to open, pass, and close the port correctly.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of a containment breach caused by out-of-sequence operator actions during transfer
needs.plain: Reduce the chance that the product container or connection is damaged because workers perform transfer steps in the wrong order. This helps prevent leaks and loss of containment.

usage.name: Detach transfer container from port
usage.statement: Detach and remove the transfer container from the port interface after undocking is complete.
usage.description: Safely releasing the bag, drum, liner, or transfer vessel from the port face once the halves have been separated, ensuring the closed container is properly handled and containment is maintained.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of material escaping from the transfer container during detachment
needs.plain: Make it as unlikely as possible for material to spill or escape when a transfer container is disconnected. This protects the product, equipment, and people nearby.

usage.name: Dock transfer port halves
usage.statement: Align the alpha and beta port interfaces and dock the two mating halves of the contained transfer port to establish a sealed connection.
usage.description: Combines interface alignment (#60) and docking to achieve a sealed connection (#59). Alignment is a sub-step of the docking action and the two entries describe a single continuous operation. The merged job retains the mechanical detail of both.
usage.userGroup: Process operators, material handlers, production technicians

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of partial port engagement being mistaken for complete docking
needs.plain: A partly connected port should not appear to be fully connected. Clear confirmation of a complete connection helps prevent leaks or an unsafe transfer.
needs.role: Process operators, material handlers, production technicians

usage.name: Document transfer event
usage.statement: Record the details of each transfer performed through the port.
usage.description: Documenting transfers during use supports traceability and compliance in regulated environments.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of transfer records linked to the wrong material batch or receiving container

usage.name: Equalize pressure across port
usage.statement: Equalize the pressure differential across the connected port before opening the transfer passage.
usage.description: Balancing the pressure on both sides of the port to prevent pressure-driven material ejection, contamination ingress, or seal stress when the passage is opened.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the pressure indication falsely shows an acceptable differential across the connected port
needs.plain: Make sure the pressure reading across the connected opening is accurate and does not falsely say the pressure difference is safe or acceptable.

usage.name: Handle liquids through port
usage.statement: Pass liquid containers or fluids through the transfer port while maintaining the seal.
usage.description: Liquid transfers demand controlled passage to avoid spills that compromise containment.

needs.stmt: Reduce the amount of liquid spilled into the port interface or surrounding area during transfer

usage.name: Handle solids through port
usage.statement: Pass powders or solid components through the transfer port without loss of containment.
usage.description: Solids require handling that keeps dust and particulates contained during passage through the port.

needs.stmt: Minimize the amount of powder escaping into the operator area during passage through the port
needs.plain: As little powder as possible should escape into the area where the operator works while it is moved through the opening. This helps protect the operator and keep the area clean.

usage.name: Interrupt Material Transfer
usage.statement: Stop material movement through the port during an active transfer.
usage.description: This is required when operating conditions become abnormal or the target quantity is reached.

needs.stmt: Reduce the time material movement continues after interruption is initiated

usage.name: Isolate Compromised Port
usage.statement: Isolate the port after detecting a containment failure.
usage.description: This limits continued material release when the transfer boundary becomes compromised.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that a material-release path remains after port isolation
needs.plain: Make sure closing off a damaged connection also blocks every route through which material could escape. This helps prevent an unintended release.

usage.name: Monitor containment integrity
usage.statement: Monitor the containment boundary of the port during material transfer for signs of breach or leakage.
usage.description: Continuously observing seals, interfaces, and surrounding surfaces for visual, audible, or instrument-indicated signs of containment failure during an active transfer.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that a containment breach remains undetected during active transfer
needs.plain: Detect a failure in the protective enclosure as soon as it happens while material is being moved. Early detection helps prevent harmful material from escaping.

usage.name: Monitor transfer progress
usage.statement: Monitor the movement of material through the port during an active transfer.
usage.description: Observing the flow, volume, and condition of material passing through the open port to detect blockages, incomplete transfer, or deviations from expected transfer behaviour.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that transfer completion is declared before all material passes through the port

usage.name: Move material through port
usage.statement: Move material through the open transfer port from one contained environment to another.
usage.description: Physically transferring powder, liquid, components, or other materials through the open port passage while maintaining containment on both sides.

needs.stmt: Reduce the amount of transferred material released into the operator workspace during passage through the port
needs.plain: Release less material into the operator's work area while it is being moved through the access opening.

usage.name: Open transfer passage
usage.statement: Open the port passage to allow material to move through the sealed transfer port.
usage.description: Actuating the door, valve, or disc mechanism within the docked port assembly to create an unobstructed pathway for material transfer between the two connected environments.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of opening the passage before the port halves are securely docked and sealed
needs.plain: Do not open the transfer opening until both sides are firmly connected and sealed. This prevents leaks or contamination during transfer.

usage.name: Operate port via remote interface
usage.statement: Operate the transfer port through a remote control interface when direct manual access is not available.
usage.description: Using remote actuation, control panels, or automated systems to dock, open, close, and undock the port from a location separate from the port itself.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of the displayed port status differing from the port physical state
needs.plain: Make it less likely that the screen shows a connection as open or closed when it is actually the opposite. Accurate information helps the operator control it safely from a distance.

usage.name: Regulate material flow rate
usage.statement: Regulate the rate of material flow through the port passage during an active transfer.
usage.description: Adjusting the port opening position or associated flow controls to modulate how quickly material passes through, to avoid spillage, overloading downstream processes, or loss of containment.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that material flow through the port exceeds downstream receiving capacity

usage.name: Select compatible container size
usage.statement: Select or confirm the container or component size that is compatible with the port opening before initiating transfer.
usage.description: Determining whether the item to be transferred fits within the port's dimensional constraints, and choosing appropriately sized containers or adapters where the port supports multiple sizes.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the selected container is physically incompatible with the port opening

usage.name: Test installed port containment integrity
usage.statement: Test the installed transfer port for containment integrity and leak-tightness to the required specification before first use.
usage.description: Combines leak/integrity testing (#31) and containment integrity testing to specification (#44). Both describe the same pre-use pressure/leak test on the installed port. Qualification documentation is separately captured in the training and validation workflows.
usage.userGroup: Installation engineers, validation engineers, quality assurance
usage.frequency: Once after installation; repeated after seal replacement or any maintenance that breaks containment

needs.stmt: Reduce the degree of installed-port leakage beyond the specified acceptance limit
needs.plain: Keep leaks from the installed opening as small as possible and within the allowed limit.
needs.role: Installation engineers, validation engineers, quality assurance

usage.name: Transfer under isolator conditions
usage.statement: Operate the transfer port while maintaining the pressure and atmosphere of a connected isolator.
usage.description: Preserving isolator pressure differentials during transfer is critical for sterile or hazardous containment work.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the isolator pressure differential leaves its specified range during port operation

usage.name: Verify port connection status
usage.statement: Verify that the port connection has achieved the correct locked and sealed status before proceeding with transfer.
usage.description: Checking available indicators, interlocks, or visual cues on the transfer port to confirm the connection is fully engaged and safe to open for material passage.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that an incompletely engaged port connection appears locked and sealed during verification
needs.plain: Make sure a partly connected port cannot look fully locked and sealed. This helps prevent leaks or contamination caused by an insecure connection.

preparation.name: Align docking mechanism
preparation.statement: Align and adjust the port's docking and locking mechanism to the mating component.
preparation.description: Aligning the docking mechanism ensures the mating port or container connects reliably without breach.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of positional or angular offset between the docking mechanism and mating component after alignment

preparation.name: Assemble port components
preparation.statement: Assemble the transfer port components in the required configuration prior to installation.
preparation.description: Some transfer port designs are supplied in sub-assemblies that must be combined before the unit is installed at the process boundary.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of sealing elements being damaged or incorrectly seated during transfer-port assembly
needs.plain: Prevent sealing parts from being damaged or placed incorrectly when a transfer opening is put together. Good seals help keep the protected area closed and clean.

preparation.name: Clean and sanitize port contact surfaces
preparation.statement: Clean and sanitize the port's transfer-contact surfaces to the required readiness condition prior to first use.
preparation.description: Removing particulates and biological burden from contact surfaces ensures the port is ready for contained transfer without cross-contamination.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of viable microorganisms remaining on port contact surfaces before first use

preparation.name: Conduct interface compatibility trial
preparation.statement: Conduct a representative trial of the candidate port with mating components before final selection.
preparation.description: Test or process engineer performs a physical fit and function trial of the candidate port against actual mating equipment to validate interface compatibility prior to committing to a purchase.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree to which the port trial configuration differs from the intended production configuration

preparation.name: Connect utilities and power
preparation.statement: Connect the port to required power, pressure, or control utilities.
preparation.description: Where powered or automated ports exist, connecting utilities is needed before operation.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of connecting the port to a utility outside specified operating limits

preparation.name: Install seals and gaskets
preparation.statement: Install the sealing rings, gaskets, and interlock components on the port.
preparation.description: Correct gasket and sealing element placement is essential to maintain containment integrity.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of port seals being installed in the wrong position or orientation

preparation.name: Mount port to enclosure
preparation.statement: Mount the transfer port into the opening of the containment enclosure.
preparation.description: Physically fitting the port into the isolator or enclosure wall is required before it can function.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of leakage at the mounted port-to-enclosure interface
needs.plain: Make sure the connection between the opening and its surrounding case does not leak. A tight connection keeps liquid or air from escaping or entering.

acquisition.name: Approve Port Supplier
acquisition.statement: Approve the proposed port supplier through the applicable procurement process.
acquisition.description: This verifies that the supplier satisfies required commercial, quality, traceability, support, and continuity controls.

needs.stmt: Minimize the percentage of applicable sterile fill-finish quality controls the proposed port supplier does not satisfy

acquisition.name: Arrange controlled delivery conditions
acquisition.statement: Arrange delivery under the specified handling and transport conditions for the port.
acquisition.description: Logistics coordinator specifies and arranges transport conditions (e.g., orientation, temperature, vibration limits) required to protect the port during shipment.

needs.stmt: Minimize the percent of shipment legs and handoffs lacking confirmed control of specified transport conditions

acquisition.name: Assess decontamination process compatibility
acquisition.statement: Assess the port's compatibility with the specified cleaning, sterilization, or decontamination process.
acquisition.description: Validation or process engineer determines whether the port materials, seals, and construction can withstand the cleaning or decontamination regime that will be applied in the intended environment.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that cumulative degradation of port materials seals or joints remains undetected across repeated decontamination cycles

acquisition.name: Confirm regulatory and certification compliance
acquisition.statement: Confirm the port meets applicable regulatory, cleanroom, and material-certification standards for the intended use.
acquisition.description: Quality or compliance specialist verifies that candidate ports carry the necessary regulatory approvals and certifications before approving them for procurement.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of required port approvals or certificates that are missing or expired

acquisition.name: Establish supply agreement
acquisition.statement: Establish a supply or framework agreement to ensure ongoing availability of matched port components.
acquisition.description: For phased or ongoing production, buyers negotiate framework agreements to secure consistent supply of matched port halves.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that agreement specifications permit incompatible port halves to be supplied

acquisition.name: Evaluate and compare candidate port solutions
acquisition.statement: Evaluate candidate contained material transfer port solutions against selection criteria, containment performance requirements, and supplier references.
acquisition.description: Covers comparing supplier offerings on features, materials, and validation documentation; seeking reference installations; evaluating stated containment performance and operator exposure ratings; and comparing eligible port configurations against approved selection criteria. Collapses four overlapping pre-selection evaluation jobs into one canonical entry.
acquisition.userGroup: Procurement engineers, process engineers, EHS/containment specialists
acquisition.frequency: Once per new port selection event or when re-qualifying suppliers

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that candidate containment claims cannot be verified against required operator exposure limits
needs.plain: Make sure claims about how well each possible opening or connection protects workers can be checked against the allowed amount of exposure. This helps people choose a safer design.
needs.role: Procurement engineers, process engineers, EHS/containment specialists

acquisition.name: Obtain supplier quotation
acquisition.statement: Obtain a quotation for the selected port configuration.
acquisition.description: Procurement specialist requests formal pricing, lead time, and commercial terms from the chosen supplier for the specified port configuration.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the quoted port configuration differs from the selected configuration

acquisition.name: Place port purchase order
acquisition.statement: Place an order for the approved contained material transfer port configuration.
acquisition.description: Purchaser or procurement specialist issues a formal purchase order for the selected and approved port configuration to the chosen supplier.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of purchase-order configuration fields that differ from the approved port configuration

acquisition.name: Research transfer port technologies
acquisition.statement: Research the available contained material transfer port technologies and their containment mechanisms.
acquisition.description: Buyer or specifier investigates the range of transfer port types, operating principles, and containment approaches available on the market before defining requirements.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of relevant contained-transfer technologies omitted from the market scan

acquisition.name: Review Qualification Evidence
acquisition.statement: Review the supplier-provided qualification evidence for the contained material transfer port.
acquisition.description: This establishes whether test reports, certificates, drawings, declarations, and validation support satisfy governance requirements.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of required port qualification artifacts missing from the supplier package

acquisition.name: Verify equipment compatibility
acquisition.statement: Verify that the transfer port is compatible with the existing enclosure, vessel, and mating port interfaces.
acquisition.description: Ports must physically and functionally mate with existing isolators, glove boxes, drums, or process vessels, so compatibility drives every purchase.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the transfer port and existing mating interface fail containment requirements after mating
needs.plain: Make sure the transfer opening and the equipment it connects to fit together tightly and keep the process sealed. This helps prevent leaks and contamination after they are joined.

acquisition.name: Verify received port traceability documentation
acquisition.statement: Verify the received port's traceability package against the purchase order requirements.
acquisition.description: Quality specialist checks that the traceability documents accompanying the delivered port (e.g., certificates of conformance, batch records) match what was specified in the purchase order.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of identifier conflicts among the delivered port traceability documents and purchase order

disposal.name: Archive lifecycle records
disposal.statement: Archive the qualification, validation, and decommissioning records for the retired contained material transfer port.
disposal.description: Consolidate and store all lifecycle documentation — including qualification, validation, and decommissioning records — in a retrievable archive.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of required port lifecycle records absent or incomplete in the archive

disposal.name: Cancel assigned services
disposal.statement: Cancel service coverage assigned to the retired contained material transfer port.
disposal.description: This prevents continued charges or scheduled support after the port leaves service.

needs.stmt: Reduce the duration of service coverage remaining active after transfer-port retirement

disposal.name: Decommission the transfer port
disposal.statement: Decommission the contained material transfer port from the process system.
disposal.description: Formally remove the transfer port from active service within the process system, completing all required decommissioning steps.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of residual process material escaping from the transfer port during disconnection
needs.plain: Prevent leftover process material from leaking out of the transfer opening when equipment is disconnected. This helps protect people, the surroundings, and other materials from spills or exposure.

disposal.name: Decontaminate the transfer port
disposal.statement: Decontaminate the contained material transfer port surfaces after removal from service.
disposal.description: Apply approved decontamination procedures to all internal and external surfaces of the transfer port to render it safe for handling, disposal, or transfer.

needs.stmt: Minimize the amount of hazardous residue remaining on internal or external transfer-port surfaces after decontamination
needs.plain: Leave as little dangerous leftover material as possible on the inside and outside surfaces of the transfer opening after cleaning. This helps protect people and materials from harmful traces.

disposal.name: Dispose of regulated waste components
disposal.statement: Dispose of contaminated contained material transfer port components through the applicable regulated waste stream.
disposal.description: Route components that cannot be decontaminated or recycled through the correct regulated waste disposal channel in compliance with applicable requirements.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that contaminated port components are assigned to an inapplicable regulated waste stream

disposal.name: Document final condition
disposal.statement: Document the final condition and disposition of the contained material transfer port.
disposal.description: Record the physical state, any defects, contamination status, and final disposition decision for the transfer port at end of life.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the transfer port contamination status is recorded incorrectly at end of life

disposal.name: Notify affected stakeholders of removal
disposal.statement: Notify affected stakeholders of the contained material transfer port's removal from service.
disposal.description: Communicate the retirement and removal of the transfer port to all parties whose processes, safety, or compliance are affected.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of affected stakeholders omitted from the port-removal notice

disposal.name: Purge residual material
disposal.statement: Purge residual contained material from the transfer port before disposal.
disposal.description: Matters before removal because residual contained material inside the port could pose contamination or exposure risk.

needs.stmt: Minimize the amount of contained material remaining inside the transfer port after purging
needs.plain: Leave as little material as possible inside the transfer opening after it has been flushed out. This helps prevent leftover material from being carried into the next operation.

disposal.name: Recycle recoverable components
disposal.statement: Separate and recycle the recoverable components of the transfer port.
disposal.description: Matters when port components are made of recoverable materials such as stainless steel or elastomers.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of recoverable component mass contaminated by pharmaceutical residue

disposal.name: Recycle uncontaminated materials
disposal.statement: Route uncontaminated contained material transfer port materials into an approved recycling stream.
disposal.description: Identify and divert clean, recyclable materials from the retired transfer port into the appropriate recycling channel, diverting them from landfill.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of port materials classified as uncontaminated that retain pharmaceutical or cleaning residue

disposal.name: Return defective port
disposal.statement: Return the defective transfer port to the supplier under warranty or recall.
disposal.description: Matters when a defective port is returned under warranty or a manufacturer recall.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of containment loss from the defective port during return transit

disposal.name: Seal the vacated interface
disposal.statement: Seal the enclosure interface left by the removed contained material transfer port.
disposal.description: Fit an approved blanking plate, cap, or seal to the opening left in the enclosure after the transfer port has been removed, maintaining containment integrity.

needs.stmt: Reduce the rate of containment checks that fail after the vacated enclosure interface is sealed

disposal.name: Segregate contaminated components
disposal.statement: Segregate contaminated contained material transfer port components from clean components prior to disposal.
disposal.description: Physically separate components that have contacted process materials from uncontaminated components to enable compliant disposal routing.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of contaminated port components incorrectly placed in the clean disposal stream

disposal.name: Transfer port ownership
disposal.statement: Transfer ownership of the serviceable contained material transfer port to another user or site.
disposal.description: Execute the legal and administrative steps required to transfer ownership of a retired or surplus transfer port to a new recipient.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of required ownership maintenance compliance or decontamination records missing from the port transfer package

disposal.name: Transition process to replacement port
disposal.statement: Transition the process from the retired contained material transfer port to its replacement.
disposal.description: Manage the changeover of process operations from the decommissioned transfer port to its successor, ensuring continuity and compliance.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of contamination entering the sterile process boundary during port changeover

disposal.name: Verify decontamination status
disposal.statement: Verify the decontamination status of the contained material transfer port before release from service.
disposal.description: Confirm through inspection or testing that the transfer port meets the required decontamination standard before it is released for disposal, transfer, or recycling.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of residual contamination remaining undetected during transfer-port verification
needs.plain: Make sure leftover contamination is not missed when checking the opening used to move materials between areas. This helps prevent contamination from being carried to another area.

maintenance.name: Back up port configuration data
maintenance.statement: Back up configuration and calibration data stored in electronically controlled transfer port units.
maintenance.description: Export and securely store parameter sets, calibration records, and interlock configuration data from ports with embedded electronic controls, enabling recovery after component replacement or firmware failure.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that backed-up port configuration and calibration data are corrupted or unreadable during recovery

maintenance.name: Calibrate interlock and alignment mechanisms
maintenance.statement: Calibrate and verify the port's interlock and alignment mechanisms to confirm they operate within specification.
maintenance.description: Perform dimensional and functional calibration of locking, alignment, and engagement mechanisms to ensure correct mating and interlock operation during transfers.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of an out-of-spec interlock or alignment condition remaining undetected during calibration verification

maintenance.name: Calibrate port instrumentation
maintenance.statement: Calibrate instrumentation integrated into the transfer port.
maintenance.description: This maintains the accuracy of equipped pressure, position, or status-monitoring devices.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of integrated port devices remaining outside specified tolerance after calibration

maintenance.name: Calibrate transfer port monitoring devices
maintenance.statement: Calibrate the transfer port's monitoring devices against applicable reference standards before first use.
maintenance.description: Calibrating sensors and gauges integrated into the port ensures measurement accuracy and compliance with process and regulatory requirements.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of deviation between port monitoring-device readings and applicable references during calibration

maintenance.name: Decontaminate transfer port contact surfaces
maintenance.statement: Decontaminate the port's contact surfaces to neutralise hazardous or biological residue following material transfer.
maintenance.description: Apply a validated decontamination procedure to all product-contact surfaces of the port to eliminate residual hazardous, toxic, or biological material and restore safe handling status.

needs.stmt: Reduce the amount of hazardous or biological residue remaining on port contact surfaces after decontamination
needs.plain: Remove as much dangerous or biological residue as possible from the surfaces where the transfer connection touches after cleaning. This helps prevent residue from being carried into the next process.

maintenance.name: Document maintenance history
maintenance.statement: Record all cleaning, inspection, and repair activities in the port's maintenance log.
maintenance.description: Regulated and validated processes require an auditable record of every maintenance action on containment equipment.

needs.stmt: Reduce the number of completed port maintenance activities omitted from the maintenance log

maintenance.name: Inspect coupling mechanisms
maintenance.statement: Inspect the port's coupling mechanism for wear that could affect engagement.
maintenance.description: This detects deterioration in the components that connect mating port sections.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that coupling wear capable of impairing engagement remains undetected during inspection

maintenance.name: Lubricate moving components
maintenance.statement: Apply approved lubricant to the port's moving latch and rotation components.
maintenance.description: Moving latch and rotation surfaces need lubrication to operate smoothly without shedding particles.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of lubricant entering the contained transfer path during lubrication

maintenance.name: Monitor port operational status
maintenance.statement: Monitor the port's seal, interlock, and pressure status for signs of degradation or impending failure.
maintenance.description: Continuously observe available indicators of port condition — including seal state, interlock engagement, and differential pressure — to detect deterioration before a containment event occurs.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of a normal indication when the actual port seal interlock or pressure condition is unsafe
needs.plain: Make sure the equipment does not show a safe status when the connection lock or pressure is actually unsafe. This helps prevent operation under dangerous conditions.

maintenance.name: Protect idle port openings
maintenance.statement: Protect exposed port openings while the transfer port is not in use.
maintenance.description: Fit dust caps, blanking plates, or protective covers to open port interfaces when the port is not engaged in a transfer, preventing ingress of contamination and damage to sealing surfaces.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of contamination entering the transfer port while its idle opening is exposed

maintenance.name: Repair damaged port mechanisms
maintenance.statement: Repair or replace damaged latches, rings, or actuators to restore specified port function.
maintenance.description: Diagnose and remedy mechanical failures in port components such as latches, actuators, or coupling rings that prevent the port from achieving its specified engagement or containment function.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of repaired port mechanisms that fail specified engagement or containment verification

maintenance.name: Replace consumable transfer components
maintenance.statement: Replace disposable liners, sleeves, or single-use transfer components between uses.
maintenance.description: Swap out single-use or limited-life consumable elements within the port assembly to maintain specified hygiene and containment performance between transfers.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of installing a consumable that is incompatible with the port assembly

maintenance.name: Replenish port consumable stock
maintenance.statement: Replenish the inventory of port seals, liners, and single-use transfer consumables to maintain operational readiness.
maintenance.description: Track and reorder consumable port components — including seals, gaskets, and single-use liners — before stock falls below the level needed to sustain scheduled maintenance and transfer operations.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percentage of reordered port consumables that do not match installed-port requirements

maintenance.name: Schedule preventive maintenance
maintenance.statement: Schedule and perform preventive maintenance on the transfer port assembly.
maintenance.description: Scheduled service prevents unplanned failures that would breach containment during operation.

needs.stmt: Reduce the frequency of scheduled port maintenance being displaced by sterile fill-finish production demands

maintenance.name: Store port properly
maintenance.statement: Store idle port components in protective covers to preserve sealing surfaces.
maintenance.description: Proper storage protects sealing surfaces from contamination and damage when the port is idle or in reserve.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of port sealing surfaces being scratched dented or deformed during storage

maintenance.name: Tighten mounting hardware
maintenance.statement: Tighten port mounting hardware to its specified condition.
maintenance.description: This prevents movement that could compromise alignment or sealing performance.

needs.stmt: Reduce the percent of port mounting hardware remaining below specified tightness after maintenance

maintenance.name: Update control firmware
maintenance.statement: Update the firmware of an electronically controlled transfer port.
maintenance.description: This maintains supported functionality in ports equipped with electronic controls.

needs.stmt: Minimize the degree of unintended change to validated port parameters or interlock behavior after the firmware update

maintenance.name: Verify closure operation
maintenance.statement: Verify that the port's closure mechanism completes its specified motion.
maintenance.description: This confirms that the port can return to its contained state after use.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of accepting incomplete port closure as complete during verification
needs.plain: Make sure a port that is only partly closed is not mistakenly accepted as fully closed during the check.

maintenance.name: Verify safety interlocks
maintenance.statement: Verify the operation of each safety interlock fitted to the port.
maintenance.description: This ensures equipped ports prevent unintended opening or incorrect transfer sequences.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of an intermittent port interlock fault remaining undetected during verification

usage.name: Clear obstruction from port passage
usage.statement: Clear a blockage or obstruction from the port passage to restore unobstructed material flow during operation.
usage.description: Removing material clogs, bridging, or foreign objects from inside the port passage while maintaining containment, to allow transfer to continue.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of containment loss while clearing the port passage
needs.plain: Prevent the barrier that keeps material contained from being lost while clearing a blockage from an opening or passage. This helps keep people and the surrounding area safe.

usage.name: Close transfer passage
usage.statement: Close the port passage after material transfer is complete.
usage.description: Actuating the internal door, valve, or disc of the port assembly to fully close the transfer pathway before undocking, restoring containment integrity on both sides.

needs.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of the port pathway remaining partially open after closure actuation
needs.plain: Make it less likely that the passage for moving liquid or product remains partly open after it is supposed to close. This helps prevent unwanted flow or contamination.

usage.name: Coordinate transfer between operators
usage.statement: Coordinate the transfer with an operator on the opposite side of the port.
